source_reference.source_text: The most urgent Priority Repairs must be identified as PR -90 Repairs on Form 1105, Property Condition Assessment , and completed within 90 days after the Origination Date. All other Priority Repairs must be addressed as soon as possible and must be completed within 365 days after the Origination Date.

examples: Origination Date is 2026-03-01; PR-90 repair must be completed by 2026-05-30 (90 days after origination).
Origination Date is 2026-03-01; a non-PR-90 priority repair must be completed by 2027-03-01 (within 365 days).
If multiple PR-90 items exist, each must be completed within 90 days of the same Origination Date.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing default remedies per Loan Documents
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit completion deadlines (90 and 365 days) tied to Origination Date and Form 1105 classification.

examples: Fixed-rate multifamily mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac
Floating-rate mortgage held in a warehouse line prior to delivery
Mortgage pooled into a 55-Day Multi PC securitization

business_rules.rule_name: Liquidity use fee daily rate uses 365 or 366 days
business_rules.description: For variable-rate Mortgages under the Targeted Affordable Housing Bond Credit Enhancement Forward Contract, the annual liquidity use fee on any Freddie Mac-provided funds (or withdrawals from the principal reserve fund) must be converted to a daily fee by dividing by 365 days (366 in a leap year) and multiplying by the actual number of days elapsed since the payment or withdrawal.
business_rules.conditions: Variable-rate Mortgage where a liquidity use fee applies to a Freddie Mac payment/withdrawal to purchase tendered bonds.
business_rules.consequences: If calculated correctly, correct fee is payable to Freddie Mac; if miscalculated, servicer/seller must correct billing/remittance and may owe underpaid amounts.
business_rules.exceptions: Leap year uses 366 days instead of 365.

source_reference.source_text: The computation of the annual fee on the amount of payment or withdrawal will be converted to a daily fee by dividing by 365  days  (366 days in a leap year) and multiplying by the actual number of days elapsed since the payment or withdrawal.

examples: Annual liquidity fee is 0.50% on a $2,000,000 withdrawal; for 30 days in a non-leap year: daily fee = (0.005/365)*2,000,000*30 = $821.92.
Same withdrawal in a leap year with 10 elapsed days: daily fee = (0.005/366)*2,000,000*10 = $273.22.
Servicer uses 365 in a leap year and overstates the fee; correction is required to match the 366-day basis.
Fixed-rate multifamily mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac
Floating-rate mortgage held in a warehouse line prior to delivery
Mortgage pooled into a 55-Day Multi PC securitization

business_rules.rule_name: Commitment Fee Due 2:00 p.m. ET on 2nd Business Day
business_rules.description: For a Forward Commitment, the Seller must deliver the refundable Commitment Fee to Freddie Mac by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following Rate Lock. The fee may be cash or a letter of credit (which must meet Section 11.2). If the Mortgage is not delivered by the Mandatory Delivery Date, Freddie Mac retains the Commitment Fee and accrued interest; if delivered by the Mandatory Delivery Date and purchased, the fee is refunded per the Guide.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Rate Lock under a Forward Commitment requiring a Commitment Fee
business_rules.consequences: Late/non-delivery risks loss of refund; non-delivery by Mandatory Date causes retention
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Commitment Fee must be delivered to Freddie Mac by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following Rate Lock. At the option of the Seller, the Commitment Fee can be in the form of cash or a letter of credit. A letter of credit must satisfy the requirements set forth in Section 11.2. If the Mortgage is not delivered to Freddie Mac by the Mandatory Delivery Date in accordance with the Forward Commitment for any reason, the Commitment Fee, along with any interest accrued thereon, will be retained by Freddie Mac. If the Mortgage is delivered to Freddie Mac by the Mandatory Delivery Date and purchased by Freddie Mac, the Commitment Fee will be refunded

examples: Rate Lock occurs Monday; Seller must deliver Commitment Fee by 2:00 p.m. ET Wednesday (2nd Business Day).
Seller delivers fee on time but fails to deliver the Mortgage by the Mandatory Delivery Date; Freddie Mac retains the fee and accrued interest.
Seller delivers Mortgage by Mandatory Delivery Date and Freddie Mac purchases; Commitment Fee is refunded per Guide.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Letters of Credit Cannot Be Issued by Seller Affiliates Without Approval
business_rules.description: Unless the Seller/Servicer has Freddie Mac’s prior written approval, prohibited issuers of letters of credit include (i) the Seller/Servicer or Affiliated Persons of the Seller/Servicer in connection with a Mortgage originated or serviced by that Seller/Servicer, and (ii) Affiliates of the Borrower. This governs acceptable counterparties for letters of credit used in transactions governed by the Guide.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when a letter of credit is used for a Mortgage-related obligation
business_rules.consequences: If violated, letter of credit is not permitted; Freddie Mac may reject delivery/require replacement
business_rules.exceptions: Permitted only with Freddie Mac prior written approval

source_reference.source_text: Unless the Seller/Servicer has obtained Freddie Mac’s prior written approval, the following issuers of letters of credit are not permitted: • The Seller/Servicer or Affiliated Persons of the Seller/Servicer in connection with a Mortgage originated or serviced by that Seller/Servicer, or • Affiliates of the Borrower

examples: Seller proposes its own bank affiliate as LOC issuer for a deposit on a loan it originated; prohibited unless prior written approval obtained.
Borrower’s parent company offers to issue the LOC; prohibited because issuer is an Affiliate of the Borrower unless Freddie Mac approves.
A third-party domestic Eligible Institution issues the LOC; permitted (subject to other LOC requirements).
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Standby Letter of Credit Initial Term Minimums 6 Months or 12 Months
business_rules.description: A letter of credit must satisfy the Guide form requirements including: it must be a clean, irrevocable, unconditional standby letter of credit; name Freddie Mac as sole beneficiary; be in the amount determined by Freddie Mac; and have an initial term not less than specified minimums (e.g., not less than six months for certain early rate-lock applications, not less than twelve months for certain reserve/extended term obligations, and not less than 60 days following applicable maturity/period end for specified programs).
business_rules.conditions: Applies when a letter of credit is required/used under the Guide for obligations
business_rules.consequences: Nonconforming LOC term/form requires correction/replacement; may delay delivery/approval
business_rules.exceptions: Different form rule noted for certain SBL obligations (stated in text)

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 11, Section 11.2(d) Form of letter of credit (06/29/17)
source_reference.source_text: The letter of credit must : 1. Be a clean, irrevocable, unconditional standby letter of credit 2. Be issued for the account of the Borrower in the case of a Borrower obligation, or for the Seller/Servicer’s account in the case of the Seller/Servicer’s obligation 3. Name Freddie Mac as the sole beneficiary 4. Be in the amount determined by Freddie Mac 5. Have an initial term of : • Not less than six months for a letter of credit issued in connection with an early rate -lock application that does not have an extended term • Not less than twelve months for a letter of credit issued in connection with a Rental Achievement Agreement or any other agreement for Reserves, debt service Reserve or an early rate -lock application with an extended term • Not less than 60 days following the maturity date

examples: Early rate-lock application without extended term proposes a 90-day LOC; rejected because initial term must be not less than six months.
Reserve agreement proposes an 11-month LOC; rejected because initial term must be not less than twelve months.
Forward Commitment-related LOC expires 30 days after maturity date; rejected because term must be not less than 60 days following the maturity date.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Cash good faith deposit must be in Chapter 52 custodial account with exact naming
business_rules.description: A cash good faith deposit must be held in a Custodial Account meeting Chapter 52 requirements and the account must be designated exactly as either: “(Name of Depositor/Servicer), as custodian and/or bailee for Freddie Mac and/or various owners of interests in Mortgages,” or “Freddie Mac GFD Custodial Account.” If the abbreviated designation is used, it is deemed equivalent to the unabbreviated designation for all Purchase and Servicing Documents purposes.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when the early rate lock good faith deposit type is cash.
business_rules.consequences: If account naming/structure is wrong, Seller must remediate or risk noncompliance.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; two exact acceptable designations are provided.

source_reference.source_text: A cash good faith deposit must be held in a Custodial Account meeting the requirements of Chapter 52 and must be designated exactly as shown in one of the following: “(Name of Depositor/Servicer), as custodian and/or bailee for Freddie Mac and/or various owners of interests in Mortgages ,” or “Freddie Mac GFD Custodial Account.” However, if the Seller uses the abbreviated designation, then for all purposes of the Purchase and Servicing Documents ... the abbreviated account designation will be deemed to be the same as the unabbreviated account designation

examples: Seller opens account titled “ABC Servicing, as custodian and/or bailee for Freddie Mac...” and deposits the cash GFD—compliant.
Seller titles account “ABC Servicing Escrow Account” for GFD funds—noncompliant naming; must correct.
Seller uses “Freddie Mac GFD Custodial Account” abbreviation—deemed equivalent per Guide language.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Corrective action demand; potential contractual remedies
business_rules.extraction_notes: Highly explicit naming requirements; numeric precision lower because naming rule (not numeric).

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: When good faith deposit is waived Seller cannot secure Breakage Fee for waived portion
business_rules.description: When Freddie Mac waives all or a portion of the early rate lock good faith deposit, the waiver is intended to benefit the Borrower. The Seller is prohibited from collecting any type of security from the Borrower to secure payment of any portion of the Breakage Fee to the extent the good faith deposit is waived. Seller may still collect funds from Borrower for purposes unrelated to the waived portion.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when early rate lock application specifies a partial or full GFD waiver.
business_rules.consequences: If Seller collects prohibited security, Freddie Mac may require refund and impose remedies.
business_rules.exceptions: Seller may collect funds unrelated to the waived portion of the good faith deposit.

source_reference.source_text: Under certain circumstances, Freddie Mac may in its sole discretion waive all or a portion of the good faith deposit. The amount of the waiver will be specified in the early rate lock application. Freddie Mac’s waiver of all or a portion of the good faith deposit is intended to benefit the Borrower. The Seller is prohibited from collecting any type of security from the Borrower to secure payment of any portion of the Breakage Fee to the extent that the good faith deposit is waived. Seller may still collect funds from the Borrower for purposes unrelated to the waived portion of the good faith deposit.

examples: Freddie Mac waives $25,000 of GFD; Seller cannot require a pledge or letter of credit to cover Breakage Fee tied to that waived $25,000.
Freddie Mac waives 50% of GFD; Seller may still collect third-party report fees not connected to the waived portion.
Seller takes a deposit labeled “breakage security” equal to waived GFD amount—prohibited and must be reversed.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear prohibition; numeric precision moderate because it depends on waiver amount rather than fixed thresholds.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: SBL loss statement paid within 10 business days
business_rules.description: After Freddie Mac issues an SBL Loss Statement, the Seller/Servicer must remit the amount due to Freddie Mac within 10 Business Days after the date of the Loss Statement. Payment must be made by wire transfer to the account designated by Freddie Mac in the Loss Statement.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Freddie Mac issues an SBL Loss Statement
business_rules.consequences: If timely paid, obligation satisfied; if late, noncompliance with Guide duty
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 46SBL, e. Loss Statement (08/18/22), Remittance Due Date
source_reference.source_text: 2. Remittance Due Date. The Seller /Servicer must remit the amount due and payable to Freddie Mac identified on the Loss Statement within 10 Business Days after the date of the Loss Statement. Payment must be made by wire transfer of funds to the account as Freddie Mac designates in the Loss Statement.

examples: Loss Statement dated 2026-03-01: wire must be received by end of day on the 10th Business Day after 2026-03-01.
Loss Statement dated 2026-07-03 (Friday): Business Day count starts from 2026-07-03; remit no later than the 10th Business Day (excluding weekends/holidays).
Seller/Servicer mails a check instead of wiring funds: violates 'Payment must be made by wire transfer' requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'must remit' with 10 Business Days and wire-transfer method.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: SBL repurchase option default is 60-day delinquency or 60-day uncured non-monetary default
business_rules.description: For an SBL Mortgage, a “Repurchase Option Eligible Default” exists if the SBL Borrower is in default due to either (i) a 60 Day Delinquency, or (ii) a Non-Monetary Default that has not been cured within 60 days. This definition controls when the Seller/Servicer may be required/allowed to trigger SBL repurchase processes.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL Mortgages when Borrower default status is evaluated
business_rules.consequences: If condition exists, repurchase option timeline obligations may be triggered; if not, repurchase option not available on this basis
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 1. SBL Borrower is in default under the applicable SBL Mortgage for either (i) a 60 Day Delinquency, or (ii) a Non- Monetary Default that has not been cured within 60 days (either a “Repurchase Option Eligible Default”).

examples: Borrower misses payments and reaches 60 days delinquent as of 06/01; this is a Repurchase Option Eligible Default.
Borrower violates a reporting covenant on 05/01 and does not cure by 06/30 (60 days); this is a Repurchase Option Eligible Default.
Borrower is 30 days delinquent; repurchase option is not eligible under this definition.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Direct definitional statement with explicit 60-day thresholds.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: SBL repurchase must be elected at least 2 business days before pricing and completed within 10 business days after confirmation
business_rules.description: When an SBL Mortgage is subject to a Repurchase Option Eligible Default, the Seller/Servicer must: (i) exercise the Repurchase Option not less than two Business Days prior to the initial pricing date of a Secondary Market Transaction for which the SBL Mortgage has been preliminarily designated as collateral, and (ii) before receipt of any Freddie Mac Repurchase Notice for an SBL Mortgage subject to a SBL Repurchase Obligation. The Seller/Servicer must provide written notice of its election including specified fields, and must repurchase within 10 Business Days after receiving Freddie Mac’s written confirmation of Repurchase Price, plus pay any Repurchase Expenses.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when SBL repurchase option is being exercised due to eligible default
business_rules.consequences: If timely elected and completed, Seller/Servicer repurchases as required; if late, Freddie Mac may issue repurchase notice and enforce repurchase obligation
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 2. The Seller/Servicer must exercise the Repurchase Option (i) not less than two Business Days prior to the initial pricing date of the Secondary Market Transaction for which the SBL Mortgage has been preliminarily designated as collateral, and (ii) prior to the Seller/Servicer’s receipt of a Repurchase Notice from Freddie Mac ... 3. The Seller/Servicer must provide written notice to Freddie Mac of its Repurchase Option election ... • Freddie Mac loan number • Property name • Reason for the repurchase ... • Seller/Servicer’s estimate of the Repurchase Price ... 5. The Seller/Servicer must repurchase the SBL Mortgage within 10 Business Days following the receipt of written confirmation of the Repurchase Price from Freddie Mac.

examples: Initial pricing date is 08/15; Seller/Servicer must elect repurchase no later than 08/13 (2 business days prior) and then close repurchase within 10 business days after Freddie Mac confirms price.
Seller/Servicer sends election notice but omits the Freddie Mac loan number; notice is incomplete and must be corrected to meet the required content.
Freddie Mac confirms Repurchase Price on 09/01; Seller/Servicer must complete repurchase by 09/15 if there are no intervening holidays/weekends (10 business days).
business_rules.extraction_notes: Multiple explicit timing thresholds and required notice fields are directly stated.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: SBL loss maximum equals 10% of principal balance as of origination date
business_rules.description: For any SBL Mortgage, the maximum Seller/Servicer loss for an SBL Loss Obligation (the “Loss Maximum”) is a hard cap equal to 10% of the principal balance of the SBL Mortgage as of the Origination Date. If calculated loss exceeds the Loss Maximum, Seller/Servicer liability is limited to the Loss Maximum; otherwise Seller/Servicer is liable up to the full calculated loss amount.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when calculating Seller/Servicer SBL Loss Obligation exposure
business_rules.consequences: If calculated loss exceeds 10% cap, liability is limited to Loss Maximum; if within cap, Seller/Servicer liable up to full calculated amount
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: c. Loss Maximum (08/18/22) The maximum Seller/Servicer loss with respect to an SBL Loss Obligation (“Loss Maximum”) for any SBL Mortgage will equal ten percent of the principal balance of the SBL Mortgage as of the Origination Date for the SBL Mortgage.

The maximum Seller /S ervicer  loss with respect to an SBL Loss Obligation (“Loss Maximum”) for any SBL Mortgage will equal ten percent of the principal balance of the SBL Mortgage as of the Origination Date for the SBL Mortgage. The amount of the SBL Loss Obligation will be calculated as provided in Section 46SBL.6(d).

examples: If principal balance at origination is $8,000,000, Loss Maximum = $800,000.
If calculated SBL Loss Obligation is $900,000 on that loan, Seller/Servicer liability is capped at $800,000.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Indemnification (loss reimbursement up to Loss Maximum)
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit numeric cap (10%) with clear measurement point (principal balance as of Origination Date).

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules define the same Loss Maximum for SBL Loss Obligation exposure: the maximum Seller/Servicer loss equals 10% of the SBL Mortgage principal balance as of the Origination Date. Threshold (10%) and measurement point (principal balance as of Origination Date) are identical; context is identical (SBL Mortgages; Seller/Servicer loss obligation). Consequences align (liability capped at 10%).
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_005_008 selected as primary: more complete explanatory language (explicitly frames as 'hard cap' on liability) while preserving identical numeric cap.

business_rules.rule_name: Forward Commitment extension request due 60 days before maturity
business_rules.description: For a first extension of a Forward Commitment, the Seller must notify Freddie Mac via email of its request no later than 60 days prior to the Forward Commitment Maturity Date. The email must specify it is a first extension request and must include: a synopsis of the deal, a reason for the extension request, and a projected stabilization timeline.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller seeks a first six-month Forward Commitment extension.
business_rules.consequences: Timely complete request: Freddie can review/approve; late/incomplete: approval jeopardized.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated for the 60-day timing requirement.

source_reference.source_text: No later than 60 days prior to the Forward Commitment Maturity Date, the Seller must notify Freddie Mac via email of its request for an extension. The email must specify that the request is for a first extension, and must include the following: • A synopsis of the deal • A reason for the extension request • Projected stabilization timeline

examples: Maturity date is 2026-09-30; Seller emails request on 2026-08-01 with synopsis/reason/timeline: timely and complete.
Maturity date is 2026-09-30; Seller emails request on 2026-09-10: late (less than 60 days) and risks denial.
Seller emails on time but omits stabilization timeline: request is incomplete and may delay or prevent approval.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Refusal to extend Forward Commitment; transaction delay/cancellation

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Forward Commitment Extension Limited to One 6-Month Term
business_rules.description: A Forward Commitment Maturity Date may be extended for one six-month period if all conditions to extension are fully and timely satisfied and the extension fee is paid as set forth in the Forward Commitment. Any extension request beyond the first six months is subject to Freddie Mac’s sole discretion, and Freddie Mac may condition approval on requirements it determines (including an extension fee amount determined by Freddie Mac).
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller seeks to extend a Forward Commitment Maturity Date
business_rules.consequences: If within first 6 months, extension may be granted; beyond that, approval is discretionary
business_rules.exceptions: Subsequent extensions beyond first 6 months only by Freddie Mac sole discretion

source_reference.source_text: The Forward Commitment Maturity Date may be extended for one six-month period, upon full and timely satisfaction of each and all of the conditions to extension set forth herein and payment of the extension fee as set forth in the Forward Commitment. Any extension request beyond the first six months will be in Freddie Mac’s sole discretion and approval may be based on the satisfaction of any conditions that Freddie Mac determines in its sole discretion, including the payment of an extension fee in an amount to be determined by Freddie Mac.

examples: Seller requests a first extension of 6 months and pays the extension fee per the Forward Commitment; extension may be granted if all conditions are met.
Seller requests a second extension after the initial 6 months; Freddie Mac may approve or deny in its sole discretion and may require an additional fee amount it sets.
Seller seeks a 9-month first extension; not permitted because the first extension is limited to one 6-month period.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Forward Commitment Extension Request Email Due 60 Days Prior
business_rules.description: No later than 60 days prior to the applicable Forward Commitment Maturity Date, the Seller must notify Freddie Mac via email of its request for an extension. The email must specify whether the request is for a first extension or a subsequent extension and must include a synopsis of the deal, a reason for the extension request, and a projected stabilization timeline.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller requests any Forward Commitment extension
business_rules.consequences: If timely and complete, Freddie Mac can review; if late/incomplete, extension risk increases
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: No later than 60 days prior to the applicable Forward Commitment Maturity Date, the Seller must notify Freddie Mac via email of its request for an extension. The email must specify whether the request is for a first extension or a subsequent extension, and must include the following: A synopsis of the deal A reason for the extension request Projected stabilization timeline

examples: Forward Commitment Maturity Date is 2026-12-31; Seller emails extension request by 2026-11-01 including synopsis, reason, and stabilization timeline.
Seller emails request 45 days before maturity; request violates the 'no later than 60 days' requirement.

business_rules.rule_name: Letter of Credit Must Extend by Extension Term Plus 60 Days
business_rules.description: If a Forward Commitment Maturity Date is extended, any letter of credit (LOC) applicable to the transaction must be extended for the length of the extension plus 60 days. The Seller must provide Freddie Mac with amendments or replacements extending the LOC stated term(s) no later than 30 days after the Forward Commitment Maturity Date and no later than 30 days prior to the expiration of the applicable LOC.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when transaction has an LOC and Forward Commitment is extended
business_rules.consequences: If deadlines met, LOC remains compliant; if missed, extension conditions may be unsatisfied
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The term of any letter of credit applicable to the transaction must be extended for the length of the extension plus 60 days. The Seller must provide Freddie Mac with amendments or replacements of the applicable letters of credit, extending their respective stated terms, no later than 30 days after the Forward Commitment Maturity Date and no later than 30 days prior to the expiration of the applicable letter of credit.

examples: Extension length is 6 months; LOC term is extended by 6 months plus 60 days and the amendment is delivered within both 30-day deadlines.
LOC expires 2027-03-01; Seller must deliver amendment no later than 30 days before 2027-03-01 (i.e., by 2027-01-30) even if the 30-days-after-maturity deadline is later.
Seller extends Forward Commitment but does not extend the LOC for the extra 60 days; LOC extension is noncompliant.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review

business_rules.rule_name: Acceptance Letter Must Be Signed and Uploaded or Breakage Fee Applies
business_rules.description: When Freddie Mac accepts an early rate lock application with no modifications or with nonmaterial modifications (without a reduction of the mortgage amount), the Seller must acknowledge and upload the Acceptance Letter (including its agreement to service the Mortgage) to DMS in accordance with the terms specified in the Acceptance Letter. Failure to sign and upload the Acceptance Letter constitutes a Nondelivery, and Freddie Mac may, at its option, collect the Breakage Fee from the Seller; the Breakage Fee is calculated based on the rate locked Mortgage amount.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Freddie Mac issues Acceptance Letter for early rate lock
business_rules.consequences: If uploaded, Seller bound and delivery proceeds; if not, Nondelivery and Breakage Fee may be collected
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Seller must acknowledge and upload the Acceptance Letter, including its agreement to service the Mortgage, to DMS in accordance with the terms specified in the Acceptance Letter. Failure to sign and upload to DMS the Acceptance Letter (including the agreement to service the Mortgage) will constitute a Nondelivery and Freddie Mac, at its option, will collect the Breakage Fee from the Seller. The Breakage Fee will be calculated based on the rate locked Mortgage amount.

examples: Seller receives Acceptance Letter on Monday and uploads signed Acceptance Letter and servicing agreement to DMS per the letter instructions; no Nondelivery occurs.
Seller fails to upload the executed Acceptance Letter; this is a Nondelivery and Freddie Mac may collect a Breakage Fee based on the rate locked amount.
Seller uploads an incomplete package missing the agreement to service; treated as failure to upload the required Acceptance Letter contents.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Breakage Fee Triggered Only When Reduction Exceeds 10% of Locked Amount
business_rules.description: When Freddie Mac accepts an early rate lock application with nonmaterial modifications that include a reduced mortgage amount, no Breakage Fee is due if the reduction is 10% or less of the rate locked mortgage amount. If the reduction is more than 10% of the rate locked mortgage amount, Freddie Mac may collect a Breakage Fee calculated on the difference between 90% of the rate locked mortgage amount and the Reduced Mortgage Amount.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Freddie Mac issues Acceptance Letter with Reduced Mortgage Amount
business_rules.consequences: If reduction ≤10%, no Breakage Fee; if reduction >10%, Breakage Fee may be charged per formula
business_rules.exceptions: Applies "Except as otherwise set forth in this chapter"

source_reference.section_id: 27.19(c)(2) Breakage Fee for Reduced Mortgage Amount (04/15/21)
source_reference.source_text: If the reduction of the Mortgage amount is 10% or less of the rate locked Mortgage amount, then no Breakage Fee will be due. If the reduction of the Mortgage amount is more than 10 percent of the rate locked Mortgage amount, then Freddie Mac, at its option, will collect a Breakage Fee from the Seller calculated on the difference between 90 percent of the rate locked Mortgage amount and the Reduced Mortgage Amount.

examples: Rate locked amount $10,000,000 reduced to $9,200,000 (8% reduction): no Breakage Fee is due.
Rate locked amount $10,000,000 reduced to $8,700,000 (13% reduction): Breakage Fee may be charged on (0.90×$10,000,000) − $8,700,000 = $300,000.
Rate locked amount $5,000,000 reduced to $4,500,000 (10% reduction): no Breakage Fee is due under this section.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Delegated TAH invoice unpaid 10 business days triggers LOC draw
business_rules.description: For a Delegated TAH Mortgage, if Freddie Mac submits an invoice for loss/liability/damage/expense and the Seller does not pay within 10 Business Days after submission, Freddie Mac may draw on the Seller/Servicer's Letter of Credit or other security under the Delegated TAH Master Agreement.
business_rules.conditions: Delegated TAH Mortgage; Freddie Mac issues invoice for losses/expenses
business_rules.consequences: If unpaid in 10 BD, Freddie Mac may draw on LOC/other security
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; timing and remedy per Delegated TAH Master Agreement

source_reference.source_text: To the extent that Freddie Mac has suffered loss, liability, damage or expense, submit an invoice to the Seller for such loss, liability, damage or expense and, if not paid within 10 Business Days after submission, draw upon the Letter of Credit or other security pursuant to the Seller/Servicer’s Delegated TAH Master Agreement as to which any case or event stated in Section 47.2 relates
source_reference.confidence_note: Word positions are estimated from provided chunk excerpt.

examples: Freddie Mac invoices $75,000 loss on a Delegated TAH loan on April 1; if not paid by April 15 (10 BD), Freddie Mac may draw on the LOC.
Seller disputes invoice but does not pay within 10 BD; Freddie Mac may still draw on LOC per agreement, with dispute handled separately.
Seller pays invoice on day 8 after submission; Freddie Mac does not have basis to draw on LOC for non-payment.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Draw upon Letter of Credit or other security

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: SBL property must conform to zoning and codes and Seller must confirm certificates of occupancy
business_rules.description: For an SBL Mortgage to be eligible for purchase by Freddie Mac, the Property must conform to all applicable zoning, subdivision and use laws/ordinances/codes and local building and housing codes (a zoning report is not required). The Seller must confirm that all required certificates of occupancy for the Property’s presently-contemplated use have been issued and are in effect, or if not available, that the absence is not a violation and will not give rise to enforcement action affecting the Property.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL Mortgages at underwriting/delivery when zoning/CO compliance is assessed.
business_rules.consequences: If confirmed, eligibility condition satisfied; if not confirmed, Freddie Mac may restrict purchase.
business_rules.exceptions: If Seller cannot confirm, Freddie Mac may purchase only if Non-Permitted Unit conditions are met.

source_reference.source_text: For an SBL Mortgage to be eligible for purchase by Freddie Mac, the Property securing it must conform to all applicable zoning, subdivision and use laws, ordinances or codes and local building and housing codes. A zoning report is not required for an SBL Mortgage. The Seller must confirm that all certificates of occupancy required for the use, operation and occupancy of the Property for its presently -contemplated use have been issued and are in effect or, if certificates of occupancy are not available, that the absence of any certificate of occupancy is not a violation of local laws, ordinance, or codes, nor will it give rise to any enforcement action affecting the Property.

examples: Seller obtains evidence that COs are issued for all units and confirms code conformity: requirement satisfied.
CO documentation is missing for certain units; Seller confirms absence is not a violation and no enforcement action risk: requirement satisfied.
Seller cannot confirm CO status for several units and cannot meet Non-Permitted Unit conditions: Freddie Mac may decline purchase.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Repurchase or indemnify within 30 days; appeal within 30 days; 15 days after denial
business_rules.description: When Freddie Mac requires repurchase or indemnification, the Seller or Servicer must repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest or indemnify Freddie Mac within 30 days of Freddie Mac’s request. The Seller/Servicer may file one written appeal within 30 days from the date of Freddie Mac’s letter; if the appeal is denied, repurchase/indemnification must be completed within 15 days from the date of the denial letter. Failure to comply may result in suspension or termination of selling and/or servicing privileges or termination of servicing.
business_rules.conditions: Applies upon receipt of Freddie Mac repurchase/indemnification request letter for a Mortgage.
business_rules.consequences: If met, repurchase/indemnification completes timely; if violated, suspension/termination may occur.
business_rules.exceptions: Only the Section 47.3 appeal process modifies timing; otherwise decision is conclusive.

source_reference.source_text: Subject only to the appeal provisions of Section 47.3, Freddie Mac’s decision to require the Seller or Servicer to repurchase a Mortgage or indemnify Freddie Mac will be conclusive. The Seller or Servicer must repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest in the identified Mortgage or indemnify Freddie Mac within 30 days of Freddie Mac’s request. Failure to comply with Freddie Mac’s repurchase or indemnification requirement may result in suspension or termination of selling and/or Servicing privileges or termination of Servicing. Within 30 days from the date of Freddie Mac’s letter requiring repurchase or indemnification, the Seller or the Servicer may submit a written appeal... If the appeal is denied, the Seller or the Servicer must complete the repurchase of the Mortgage or indemnification of Freddie Mac within 15 days from the date of Freddie Mac’s denial letter.

examples: Freddie Mac letter dated May 1: Seller/Servicer must repurchase or indemnify by May 31 unless a timely appeal is filed.
Seller files appeal on day 35: appeal is unavailable; Seller must still meet the original 30-day repurchase/indemnification deadline.
Appeal denied June 10: repurchase/indemnification must be completed by June 25 (15 days after denial).
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand; suspension or termination

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_016_010 provides a general repurchase/indemnification timeline (30 days; appeal mechanics). BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_030_003 provides an SBL-specific repurchase process and 'Mandatory Repurchase Date' (10 Business Days after receipt of Repurchase Notice). In an automated workflow, the more specific SBL rule should control the deadline calculation to prevent false compliance comfort under a longer general timeframe.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the override relationship is not implemented: (1) Seller may rely on 30-day window and miss the 10-BD SBL deadline, (2) repurchase becomes late/defaulted under Guide, (3) Freddie Mac remedies/escalations are more likely, (4) appeals may be mishandled if the wrong clock is applied.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_022_007 sets a securitization ineligibility threshold post-Condemnation and explicitly contemplates Freddie Mac enforcing repurchase. When enforcement occurs, BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_016_010 provides the general operational rule for completing repurchase/indemnification after a Freddie Mac request letter, including appeal steps.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_022_007 is triggered but BR_016_010 is not followed: (1) repurchase completion may be late, (2) appeal rights may be lost or mishandled, (3) Seller/Servicer faces suspension/termination risk per BR_016_010 consequences.

dependencies.rationale: BR_MORTGAGE_PROHIBITION_001_003 describes a prohibited advance-of-funds practice in the 3 months pre-delivery that supports the 'Mortgage is not in default' condition and can cause delivery/purchase rejection. If such a defect is discovered after purchase, it can be a basis for Freddie Mac to require repurchase or indemnification, activating the timelines and appeal process in BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_016_010.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_MORTGAGE_PROHIBITION_001_003 is violated and later discovered, Freddie Mac may issue a repurchase/indemnification request; Seller/Servicer must comply within 30 days (or appeal and then comply within 15 days after denial) or face suspension/termination risk.

dependencies.rationale: BR_MORTGAGE_ELIGIBILITY_007_004 establishes a hard ineligibility condition for sale. If the loan is nevertheless delivered/purchased and later found to violate this condition (e.g., PML > 40% with no retrofit), Freddie Mac may pursue contractual remedies via repurchase/indemnification, which are executed under the deadlines and appeal mechanics in BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_016_010.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Failure of BR_MORTGAGE_ELIGIBILITY_007_004 can escalate to a repurchase/indemnification demand, triggering strict response timelines and potential counterparty sanctions if not met.

dependencies.rationale: BR_MORTGAGE_ELIGIBILITY_007_005 creates a sale ineligibility condition tied to SFHA status, NFIP participation, and required flood insurance. If the mortgage is purchased and a later review finds NFIP non-participation or missing coverage, Freddie Mac may exercise repurchase/indemnification rights that must be executed under BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_016_010 timelines.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Noncompliance with 007_005 can escalate from ineligibility to financial remedy demands, increasing loss exposure and creating counterparty enforcement risk if 16_010 timelines are missed.

dependencies.rationale: BR_MORTGAGE_CONSTRAINT_001_005 is an eligibility/sizing constraint that can render a loan ineligible if exceeded. If the loan is nevertheless delivered and later found to violate this sizing rule (e.g., total acquisition cost overstated or limiter not applied), Freddie Mac may require repurchase or indemnification, triggering BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_016_010.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Post-purchase discovery of sizing noncompliance can cause significant remediation obligations (repurchase/indemnification) and strict response deadlines, increasing financial exposure and operational burden.

business_rules.rule_name: E&O Deductible Maximum Higher of $250,000 or 10%
business_rules.description: The Seller/Servicer’s maximum deductible, self-insured retention (SIR), or combined deductible and SIR for any single mortgagee’s E&O loss must not exceed the higher of (i) $250,000 or (ii) 10% of the minimum E&O limit required by Freddie Mac per occurrence. The deductible/SIR cannot be calculated based on the actual limit of insurance in force.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller/Servicer maintains mortgagee’s E&O insurance coverage
business_rules.consequences: If exceeded, coverage is noncompliant; Freddie Mac may require correction/approval
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; Freddie Mac may review/approve policy terms

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 3 – Seller/Servicer Eligibility Requirements (E&O deductible/SIR)
source_reference.source_text: The maximum deductible or SIR or combined deductible and SIR allowed for any one E&O loss is the higher of (i) $250,000 or (ii) ten percent of the minimum limit of E&O insurance required by Freddie Mac per occurrence. The deductible or SIR or combined deductible and SIR may not be calculated based on the actual limit of insurance in force. Freddie Mac reserves the right to review and approve the terms of such a policy.

examples: Required minimum E&O per occurrence is $5,000,000; max deductible = higher of $250,000 or $500,000 → $500,000 cap; $600,000 deductible is noncompliant.
Required minimum E&O is $2,500,000; 10% is $250,000, so max deductible = $250,000; $300,000 deductible violates the rule.
Policy has $10,000,000 actual limit in force; Seller/Servicer cannot compute deductible cap using $10,000,000 instead of Freddie Mac’s minimum required limit.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Suspension/termination risk; required corrective action plan

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_017_007 sets a maximum deductible/SIR rule for mortgagee’s E&O coverage and prohibits calculating it based on the actual limit in force. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_017_008 requires submission of acceptable proof (ACORD 25, certificate, declarations, policy) within 30 days of obtaining/renewing. Those documents are the primary data sources used by Counterparty Risk & Compliance to validate deductible/SIR compliance.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_017_008 fails: (1) Freddie Mac cannot validate compliance with BR_017_007, (2) Seller/Servicer may be treated as out of compliance, (3) corrective action requests or escalations may occur until documentation is provided.

business_rules.rule_name: Insurance Proof Due Within 30 Days of Renewal
business_rules.description: Within 30 days of obtaining or renewing fidelity and/or mortgagee’s E&O insurance, the Seller/Servicer must submit acceptable proof of insurance to Multifamily Counterparty Risk & Compliance. Acceptable proof includes ACORD 25 (or other appropriate ACORD form), certificate of insurance, evidence of insurance, declarations page, or the policy.
business_rules.conditions: Applies upon obtaining new or renewed fidelity and/or E&O insurance
business_rules.consequences: If not submitted timely, Seller/Servicer is out of compliance and subject to action
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 3 – Seller/Servicer Eligibility Requirements (documentation timing)
source_reference.source_text: Within 30 days of obtaining or renewing fidelity and/or mortgagee’s E&O insurance, the Seller/Servicer must submit acceptable proof of insurance to Multifamily Counterparty Risk & Compliance on one of the following: • ACORD 25, Certificate of Liability Insurance (or other appropriate ACORD form) • Certificate of insurance • Evidence of insurance • Declarations page • Policy

examples: E&O policy renews on 06/01; Seller/Servicer must submit proof by 07/01.
Fidelity bond is obtained on 03/15; proof must be submitted by 04/14 (30 days after).
Seller/Servicer sends only an email summary with no certificate/policy; this is not acceptable proof.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Suspension/termination risk; required corrective action plan

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review

business_rules.rule_name: Seller Cash Management Authorization Required Before Payments
business_rules.description: Before instructing Freddie Mac to make any payment via wire transfer, ACH (if applicable), check, or any other electronic payment system, the Seller/Servicer must submit Multifamily Cash Management authorization documentation per Section 32.12(a). Freddie Mac cannot make payments unless the authorization documentation is on file with Freddie Mac and is compliant; wire payments also require compliance with Section 32.12(b).
business_rules.conditions: When Seller/Servicer instructs Freddie Mac to make a payment
business_rules.consequences: If authorization is on file, payment may proceed; if not, payment cannot be made
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; wire transfers also require Section 32.12(b) compliance

source_reference.source_text: Before instructing Freddie Mac to make any payment via wire transfer, Automated Clearing House (ACH) (if applicable), check or any other electronic payment system, a Seller/Servicer must submit to Freddie Mac Multifamily Cash Management authorization documentation in accordance with the requirements of Section 32.12(a). Payments cannot be made if such authorization documentation is not on file with Freddie Mac and in compliance with the requirements of Section 32.12(a). For payments to be made via wire transfer, a Seller/Servicer also must comply with the requirements of Section 32.12(b).

examples: Seller/Servicer requests a wire to remit a fee but has not submitted Section 32.12(a) authorization; Freddie Mac does not process the payment
Seller/Servicer submits compliant cash management authorization and then instructs Freddie Mac to pay via ACH; payment can be made
Seller/Servicer requests a wire transfer after filing 32.12(a) docs but does not meet 32.12(b) wire requirements; wire is not permitted until compliant
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Exclusionary List Screenings Use Current List Updated Monthly
business_rules.description: The Exclusionary List is updated at least monthly by Freddie Mac and the Seller/Servicer must ensure it uses only the most current version. Except as provided in Section 2.18(f), if a party on the Exclusionary List played one of the specified roles with respect to the origination of a Mortgage, a Transfer of Ownership, or the underlying real estate transaction, the Mortgage is not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac or for Freddie Mac’s approval of a Transfer of Ownership. The Seller/Servicer must maintain evidence in the Mortgage File that the Exclusionary List was used to screen applicable individuals and entities, including the date the list was screened, and must have written practices and procedures instructing employees how to conduct searches and how to verify and address potential matches.
business_rules.conditions: When screening parties/employees for origination or Transfer of Ownership
business_rules.consequences: If screened and no prohibited match, loan may proceed; if role-party match, loan is ineligible
business_rules.exceptions: Section 2.18(f) exception applies; details not provided in this excerpt

source_reference.source_text: The Exclusionary List is updated at least monthly by Freddie Mac and is electronically available to authorized Seller/Servicers as a document as part of the Multifamily secure content. The Seller/Servicer must ensure that it uses only the most current version of the Exclusionary List. ... Except as provided in Section 2.18(f), if a party on the Exclusionary List has played one of the roles set forth in this Section or in Chapter 7 with respect to the origination of a Mortgage, a Transfer of Ownership, or the underlying real estate transaction, the Mortgage is not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac or for Freddie Mac’s approval of a Transfer of Ownership, as applicable. ... The Seller/Servicer must maintain evidence in the Mortgage File that the Seller/Servicer has used the Exclusionary List to screen the applicable individuals and entities in accordance with this section, including the date that the Exclusionary List was screened.

examples: Before underwriting submission, Seller/Servicer screens Borrower Transaction Parties using the current month’s Exclusionary List and retains screenshots plus the screening date in the Mortgage File
A third-party vendor involved in origination is found on the Exclusionary List in a covered role; unless Section 2.18(f) applies, the mortgage is not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac
Seller/Servicer uses an outdated Exclusionary List version (not the most current); screening is noncompliant and must be redone using the current list with evidence retained
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_021_007 mandates screening employees/contractors against Restricted Party Lists at hire and annually. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 mandates using the current Exclusionary List to screen transaction roles and determine mortgage eligibility. While neither is a strict prerequisite for the other, they are complementary controls that should share list management, attestation, and audit workflows.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_021_007 fails: (1) insider/third-party fraud risk increases, (2) compliance program weakness may undermine the reliability of transaction-level screening. If BR_018_009 fails: (1) ineligible mortgages may be originated/sold, (2) purchase refusals and repurchase exposure increase.

business_rules.rule_name: Seller/Servicer Assignment Prohibited Without Written Consent
business_rules.description: A Seller/Servicer must not assign, transfer, or grant a security interest in any obligations, rights, or interests under any Purchase Contract or the unitary master Servicing contract (including rights/obligations under the Guide or Purchase and Servicing Documents) without Freddie Mac's prior written consent. Any attempted assignment/transfer/security interest without such consent is prohibited and is null and void.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to any assignment/transfer/security interest in Purchase/Servicing rights
business_rules.consequences: If consent obtained, assignment may proceed; if not, action is null/void
business_rules.exceptions: Only with Freddie Mac prior written consent

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 1 – Introduction, e. Assignments; security interests (05/01/14)
source_reference.source_text: A Seller/Servicer may not, in whole or in part, assign or transfer, or grant a security interest in, any of its obligations, rights or interest under any Purchase Contract or under the unitary, indivisible master Servicing contract, including any of its rights or obligations under the Guide or any of the Purchase and Servicing Documents, without Freddie Mac's prior written consent. Any purported or attempted assignment or transfer of, or grant of a security interest in, any such obligations, rights, or interest is prohibited and will be null and void.

examples: Seller/Servicer attempts to pledge its servicing rights as collateral for a loan without Freddie Mac written consent; the pledge is null and void.
Seller/Servicer seeks Freddie Mac written consent before transferring certain servicing obligations to an affiliate; transfer may proceed only after consent.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit prohibition and null/void consequence; no numeric threshold in text.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Guide Copying Revocable in 60 Days and No Electronic Copies
business_rules.description: Permission to photocopy the Guide is limited to Freddie Mac Seller/Servicers strictly for their own use in originating, selling, and servicing Mortgages for Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac may revoke permission to reproduce the Guide upon 60 days' notice. Seller/Servicers are prohibited from reproducing the Guide by any electronic or mechanical means, including reproduction in or as a component of any information storage and retrieval system.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller/Servicer reproduces or distributes Guide content
business_rules.consequences: If violated, reproduction is prohibited and permission may be revoked in 60 days
business_rules.exceptions: Express written permission from Freddie Mac for other reproduction

source_reference.source_text: Limited permission to photocopy the Guide is granted to Freddie Mac Seller/Servicers strictly for their own use in originating Mortgages and in selling Mortgages to, and servicing Mortgages for, Freddie Mac. No part of the Guide may be reproduced for any other reason (in any form or by any means) without the express written permission of Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac reserves the right to revoke permission to reproduce the Guide upon 60 days' notice to any and all Seller/Servicers. Under no circumstances may a Seller/Servicer reproduce the Guide by any electronic or mechanical means, including reproduction in, or as a component of, any information storage and retrieval system.

examples: Seller/Servicer uploads the Guide PDF into its internal knowledge base search system; this is prohibited electronic reproduction.
Freddie Mac issues a notice revoking photocopy permission; Seller/Servicer must cease permitted reproduction within 60 days.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 60-day notice and categorical prohibition on electronic/mechanical reproduction.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: SBL application fee due 2:00 p.m. ET on 2nd business day after lock or Lost
business_rules.description: For SBL application fees deemed earned by Freddie Mac, the Seller must remit by wire transfer per timing rules: (i) if the Seller locks the Coupon Rate, remit by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following the Coupon Rate Lock; (iv) if Under Application status in PMT is changed to Lost by the Seller or Freddie Mac, remit by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following the status change to Lost; (ii) if Freddie Mac determines it will not issue a Letter of Commitment for any reason, remit by the date that is 90 days after the fee was deemed earned (unless already remitted); (iii) if Freddie Mac issues a Letter of Commitment and Seller fails to accept or fails to lock by Commitment Expiration Date, remit by the date that is 90 days after the fee was deemed earned (unless already remitted).
business_rules.conditions: SBL Mortgage application fee is deemed earned under Chapter 18SBL
business_rules.consequences: If remitted on time, fee obligation satisfied; if late, noncompliance with remittance timing
business_rules.exceptions: 90-day deadlines do not apply if already remitted under 18SBL.6(e)(iv)

source_reference.source_text: e. Application fee ( 12/16/25 ) The application fee will be deemed earned by Freddie Mac ... payable by the Seller by wire transfer to Freddie Mac as follows: (i) If the Seller locks the Coupon Rate ... the Seller must remit the application fee to Freddie Mac by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following the Coupon Rate Lock. (ii) If Freddie Mac determines that it will not issue a Letter of Commitment ... remit ... by the date that is 90 days after the date the application fee was deemed earned ... (iii) If Freddie Mac issues a Letter of Commitment and the Seller ... fails to lock ... by the Commitment Expiration Date, the Seller must remit ... by ... 90 days ... (iv) If the Under Application status ... is changed to Lost ... remit ... by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following the status change to Lost.

examples: Coupon Rate locked on Monday: application fee must be wired by 2:00 p.m. ET on Wednesday (2nd business day).
PMT status changed to Lost on Thursday: application fee must be wired by 2:00 p.m. ET on Monday (assuming no holiday).
Freddie Mac decides not to issue a commitment; fee deemed earned on Jan 10: Seller must remit by Apr 10 (90 days) unless already remitted.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_020_008 requires remittance of 'SBL application fees deemed earned by Freddie Mac' by specified wire deadlines. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CALCULATION_029_002 defines (a) the fee amount and (b) when it is deemed earned (2:00 pm ET on the 5th Business Day after Under Application status in PMT). Without that calculation and earning timestamp, the remittance obligation is ambiguous and cannot be validated or automated.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CALCULATION_029_002 is not applied/available: (1) Seller may remit the wrong dollar amount, (2) Seller may miss the 'earned' trigger timing, (3) reconciliation breaks (earned vs remitted), (4) Freddie Mac can cite noncompliance and require cure or assess remedies/fees per Guide/commitment terms.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_020_008 mandates remittance 'by wire transfer'. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_018_008 requires authorization documentation before instructing Freddie Mac to make any payment via wire/ACH/check and states Freddie Mac cannot make payments without it. Operationally, the remittance-by-wire workflow should validate authorization-on-file before initiating payment instructions.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_018_008 fails: (1) wire instructions cannot be executed/accepted, (2) remittance timing in BR_020_008 is missed, (3) late remittance noncompliance is triggered, (4) transaction status may be frozen until authorization is cured.

business_rules.rule_name: Screen employees at hire and at least annually
business_rules.description: The Seller/Servicer must screen Freddie Mac Matters Employees and Freddie Mac Matters Contractors against the Restricted Party Lists at the time of hire/engagement and at least annually thereafter to ensure compliance with the fraud prevention requirements.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Freddie Mac Matters Employees/Contractors screened vs Restricted Party Lists
business_rules.consequences: If performed, meets minimum screening cadence; if not, violates Guide screening practices
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Seller/Servicer must also screen, or caused to be screened, all Freddie Mac Contractors against the Restricted Party Lists to ensure compliance with the provisions of this chapter . The screening of Freddie Mac Matters Contractors and Freddie Mac Matters Employees should be conducted, at a minimum, at the time of hire or engagement, and at least annually thereafter .

examples: A new QC analyst hired on 2026-01-15 is screened against OFAC/SCP lists on the hire date and again by 2027-01-15.
A contractor engaged on 2025-08-01 is screened on 2025-08-01 and again during the Seller/Servicer’s annual rescreening cycle in 2026.
If a contractor is engaged but not screened until after work begins, the Seller/Servicer fails the 'time of engagement' minimum cadence requirement.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: SBL Repurchase Only for Events After 2020-03-30
business_rules.description: SBL Repurchase Obligations apply only if the underlying event giving rise to the repurchase obligation occurs during the SBL Repurchase Period (as described in Section 46SBL.4(e)). The obligations apply even if the Seller/Servicer endorsed the note or other loan documents to Freddie Mac “without recourse,” and they do not limit Freddie Mac’s other Guide rights against the Seller/Servicer.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Freddie Mac asserts SBL Repurchase Obligations under 46SBL.4
business_rules.consequences: If event occurs in Repurchase Period, Freddie Mac may require repurchase; else no repurchase
business_rules.exceptions: None; applies notwithstanding “without recourse” endorsement language

source_reference.source_text: SBL Repurchase Obligations only apply if the underlying event giving rise to the SBL Repurchase Obligation occurs during the SBL Repurchase Period, as described in Section 46SBL.4(e). The SBL Repurchase Obligations apply notwithstanding that the Seller /Servicer may have endorsed any SBL Mortgage Note or other Loan Document to Freddie Mac “without recourse.”

examples: A payment default occurs during the Repurchase Period; Freddie Mac may issue a repurchase notice requiring repurchase.
A defect is discovered after the Repurchase Period ends; this section indicates the repurchase obligation does not apply based on timing.
Seller/Servicer endorsed the note “without recourse,” but an in-period repurchase trigger occurs; repurchase can still be required.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit applicability limitation and ‘without recourse’ carve-out stated in 46SBL.4(a).

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Qualified Mortgage Needs 80% FMV and 125% LTV
business_rules.description: An SBL Mortgage is deemed ineligible for inclusion as an asset of a securitization Secondary Market Transaction if it is not a “Qualified Mortgage.” For this purpose, “principally secured” means the fair market value (FMV) of the real property collateral is at least 80% of the outstanding principal balance (OPB) (i.e., loan-to-value ratio of 125% or less). This test must be satisfied both as of the Origination Date and when the SBL Mortgage is put into the applicable securitization.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL Mortgages evaluated for inclusion in securitization transactions
business_rules.consequences: If FMV < 80% of OPB (LTV > 125%), mortgage is ineligible for securitization asset inclusion
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 1. Qualified Mortgage. The SBL Mortgage will be deemed ineligible for inclusion as an asset of a Secondary Market Transaction that is a  securitization if the SBL Mortgage is not a “Qualified Mortgage.” For the purpose of this Section 46SBL.4(b), a Qualified Mortgage is a mortgage that is “principally secured” by an interest in real property, and “principally secured” means that the fair m arket value of the real property collateral for the SBL Mortgage is at least 80 percent of the outstanding principal balance of the SBL Mortgage (or a loan- to-value ratio of 125 percent or less), tested both as of the Origination Date and when the SBL Mortgage is put into the applicable securitization.

examples: Origination: OPB $8,000,000 and FMV $6,500,000 (FMV/OPB=81.25%); passes 80% test and may be eligible (subject to other criteria).
Securitization date: OPB $10,000,000 and FMV $7,500,000 (FMV/OPB=75%); fails and is deemed ineligible for securitization inclusion.
Origination passes but securitization-date test fails; the mortgage is deemed ineligible because both tests must be met.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit eligibility/ineligibility rule with two time-based tests and exact numeric thresholds.

Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review

business_rules.rule_name: Condemnation LTV Above 125% Triggers Ineligibility
business_rules.description: An SBL Mortgage is deemed ineligible for inclusion in a securitization if any portion of the property is released from the lien due to a Condemnation and the ratio of UPB to the value of the property (land and buildings only; excluding personal property and going-concern value) is greater than 125% immediately after the Condemnation and before any Restoration/repair (but taking into account planned Restoration/repair as if completed). If Freddie Mac enforces repurchase for this reason, Freddie Mac credits net condemnation proceeds/awards received, minus Freddie Mac’s costs/expenses related to the condemnation and repurchase.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when a lien release occurs due to Condemnation for an SBL Mortgage in securitization
business_rules.consequences: If UPB/value > 125%, mortgage is ineligible; repurchase may be enforced with proceeds crediting
business_rules.exceptions: Value is determined by Freddie Mac using commercially reasonable securitization valuation methods

source_reference.source_text: 2. Condemnation Valuation. The SBL Mortgage will be deemed ineligible for inclusion as an asset of a Secondary Market Transaction that is a securitization if any portion of the Property is released from the lien of the SBL Mortgage in connection with a Condemnation (as defined in the Loan Agreement) and the ratio of (i) the unpaid principal balance of the SBL Mortgage to (ii) the value of the Property (taking into account only the related land and buildings and not any personal property or going- concern value), as determined by Freddie Mac in its sole and absolute discretion based on a commercially reasonable valuation method permitted in connection with a securitization, is greater than 125 percent immediately after the Condemnation and before any Restoration (as defined in the Loan Agreement) or repair of the Property (but taking into account any planned Restoration or repair of the Property  as if such planned Restoration or repair were completed). If Freddie Mac enforces an SBL Repurchase Obligation with respect to an SBL Mortgage for this reason, Freddie Mac will credit to the Seller /Servicer any net proceeds or awards from such Condemnation received by Freddie Mac, less any costs and expenses incurred by Freddie Mac in connection with the Condemnation and the Repurchase.

examples: After condemnation, UPB $9.0M and post-condemnation value $7.0M; ratio 128.6% (>125%); mortgage becomes ineligible for securitization inclusion.
UPB $6.0M and value $5.0M immediately after condemnation; ratio 120%; does not trigger this ineligibility test.
Freddie Mac enforces repurchase due to >125% ratio and received $300,000 net award; Freddie Mac credits the net award minus its condemnation/repurchase costs.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Threshold (125%) and valuation scope/exclusions are explicit; proceeds credit is explicitly described.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review

business_rules.rule_name: Repurchase for 60-Day or 120-Day Payment Delinquency
business_rules.description: As of March 30, 2020, a Seller/Servicer is subject to an SBL Repurchase Obligation for a monetary default when required loan payments are delinquent for the applicable consecutive-day period, calculated without considering any grace or cure period in the loan documents: (i) 120 consecutive days for SBL Mortgages where the borrower participates in COVID-19 forbearance, beginning on the due date of the first required payment after forbearance ends; (ii) 60 consecutive days for SBL Mortgages that include a COVID-19 Debt Service Reserve, beginning on the required payment due date; and (iii) 60 consecutive days for all other SBL Mortgages, beginning on the required payment due date.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL Mortgages when borrower fails to pay/deposit a Required Loan Payment
business_rules.consequences: If delinquency meets the stated consecutive-day period, Seller/Servicer is subject to repurchase
business_rules.exceptions: Delinquency is calculated without any grace/cure periods in Loan Documents

source_reference.source_text: c. Monetary default  (04/22/25) As of March 30, 2020, the Seller /Servicer  is subject to an SBL Repurchase Obligation if an SBL Borrower is in default under the applicable SBL Mortgage for failure to pay or deposit when due any amount required by the Loan Documents (“Required Loan Payment(s)”) when the Required Loan Payment has been delinquent for the period of time specified in the chart below (“Delinquency Period”): Delinquency Period SBL Mortgage for which Borrower participates in the COVID -19 forbearance  Applicable Delinquency Period*  120 consecutive days beginning on the date the Required Loan Payment following the end of the forbearance period was due ... SBL Mortgage includes a COVID -19 Debt Service Reserve 60 consecutive days beginning on the date the Required Loan Payment was due ... All other SBL Mortgages 60 consecutive days beginning on the date the Required Loan Payment was due (a “60 Day Delinquency”) *Calculated without considering any grace or cure period that may be applicable to the Required Loan Payment in the Loan Documents.

examples: Borrower exits COVID forbearance; first post-forbearance payment due April 1 is unpaid through July 30 (120 consecutive days); repurchase obligation is triggered.
SBL Mortgage has a COVID-19 Debt Service Reserve; payment due May 1 is unpaid through June 30 (60 consecutive days); repurchase obligation is triggered.
Loan documents provide a 10-day grace period, but the 60/120-day count ignores the grace period per the rule.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Chart-based delinquency periods are explicit and include the ‘no grace/cure’ calculation rule.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_022_005 limits SBL Repurchase Obligations to events occurring during the defined Repurchase Period. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_022_008 states the conditions for being 'subject to an SBL Repurchase Obligation' for monetary default. Therefore, repurchase-period eligibility must be evaluated before concluding BR_022_008 creates an enforceable repurchase obligation.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_022_005 is not checked: (1) Seller may incorrectly accept/contest a repurchase demand, (2) disputes and appeals may be mishandled, (3) Freddie Mac enforcement actions may escalate due to improper posture, (4) financial reporting and reserves may be misstated.

business_rules.rule_name: Delegated inspection cannot be re-delegated, exception only
business_rules.description: If Freddie Mac delegates any inspection requirement to the Seller, the Seller may not further delegate that inspection requirement. Such delegation to the Seller must be approved by Freddie Mac and is permitted only on an exception basis.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Freddie Mac has delegated an inspection to Seller
business_rules.consequences: Met: Seller performs inspection; Violated: noncompliance, rework/approval risk
business_rules.exceptions: Delegation to Seller permitted only if Freddie Mac approves as an exception

source_reference.source_text: 9. If Freddie Mac delegates any inspection requirement to the Seller, the Seller may not further delegate the inspection requirement . Delegation to the Seller must be approved by Freddie Mac and is permitted on an exception basis only.

examples: Freddie delegates an inspection to Seller; Seller must use its own staff/approved process and cannot subcontract delegation further.
Seller attempts to delegate the delegated inspection to a third party without Freddie approval; this violates the Guide requirement.
Freddie approves delegation to Seller as an exception; Seller still cannot further delegate beyond the Seller.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Purchase denial or required corrective action

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
