business_rules.rule_name: Conversion package due at least 60 days before conversion
business_rules.description: After completing all Conversion due diligence/analysis and satisfying all Conditions to Conversion, the Seller must prepare the Conversion Underwriting Package and submit it to Freddie Mac with the proposed Conversion schedule no later than 60 days prior to the proposed Conversion date.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to TAH funded forward commitment conversions after conditions satisfied
business_rules.consequences: Met: Freddie can underwrite conversion; Violated: conversion timeline disruption/denial
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: 19A.13(b) Conversion Underwriting Package and Conversion schedule
source_reference.source_text: Once the Seller has completed all Conversion due diligence and analysis and all Conditions to Conversion have been satisfied, the Seller must prepare the Conversion Underwriting Package and submit it along with the proposed Conversion schedule to Freddie Mac no later than 60 days prior to the proposed date of the Conversion.

examples: Proposed conversion date is 2026-09-30; Seller must submit the Conversion Underwriting Package by 2026-08-01 at the latest.
Seller satisfies all Conditions to Conversion on 2026-07-15 but targets 2026-08-15 conversion; submission must still occur by 2026-06-16 (60 days prior).
Seller submits the package 45 days before conversion; Freddie may require rescheduling or may reject conversion for untimely submission.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Coinsurance allowed only with written approval before Rate Lock
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac generally will not accept coinsurance (multiple title policies from multiple underwriters for the same transaction). Coinsurance may be allowed only if each title underwriter and policy meets Chapter 29 requirements and Freddie Mac approves the use of coinsurance in writing prior to Rate Lock. Prior to the Origination Date, the Seller/Servicer must submit the coinsured Title Policies to Freddie Mac for review and approval. Freddie Mac also states it does not expect coinsurance for any single SBL Mortgage or pool of SBL Mortgages of $40 million or less; coinsurance for SBL pools in excess of $40 million is subject to Section 29.1(a) and (b).
business_rules.conditions: When a Mortgage transaction proposes coinsurance for title insurance
business_rules.consequences: If not pre-approved in writing, Freddie Mac will not accept coinsured Title Policies
business_rules.exceptions: Permitted only with Freddie Mac written approval prior to Rate Lock

source_reference.source_text: Usually, Freddie Mac will not accept coinsurance (multiple Title Insurance Policies issued by multiple Title Insurance Underwriters for the same transaction). Freddie Mac will consider allowing coinsurance only if the Title Insurance Underwriters and Title Policies each meet the requirements of this chapter and the use of coinsurance is approved in writing by Freddie Mac prior to Rate Lock. Prior to the Origination Date, the Seller/Servicer must submit to Freddie Mac for its review and approval the Title Policies for any Mortgage that will be coinsured. Freddie Mac does not expect that any single SBL Mortgage or pool of SBL Mortgages $40 million or less will require coinsurance. For the financing of a pool of SBL Mortgages in excess of $40 million, coinsurance for such pool is subject to the provisions of Section 29.1(a) and (b).

examples: A Seller proposes two title policies from two underwriters; without written Freddie Mac approval before Rate Lock, the coinsurance structure is rejected for delivery.
An SBL pool of $55,000,000 proposes coinsurance; Seller must obtain Freddie Mac written approval before Rate Lock and comply with Section 29.1(a)-(b).
An SBL pool of $35,000,000 requests coinsurance; Freddie Mac indicates it is not expected and may refuse absent compelling justification and written approval.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_026_001 allows coinsurance only if each underwriter/policy meets requirements and Freddie Mac approves in writing. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 prohibits using a Title Company on the Multifamily Restricted Vendor List. Therefore, restricted-vendor screening is a prerequisite check before pursuing coinsurance approval and submitting coinsured policies for review.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 fails: (1) coinsurance request/policies will be rejected, (2) closing/title workflows must be restructured with new underwriters, (3) rate lock/closing deadlines may be missed, (4) Freddie Mac may refuse purchase/assumption approval.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_026_001 requires Freddie Mac written approval prior to rate lock. Freddie Mac’s decision will be impacted if the title company/underwriter selection violates BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 (selection based on improper consideration). Therefore, vendor selection integrity is a prerequisite compliance gate before requesting coinsurance approval.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 fails: (1) Freddie Mac may refuse the transaction/title structure, (2) potential fraud/anti-kickback concerns, (3) remediation may require vendor replacement and re-issuance of title work, (4) counterparty sanctions risk.

business_rules.rule_name: Title company selection cannot be based on anything of value
business_rules.description: The Seller/Servicer must select or accept any Title Company based solely on prudent institutional lender considerations in the relevant jurisdiction, as permitted by law, and in Freddie Mac’s best interests. The Seller/Servicer must not base the selection on receipt of anything of value or other consideration paid by or on behalf of a Title Company to the Seller/Servicer or its personnel.
business_rules.conditions: When Seller/Servicer selects or accepts a Title Company for a Mortgage
business_rules.consequences: If violated, selection is noncompliant and may trigger refusal, remediation, or sanctions
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 29.1(c)(1) Selection of the Title Company (04/22/25)
source_reference.source_text: 1. The Seller/Servicer's selection or acceptance of any Title Company must be based solely on considerations typically used by prudent institutional lenders originating or purchasing Mortgages in the jurisdiction where the Property is located, as permitted by applicable law, and acting in the best interests of Freddie Mac. The Seller/Servicer must not base this selection on receipt of anything of value or other consideration by the Seller/Servicer or its employees, officers, or directors paid by or on behalf of a Title Company.

examples: A Title Company offers free ancillary services to win business; Seller/Servicer must reject the inducement and base selection on competence, coverage, and jurisdictional fit.
A Seller/Servicer employee receives a referral fee from a Title Company; the Title Company cannot be selected on that basis and the arrangement must be terminated and escalated.
A Borrower proposes a Title Company; Seller/Servicer still must evaluate selection using prudent lender criteria and cannot accept due to gifts or other consideration.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Restricted Vendor List title company cannot be used until cleared
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac may refuse to accept Mortgages for purchase or approve assumptions involving a specific Title Company on the Multifamily Restricted Vendor List. If a Title Company appears on the Multifamily Restricted Vendor List, the Seller/Servicer may not use that Title Company until Freddie Mac notifies otherwise.
business_rules.conditions: When a Title Company is on Freddie Mac’s Multifamily Restricted Vendor List
business_rules.consequences: Use is prohibited; Mortgage purchase/assumption may be refused by Freddie Mac
business_rules.exceptions: Only permitted after Freddie Mac notifies otherwise

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 29.1(c)(2) Selection of the Title Company (04/22/25)
source_reference.source_text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to: (i) refuse to accept Mortgages for purchase, or (ii) approve the assumption of a Mortgage, in each case involving any specific Title Company on the Multifamily Restricted Vendor List. If a Title Company appears on the Multifamily Restricted Vendor List, the Seller/Servicer may not use that Title Company until notified otherwise by Freddie Mac.

examples: A Borrower insists on using a Title Company that appears on the Restricted Vendor List; Seller/Servicer must require a different Title Company before proceeding.
A Mortgage is delivered with a title policy issued by a listed Title Company; Freddie Mac may refuse purchase and require corrective action.
A Title Company is removed from the list and Freddie Mac notifies Seller/Servicer; only then may it be used again.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Title underwriter must perform funding functions for acquisitions after 08-15-2024
business_rules.description: For acquisition Mortgage origination transactions taken under a Seller Application submitted on or after August 15, 2024, and for all Mortgage origination transactions under Seller Applications submitted on or after October 31, 2025, the Title Insurance Underwriter (or its affiliate under identical ownership, or wholly-owned subsidiary) must directly perform all Funding Functions for both the mortgage origination and (if applicable) the property acquisition. The settlement statement must be delivered directly to the Seller/Servicer or its counsel by the underwriter/affiliate/subsidiary. Either the underwriter or the title agent may perform Escrow Functions. If jurisdictional law requires a licensed attorney for Funding Functions and the underwriter group lacks an on-staff licensed attorney in that jurisdiction, Seller/Servicer’s counsel must notify the Freddie Mac transactional attorney on or prior to submission of the full underwriting package and include analysis in the Legal Issues Analysis.
business_rules.conditions: When funding functions are performed for covered origination transactions by application date
business_rules.consequences: Noncompliance may delay underwriting review/closing and jeopardize Freddie Mac acceptance
business_rules.exceptions: TEL Mortgages fiscal agent may perform certain escrow/settlement functions (not in excerpt)

source_reference.source_text: Effective for any Mortgage origination transaction that is an acquisition which was taken under Seller Application submitted on or after August 15, 2024, and effective for all Mortgage origination transactions (including acquisitions) under Seller Applications submitted on or after October 31, 2025, the Title Insurance Underwriter, its affiliate under identical ownership, or its wholly-owned subsidiary must directly perform all Funding Functions for both the Mortgage origination transaction and, if applicable, the acquisition of the Property. The settlement statement must be delivered to the Seller/Servicer or the Seller/Servicer’s counsel directly by the Title Insurance Underwriter or such affiliate or subsidiary. (See Section 32.3(c) for additional settlement statement requirements.) Either the Title Insurance Underwriter or Title Agent may perform Escrow Functions.

examples: A property acquisition is taken under a Seller Application dated 2024-08-20; the title underwriter (or identical-ownership affiliate/wholly-owned subsidiary) must directly perform all Funding Functions.
A refinance (not an acquisition) is taken under a Seller Application dated 2025-11-15; Funding Functions must still be performed directly by the title underwriter group per the 10/31/2025 trigger.

A state requires attorney closings and the title underwriter has no in-state licensed attorney; Seller/Servicer’s counsel notifies Freddie Mac transactional attorney before submitting the full underwriting package and includes a Legal Issues Analysis.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_004 assigns core settlement/funding duties to the Title Insurance Underwriter (or qualifying affiliate). If that underwriter is on the Restricted Vendor List, BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 prohibits use—making the funding-function model non-executable until a compliant underwriter is selected.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 fails: (1) settlement/funding structure violates Guide, (2) underwriting review/closing may be delayed, (3) Freddie Mac acceptance jeopardized due to prohibited vendor usage.

dependencies.rationale: Because BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_004 requires the title underwriter to perform core settlement functions, Freddie Mac will scrutinize the underwriter relationship. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 is a baseline compliance condition for selecting/accepting that underwriter. Therefore, vendor integrity is a prerequisite check to avoid invalidating the funding functions structure.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 fails: (1) Freddie Mac may refuse/condition acceptance, (2) transaction may require vendor replacement and re-papering, (3) delays to closing and delivery.

business_rules.rule_name: Seller counsel must have 2 years multifamily closings
business_rules.description: An attorney representing the Seller/Servicer for structuring, origination, or delivery of a Mortgage (or transfer/special servicing request) must have at least two years of experience closing commercial or multifamily housing mortgage loans on behalf of lenders.
business_rules.conditions: When Seller/Servicer retains legal counsel for a Mortgage or transfer
business_rules.consequences: Meets: counsel qualifies; Violated: Freddie Mac may reject counsel work/product
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 6.2 Qualifications of Seller/Servicer's legal counsel (02/28/13) An attorney who represents the Seller/Servicer in connection with the structuring, origination or delivery of a Mortgage or in connection with a Transfer of Ownership or other Special Servicing Request must have a minimum of two years' experience closing commercial or multifamily housing mortgage loans on behalf of lenders.

examples: Seller uses counsel with 5 years multifamily closings to deliver an SBL Mortgage; requirement satisfied.
Seller retains a newly licensed attorney with 1 year closing experience for a delivery; must replace/add qualified attorney.
Seller uses a 3-year commercial mortgage closer who has no multifamily closings; still qualifies because commercial OR multifamily is allowed.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review

business_rules.rule_name: SBL application fee is 10 bps earned on day 5 at 2 pm
business_rules.description: For an SBL Mortgage, the application fee equals 10 basis points (0.10%) of the Loan Amount and is deemed earned by Freddie Mac at 2:00 pm Eastern time on the fifth Business Day following the day the Seller/Servicer entered the loan status as Under Application in the Pipeline Management Tool (PMT). The fee is nonrefundable except for Top Markets or as otherwise specified in writing by Freddie Mac; for Top Markets, the fee is waived upon written confirmation that the Coupon Rate has been locked unless otherwise specified.
business_rules.conditions: When Seller/Servicer enters SBL loan status as Under Application in PMT
business_rules.consequences: Meets: fee earned/charged per timing; Violated: Seller still owes fee per Guide terms
business_rules.exceptions: Top Markets waiver upon written rate-lock confirmation; other written Freddie Mac terms

source_reference.source_text: 18SBL.5     Standard delivery —application fee ( 04/22/25 ) An application fee equal to 10 basis points of the Loan Amount will be deemed earned by Freddie Mac from Seller/Servicer  at 2:00 pm Eastern time on the fifth Business Day following the day the Seller/Servicer entered the loan status as Under Application in the Pipeline Management Tool (PMT). The application fee will be nonrefundable, except  for Properties located in Top Markets , or unless otherwise specified in writing by Freddie Mac.

examples: Loan Amount $7,500,000; fee = $7,500 (0.10%) and is earned at 2:00 pm ET on the 5th Business Day after PMT status entry.
Property is a Top Market; after written confirmation that the coupon is locked, the 10 bps fee is waived unless Freddie Mac states otherwise.
Seller requests refund after withdrawal on a non-Top Market deal; refund not permitted absent written Freddie Mac exception.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Application fee charged / due to Freddie Mac

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority

business_rules.rule_name: Seismic risk consultant requires 3 lender references
business_rules.description: For Manufactured Housing Community (MHC) seismic risk consultants, the Seller/Servicer must review and verify credentials (including licensing, certifications, memberships, affiliations). For new seismic risk consultants, the Seller/Servicer must check at least three references from lenders that retained/employed the consultant. The Seller/Servicer must maintain a separate seismic risk consultant file for Freddie Mac that includes ongoing performance evaluations, the consultant’s current resume, required references, and current certificate(s) of liability insurance per Section 11.5.
business_rules.conditions: When using a seismic risk consultant for an MHC transaction
business_rules.consequences: If met: consultant eligible; if violated: Freddie may reject/condition file
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Seller/Servicer must review and verify the seismic risk consultant’s credentials, licensing, certifications, memberships and affiliations. For new seismic risk consultants, the Seller must check at least three references from lenders who have retained or employed the seismic risk consultant to sufficiently evaluate the seismic risk consultant’s capabilities and performance. The Seller must maintain a separate seismic risk consultant file for Freddie Mac’s use that includes the Seller’s ongoing evaluations of each seismic risk consultant’s performance, as well as the seismic risk consultant’s current resume, required references and current certificate(s) of liability insurance in accordance with the requirements of Section 11.5.

examples: New consultant proposed for an MHC SRA: Seller verifies licenses/certs and obtains 3 lender references before engagement.
Existing consultant: Seller updates file with current liability insurance certificates and ongoing performance evaluations.
Seller cannot produce the consultant’s references on request: Freddie Mac conditions the underwriting/delivery until provided.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery condition / purchase ineligibility until cured
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'must' language with numeric 'at least three references'.

business_rules.rule_name: Borrower may not retain or direct seismic consultant
business_rules.description: The Borrower must not retain or direct the seismic risk consultant. The Borrower may be responsible for paying the costs of all SRA services, but the engagement and direction of the consultant cannot be controlled by the Borrower.
business_rules.conditions: When a seismic risk consultant/SRA is required for the Property
business_rules.consequences: If met: SRA independence preserved; if violated: SRA not acceptable
business_rules.exceptions: Borrower may pay costs, but cannot retain/direct

source_reference.source_text: The Borrower must not retain or direct the seismic risk consultant, but the Borrower may be responsible for paying the costs of all SRA services.

examples: Borrower proposes to hire its preferred SRA firm and instruct scope: prohibited; Seller must control retention/direction.
Borrower pays the SRA invoice but Seller executes the engagement letter and manages the consultant: compliant.
Property manager tries to direct the consultant to avoid entering certain buildings: prohibited influence; SRA may be rejected.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery condition / require new independent SRA
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear prohibition; no numeric thresholds in sentence.

business_rules.rule_name: SBL repurchase notice 5 days and repurchase 10 days
business_rules.description: For SBL Mortgages, within five Business Days after discovering a 60 Day Delinquency or Non-Monetary Default, the Seller/Servicer must provide Freddie Mac written notice. If Freddie Mac elects repurchase and issues a Repurchase Notice, the Seller/Servicer must repurchase the SBL Repurchase Mortgage no later than 10 Business Days after receipt of the Repurchase Notice (Mandatory Repurchase Date) by paying the Repurchase Price. Failure to repurchase by the Mandatory Repurchase Date constitutes a Repurchase Obligation Default and can result in additional fees and remedies including suspension or termination of selling and servicing rights.
business_rules.conditions: When an SBL Mortgage has a 60 Day Delinquency or Non-Monetary Default
business_rules.consequences: If met: timely notice/repurchase; if violated: default and remedies apply
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 46SBL – Page 9 (Notice and Mandatory Repurchase Date)
source_reference.source_text: Within five Business Days following its discovery of the occurrence of a 60 Day Delinquency or a Non- Monetary Default, the Seller /Servicer will provide Freddie Mac written notice of the occurrence of such event. If Freddie Mac discovers the occurrence of a 60 Day Delinquency or a Non- Monetary Default, Freddie Mac will provide written notice to the Seller /Servicer of such event. If an SBL Mortgage is subject to an SBL Repurchase Obligation during a Repurchase Period and Freddie Mac elects to require the Seller /Servicer to repurchase the SBL Mortgage (“SBL Repurchase Mortgage”), Freddie Mac will provide written notice to the Seller /Servicer of such election (“Repurchase Notice”). The Seller /Servicer must purchase the SBL Repurchase Mortgage from Freddie Mac no later than 10 Business Days after its receipt of a Repurchase Notice from Freddie Mac (“Mandatory Repurchase Date”)

examples: Seller discovers loan is 60 days delinquent on Monday: written notice to Freddie due by following Monday (5 Business Days).
Freddie issues Repurchase Notice on June 1: Seller must wire Repurchase Price and repurchase by June 15 (10 Business Days).
Seller misses the 10-business-day deadline: repurchase obligation default; Freddie may suspend selling/servicing rights.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Loan repurchase demand; suspension or termination of selling/servicing rights
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timeframes: 5 and 10 Business Days; clear compliance action.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_030_003 requires notice and potential repurchase based on delinquency/default discovery. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_022_008 supplies the definitional standard for counting delinquency days (and specifies not to consider grace/cure periods). This is a mathematical/definition dependency that determines whether BR_030_003 applies and when its timers start.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_022_008 is not applied: (1) Seller may miscount delinquency (e.g., incorrectly netting out grace), (2) late notice to Freddie Mac (miss 5 BD), (3) late repurchase after Repurchase Notice (miss 10 BD), (4) escalation to enforcement/default remedies and potential counterparty actions.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_030_003 is explicitly an SBL repurchase process ('SBL Repurchase Mortgage', 'Repurchase Notice', 'Mandatory Repurchase Date'). BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_022_005 limits when SBL repurchase obligations apply (Repurchase Period). Hence, systems should evaluate BR_022_005 before triggering BR_030_003 timers/workflows.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_022_005 is ignored: (1) unnecessary repurchase workflow triggers, (2) incorrect notices sent to Freddie Mac, (3) operational and legal costs from avoidable repurchase actions, (4) misaligned KPI/compliance reporting.

business_rules.rule_name: Commitment Fee Due 2 p.m. ET 2nd Business Day
business_rules.description: For TAH Cash Forward Commitments, the Seller must deliver the refundable Commitment Fee to Freddie Mac by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following Rate Lock. The Commitment Fee may be cash or a letter of credit meeting Section 11.2 requirements. If the Mortgage is not delivered to Freddie Mac by the Mandatory Delivery Date for any reason, the Commitment Fee (and accrued interest) will be retained by Freddie Mac; if delivered by the Mandatory Delivery Date and purchased, it will be refunded per the Guide.
business_rules.conditions: TAH Forward Commitment after Rate Lock where a Commitment Fee is required
business_rules.consequences: If paid timely, fee obligation satisfied; if late/not delivered, fee may be retained and delivery may be impacted
business_rules.exceptions: Form may be cash or LOC satisfying Section 11.2

source_reference.source_text: The Seller is obligated to pay a refundable commitment fee (“ Commitment Fee ”) to Freddie Mac in the amount set forth in the Forward Commitment.  The Commitment Fee must be delivered to Freddie Mac by 2:00 p.m. Eastern time on the second Business Day following Rate Lock.  At the option of the Seller, the Commitment Fee can be in the form of cash or a letter of credit.  A letter of credit must satisfy the requirements set forth in Section 11.2.   If the Mortgage is not delivered to Freddie Mac by the Mandatory  Delivery Date in accordance with the Forward Commitment for any reason, the Commitment Fee, along with any interest accrued thereon, will be retained by Freddie Mac.  If the Mortgage is delivered to Freddie Mac by the Mandatory Delivery Date and purchased  by Freddie Mac, the Commitment Fee will be refunded in accordance with the Guide .

examples: Rate Lock occurs Monday; Seller wires the Commitment Fee by 2:00 p.m. ET Wednesday (2nd Business Day): compliant.
Seller posts a letter of credit meeting Section 11.2 by the deadline: compliant form of payment.
Mortgage misses Mandatory Delivery Date due to construction delays: Freddie Mac retains Commitment Fee and accrued interest.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_031_009 allows delivery of the Commitment Fee in cash or LOC. If cash is used, payment rails (wire/ACH/check) fall under BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_018_008’s authorization requirement. If LOC is used, the dependency does not apply.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_018_008 fails and cash is intended: (1) the Commitment Fee cannot be transmitted, (2) Rate Lock-to-fee timeline breaches (2nd Business Day), (3) commitment processing may stall and could jeopardize delivery timelines and pricing.

business_rules.rule_name: Commitment changes require prior written approval 03/03/14
business_rules.description: After executing the Commitment, the Seller may not transfer, assign, or otherwise modify the Commitment unless Freddie Mac provides prior written approval.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Seller executes the Commitment for the transaction
business_rules.consequences: If approval obtained, change may proceed; if not, change is prohibited
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; only with Freddie Mac prior written approval

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 28, Freddie Mac’s fees (03/03/14) – preceding paragraph
source_reference.source_text: After the Seller executes the Commitment, the Seller may not transfer, assign or otherwise modify the Commitment without Freddie Mac's prior written approval.

examples: Seller executes Commitment then seeks to assign it to an affiliate; prohibited unless Freddie Mac gives prior written approval.
Seller attempts to change a material term in the Commitment post-execution; must obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval before doing so.
Seller makes no changes after executing the Commitment; rule is satisfied without additional action.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Freddie Mac withholds approval; transaction terms may be rejected

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Legal Counsel Available at All Times From Consideration
business_rules.description: For each Mortgage sold to Freddie Mac and each Transfer of Ownership or other Special Servicing Request submitted for approval, the Seller/Servicer must have its legal counsel available at all times from and after the commencement of consideration of the transaction, including before submission of an early rate-lock application, issuance of a Letter of Commitment, or issuance of a transfer approval letter. Legal counsel must be available to assist with structuring and negotiation and to perform listed support functions (e.g., identifying nonstandard legal issues and responding to Freddie Mac questions).
business_rules.conditions: When considering a Mortgage sale or servicing approval request to Freddie Mac
business_rules.consequences: If met: transaction support available; if violated: delays/defects in approval
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 6.3 Availability of Seller/Servicer's legal counsel (09/08/04) For each Mortgage that it sells to Freddie Mac and for each Transfer of Ownership and other Special Servicing Requests submitted to Freddie Mac for approval, the Seller/Servicer must have its legal counsel available at all times. This availability must continue from and after the commencement of consideration of the proposed transaction (including before the submission of an early rate -lock application to Freddie Mac, Freddie Mac's issuance of a Letter of Commitment or Freddie Mac' s issuance of a letter approving a proposed Transfer of Ownership).

examples: Before submitting an early rate-lock application, Seller/Servicer ensures counsel is reachable to address structure questions.
During a Transfer of Ownership request, counsel remains available to negotiate document content with Freddie Mac.
If counsel is unavailable during key negotiations, Freddie Mac approval may be delayed until counsel participation occurs.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Purchase denial or servicing request rejection/suspense

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_ELIGIBILITY_029_001 defines that the attorney representing the Seller/Servicer must have at least two years of experience closing relevant loans. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_035_003 requires legal counsel be available throughout transaction consideration. In practice, availability must be satisfied with qualified counsel; otherwise, the availability requirement is functionally unmet for Freddie Mac purposes.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_ELIGIBILITY_029_001 fails: (1) Freddie Mac may reject counsel work/product, (2) early rate-lock/commitment/transfer submissions may be delayed pending counsel replacement, (3) additional legal fees and re-documentation occur.

business_rules.rule_name: OFAC Screening Evidence Must Include Date and Screenshots
business_rules.description: The Seller/Servicer must verify that names of applicable individuals and entities do not appear on the most current OFAC Lists in accordance with the Guide and Public Records Search Requirements. The Seller/Servicer must maintain evidence of the screening in the Mortgage File, including the date of the search, such as screenshots of the searches, for Mortgage origination or any servicing-related transaction (as applicable).
business_rules.conditions: When originating a Mortgage or processing a servicing-related transaction
business_rules.consequences: If met: file supports compliance; if violated: compliance finding and cure
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 2 OFAC paragraph (as shown on Page 29)
source_reference.source_text: It is the Seller/Servicer's responsibility to determine compliance with these OFAC requirements, and to verify that the names of any applicable individuals and entities do not appear on the most current OFAC Lists in accordance with the requirements set forth in the Guide and the Public Records Search Requirements. The Seller/Servicer must maintain evidence (including the date of the search) of the screening (e.g., screenshots of the searches) in the Mortgage File in connection with the origination of a Mortgage or any Servicing -related transaction, as applicable.

examples: At origination, Seller/Servicer stores OFAC search screenshots dated the same day underwriting is finalized.
Before approving a servicing-related consent request, Servicer retains the OFAC search record and date in the file.
If no screenshot/date is retained, QC flags the file and Seller/Servicer must remediate by re-screening and documenting.
Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 requires use of the most current Exclusionary List and makes mortgages ineligible if a listed party played a covered role (subject to exceptions). BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_035_004 requires screening against current OFAC lists and retaining evidence in the file. In practice, compliance workflows should require both screenings to be completed and evidenced before eligibility/delivery decisions.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 fails while OFAC is done: (1) mortgage can still be ineligible due to Freddie Mac’s Exclusionary List even without OFAC hits, (2) purchase/approval can be refused. If BR_035_004 fails while Exclusionary is done: (1) sanctions compliance finding, (2) file defects and cure requirements, (3) heightened regulatory risk.

business_rules.rule_name: Equity Conflict Allowed Only Under 5% or Under 25% Limits
business_rules.description: Equity conflicts of interest are restricted; however, limited equity interests are permitted if conditions are met. A non-executive employee engaged in origination/underwriting/servicing may hold up to 5% total direct and indirect equity interest in the Borrower if (i) the employee/family member cannot assume control of the Borrower, (ii) property inspection and lease audit are not delegated to the Seller/Servicer, and (iii) if multiple employee-level owners exist, the 5% threshold applies to the total combined interests per Seller/Servicer. A Seller/Servicer, its affiliate, an executive employee, or an executive employee’s family member may hold less than 25% total direct and indirect interest in the Borrower if (i) it cannot assume control of the Borrower and (ii) property inspection and lease audit are not delegated to the Seller/Servicer.
business_rules.conditions: When Seller/Servicer staff/affiliates have equity interest in a Borrower
business_rules.consequences: If met: equity interest may be acceptable; if violated: escalation/disclosure needed
business_rules.exceptions: Third-party investment vehicles excluded (per section (b))

source_reference.source_text: Such Employee -Level Owner may hold up to five percent of total direct and indirect equity interest in the Borrower so long as: • The Employee -Level Owner does not currently have, or have the ability to assume, control of the Borrower • The property inspection and lease audit is not delegated by Freddie Mac to the Seller/Servicer • If there are multiple Employee -Level Owners with equity interests in the same Borrower, the five percent threshold is applied to total combined interests per Seller/Servicer ... Such Seller/Servicer -Level Owner may hold less than 25 percent of total direct and indirect interest in the Borrower so long as: • The Seller/Servicer -Level Owner does not currently have, or have the ability to assume, control of the Borrower • The property inspection and lease audit is not delegated by Freddie Mac to the Seller/Servicer

examples: Non-executive underwriter owns 3% of Borrower equity and has no control rights; inspection not delegated: permitted within the 5% cap.
Two non-executive employees own 3% and 2.5% in same Borrower; combined 5.5% exceeds 5% cap: must contact Freddie Mac representative.
Seller/Servicer affiliate owns 24.9% in Borrower but cannot assume control and inspection not delegated: permitted under the <25% limit.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Freddie Mac refusal to proceed or require remediation/disclosure

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Disclose Critical Repairs and Mold After Form 1105 Review
business_rules.description: After receiving and reviewing Form 1105 (Property Condition Assessment), the Seller must disclose to Freddie Mac any Critical Repairs (including actual or suspected structural, mechanical, electrical, or other material physical deficiencies or Mold at the Property) and must inform the applicable Freddie Mac regional office or the Multifamily TAH underwriter of unusual or questionable conditions.
business_rules.conditions: After Seller receives and reviews Form 1105 for the Property
business_rules.consequences: If disclosed, Freddie Mac can assess; if not, commitment/purchase may be withheld
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Once the Seller has received and reviewed Form 1105 , the Seller must disclose to Freddie Mac any Critical Repairs, as defined in Section 62.3(b), including actual or suspected structural, mechanical, electrical or other material physical deficiencies or Mold at the Property and inform the Applicable Freddie Mac Multifamily Regional Office or the Multifamily TAH Underwriter of unusual or questionable conditions.

examples: Form 1105 flags suspected mold in multiple units; Seller discloses mold as a Critical Repair item to Freddie Mac and alerts the regional office.
Engineering report notes possible structural settlement; Seller discloses the suspected structural deficiency as a Critical Repair before commitment issuance.
Report reveals questionable electrical panel conditions; Seller discloses and escalates as an unusual condition for Freddie Mac review.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'must disclose' requirement; no numeric thresholds included.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: Evidence of Critical Repair Completion Required Before Commitment
business_rules.description: The Seller must verify that all Critical Repairs have been addressed and must provide evidence of completion to Freddie Mac prior to Freddie Mac’s issuance of the Letter of Commitment or Freddie Mac’s acceptance of the early rate-lock application.
business_rules.conditions: When Critical Repairs are identified in the property condition report/Form 1105
business_rules.consequences: If evidence provided, Freddie Mac may proceed; if not, commitment/rate-lock acceptance is blocked
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Verify that all Critical Repairs have been addressed and provide evidence of completion to Freddie Mac prior to Freddie Mac’s issuance of the Letter of Commitment or acceptance of the early rate -lock application

examples: Critical Repair is a failed fire alarm panel; Seller obtains contractor completion certificate and provides it before commitment issuance.
Critical Repair is roof leak affecting units; Seller provides photos and paid invoice as evidence of completion prior to early rate-lock acceptance.
Critical Repair is suspected mold remediation; Seller provides mold remediator clearance documentation before Freddie Mac proceeds.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Freddie Mac will not issue Letter of Commitment
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear timing requirement tied to commitment/rate-lock acceptance; no numeric threshold.

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_036_003 requires disclosure of Critical Repairs after receiving/reviewing Form 1105. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_VALIDATION_036_004 requires verifying all Critical Repairs have been addressed and providing evidence prior to Letter of Commitment issuance or early rate-lock acceptance. The verification step cannot be scoped correctly unless the Critical Repairs have been identified and disclosed.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_036_003 fails: (1) Freddie Mac may not be informed of repair conditions, (2) BR_036_004 evidence package may be incomplete/misaligned, (3) commitment/rate-lock acceptance can be delayed or later challenged, (4) heightened rep/warranty and reputational exposure.

business_rules.rule_name: Terminated Seller Servicer Cannot Reapply for 12 Months
business_rules.description: A Seller/Servicer terminated by Freddie Mac may not apply for the approval that was terminated for at least 12 months after the termination date. If the termination was with cause, the reinstatement application must document appropriate corrective actions. Any reinstatement applicant must meet all approval criteria in effect at the time of application.
business_rules.conditions: When Seller/Servicer approval was terminated and reinstatement is sought
business_rules.consequences: Apply after 12 months with documentation: eligible to be considered; earlier: rejected
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 4.9 Reapplication for selling or Servicing approval (07/31/12) For at least 12 months after the date of the termination, a Seller/Servicer (or branch production office, if applicable) that has been terminated by Freddie Mac may not apply for the approval that was terminated. If the termination was with cause, the application for reinstatement must document that the Seller/Servicer has taken all appropriate corrective actions.

examples: Termination date 2026-01-10; Seller/Servicer applies 2026-10-01: prohibited (less than 12 months).
Termination date 2026-01-10; apply 2027-01-11: satisfies 12-month waiting period; may be reviewed if criteria met.
Termination with cause; application omits corrective action documentation: incomplete and subject to denial.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Suspension/termination remains in effect; reapplication rejected

examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings

business_rules.rule_name: IRS Forms 1099-A and 1099-C File by March 31 and Mail by Jan 31
business_rules.description: The Servicer must file IRS Forms 1099-A and 1099-C with the IRS electronically (paper is not an option under Freddie Mac’s TIN volume). The Servicer must file no later than March 31 of the year following the calendar year of the reportable event (abandonment for 1099-A; cancellation for 1099-C) and must furnish the Borrower a copy of any filed forms on or before January 31 of the calendar year the form is filed with the IRS.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when filing Forms 1099-A/1099-C for Freddie Mac-owned/guaranteed Mortgages
business_rules.consequences: Late/incorrect filing may trigger IRS penalties and Servicer reimbursement obligations
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in the excerpt

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must file all IRS Forms 1099 -A and 1099 -C with the IRS electronically. Paper filing is not an option as the number of forms that will be filed under Freddie Mac’s tax identification number will exceed the maximum allowed for paper filing. The Servicer must file its forms with the IRS no later than March 31 of the year following the calendar year that the Property is abandoned (Form 1099 -A) or the debt is canceled (Form 1099 -C). The Servicer must furnish the Borrower with a copy of any Forms 1099 -A and 1099 -C filed with the IRS on or before January 31 of the calendar year the form is filed with the IRS.

examples: Debt is canceled in 2026: Servicer must e-file 1099-C by March 31, 2027 and mail Borrower copy by January 31, 2027.
Property is abandoned in 2025: Servicer must e-file 1099-A by March 31, 2026 and mail Borrower copy by January 31, 2026.
Servicer attempts paper filing due to low volume for its own portfolio; still prohibited here because filings are under Freddie Mac’s TIN volume.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Reimbursement to Freddie Mac for penalties/fees

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Flood insurance added within 120 days and notice in 10 days
business_rules.description: The Seller/Servicer must monitor FEMA NFIP map changes to identify newly-designated SFHA buildings. Flood insurance must be placed for newly-designated buildings no later than 120 days after the map change effective date. Within 10 days of notifying the Borrower that additional buildings require flood insurance (or authorizing discontinuance for buildings no longer in SFHA), the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac by email and must submit an updated Summary Update record in ICT including supporting documentation (e.g., LODR/LOMA/LOMR).
business_rules.conditions: When FEMA NFIP map changes affect SFHA status of any building at the Property
business_rules.consequences: If met: insurance compliance maintained; if violated: insurance noncompliance and potential force-place/cure
business_rules.exceptions: Flood insurance may be discontinued for buildings no longer in SFHAs

source_reference.source_text: The Seller/Servicer must have a process in place that allows it to monitor any FEMA NFIP map changes and identify whether any buildings at the Property are newly designed in an SFHA. Flood insurance is required for all buildings located in SFHAs per this chapter. For newly -designated buildings, flood insurance is to be in place no later than 120 days after the effective date of the FEMA NFIP map change. For buildings no longer located in SFHAs, flood insurance may be discontinued. Within 10 days of notifying the Borrower that flood insurance is required for additional buildings, or authorizing the Borrower to discontinue flood insurance coverage, the Servicer must give written notice to Freddie Mac via email at MF_Insurance_Compliance@freddiemac.com. The Servicer must also complete and submit a Summary Update record in ICT with updated flood zone and flood coverage information.

examples: A FEMA map change effective 2026-03-01 newly places Building 3 in an SFHA; flood insurance for Building 3 must be in place by 2026-06-29 (120 days).
Servicer emails Borrower on 2026-03-10 that two additional buildings require flood insurance; Servicer must email Freddie Mac by 2026-03-20 and submit the ICT Summary Update.
A building is removed from SFHA; Servicer authorizes discontinuance and must notify Freddie Mac within 10 days and update ICT.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Force-place insurance and/or Freddie Mac compliance action

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Form 1110M Due 90 Days After Fiscal Year End
business_rules.description: Any Servicer with a Multifamily Servicing-only approval must certify in writing (using Form 1110M via the Multifamily Eligibility System) that insurance premiums, ground rents, assessments, taxes, and other potential prior-lien charges were paid during the preceding fiscal year and are not delinquent (except as stated). The Servicer must submit the completed Form 1110M within 90 days after the end of the Servicer’s fiscal year; if items are delinquent, the certification must describe the delinquency and steps to cure.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Servicers with Multifamily Servicing-only approval after fiscal year end
business_rules.consequences: If submitted timely, certification is compliant; if late/missing, compliance breach
business_rules.exceptions: Delinquencies allowed only if disclosed with cure steps in certification

source_reference.source_text: Any Servicer that has a Multifamily Servicing-only approval, as described in Section 3.1(e), must certify to Freddie Mac in writing that all of the following have been paid during the preceding fiscal year and are not then delinquent, except as otherwise set forth in the certification: 1. Insurance premiums 2. Ground rents 3. Assessments 4. Taxes 5. Other charges that may, if not paid on a timely basis, become prior liens on the Property If any such items are delinquent, the certification must describe the nature of the delinquency and the steps being taken to cure that delinquency. The certification must be made using Form 1110M, Multifamily Annual Certification Report – Servicer Only. Servicers must use the Multifamily Eligibility System, available on FreddieMac.com, to complete the Form and to submit it to Freddie Mac. The Servicer must submit the completed Form 1110M within 90 days after the end of the Servicer’s fiscal year

examples: Servicer fiscal year ends 12/31; Form 1110M must be submitted by 03/31 with certification that taxes/insurance are current.
Taxes are delinquent as of certification date; Form 1110M can still be submitted if it describes the delinquency and cure steps.
Servicer submits Form 1110M 120 days after fiscal year end; this violates the 90-day submission requirement.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Annual Financial Due Date Adjustment When June 30 Is Impossible
business_rules.description: The Servicer may request an adjustment of the Annual Financial due date by contacting Freddie Mac Multifamily Asset Management via PRS if the Borrower’s fiscal year end prevents submission by June 30 (or other Freddie-required date), or if the Mortgage was an acquisition occurring so late in the prior calendar year that available months of data prevent meaningful evaluation.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Annual Financial timing/data constraints prevent meaningful/required submission
business_rules.consequences: If conditions met, Servicer may request due date adjustment; otherwise due date remains
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer may contact Freddie Mac Multifamily Asset Management, Asset Performance and Compliance via PRS to request an adjustment of the Annual Financial due date if: • The Borrower's fiscal year end would prevent the Servicer from submitting the Annual Financial by June 30 or other date required by Freddie Mac. • The Mortgage was for the acquisition of the Property, and the purchase occurred so late in the preceding calendar year that the number of months of data available for review would prevent the Servicer from making a meaningful evaluation.

examples: Fiscal year ends Dec 31; audited financials not available by June 30—Servicer requests adjusted due date via PRS.
Acquisition closed Dec 20; only 11 days of prior-year operations exist—Servicer requests a due date adjustment.
Borrower fiscal year end and acquisition timing do not hinder submission—Servicer should not request adjustment under this rule.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Partial Payment Shortfall Over $15 Must Be Remitted
business_rules.description: For bond credit enhancement accounting, when a partial payment results in a shortfall of more than $15 in the amount due to be remitted to Freddie Mac, the Servicer must notify Multifamily Loan Accounting of the shortfall and, on the applicable remittance date, must remit the partial payment to Freddie Mac rather than holding it in suspense or escrow.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to bond credit enhancement partial payments with >$15 remittance shortfall
business_rules.consequences: If met, Freddie receives partial remittance; if violated, Servicer noncompliance occurs
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.source_text: a. Partial payments (10/31/12) In the event of a partial payment entailing a shortfall of over $15 in an amount due to be remitted to Freddie Mac, the Servicer must notify Multifamily Loan Accounting of the shortfall. On the applicable remittance date, the Servicer must remit the partial payment to Freddie Mac, rather than holding the partial payment in suspense or escrow.

examples: Borrower pays $9,980 when $10,000 is due; $20 shortfall (> $15): Servicer notifies and remits $9,980 on remittance date.
Borrower pays $9,990 when $10,000 is due; $10 shortfall (≤ $15): this rule does not trigger (follow other guidance).
Servicer receives partial payment with $25 shortfall and mistakenly places it in suspense—violates rule.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Priority Repair Follow-Up Uses 30-Day Response and 10-Day Rights Letter
business_rules.description: For SBL Mortgages in scope, the Servicer must (i) update the PRS Loan Item Tracking (LIT) entry within 30 calendar days of deeming a Priority Repair or PR-90 Repair complete; (ii) if any Priority Repair is not completed, notify the Borrower and if the Borrower does not respond within 30 calendar days or responds without a satisfactory action plan, issue a “reservation of rights” letter as soon as possible and no later than 10 Business Days following Borrower inaction; and (iii) within five Business Days of sending the “reservation of rights” letter, provide Freddie Mac a copy via email and upload a copy to DMS.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL Priority Repairs/PR-90 Repairs tracked in PRS LIT
business_rules.consequences: If completed timely, PRS reflects compliance; if missed, Servicer violates required notice/reporting steps
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: 39.5(a)(1)-(2) Monitoring and completion of Priority Repairs (08/15/24)
source_reference.source_text: Within 30 calendar days of deeming any Priority Repair or PR-90 Repair item to be complete, the Servicer must update the respective PRS LIT accordingly. If the Servicer determines that any Priority Repair (including PR-90 Repairs) has not been completed, the Servicer must issue a notification to the Borrower... If the Borrower does not respond within 30 calendar days following such Servicer notification... Freddie Mac requires the Servicer to issue a “reservation of rights” letter as soon as possible, and no later than 10 Business Days following such Borrower inaction... Within five Business Days of sending the “reservation of rights” letter... provide Freddie Mac a copy... and upload a copy to the Document Management System (DMS).

examples: Servicer confirms PR-90 repair is complete on April 1; Servicer updates PRS LIT by May 1 (within 30 calendar days).
Servicer notifies Borrower of outstanding Priority Repair on June 1; Borrower gives no response by July 1; Servicer must send reservation of rights letter no later than 10 Business Days after July 1.
Servicer emails reservation of rights letter to Borrower on Aug 5; Servicer must email Freddie Mac a copy and upload to DMS within five Business Days (by Aug 12 if no holidays).
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans

business_rules.rule_name: LMF must be submitted within 10 business days and by Dec 31
business_rules.description: During any period Freddie Mac holds a Mortgage, the Servicer must submit the Loan Management Form (LMF) via PRS within 10 Business Days after determining that any LMF-trigger condition in Section 40.6(a) has occurred, regardless of the PRS due date. If the LMF was not submitted earlier in the year, it must also be submitted annually on or before December 31 via PRS.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Freddie Mac holds the Mortgage and an LMF trigger is determined.
business_rules.consequences: If met, Freddie Mac deems LMF timely; if violated, servicing noncompliance may be cited.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated (submission required regardless of PRS due date).

source_reference.section_id: 40.6(c) Timing of submission of the LMF (10/19/23)
source_reference.source_text: Servicers must submit the LMF within 10 Business Days of determining that any of the conditions in Section 40.6(a) above have occurred regardless of the due date in PRS. If not submitted earlier in the year, the LMF must be submitted annually on or before December 31st via PRS. See also Section 40.2(b).

examples: Servicer identifies Borrower non-monetary default on Monday; LMF must be submitted by end of day on the 10th business day after Monday.
Servicer does not identify any triggers all year; LMF still must be submitted by December 31 via PRS.
PRS indicates a later due date than 10 business days; Servicer must still submit within 10 business days.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing performance finding; corrective action requirement

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Random unit inspection sample is 5% with min 5 max 15 units
business_rules.description: For a comprehensive property inspection, the inspector must inspect a minimum number of randomly selected occupied and vacant units equal to 5% of total occupied and vacant units, subject to minimum and maximum unit counts. Standard minimum is 5 units and maximum is 15 units. For SBL Mortgages where the Property has 50 units or less, the minimum is 3 units and maximum is 15 units.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Servicer-required property inspections with occupied/vacant unit sampling.
business_rules.consequences: If met, inspection meets minimum sampling; if violated, report is deficient and may be rejected.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated for occupied/vacant sampling minima/maxima.

source_reference.source_text: Taking into account the cross -section of unit types and locations described above, the inspector must inspect at least the following number of units: • Occupied and vacant units: o 5 percent of total occupied and vacant units, with a minimum of five units and a maximum of 15 units o For SBL Mortgages where the Property has a total of 50 units or less: 5 percent of total occupied and vacant units, with a minimum of three units and a maximum of 15 units

examples: 100-unit property: 5% = 5 units; inspector must inspect at least 5 occupied/vacant units (cap 15).
20-unit SBL property: 5% = 1 unit, but minimum is 3 units; inspector must inspect 3 units.
400-unit property: 5% = 20 units, but maximum is 15; inspector inspects 15 units.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Down units and commercial space inspection is 100% unless sampling allowed
business_rules.description: For property inspections, the inspector must inspect 100% of Down Units and 100% of commercial space. If there is a significant number of Down Units due to a large renovation project or hazard loss, the inspector may choose to inspect a representative sampling of Down Units instead of 100%.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when inspection identifies Down Units or any commercial space at the Property.
business_rules.consequences: If met, inspection scope complies; if violated, inspection is incomplete and may require remediation.
business_rules.exceptions: Representative sampling allowed only for significant Down Units due to renovation/hazard loss.

source_reference.source_text: • Down Units: 100 percent (Note: If there is a significant number of Down Units due to large renovation project or hazard loss, inspector may choose to inspect a representative sampling of Down Units) • Commercial space: 100 percent The inspector must conduct additional occupied and vacant unit inspections, as needed, to determine whether any problems detected in units or specific buildings are restricted to the specific units/buildings or pervasive throughout the Property.

examples: Property has 2 Down Units: inspector must inspect both Down Units (100%).
Property has 40 Down Units due to a major renovation: inspector may inspect a representative sampling rather than all 40, documenting rationale.
Property includes 3 retail suites: inspector must inspect all commercial spaces (100%).
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Servicer Must Email Freddie Mac 30 Days Before Earn-Out Expiration
business_rules.description: If a rental achievement agreement includes an earn-out expiration date, the Servicer must monitor the earn-out expiration date and must contact Freddie Mac by email at least 30 days prior to the earn-out expiration date. The email must include the Servicer's discussion of property performance relative to the performance standard and must advise whether the Servicer expects the property to meet the standard before expiration, whether an extension request is likely, and whether the Servicer recommends any action by Freddie Mac.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when a rental achievement agreement includes an earn-out expiration date
business_rules.consequences: If timely emailed, Freddie Mac can act; if not, Servicer is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 39 – a. Monitoring; earn-out expiration notice (09/18/14)
source_reference.source_text: If the rental achievement agreement includes an earn -out expiration date, the Servicer must monitor the earn -out expiration date, and must contact Freddie Mac, via email at least 30 days prior to the earn -out expiration date, providing the Servicer's discussion of the Property's performance in relation to the performance standard. In the email, the Servicer must advise Freddie Mac whether: • The Servicer expects the Property to meet the performance standard prior to the earn -out expiration date, • The Borrower or Guarantor is likely to request an extension of the earn -out expiration date, and • The Servicer recommends any action by Freddie Mac.

examples: Earn-out expires 2026-09-30; Servicer emails Freddie Mac no later than 2026-08-31 with performance analysis and recommended action.
Servicer fails to email Freddie Mac until 10 days before expiration; this violates the 30-day notice requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing requirement (>=30 days) and explicit required email content.

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Transfer UCC search must be dated within 30 days and prior filings approved
business_rules.description: For a proposed Transfer of Ownership, the Servicer must obtain a statement from Servicer counsel confirming it examined the UCC search for the proposed transferee; the search must be dated no earlier than 30 days from the Transfer of Ownership; and the search must not indicate any previous filings, or if previous filings exist, those filings must have been approved by Freddie Mac.
business_rules.conditions: When delivering documentation for a proposed Transfer of Ownership
business_rules.consequences: If met, UCC search recency/filing status is validated; if violated, transfer documentation is deficient
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in excerpt

source_reference.source_text: 9. Statement from the Servicer's counsel that it has examined the UCC search for the proposed transferee, that the search is dated no earlier than 30 days from the Transfer of Ownership and that the search does not indicate any previous filings (or, in the ca se of previous filings, that any such filings have been approved by Freddie Mac) (copy)

examples: Transfer closing date June 30: UCC search dated June 10 (20 days prior) is acceptable if no prior filings are shown.
Transfer closing date June 30: UCC search dated May 15 (46 days prior) is noncompliant because it is earlier than 30 days.
UCC search shows prior filings: acceptable only if documentation shows Freddie Mac approved those filings.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICER_VALIDATION_020_009 mandates counsel involvement (counsel statement examining UCC search). BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_035_003 ensures counsel is available from the commencement of consideration through key milestones. The documentation validation step depends on having counsel engaged/available to prepare and sign the required confirmation.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_035_003 fails: (1) UCC search statement is delayed or missing, (2) Transfer of Ownership package is deficient, (3) Freddie Mac approval timeline slips, potentially delaying closing/assumption/transfer effective date.

dependencies.rationale: The transfer package requires a counsel statement (BR_020_009). Counsel eligibility (BR_029_001) is a foundational requirement for Freddie Mac acceptance of counsel work/product. Therefore, counsel must satisfy BR_029_001 for the BR_020_009 deliverable to be reliable and acceptable.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_ELIGIBILITY_029_001 fails: (1) Freddie Mac may reject the counsel statement, (2) transfer approval may be withheld pending reissuance by qualified counsel, (3) transfer closing can be delayed or canceled.

business_rules.rule_name: Title update report must be dated no earlier than 45 days before request
business_rules.description: For a Subordinate Financing request package, the Servicer must obtain a title update report dated no earlier than 45 days before the date of the Subordinate Financing request (i.e., report age must be ≤ 45 days as of the request date).
business_rules.conditions: When compiling documentation for a Subordinate Financing request
business_rules.consequences: If older than 45 days, request package is deficient and may be rejected/delayed
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 43 – Specific Servicing Responsibilities (Bulletin Update 02/24/26, Page 55, Item 15)
source_reference.source_text: 14. Current rent roll meeting the requirements set forth in Chapter 55 15. Title update report dated no earlier than 45 days before the date of the Subordinate Financing request 16. For all transactions in which the Borrower is a Cooperative (Co-op),

examples: Subordinate Financing request date is 2026-03-15; acceptable title update report date is 2026-01-29 or later (≤45 days old).
Request date is 2026-03-15 and report date is 2026-01-20 (54 days old); Servicer must order an updated report before submitting.
Request date is 2026-03-15 and report date is 2026-03-01 (14 days old); requirement is satisfied.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Recorded subordinate financing documents must be certified with recorder details
business_rules.description: Within the time specified in the Freddie Mac approval letter, the Servicer must electronically deliver executed Subordinate Financing documents (copies, certified copies, or originals as noted). If a certified copy of a filed/recorded document is required, it must show the recorder’s stamp and the book/page or instrument numbers. If recorder/clerk delays prevent timely delivery of a copy showing the required information, the Servicer may deliver a Title Company/closing attorney certified “true and correct copy” and must provide the recorded/filed original showing the required information as soon as available.
business_rules.conditions: After settlement of approved Subordinate Financing, per approval letter timeframe
business_rules.consequences: If not delivered/certified correctly, Freddie Mac may treat file as incomplete and require cure
business_rules.exceptions: Recorder delay allows interim 'true and correct' certified copy, then final later

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 43(d) Documents required after settlement of Subordinate Financing (04/18/24)
source_reference.source_text: Within the time specified in the Freddie Mac approval letter, the Servicer must electronically deliver to Freddie Mac a copy, a certified copy or an original (as noted) of the following executed Subordinate Financing documents listed below. If the provisions of this subsection require delivery of a certified copy of a filed or recorded document, the copy must show the recorder's stamp, book and page numbers or instrument numbers. If recorder or clerk delays make it impossible to effect timely delivery of a copy showing the required information, the Servicer may provide a copy that the Title Company or closing attorney has certified as a "true and correct copy of the recorded/filed original." The Servicer must then deliver to Freddie Mac a copy of the recorded/filed original showing the required information as soon as the copy becomes available.

examples: Servicer delivers a certified recorded Subordination Agreement showing recorder stamp and instrument number within the approval letter deadline; requirement is met.

County recorder backlog prevents obtaining a stamped copy by the deadline; Servicer sends a closing attorney-certified 'true and correct copy' and later sends the recorder-stamped version when available.
Servicer submits an uncertified PDF of a recorded security instrument without stamp/book/page; Freddie Mac requires a corrected certified copy showing recording data.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Unauthorized subordinate financing requires immediate written notice to Freddie Mac
business_rules.description: If the Servicer learns that unauthorized Subordinate Financing has been, or is about to be, placed on the Property, the Servicer must immediately notify Freddie Mac in writing.
business_rules.conditions: When Servicer becomes aware of actual or imminent unauthorized subordinate lien
business_rules.consequences: Servicer must escalate immediately; failure may increase loss exposure and trigger remedies
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must carry out Freddie Mac's instructions in dealing with proposed Subordinate Financing and must be alert to the unauthorized placement of Subordinate Financing on the Property. If the Servicer learns that unauthorized Subordinate Financing has been, or is about to be, placed on the Property, it must immediately notify Freddie Mac in writing.

examples: Borrower records a second mortgage without Freddie Mac approval; Servicer sends written notice to Freddie Mac immediately upon discovery.
Servicer receives draft payoff/closing instructions indicating an unapproved subordinate loan will close next week; Servicer immediately notifies Freddie Mac in writing.
A property management report hints at new subordinate debt but is unconfirmed; Servicer investigates promptly and, once learned, notifies Freddie Mac in writing.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 026_007 requires notification when the Servicer learns of 'unauthorized Subordinate Financing.' In operational compliance, 'unauthorized' is determined by comparing the financing against permission criteria (including term/amount constraints in 026_008). If those constraints are not met (and the lien is placed or imminent), the notification workflow must activate.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 026_008 is not applied, the Servicer may misclassify an impermissible lien as authorized, delaying required notification and increasing Freddie Mac loss exposure and remedies risk.

dependencies.rationale: 026_009 defines hard limits (combined LTV <= 80% and DCR >= 1.25x) for permissible Subordinate Financing. When the Servicer becomes aware of a lien that would violate these limits, it is operationally 'unauthorized' and the notification obligation in 026_007 becomes actionable.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 026_009 is not evaluated, impermissible leverage/cashflow deterioration can go unreported, delaying Freddie Mac intervention and increasing credit risk and potential servicing remedies.

dependencies.rationale: When a mortgage is purchased for securitization, BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 states subordinate financing is not permitted except the Supplemental Mortgage Product. If subordinate financing is placed or imminent outside the exception, it is by definition unauthorized/prohibited, which should trigger the immediate notice requirement in BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_007 upon the Servicer learning of it.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Servicer does not apply the securitization prohibition logic, it may fail to classify a subordinate lien as unauthorized and miss the immediate written notice requirement, increasing Freddie Mac risk and exposing the Servicer to remedies.

business_rules.rule_name: SBL subordinate financing requires at least 3 years remaining term and minimum $750,000
business_rules.description: For an SBL Mortgage securitized in an SBL Securitization, Subordinate Financing is permitted only if (among other conditions) the SBL Mortgage has three years or more remaining in its loan term, the Subordinate Financing amount is not less than $750,000, and the Subordinate Financing term is at least the remaining term of the SBL Mortgage but may not exceed 30 years.
business_rules.conditions: When requesting/approving Subordinate Financing on an SBL Mortgage in securitization
business_rules.consequences: If any threshold fails, Subordinate Financing is not permitted/approvable
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Section 43.30(a) Conditions for Freddie Mac approval (Items 6-8)
source_reference.source_text: 6. The SBL Mortgage has three years or more remaining in its loan term. 7. The Subordinate Financing is not less than $750,000. 8. The Subordinate Financing has a loan term equal to or longer than the remaining loan term of the SBL Mortgage but cannot exceed 30 years.

examples: An SBL Mortgage has 3.5 years remaining; proposed subordinate loan is $900,000 with a 10-year term; thresholds in this rule are met (other conditions still apply).
An SBL Mortgage has 2.8 years remaining; subordinate financing is $1,200,000; not permitted because remaining term is under 3 years.
An SBL Mortgage has 5 years remaining; subordinate financing is $700,000; not permitted because amount is below $750,000.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: One rule sets a broad prohibition for securitized mortgages; the other provides conditional permission for a specific securitized SBL scenario. This is either (a) an intended exception (e.g., 026_008 applies only when the subordinate financing is the permitted Supplemental Mortgage Product), or (b) a true inconsistency between guide sections. Either way, rule logic must be reconciled in automation and policy guidance.
