dependencies.impact_if_fails: If unresolved, the Servicer may (i) incorrectly deny permissible supplemental subordinate financing (business disruption), or (ii) incorrectly approve subordinate financing that is prohibited under securitization rules (high compliance breach, forced remediation, Freddie Mac enforcement/remedies).

business_rules.rule_name: SBL subordinate financing combined LTV must be at most 80% and DCR at least 1.25x
business_rules.description: For Subordinate Financing on an SBL Mortgage securitized in an SBL Securitization, the combined loan amount after the Subordinate Financing must not produce an LTV Ratio exceeding 80%, and combined debt service must not produce a DCR below 1.25x. For purposes of calculating LTV, the combined loan amount must be the aggregate outstanding principal balances of the SBL Mortgage, any other previously originated subordinate financing, and the proposed subordinate loan amount.
business_rules.conditions: When evaluating SBL Subordinate Financing under Section 43.30 conditions
business_rules.consequences: If combined LTV > 80% or DCR < 1.25x, Subordinate Financing is not permitted/approvable
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Section 43.30(a)(10) LTV/DCR thresholds and combined loan amount definition
source_reference.source_text: 10. The combined loan amount after the making of the Subordinate Financing does not result in a Loan-to-Value (LTV) Ratio that exceeds 80 percent and the combined debt service after the making of the Subordinate Financing does not result in a Debt Coverage Ratio (DCR) that is below 1.25x, using the following to calculate the LTV and DCR: • The combined loan amount used to calculate the LTV must be the aggregate outstanding principal balances of all of the following: o The SBL Mortgage o Any other previously originated subordinate financing o The proposed loan amount of the Subordinate Financing

examples: SBL UPB $20,000,000 + existing subordinate $1,000,000 + proposed $2,000,000 = $23,000,000 combined; if value is $30,000,000 then LTV=76.67% and passes LTV test (must also meet DCR≥1.25x).
Combined loan amount is $24,500,000 and value is $30,000,000; LTV=81.67% and fails (cannot approve as stated).
Projected combined debt service produces DCR=1.20x; fails because DCR is below 1.25x even if LTV is ≤80%.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 026_008 defines term/amount/remaining-term thresholds for permitted Subordinate Financing in an SBL Securitization. 026_009 separately defines combined leverage and cashflow thresholds (LTV and DCR). In practice, a Servicer’s approval workflow must satisfy BOTH sets to determine permissibility; they function as parallel mandatory checks for the same decision.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 026_008 is not satisfied/checked, the Servicer may approve Subordinate Financing that is structurally ineligible (e.g., too short remaining term) even if LTV/DCR pass, leading to an impermissible transaction and potential Freddie Mac remedies.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_026_009 is meaningful only if subordinate financing is allowed to be considered. BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 may categorically bar consideration unless the request falls within the Supplemental Mortgage Product exception. The knowledge graph should force a rule selection step: determine whether 026_009 is reachable under the 025_005 exception logic.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If unresolved, automation may approve a transaction based on 80% LTV / 1.25x DCR that is still prohibited for securitization purposes, or may incorrectly block a permitted supplemental transaction by never evaluating 026_009.

business_rules.rule_name: Material Vendor cannot manage loans with risk rating above 6 without Freddie Mac approval
business_rules.description: A Servicer may not use a Material Vendor to make decisions related to a Mortgage’s performance or credit risk unless approved by Freddie Mac. Specifically, without prior Freddie Mac approval, a Material Vendor may not monitor or manage a Mortgage with a Risk Rating greater than six, a delinquent or maturing Mortgage, or any Mortgage assigned to Freddie Mac Multifamily Asset Resolution (and may not perform other enumerated credit-risk decision functions).
business_rules.conditions: When Servicer uses a Material Vendor for servicing functions touching credit/performance risk
business_rules.consequences: If violated, delegation is prohibited and may trigger audit findings or approval restrictions
business_rules.exceptions: Freddie Mac approval allows delegation; then may delegate to a Servicing Agent

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 36(b) Servicing functions prohibited without prior approval (10/20/22)
source_reference.source_text: In general, the Servicer may not use a Material Vendor to make decisions related to the Mortgage's performance or credit risk, unless approved by Freddie Mac. Specifically, Servicers may not use a Material Vendor to: 1. Perform any required Mortgage performance risk assessment or make related recommendations 2. Monitor or manage a Mortgage with a Risk Rating greater than six, a Delinquent or maturing Mortgage, or any Mortgage assigned to Freddie Mac Multifamily Asset Resolution

examples: A Servicer assigns an offshore vendor to manage workouts for a Mortgage with Risk Rating 7; this is prohibited without Freddie Mac approval.
A vendor is asked to monitor and recommend actions for a 30+ day delinquent Mortgage; prohibited absent Freddie Mac approval.
A Servicer obtains Freddie Mac approval to delegate a restricted function; only then may the function be delegated in the approved manner.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Remit Monthly Payments by Second Business Day with 2:00 p.m. Cutoff
business_rules.description: For regular monthly payments received, the Servicer must remit to Freddie Mac not later than the second Business Day of the month following the month of receipt. If a payment due in a month is not received by the Servicer before that month’s last day, the Servicer must remit within one Business Day after receipt. For this requirement, Freddie Mac treats funds received from the Borrower prior to 2:00 p.m. Eastern Time as received that Business Day, with time being of the essence.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Servicer remittances of regular monthly payments under Credit Agreement
business_rules.consequences: Meets rule: timely remittance; violated: remittance late and subject to Freddie Mac remedies/escalation
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in cited text

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must remit all regular monthly payments due in accordance with the terms of the Credit Agreement and received by the Servicer to Freddie Mac not later than the second Business Day of the month following the month in which the Servicer receives those payments. If any such payment is not received by the Servicer prior to the last day of the month in which it is due, then the Servicer must remit such payment to Freddie Mac within one Business Day after the Servicer receives such payment. For the purposes of this section, Freddie Mac will consider all funds received from the Borrower prior to 2:00 p.m., Eastern Time (time being of the essence) to be received on that Business Day.

examples: Payment received March 10: remittance must be made no later than the second Business Day of April.
April payment due April 1 is received April 30 after 2:00 p.m. ET: treated as received next Business Day; remittance due within 1 Business Day after receipt.
Payment received May 31 at 1:30 p.m. ET: counted as May receipt; remittance deadline is the second Business Day of June.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear deadlines (2nd business day, 1 business day) and explicit 2:00 p.m. ET receipt cutoff.

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 033_005 governs how quickly borrower payments/reserves enter custodial accounts (by next business day). 027_006 governs when funds must be remitted to Freddie Mac (e.g., by the 2nd Business Day of the following month or within one day after receipt in certain cases). Operationally, the Servicer typically remits from custodial accounts; therefore the deposit step must happen first to reliably meet remittance deadlines.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 033_005 fails (late deposit), the Servicer risks missing 027_006 remittance windows, triggering late remittance exposure and potential Freddie Mac remedies/fees.

dependencies.rationale: 027_006 imposes remittance deadlines for regular monthly payments. 039_002 requires that specified collections (including monthly principal and interest) be remitted via the myOptigo Servicer Remittance system. To satisfy 027_006 in a compliant manner for those payment types, the Servicer must use the channel in 039_002.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 039_002 fails (wrong channel), the remittance may be rejected, misapplied, or credited late, causing noncompliance with 027_006 deadlines and increasing fee/escalation risk.

business_rules.rule_name: Send Monthly Payment Statement at Least 5 Business Days Before Month Start
business_rules.description: For each regular monthly payment due under the Credit Agreement, the Servicer must provide the Borrower (by fax or other electronic transmittal) with the Monthly Payment Statement at least five Business Days prior to the first day of the calendar month. The statement must detail all amounts due and payable, including Seasoning Fees and other fees due for that calendar month, including amounts that will accrue prior to the first day of the succeeding calendar month.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to recurring monthly billing for payments due under Credit Agreement
business_rules.consequences: Meets rule: timely borrower notification and billing; violated: late statement issuance and compliance breach
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in cited text

source_reference.source_text: For each regular monthly payment due under the Credit Agreement, the Servicer must provide the Borrower by fax, or by other electronic transmittal, with the Monthly Payment Statement, as set forth in the Credit Agreement, at least five Business Days prior to the first day of the calendar month. The Monthly Payment Statement must detail all amounts due and payable, together with any Seasoning Fees and any other fees due and payable for that calendar month, including all amounts which will accrue prior to the first day of the succeeding calendar month, pursuant to the requirements of the applicable Credit Agreement.

examples: For the July 1 payment cycle, the statement must be sent at least 5 Business Days before July 1 (per applicable calendar).
A statement sent 2 Business Days before the first of the month violates the timing requirement even if the borrower pays on time.
A statement that omits a known Seasoning Fee due that month fails the content requirement even if sent on time.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear advance notice timing (≥5 business days) and required statement content elements.

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Prepayment Payoff Statement Timing Uses 12:00 p.m. and 10 Business Days
business_rules.description: For a payoff prior to maturity of a Borrowing Tranche, the Servicer must provide the Borrower a statement of interest detailing the aggregate payoff amount on the same day it receives the Borrower’s prepayment notice if (i) notice is received prior to 12:00 p.m. Eastern Time and (ii) the notice day is at least 10 Business Days prior to the proposed prepayment date; otherwise the statement must be delivered on the next Business Day after receipt. Freddie Mac must approve the Servicer’s statement of interest before it is issued to the Borrower.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to prepayment payoff processing for Borrowing Tranche prior to maturity
business_rules.consequences: Meets rule: proper payoff disclosure timing and required Freddie Mac approval; violated: cannot issue statement and is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: If notice received after 12:00 p.m. ET or <10 Business Days, deliver next Business Day

source_reference.source_text: For a payoff prior to maturity of a Borrowing Tranche, the Servicer must provide the Borrower by fax, or by other electronic transmittal, with a statement of interest detailing the aggregate payoff amount including any applicable Accrued Interest on the same day the Servicer receives such Borrower’s notice of its intent to prepay (if the Servicer receives the Borrower’s notice prior to 12:00 p.m., Eastern Time on the day which is at least 10 Business Days prior to the proposed prepayment date), otherwise the statement of interest must be delivered on the Business Day following the receipt of the Borrower’s notice. Freddie Mac must approve the Servicer’s statement of interest detailing the aggregate payoff prior to the Servicer’s issuance of the statement of interest to the Borrower.

examples: Borrower notice received at 11:00 a.m. ET on a day that is 12 Business Days before prepay date: statement must be delivered the same day and approved by Freddie Mac first.
Borrower notice received at 2:00 p.m. ET (after noon): statement must be delivered the next Business Day, with Freddie Mac approval before issuance.
Borrower notice received at 10:00 a.m. ET but only 7 Business Days before proposed prepay date: statement must be delivered the next Business Day (not same day).
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing logic with two numeric triggers (12:00 p.m. ET and 10 business days) plus approval prerequisite.

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: A payoff statement must reflect all amounts due (principal, accrued interest, and any prepayment premium). BR_MORTGAGE_CONSTRAINT_004_009 governs prepayment premium applicability (yield maintenance period, post-YM 1% premium, and the window period exception). Therefore, payoff statement generation and validation depend on applying the prepayment premium rule correctly.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If yield maintenance/window period rules are not applied, the payoff quote may be incorrect, leading to borrower disputes, failed payoff execution, Freddie Mac approval delays (since Freddie Mac must approve the statement), and potential financial loss from under-collecting premiums.

business_rules.rule_name: Transfer of Ownership Requires Updated AIF if Older Than 6 Months or 12 for SBL
business_rules.description: For a Transfer of Ownership request, if neither a property condition report nor a physical risk report is required, and the most recent Annual Inspection Form (AIF) is dated more than six months prior to submission of the transfer request (or 12 months for an SBL Mortgage), then the Servicer must conduct a physical inspection of the Property and deliver an updated AIF to Freddie Mac.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Transfers of Ownership where AIF recency threshold is exceeded
business_rules.consequences: Meets rule: AIF/inspection requirement satisfied; violated: transfer submission incomplete and may be delayed/denied
business_rules.exceptions: Not applicable if PCR/physical risk report is required under 41.4(a)(10)

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 41, Section 41.4(a)(11) (as shown on Chapter 41 update 02/24/26)
source_reference.source_text: If neither a property condition report nor a physical risk report is required pursuant to Section 41.4(a)(10) above, and if the most recent AIF is dated more than six months prior to submission of the transfer request (or 12 months for an SBL Mortgage), then the Servicer must conduct a physical inspection of the Property and deliver an updated AIF to Freddie Mac.

examples: AIF is 8 months old at transfer submission and PCR is not required: servicer must inspect and provide updated AIF.
SBL Mortgage transfer submission with AIF dated 13 months ago and no PCR required: updated AIF is required.
AIF is 4 months old at submission: no updated AIF required under this rule (unless other requirements trigger).
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear recency thresholds and required actions (inspection + updated AIF).

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 027_009 mandates conducting a physical inspection and delivering an updated Annual Inspection Form (AIF) for certain Transfer of Ownership scenarios. 032_003 defines what must be assessed and included when evaluating a property’s physical condition—effectively governing the inspection content needed to produce a compliant updated AIF assessment.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 032_003 is not met, the 'updated AIF' produced to satisfy 027_009 may be incomplete, leading to Freddie Mac requesting resubmission or delaying/denying the transfer request.

dependencies.rationale: 027_009 begins with 'if neither a property condition report nor a physical risk report is required'. 027_010 defines the triggers that require delivering a property condition report or a Form 1108 Physical Risk Report for Transfers of Ownership. Therefore, the workflow must first apply 027_010 to decide whether 027_009 is even applicable.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 027_010 is skipped, the Servicer may incorrectly rely on an updated AIF inspection (027_009) when a property condition report/1108 is required, resulting in an incomplete transfer package and processing delays/denial.

dependencies.rationale: BR_027_009 is a conditional documentation/process step within Transfer of Ownership submissions (updated AIF/inspection when neither PCR nor physical risk report is required and AIF is stale). BR_018_009 is an eligibility blocker for Transfer of Ownership approval if an excluded party played a specified role. Therefore, BR_018_009 must be checked before performing BR_027_009 to avoid unnecessary inspections and incomplete/invalid submissions.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_018_009 is missed, the Servicer may conduct inspections and prepare updated AIFs, but the transfer can still be rejected as ineligible, generating cost, delays, and potential audit issues for not using the most current Exclusionary List.

dependencies.rationale: BR_027_009 is part of the Transfer of Ownership submission workflow. BR_007_003 is an absolute approval prohibition for a confirmed sanctions match. In compliance workflow automation, sanctions screening is therefore a must-pass gate before completing BR_027_009 tasks.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 fails, Transfer of Ownership is denied/blocked; performing BR_027_009 inspections and updating AIF creates avoidable operational cost and delay without any path to approval.

dependencies.rationale: BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 states Freddie Mac will not allow or approve any Transfer of Ownership if a Borrower/Borrower Principal/property management company is a Named Party on the FHFA SCP List. Therefore, Transfer-of-Ownership processing steps (including AIF/inspection requirements in BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_009) are subordinate to and overridden by the SCP screening outcome.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the SCP-screening prohibition is triggered, completing BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_009 does not salvage the transaction; the Transfer of Ownership approval must be denied/blocked. If the SCP check is missed, the Servicer risks advancing a prohibited transaction and creating severe compliance/audit findings.

business_rules.rule_name: Transfer Requires PCR for 15+ Years or Form 1108 Only if 20M or 25M and Not Rehab
business_rules.description: For Transfers of Ownership, the Servicer must deliver either (i) a property condition report prepared by a property condition consultant in compliance with Chapter 62 or (ii) a Form 1108 Physical Risk Report prepared in compliance with Chapter 66 if the Property was built more than 15 years previously, the most recent AIF noted significant deferred maintenance, or the buyer proposes significant capital improvements. Form 1108 is permitted only for a Mortgage with initial principal balance of $20 million or less, or a Supplemental Mortgage where combined initial principal (supplemental + senior UPB) is $25 million or less, and only if the Mortgage is not a Moderate Rehabilitation, Lease-up, Value-add, or Forward Commitment Mortgage.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Transfer of Ownership submissions meeting PCR/1108 trigger conditions
business_rules.consequences: Meets rule: transfer package includes correct physical condition evidence; violated: transfer request deficient and may be delayed/denied
business_rules.exceptions: Form 1108 not allowed if excluded mortgage types apply regardless of balances

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 41, Section 41.4(a)(10) (as shown on Chapter 41 update 02/24/26)
source_reference.source_text: Either a property condition report prepared by a property condition consultant in compliance with Chapter 62 or a Form 1108, Physical Risk Report prepared in compliance with Chapter 66 if any of the following apply: • The Property was built more than 15 years previously • The most recent Annual Inspection Form (AIF) noted significant Deferred Maintenance • The buyer proposes significant capital improvements Form 1108 is an option only for a Mortgage • With an initial principal balance of $20 million or less, or a Supplemental Mortgage where the combined initial principal balance of the Supplemental Mortgage and the unpaid principal balances of any senior Mortgages encumbering the Property are $25 million or less in the aggregate • That is not a Moderate Rehabilitation Mortgage, a Lease-up Mortgage, a Value-add Mortgage or a Forward Commitment Mortgage

examples: Property built 18 years ago: PCR or Form 1108 is required; if the loan is $18M and not rehab/lease-up/value-add/forward, Form 1108 may be used.
Supplemental Mortgage transfer where combined balances equal $26M: Form 1108 not allowed; must provide a property condition report (PCR) if trigger applies.
Value-add Mortgage transfer (any balance): Form 1108 is not permitted; use a PCR if any trigger condition applies.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Multiple explicit triggers and numeric balance thresholds with product-type exclusions for Form 1108.

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: BR_018_009 explicitly states that when an Exclusionary List party played a specified role with respect to a Transfer of Ownership or underlying real estate transaction, the Mortgage is not eligible for Freddie Mac's approval of the Transfer of Ownership. BR_027_010 governs which property condition/physical risk reports must be delivered as part of a Transfer of Ownership submission. Eligibility screening must occur first because an ineligible transfer should be stopped/exceptioned before spending time and cost on ordering and compiling third-party reports.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_018_009 fails (not performed or performed using an outdated list), the Servicer may order/submit PCR/1108 documentation for a transfer that is categorically ineligible, causing (1) avoidable third-party report costs, (2) processing delays, and (3) potential compliance findings for failure to use the current Exclusionary List.

dependencies.rationale: BR_007_003 states Freddie Mac will not 'allow a Transfer of Ownership' if the Borrower/Borrower Principal/Guarantor is a confirmed sanctions/exclusion match. Therefore, sanctions clearance is a hard gate before expending effort on BR_027_010 documentation to support a transaction that cannot be approved.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 fails (confirmed match), the Transfer of Ownership cannot be approved regardless of BR_027_010 compliance; continuing processing wastes time/cost and can create escalation/audit issues for servicing-related approvals.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_027_010 governs which physical condition reports are required for a Transfer of Ownership submission. BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 blocks Freddie Mac approval of any Transfer of Ownership if an SCP match exists, overriding the otherwise-applicable transfer documentation requirements.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Missing the SCP screen may lead to extensive PCR/1108 procurement for a transfer that must be denied; conversely, detecting SCP match early prevents unnecessary third-party report costs and prevents prohibited approval attempts.

business_rules.rule_name: Eligible depository must meet 2 ratings: S&P A/A-1, Moody’s A2/P-1, Fitch A/F1
business_rules.description: An FDIC-insured depository institution is an eligible depository for custodial accounts only if it satisfies at all times the minimum rating requirements of at least two of the specified rating services for both (i) long-term senior unsecured debt obligations or long-term deposit ratings and (ii) short-term unsecured debt obligations or short-term deposit ratings. Minimums: Standard & Poor’s (Long-term A; Short-term A-1), Moody’s (Long-term A2; Short-term P-1), Fitch (Long-term A; Short-term F1).
business_rules.conditions: When Servicer selects/monitors an FDIC-insured depository for custodial funds
business_rules.consequences: Meets: depository eligible; Violated: depository ineligible and custodial placement fails
business_rules.exceptions: Holding company/affiliate risk may render otherwise-rated institution ineligible

source_reference.source_text: An FDIC -insured depository institution must satisfy at all times the minimum rating requirements of at least two of the rating services identified below for (i) long -term senior unsecured debt obligation ratings or long -term deposit ratings and (ii) short -term unsecured debt obligation ratings or short -term deposit ratings to be considered an eligible depository. MINIMUM RATING REQUIREMENTS Standard & Poor’s Ratings  A  A-1 Moody’s Investor’s Service  A2  P-1 Fitch Ratings  A  F1

examples: Depository has S&P A/A-1 and Fitch A/F1; qualifies (2 agencies meet both long/short thresholds).
Depository has only Moody’s A2/P-1 but no second agency rating; does not qualify as eligible depository.
Depository meets ratings but becomes affected by a troubled affiliate; Freddie Mac may deem it ineligible case-by-case.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Suspension / required movement of custodial funds

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Servicer must answer delinquency info requests within 3 business days
business_rules.description: The Servicer must fulfill all Freddie Mac requests for further information regarding the Delinquency within three Business Days after the request.
business_rules.conditions: When Freddie Mac requests additional delinquency information from Servicer
business_rules.consequences: Meets: request satisfied timely; Violated: Servicer is noncompliant and may face fees/actions
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must fulfill all Freddie Mac requests for further information regarding the Delinquency within three Business Days after the request.

examples: Freddie Mac requests updated rent roll supporting delinquency risk; Servicer provides within 2 Business Days; compliant.
Freddie Mac requests borrower contact log; Servicer responds on day 4; noncompliant with 3 Business Day requirement.
Request arrives Friday; Servicer responds by Wednesday (3 Business Days later); compliant.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: REO Proceeds Wire Immediately; After 4 p.m. Next Day
business_rules.description: The escrow agent facilitating the REO purchase process must remit multifamily REO proceeds by wire transfer immediately after settlement. Wire transfers received after 4 p.m. Eastern Time are credited on the next Business Day. The wire transfer must reference identifying information including Property/Project name, the Freddie Mac loan number, remittance type, and appropriate Freddie Mac contact person(s).
business_rules.conditions: REO purchase process settlement occurs and proceeds are due to Freddie Mac
business_rules.consequences: If met, proceeds credited timely; if violated, crediting delayed and remittance is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 53 – Determining and Remitting Payments; REO proceeds (06/12/23)
source_reference.source_text: The escrow agent facilitating the REO purchase process must remit multifamily REO proceeds by wire transfer immediately after settlement.  Wire transfers received after 4 p.m.  Eastern Time will be credited on the next Business Day.  The escrow agent will receive wire transfer instructions prior to the closing from the applicable Freddie Mac Multifamily Attorney .  The wire transfer must reference the appropriate identifying information including  Property /Project  name, the Freddie Mac loan number , remittance type and a ppropriate Freddie Mac contact person(s) .

examples: Settlement completes at 2:30 p.m. ET and wire is sent at 3:00 p.m. ET: credited same day and compliant.
Settlement completes at 5:15 p.m. ET and wire arrives after 4 p.m. ET: credited next Business Day per rule.
Wire sent without the Freddie Mac loan number in the reference: noncompliant; may require reprocessing/clarification.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Noncompliance fee assessment / remittance exception reporting

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: REO Repurchase Proceeds Due Within 10 Business Days
business_rules.description: For repurchases of REO, within 10 Business Days following the date of Freddie Mac’s letter requiring or approving the repurchase, the Servicer must remit the multifamily REO repurchase proceeds by wire transfer. The Servicer must obtain wire transfer instructions from Freddie Mac at MF_Borrower_Transactions@freddiemac.com and must include identifying references in the wire (e.g., Property/Project name, Freddie Mac loan number, remittance type, and contact person(s)). Late remittance is noncompliant and may trigger fees/escalation/remedies.
business_rules.conditions: Freddie Mac issues a letter requiring or approving an REO repurchase
business_rules.consequences: If remitted within 10 Business Days, proceeds timely; if late, remittance is noncompliant and may trigger fees/escalation
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 53 – Determining and Remitting Payments; Proceeds of repurchases of REO (06/12/23)
source_reference.source_text: For repurchases of REO, within 10 Business Days following the date of Freddie Mac's letter requiring or approving the repurchase, the Servicer must remit the multifamily REO repurchase proceeds by wire transfer.  The Servicer must obtain wire transfer instructions from Freddie Mac  at MF_Borrower_Transactions@freddiemac.com .  The wire transfer must reference the appropriate  identifying information including Property /Project  name, the Freddie Mac loan number , remittance type and appropriate Freddie Mac contact person(s).

For repurchases of REO, within 10 Business Days following the date of Freddie Mac's letter requiring or approving the repurchase, the Servicer must remit the multifamily REO repurchase proceeds by wire transfer. The Servicer must obtain wire transfer instructions from Freddie Mac at MF_Borrower_Transactions@freddiemac.com. The wire transfer must reference the appropriate identifying information including Property/Project name, the Freddie Mac loan number, remittance type and appropriate Freddie Mac contact person(s).
source_reference.confidence_note: Word positions are computed from visible excerpt beginning.

examples: Freddie Mac letter dated June 1 → proceeds due by end of day on the 10th Business Day after June 1.
Wire must include the Freddie Mac loan number to ensure correct crediting.
Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules require the same remittance action for REO repurchase proceeds: within 10 Business Days following Freddie Mac’s letter requiring or approving the repurchase, the Servicer must remit proceeds by wire transfer, obtain wire instructions from MF_Borrower_Transactions@freddiemac.com, and include identifying references. Same timing (10 BD), same trigger (Freddie Mac letter), same channel (wire), same consequence (late remittance noncompliance).
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_031_008 selected as primary: more complete operational detail (explicitly includes obtaining wire instructions email and reference fields).

business_rules.rule_name: Inspection Photos Must Be Compressed and Captioned 40.7
business_rules.description: For an inspection submission, the Servicer must compress photographs and add them to the inspection submission. Each photograph must include an identifying caption.
business_rules.conditions: When submitting photographs as part of an inspection submission
business_rules.consequences: If met, submission satisfies photo format rules; if not, submission is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 3. Photographs must be compressed and added to the inspection submission. All photographs must include identifying captions.

examples: Servicer submits 22 JPEG photos, compressed, each captioned (e.g., 'Building 3 roof drain')—compliant.
Servicer uploads 18 photos but several have no captions—noncompliant and must be corrected before acceptance.
Servicer attaches uncompressed photo files that exceed system limits—noncompliant; resubmission required with compressed files.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'must' requirements; no numeric threshold stated for compression/captions.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Noncompliance finding; require corrected inspection submission

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Submit 20-25 Photos or Justify Fewer Than 20
business_rules.description: The Servicer should submit 20 to 25 photographs with the inspection submission based on property size/characteristics. For properties with fewer than 100 units, limited common areas, and/or few amenities, fewer photographs may be sufficient. If fewer than 20 photographs are submitted, the Servicer must provide a justification in the inspection submission comment section.
business_rules.conditions: When preparing an inspection submission with property photographs
business_rules.consequences: If <20 photos and no justification, submission is noncompliant; with justification, acceptable
business_rules.exceptions: Fewer photos permitted for <100 units with limited common areas/amenities

source_reference.source_text: Taking into account the above requirements, the size of the Property and the characteristics of the Property, the Servicer should submit 20 to 25 photographs with the inspection submission. For Properties with fewer than 100 units, limited common areas and/or few amenities, fewer photographs may be sufficient to meet the requirements in Section 40.7(b). If the Servicer believes that fewer than 20 photographs are sufficient for a Property, the Servicer must provide a justification in the comment section of the inspection submission.

examples: Property has 250 units and multiple amenities; servicer submits 24 photos—meets the 20–25 guidance.
Property has 80 units and minimal amenities; servicer submits 15 photos with a comment explaining limited common areas—compliant.
Property has 120 units; servicer submits 18 photos with no comment justification—noncompliant and must be remediated.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Uses both 'should' (20–25) and 'must' (justify <20). Enforced portion is justification requirement.

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 032_001 imposes formatting and caption requirements for each photograph. 032_002 imposes quantity expectations (20–25 photos) and requires justification if fewer than 20 are provided. These requirements operate together for a compliant inspection submission; while one does not strictly block the other, they are jointly required for a complete package.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 032_001 fails, photo artifacts may be rejected as noncompliant even if quantity/justification is met; if 032_002 fails, submission can be deemed deficient despite properly captioned/compressed photos.

business_rules.rule_name: Inspection Must Assess 9 Property Risk Item Types
business_rules.description: When evaluating a property’s physical condition, the Servicer must assess the current condition and trend of all major components and must include specified risk/condition items (repair agreement items, operational repairs, deferred maintenance, life safety hazards, environmental and moisture/mold issues, capital improvements, status of prior deferred maintenance, and property manager concerns).
business_rules.conditions: When completing the physical condition portion of an inspection assessment
business_rules.consequences: If included, assessment meets required scope; if missing items, assessment is incomplete
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: In evaluating the Property's physical condition, the Servicer must assess the current condition and trend of all major components of the Property. The Servicer must include in its assessment: • Incomplete items in any Repair Agreement, • Items in any Repair Agreement that were completed subsequent to the previous inspection, • Incomplete Operational Repair items, • Deferred Maintenance items, • Imminent Life Safety Hazards and Potential Life Safety Hazards, as defined in the Glossary, • Environmental issues and any moisture or Mold concerns

examples: Inspection narrative addresses deferred maintenance, prior repair agreement completion status, and current mold concerns—compliant.
Servicer reports only aesthetic issues and omits life safety hazards review—noncompliant due to missing required assessment items.
Property manager reports elevator reliability concerns; servicer includes these concerns in the assessment—compliant.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'must' scope; numeric element is the enumerated item set rather than a threshold.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Noncompliance finding; require revised assessment submission

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Report Remediation Cost Estimates Using 3 Source Types
business_rules.description: The Servicer must estimate and report remediation costs for deferred maintenance, life safety hazards, environmental issues, and moisture/mold issues. Acceptable sources for cost estimates include: (1) bids received by the property manager, (2) cost projections by the property manager, and (3) the inspector’s estimate based on experience or industry cost standards.
business_rules.conditions: When identified issues require remediation cost reporting in an assessment
business_rules.consequences: If cost estimates and sources provided, reporting is complete; if absent, reporting is deficient
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must estimate and report the cost to remediate any Deferred Maintenance, Life Safety Hazards, environmental issues and moisture or Mold issues. Sources for these cost estimates include: • Bids received by the property manager, • Cost projections by the property manager, and • The inspector’s estimate based on experience or industry cost standards

examples: Servicer reports $48,000 mold remediation based on two vendor bids provided by the property manager—compliant.
Servicer identifies a life safety hazard but provides no cost estimate—noncompliant reporting.
Servicer uses inspector estimate referencing industry unit costs for roof replacement—compliant source type.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'must estimate and report'; sources are enumerated clearly.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Noncompliance finding; require updated cost reporting

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 032_003 defines the required scope of physical condition evaluation and the specific risk/condition items to be assessed (deferred maintenance, life safety hazards, environmental/moisture issues, etc.). 032_004 requires estimating and reporting remediation costs for those same issue categories. The estimation obligation depends on the identification and evaluation outputs from 032_003.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 032_003 is incomplete, 032_004 outputs will be incomplete/incorrect, leading to deficient inspections, improper risk rating, and missed borrower/Freddie Mac notifications.

business_rules.rule_name: Borrower Notice Within 30 Days if Repairs >$10k or 10% UPB
business_rules.description: If specified property-related issues occur following an inspection (per the Guide’s trigger conditions), the Servicer must notify the Borrower within 30 days after the inspection that corrective action is required and must refer the Borrower to the applicable Loan Agreement obligations. The notice must detail all Imminent Life Safety Hazards and Potential Life Safety Hazards, and the Servicer must confirm that the Borrower received the notice. Failure to provide timely notice/confirmation is a servicing noncompliance requiring remediation.
business_rules.conditions: After an inspection when any listed trigger condition is identified
business_rules.consequences: If sent/confirmed timely, satisfies corrective-action notice; if not, noncompliant servicing action
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: If any of the property related issues identified below occur, the Servicer must issue a notification to the Borrower within 30 days following the inspection of the need to undertake corrective action to address all identified risks and refer the Borrower to its obligations under the Loan Agreement. The foregoing notice must detail all Imminent Life Safety Hazards and Potential Life Safety Hazards. The Servicer must confirm that the Borrower has received such notice. • The estimated sum for remediation of all Deferred Maintenance items is greater than $10,000 or 10% of the UPB • An Imminent or Potential Life Safety Hazard • The Property received an overall property condition rating of 4 or 5

If any of the property related issues identified below occur, the Servicer must issue a notification to the Borrower within 30 days following the inspection of the need to undertake corrective action to address all identified risks and refer the Borrower to its obligations under the Loan Agreement. The foregoing notice must detail all Imminent Life Safety Hazards and Potential Life Safety Hazards. The Servicer must confirm that the Borrower has received such notice. • The estimated sum for remediation of all Deferred Maintenance items is greater than $10,000 or 10% of the UPB • An Imminent or Potential Life Safety Hazard • A significant deficiency affecting the structure or value of the Property (including moisture or Mold issues or environmental concerns), or that could reasonably affect the health or safety of tenants • The Property has had an unauthorized change in property manager • The Property has any unauthorized significant capital improvements or renovations that are likely to or are already negatively impacting occupancy or cash flow • The Property received an overall property condition rating of 4 or 5

examples: Inspection identifies life safety hazards → written notice must be issued within 30 days and receipt confirmed.
If remediation estimates exceed a stated trigger threshold in the Guide, the same 30-day notice requirement applies.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Multiple explicit triggers with precise thresholds and deadlines; excerpt includes key thresholds and rating trigger.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing noncompliance; corrective action demand by Freddie Mac

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules impose the same post-inspection Borrower notice requirement: when specified property-related trigger issues are found after an inspection, the Servicer must notify the Borrower within 30 days after the inspection, refer to Loan Agreement obligations, detail all Imminent Life Safety Hazards and Potential Life Safety Hazards, and confirm Borrower receipt. Rule 011_009 is a shortened version of 032_005 but does not change the duty, timing, or required content.
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_032_005 selected as primary: more complete trigger framing and clearer articulation of notice content requirements; preserves the same timing obligation.

dependencies.rationale: 032_005 is triggered by specified issues, including '(a) estimated sum for remediation of all Deferred Maintenance items > $10,000'. 032_004 is the rule that requires estimating and reporting remediation costs. Therefore, 032_004 is a mandatory prerequisite to determine whether the threshold is met and to populate notice content accurately.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 032_004 fails, the Servicer may miss the 30-day borrower notification requirement or send an incomplete notice, resulting in servicing noncompliance and increased life-safety/legal risk.

dependencies.rationale: 032_005 requires borrower notification when specified property-related issues occur after an inspection and requires detailing Imminent/Potential Life Safety Hazards. 032_003 defines the assessment scope that identifies these hazards and deferred maintenance. Without properly performing 032_003, the Servicer cannot reliably determine whether 032_005 is triggered or what must be included in the notice.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 032_003 is deficient, the Servicer may fail to send required notice within 30 days, omit required hazard details, or fail to confirm borrower receipt, increasing safety and compliance exposure.

business_rules.rule_name: Notify Freddie Within 10 Business Days After PRS AIF Submission
business_rules.description: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac by email promptly and no later than 10 Business Days after submitting the AIF assessment into PRS if specified property-related issues occur and have not been previously reported to Freddie Mac. Notification must be sent to MF_Surveillance@freddiemac.com.
business_rules.conditions: After AIF assessment submission in PRS when listed issues occur and are new
business_rules.consequences: If timely emailed, meets surveillance notification; if late/omitted, noncompliant reporting
business_rules.exceptions: Does not apply if the issue was previously reported to Freddie Mac

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must promptly and, in any case, no later than 10 Business Days from submitting the AIF assessment into PRS, notify Freddie Mac via email at MF_Surveillance@freddiemac.com if any of the following property related issues occur and have not been previously reported to Freddie Mac: • The Property has had an unauthorized change in property manager • The Property has a significant deficiency affecting the structure or value of the Property

examples: AIF submitted May 1; unauthorized manager change identified; servicer emails Freddie Mac May 8 (6 business days)—compliant.
AIF submitted June 3; significant structural deficiency identified; servicer emails June 25—noncompliant (>10 business days).
Issue was already reported in a prior surveillance email; servicer does not resend—per exception, acceptable.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit deadline and channel; exception for previously reported issues is stated.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing noncompliance; escalation to Asset Performance and Compliance

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 032_006 requires notifying Freddie Mac when specified issues occur and have not been previously reported, after submitting the AIF assessment into PRS. 032_003 defines the required assessment scope that surfaces those issues. The decision to send the email and the content depend on completing the 032_003 assessment properly.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 032_003 fails, the Servicer may not identify reportable issues and may miss the 10 Business Day notification deadline, leading to surveillance reporting noncompliance.

dependencies.rationale: 032_006 is triggered by specified issues occurring after AIF submission; several of these issue categories are the same ones for which 032_004 mandates cost estimates (deferred maintenance, life safety, environmental, moisture/mold). Practically, Freddie Mac surveillance notification requires accurate identification and characterization, which is materially supported by the 032_004 remediation cost estimation outputs.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 032_004 fails, Freddie Mac may receive incomplete surveillance reporting (missing cost/severity context), leading to follow-up requests, processing delays, and potential noncompliance findings.

business_rules.rule_name: Reserve Custodial Accounts Must Be Separate and Titled Exactly
business_rules.description: A Servicer must establish and maintain at all times one or more Reserve Custodial Accounts that are separate from any principal and interest Custodial Account and separate from any collection clearing account. Each Reserve Custodial Account must be titled exactly as “(Name of Depositor/Servicer), as trustee for Freddie Mac”. In establishing the account(s), the Servicer must use the Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer number used in connection with its agreement to service the related Mortgage.
business_rules.conditions: Applies whenever a Servicer holds Borrower reserve funds for Freddie Mac Mortgages
business_rules.consequences: If structured/titled correctly, reserve custody is compliant; if not, Freddie may require separate accounts
business_rules.exceptions: Servicer may maintain accounts in-house if Servicer is an eligible depository

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 52 – Custodial Accounts (52.4 Reserve Custodial Accounts)
source_reference.source_text: 52.4 Reserve Custodial Accounts (09/ 14/23) A Servicer must establish, and maintain at all times, one or more Reserve Custodial Accounts, separate from any principal and interest Custodial Account, and separate from any collection clearing account, at an eligible depository, as described in Section 52.2. In establishing the Reserve Custodial Accounts, the Servicer must use the Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer number that it used in connection with its agreement to service the Mortgage relating to that account. The Reserve Custodial Accounts must each be titled exactly in the following manner: (Name of Depositor/Servicer), as trustee for Freddie Mac

examples: Servicer opens a reserve custodial account at an eligible bank titled “ABC Servicing, as trustee for Freddie Mac” and uses its Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer number for the account setup.
Servicer holds reserve funds in a general operating account or collection clearing account; this violates the separation requirement and may trigger Freddie Mac remediation.
Servicer is an eligible depository and maintains reserve custodial accounts in-house, but still titles the accounts exactly as required.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Remediation directive; potential servicing enforcement action
business_rules.extraction_notes: Exact titling requirement and structural separation are explicit 'must' statements.

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 033_004 requires establishing and maintaining Reserve Custodial Accounts. 029_003 defines when an FDIC-insured institution is an 'eligible depository' based on ratings and ongoing compliance. Account establishment/maintenance must therefore be done with an institution that satisfies 029_003 (or the custodial setup fails eligibility).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 029_003 is not satisfied, Reserve Custodial Accounts may be placed at an ineligible bank, creating custodial noncompliance and potential remediation (moving accounts, retitling, operational disruption).

business_rules.rule_name: Custodial Deposits Due by First Business Day After Receipt
business_rules.description: All principal and interest payments and all Reserve funds must be deposited into the applicable Custodial Accounts no later than the first Business Day after receipt by the Servicer. If Borrowers remit payments to a lockbox service or other agent, the agent must deposit all payments into a collection clearing account no later than the first Business Day after receipt, and the Servicer must then deposit principal, interest, and Reserve funds into the appropriate Custodial Accounts no later than the first Business Day after the agent deposit date.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to all Borrower payments received by Servicer directly or via lockbox/agent
business_rules.consequences: If timely, custodial compliance met; if late, custodial exceptions and potential servicing remedies
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 52 – Custodial Accounts (52.7 Deposits to Custodial Accounts)
source_reference.source_text: 52.7 Deposits to Custodial Accounts (09/14/23) a. Borrower payment sent to the Servicer (10/31/12) All principal and interest payments and all Reserve funds must be deposited into the applicable Custodial Accounts no later than the first Business Day after their receipt by the Servicer. b. Borrower payments sent to a lockbox ( 09/14/23) If a Servicer requires Borrowers to remit Mortgage payments (including partial payments and monies to be applied at a future date) to a lockbox service or other agent, the agent must deposit all payments into a collection clearing account no later than the first Business Day after their receipt.

examples: Servicer receives a payment on Tuesday; deposits P&I and reserves into custodial accounts by Wednesday (first Business Day after receipt).
Lockbox receives payment Friday; lockbox deposits to collection clearing account by Monday; Servicer deposits funds to custodial accounts by Tuesday.
Servicer receives payment Monday but deposits into custodial accounts on Thursday; this violates the first-Business-Day requirement.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing breach; potential transfer for cause or other remedies
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear timing rule with explicit 'no later than the first Business Day' requirement for both direct and lockbox scenarios.

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 033_005 mandates deposit of principal/interest and Reserve funds into applicable Custodial Accounts by the first Business Day after receipt. 033_004 defines the required Reserve Custodial Accounts (separate, titled, trustee language, and correct Seller/Servicer number). Deposit compliance requires the accounts to be established correctly first.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 033_004 fails, 033_005 cannot be executed compliantly (no compliant account to deposit into), leading to custodial exceptions, commingling risk, and potential servicing remedies.

business_rules.rule_name: Servicing Transfer Requires Nonrefundable $1,000 Wire Fee
business_rules.description: When submitting Form 981M (Transfer of Servicing Agreement) or Form 983M (Transfer of Securitized Servicing Agreement), as applicable, the transferor must remit a nonrefundable $1,000 transfer processing fee by wire transfer. The wire must reference the Seller/Servicer name, Seller/Servicer number, and the Freddie Mac contact person.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when a Transfer of Servicing request is submitted to Freddie Mac
business_rules.consequences: If fee remitted as required, request can be processed; if not, transfer approval may be delayed/blocked
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 42 – Transfers of Servicing (42.3(b) Fee)
source_reference.source_text: b. Fee ( 08/15/24) With the Form 981M, Transfer of Servicing Agreement , or Form 983M, Transfer of Securitized Servicing Agreement , as applicable, the transferor must remit a nonrefundable transfer processing fee of $1000. The transferor must remit the transfer processing fee by wire transfer. The Servicer must obtain wire transfer instructions from Freddie Mac Multifamily Counterparty Risk & Compliance .

examples: Transferor submits Form 981M and wires $1,000 with reference including Seller/Servicer number and Freddie Mac contact; fee requirement satisfied.
Transferor submits Form 983M but mails a check instead of wiring funds; does not meet the wire-transfer requirement and may delay processing.
Transferor wires $1,000 but omits the Seller/Servicer number from the wire reference; wire may require correction to be properly applied.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Transfer request processing delay or non-acceptance
business_rules.extraction_notes: Exact dollar amount and payment method are explicitly stated in Chapter 42 fee subsection.

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Credit facility fee is percent of UPB excess 03/31/11
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac’s credit facility fee is calculated as a percentage of the excess of the Mortgage UPB over the balance in any principal reserve fund, disregarding any investment earnings accumulated in the principal reserve fund. The Servicer must remit this credit facility fee to Freddie Mac each month from payments collected under the reimbursement and security agreement.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to TAH BCE transactions with a credit facility fee due monthly
business_rules.consequences: If calculated and remitted monthly, obligation is met; if not remitted, fee remains due
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Freddie Mac will receive a credit facility fee, which will be expressed as a percentage of the excess of the UPB of the Mortgage over the balance in any principal reserve fund (disregarding any investment earnings accumulated in the principal reserve fund) . The Servicer must remit the credit facility fee to Freddie Mac each month from payments collected under the reimbursement and security agreement.

examples: UPB $10,000,000; principal reserve fund balance $1,500,000 (ignore earnings): fee base = $8,500,000; apply the negotiated percentage and remit monthly.
UPB $10,000,000; principal reserve fund has $1,500,000 plus $20,000 earnings: fee base still uses $1,500,000 (earnings disregarded).
Servicer collects reimbursement-and-security-agreement payments but fails to remit the facility fee in a month; fee remains due for that month.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Fee collection through remittance; potential noncompliance remedies

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 034_002 requires calculating and remitting a credit facility fee each month 'from payments collected under the reimbursement and security agreement.' 033_005 governs timely deposit of payments and Reserve funds after receipt. While the exact fee source is not limited to custodial accounts here, deposit controls are foundational to ensuring collected funds are available and tracked for monthly fee remittance.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 033_005 fails, collected funds may be delayed/misapplied, potentially causing late/incorrect monthly fee remittance and reconciliation issues.

business_rules.rule_name: Variable-rate liquidity fee is percent of UPB 03/31/11
business_rules.description: For a variable-rate Mortgage, Freddie Mac’s liquidity facility fee is calculated as a percentage of the UPB of the Mortgage without reduction by amounts on deposit in any principal reserve fund. The Servicer will remit the liquidity facility fee to Freddie Mac each month from payments collected under the reimbursement and security agreement.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when the Mortgage is variable-rate and liquidity fee is applicable
business_rules.consequences: If fee is computed on full UPB and remitted monthly, obligation is met; if reduced by reserve deposits, calculation is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: In the case of a variable -rate Mortgage, Freddie Mac will receive a liquidity facility fee, which will be expressed as a percentage of the UPB of the Mortgage (without reduction of the UPB by amounts on deposit in any principal reserve fund). The Servicer will remit the liquidity facility fee to Freddie Mac each month from payments collected under the reimbursement and security agreement.

examples: Variable-rate UPB $12,000,000; principal reserve deposit $2,000,000: fee base remains $12,000,000 (no reduction) and is remitted monthly.
Servicer incorrectly reduces UPB by reserve deposits before applying liquidity fee percentage; calculation violates the rule and must be corrected.
Fixed-rate Mortgage: liquidity facility fee section does not apply because it is limited to variable-rate mortgages.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Fee collection through remittance; potential noncompliance remedies

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 034_003 requires calculating and remitting a liquidity facility fee monthly from collected payments under the reimbursement and security agreement. 033_005 ensures prompt deposit/handling of received funds, supporting availability and traceability for monthly remittance processing.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 033_005 fails, remittance execution and reconciliation of monthly liquidity facility fee may be delayed or incorrect, increasing compliance and operational risk.

business_rules.rule_name: Form 1110M Annual Reserve Certification Due Within 90 Days
business_rules.description: Any Servicer with a Multifamily Servicer-only approval must certify to Freddie Mac in writing within 90 days after the end of the Servicer’s fiscal year that all Reserve collections and disbursements were made in accordance with the applicable Reserve agreements. The Servicer must explain in writing any discrepancies and describe steps to resolve each matter. The certification must be made using Form 1110M via the Multifamily Eligibility System and submitted within the same 90-day timeframe following the form’s instructions.
business_rules.conditions: When Servicer has Multifamily Servicer-only approval and fiscal year ends
business_rules.consequences: If met: annual compliance satisfied; if violated: noncompliance finding/escalation
business_rules.exceptions: Applies only to Servicer-only approval (Section 3.1(e))

source_reference.source_text: Any Servicer that has a Multifamily Servicer -only approval, as described in Section 3.1(e), must certify to Freddie Mac in writing within 90 days after the end of the Servicer's fiscal year that all Reserve collections and disbursements have been made in accordance with the applicable Reserve agreements. The Servicer must explain in writing any discrepancies from the agreements and provide a description of the steps being taken to resolve each matter. The certification must be made using Form 1110M

examples: Fiscal year ends 12/31; Form 1110M must be submitted no later than 03/31 with discrepancy explanations if any.
Servicer discovers an out-of-sequence reserve disbursement; reports discrepancy and remediation plan in the certification package.
If Form 1110M is submitted after day 90, Freddie Mac may issue a compliance finding and require corrective action.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Corrective action plan; potential servicing remedies

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 035_007 requires an annual certification that Reserve collections and disbursements were made in accordance with applicable Reserve agreements. 033_005 is a core operational control for Reserve fund handling (timely deposit to custodial accounts). While certification also depends on disbursement rules not included here, 033_005 is a foundational prerequisite for accurate, compliant Reserve accounting and the ability to certify.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 033_005 fails, the Servicer’s Reserve handling may be noncompliant, making 035_007 certification inaccurate or requiring discrepancy explanations and remediation steps.

dependencies.rationale: 035_007 requires annual certification and explanation of discrepancies, which commonly relies on retained records supporting Reserve activity. 039_001 prohibits surrender/destruction of canceled checks, bank statements, or other mortgage records without Freddie Mac approval. Record retention under 039_001 enables evidentiary support for the 035_007 certification process.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 039_001 fails (records destroyed without approval), the Servicer may be unable to evidence Reserve compliance during certification or audit, increasing compliance findings and remediation workload.

business_rules.rule_name: Annual Evaluation Items Delivered to Freddie Mac Within 30 Days
business_rules.description: Annually (as prescribed in the Credit Agreement), the Servicer must notify the Borrower to deliver the items set forth in the Credit Agreement. After the Servicer receives those items from the Borrower, the Servicer must deliver all such items to Freddie Mac within 30 days following receipt.
business_rules.conditions: When annual Borrower/Guarantor evaluation items are received by Servicer
business_rules.consequences: If met: Freddie Mac receives timely monitoring info; if violated: escalation/finding
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 43 g. Annual Borrower and Guarantor evaluation (06/29/17)
source_reference.source_text: g. Annual Borrower and Guarantor evaluation (06/29/17) Annually, as prescribed in the Credit Agreement, the Servicer must notify the Borrower to deliver the items set forth in the Credit Agreement. The Servicer must deliver all such items to Freddie Mac within 30 days following receipt from the Borrower.

examples: Borrower delivers annual financials on 02/01; Servicer delivers the package to Freddie Mac by 03/03 (within 30 days).
Borrower delivers items late; once received, Servicer still must transmit to Freddie Mac within 30 days of receipt.
If Servicer receives items but waits 45 days to deliver, Freddie Mac may require corrective action and monitoring.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Corrective action plan; potential servicing remedies

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Post-Transfer Funds Must Be Sent to Transferee in 1 Day
business_rules.description: After a transfer of servicing becomes effective, any funds for, or correspondence regarding, transferred mortgages that the transferor receives on or after the effective date must be delivered to the transferee within one day of receipt.
business_rules.conditions: When transferor receives funds/correspondence after effective transfer date
business_rules.consequences: Delivered within 1 day: compliant; late delivery: servicing noncompliance exposure
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 42.12 Funds and correspondence received after transfers of Servicing (10/07/02) Within one day of receipt, the transferor must deliver to the transferee any funds for, or correspondence regarding, any of the transferred Mortgages received on or after the effective date of transfer.

examples: Transfer effective 06/01; transferor receives borrower payment on 06/03 and forwards to transferee by 06/04: compliant.
Transferor receives escrow notice on 06/10 but delivers on 06/15: noncompliant (exceeds 1 day).
Transferor receives both funds and correspondence on 07/01; both must be delivered to transferee by 07/02.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Indemnification / servicing performance remedy per contract

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 033_006 governs remitting the transfer processing fee when submitting transfer forms. 037_008 governs handling funds/correspondence received after the transfer becomes effective. While 037_008 is triggered by effectiveness (which may involve other steps not listed), 033_006 is part of the upstream transfer workflow and is operationally sequenced before post-effective-date handling obligations apply.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 033_006 is not done, transfer processing may be delayed, extending the period the transferor remains responsible and increasing risk of misdirected funds/correspondence handling under 037_008.

business_rules.rule_name: Engineer certification required for structural or major system repairs
business_rules.description: The Servicer must cause a qualified engineer to certify repairs were completed in a good and workmanlike manner when: (i) improper work might contribute to material failure of any building component and/or physical deterioration; (ii) the repair is structural; or (iii) the repair is to major building systems (e.g., electrical, mechanical, fire protection). The engineer must meet Section 62.8 or Section 62SBL.17 qualifications (as applicable). If the Servicer has no qualified engineer on staff, the Servicer must retain a qualified third-party engineer; and when specialized knowledge is needed, retain an engineer with appropriate expertise.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when repairs pose material failure risk, are structural, or major systems
business_rules.consequences: If required engineer cert obtained, repair completion is properly documented; if not, Servicer is noncompliant and must remediate per Freddie Mac direction
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must cause a qualified engineer to certify that repairs have been completed in a good and workmanlike manner when any one of the following conditions is present: • The repair, if improperly done, might contribute to material failure of any building component and/or the physical deterioration of the facility • The repair is a structural repair • The repair consists of repair to major building systems (for example, electrical, mechanical, fire protection, etc.) The engineer must meet or exceed the qualification requirements in Section 62.8 or Section 62SBL.17, as applicable.

examples: A fire protection system repair is completed; Servicer must obtain a qualified engineer certification before treating the repair as satisfactorily completed.
A structural beam repair is performed; Servicer must retain a third-party qualified engineer if no qualified engineer is on staff.
Repairs to roofing that, if improperly done, could cause deterioration; Servicer must require an engineer certification due to material failure/deterioration risk.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'must cause' requirement with clear triggering conditions; qualifications referenced by section.

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

business_rules.rule_name: Mortgage file must include color photos and final repair date
business_rules.description: For each completed repair, the Servicer must maintain supporting documentation in the Mortgage File including: (i) a list of completed items, (ii) color photographs documenting completed repairs, (iii) the final completion date, and (iv) the engineer’s certification when required that repairs were completed in a good and workmanlike manner.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to each completed repair governed by a Repair Agreement
business_rules.consequences: If documentation is complete, repair servicing file supports compliance; if missing, Servicer must cure file deficiencies and may be cited for noncompliance
business_rules.exceptions: Engineer certification only when required under Guide triggers

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must maintain supporting documentation in the Mortgage File for each completed repair, including: • A list of the items that have been completed, • Color photographs documenting the completed repairs, • The final completion date, and • The engineer’s certification, when required, that repairs have been completed in a good and workmanlike manner.

examples: Repair escrow funds are released after completion; Mortgage File contains item list, color photos, and the final completion date.
Structural repairs completed; Mortgage File includes the engineer certification in addition to photos and completion list.
Photos are black-and-white only; Servicer must obtain and retain color photos to satisfy the documentation requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'must maintain' list of required file contents; exception embedded ('when required').

examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors

dependencies.rationale: 038_001 sets the conditions under which a qualified engineer certification must be obtained. 038_002 mandates that the engineer’s certification be maintained in the Mortgage File when required. Therefore, determining and obtaining the certification per 038_001 is necessary before 038_002 can be satisfied for those repairs.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 038_001 is not complied with, 038_002 documentation will be incomplete, exposing the Servicer to repair-agreement compliance findings and potential Freddie Mac-directed remediation.

dependencies.rationale: 038_002 requires maintaining supporting repair documentation (photos, lists, dates, engineer cert) in the Mortgage File. 039_001 prohibits surrender/destruction of mortgage records without Freddie Mac approval. Record retention under 039_001 supports the ongoing ability to meet the 'maintain' requirement in 038_002.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 039_001 is violated, repair documentation may be missing during audits or asset management reviews, triggering file deficiency findings and remediation requirements.

business_rules.rule_name: Extension review required if repairs late by 30 days after due date
business_rules.description: If it becomes apparent that all required repairs will not be completed by the applicable completion date, or if repairs are not complete within 30 days of the applicable completion date, the Servicer must work with the Borrower to determine whether an extension is appropriate and must document the extension as required by the Guide section.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when repairs will miss the due date or remain incomplete 30 days after due date
business_rules.consequences: If extension process completed, Servicer can seek/record approved extension; if not, Servicer is noncompliant and may be required to remediate and report
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: If it becomes apparent to the Servicer that all required repairs will not be completed by the applicable completion date, or the required repairs are not complete within 30 days of the applicable completion date, the Servicer must work with the Borrower to determine whether an extension is appropriate, and to document that extension as required in this section.

examples: Two weeks before the repair deadline, contractor delays make completion unlikely; Servicer must engage Borrower to evaluate and document an extension request.
Repairs are still incomplete 20 days after the due date; Servicer must continue monitoring and prepare to initiate the extension process if not completed by day 30.
