business_rules.conditions: When updated PCR or the required inspection is received/completed for reserve adequacy evaluation
business_rules.consequences: If met: timely Freddie Mac review of reserve adjustment; if violated: late reporting and servicing noncompliance
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.source_text: 3. Within 15 days following receipt of the updated property condition report or property inspection, as applicable, the Servicer must electronically submit a recommendation on the General Servicing Request to Freddie Mac regarding the need for adjustments to the Replacement Reserve amount.

examples: Servicer receives updated PCR on 2026-05-01; recommendation must be submitted by 2026-05-16 (15 days).
Servicer completes internal inspection on 2026-08-10 (where inspection is the applicable trigger); recommendation must be submitted by 2026-08-25.
Recommendation submitted 25 days after PCR receipt → noncompliant; subject to servicing QC finding.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_010_006 establishes the requirement to conduct a re-evaluation on the 10th anniversary (when term >10 years and adjustment is permitted). BR_010_007 mandates a time-bound submission “after… receives the updated property condition report or conducts the required property inspection,” which is part of executing the BR_010_006 re-evaluation workflow.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_010_006 is not performed/triggered, BR_010_007 submissions may never occur, causing missed Freddie Mac visibility into reserve adequacy and resulting in servicing noncompliance.

business_rules.rule_name: Default letter due after 30-day cure and within 10 business days
business_rules.description: If the Borrower does not fully remediate identified adverse matters or provide an action plan acceptable to Lender within 30 days of the Servicer’s notification, the Servicer must issue a notice of default letter as soon as possible and no later than 10 Business Days after the expiration of the initial 30-day cure period. The Servicer must forward a copy of any reservation of rights letter and notice of default to Freddie Mac via email within 10 Business Days of letter issuance, including an evaluation of the noncompliance, potential risks, and proposed remedial actions.
business_rules.conditions: When adverse matters are noticed and Borrower fails to cure or provide acceptable plan within 30 days
business_rules.consequences: If met: timely default escalation and Freddie Mac notice; if violated: servicing breach and oversight failure
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 40 – Assessments and Other Post-purchase Reporting, item 7 (10/21/25)
source_reference.source_text: 7. If the Borrower does not fully remediate identified adverse matters or provide an action plan acceptable to Lender within 30 days from the Servicer’s notifications, the Servicer must issue a notice of default letter as soon as possible, and no later than 1 0 Business Days after the expiration of such initial 30 day cure period. The Servicer must use the Notice of Default and Reservation of Rights (Conventional) or the Notice of Default and Reservation of Rights (SBL) form. The Servicer must forward a copy of any reservation of rights letter and notice of default to Freddie Mac via email at MF_Surveillance@freddiemac.com within 10 Business Days of letter issuance.

examples: Servicer notifies Borrower of unauthorized property manager change on 2026-01-01; Borrower does not cure by 2026-01-31 → Servicer must issue default letter by the 10th business day after 2026-01-31.
Servicer issues default letter on 2026-02-20; Servicer must email Freddie Mac a copy by the 10th business day after 2026-02-20 with risk evaluation and remedy proposal.
Borrower provides an acceptable action plan on day 25 after notice → default letter timing requirement is not triggered by item 7.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicer corrective action; potential escalation under servicing contract

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: Repair non-completion is a common ‘adverse matter.’ BR_013_010 requires reservation of rights and Freddie Mac notification after missed repair due date; BR_010_008 requires issuing a notice of default after borrower fails to remediate or provide an acceptable plan within 30 days, with subsequent Freddie Mac notification. In practice, BR_013_010 often precedes or accompanies the broader BR_010_008 escalation.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_013_010 is performed without aligning to BR_010_008 escalation thresholds, the file may lack timely default escalation. If BR_010_008 is triggered without the BR_013_010 reservation-of-rights steps, legal posture and Freddie reporting may be deficient.

business_rules.rule_name: Fixed-Rate Late Charges 30 Days Then 50% Remit
business_rules.description: For fixed-rate Mortgages (other than TAH bond credit enhancement Mortgages) originated on or after July 8, 1992: the Servicer may retain all late charges collected within 30 days after the Due Date of the related installment; the Servicer must remit to Freddie Mac Multifamily Loan Accounting 50% of any late charges collected more than 30 days after the Due Date. Freddie Mac reserves the right to waive any late charge at its discretion.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to fixed-rate mortgages originated on/after 1992-07-08 (non-TAH)
business_rules.consequences: If followed, remittance is correct; if not, Freddie Mac may adjust remittance
business_rules.exceptions: Freddie Mac may waive any late charge in its discretion

source_reference.source_text: For Mortgages originated on or after July 8, 1992, the Servicer must remit collected late charges and default interest to Freddie Mac as described below: • For fixed-rate Mortgages, other than TAH bond credit enhancement Mortgages, the Servicer may retain all late charges collected by the Servicer within 30 days after the Due Date of the related installment. The Servicer must remit to Freddie Mac Multifamily Loan Accounting 50 percent of any late charges collected by the Servicer more than 30 days after the Due Date of the related installment. Freddie Mac reserves the right to waive any late charge, in its discretion.

examples: Late fee collected on day 20 after due date for a fixed-rate non-TAH loan: Servicer may retain 100% of the late fee.
Late fee collected on day 45 after due date for a fixed-rate non-TAH loan: Servicer must remit 50% of that late fee to Freddie Mac.
Freddie Mac waives a late charge in writing; Servicer does not collect/remit that waived amount.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_011_004 addresses how late charges are split/remitted based on when collected. BR_011_011 sets non-negotiable prohibitions on how unpaid late charges/default interest can be collected. Therefore, any operational workflow implementing BR_011_004 must first ensure collections comply with BR_011_011 or remittance numbers and audit trails will be invalid.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_011_011 is violated, late charge transactions may need to be reversed and re-applied, and remittances recalculated, causing accounting breaks and servicing remedies.

business_rules.rule_name: ARM Late Charges Require 50% Remittance Always
business_rules.description: For ARMs originated on or after July 8, 1992, the Servicer must remit to Freddie Mac Multifamily Loan Accounting 50% of all late charges collected by the Servicer. Freddie Mac reserves the right to waive any late charge at its discretion.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to ARM mortgages originated on/after 1992-07-08
business_rules.consequences: If followed, remittance is compliant; if not, Freddie Mac may adjust remittance
business_rules.exceptions: Freddie Mac may waive any late charge in its discretion

source_reference.source_text: • For ARMs, the Servicer must remit to Freddie Mac Multifamily Loan Accounting 50 percent of all late charges collected by the Servicer. Freddie Mac reserves the right to waive any late charge, in its discretion.

examples: ARM late fee collected on day 5 after due date: Servicer must remit 50% of the late fee to Freddie Mac.
ARM late fee collected on day 40 after due date: Servicer must still remit 50% (timing does not change the split).
Freddie Mac waives the late charge; Servicer does not collect or remit the waived amount.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_011_005 requires remitting 50% of all late charges collected for certain ARMs. BR_011_011 restricts collection methods, which affects what can be considered ‘properly collected’ and prevents impermissible netting/charging practices that would distort remittance amounts and compliance status.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_011_011 is violated, remittance reporting under BR_011_005 may be based on impermissible collections and require corrective transaction reapplication and remittance adjustments.

business_rules.rule_name: All Default Interest Collected Must Be Remitted
business_rules.description: For Mortgages originated on or after July 8, 1992, the Servicer must remit all collected default interest to Freddie Mac Multifamily Loan Accounting.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when default interest is collected on covered mortgages
business_rules.consequences: If remitted, accounting is compliant; if not, Freddie Mac may assess shortages
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: • The Servicer must remit all collected default interest to Freddie Mac Multifamily Loan Accounting.

examples: Borrower pays $12,500 of default interest as part of a cure; Servicer must remit the full $12,500 to Freddie Mac.
Default interest is collected in two partial payments; Servicer must remit each collected amount (100%) per remittance cycle.
Servicer mistakenly retains default interest as servicing compensation; this violates the requirement and triggers remittance correction.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_011_006 governs remittance of default interest; BR_011_011 prohibits collecting default interest via reserves, payment deductions, delinquency cure payment deductions, or capitalization to principal. Therefore, correct remittance depends on compliant collection methods and correct transactional application.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Violations trigger reversals/reapplications and could create REMIC/accounting issues if default interest is improperly capitalized or netted from borrower payments.

business_rules.rule_name: No Late Charge If Payment Received Within 10 Days
business_rules.description: For Mortgages (other than TAH bond credit enhancement Mortgages) originated prior to July 8, 1992, the Servicer may not impose any late charge for any monthly installment received within 10 days after the payment is due. If the grace period ends on a weekend or holiday, the grace period is extended to the next Business Day.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to pre-1992-07-08 mortgages when imposing late charges
business_rules.consequences: If complied, late charges are enforceable; if violated, late fee must not be charged/collected
business_rules.exceptions: Grace period extends to next Business Day if ending on weekend/holiday

source_reference.source_text: • May not impose any late charge for any monthly installment received within 10 days after the payment is due (If the grace period ends on a weekend or holiday, it is extended to the next Business Day.)

examples: Pre-1992 loan payment is received 8 days after due date; Servicer must not impose a late charge.
Due date is Friday; the 10th day falls on a Sunday, so grace extends to Monday—payment received Monday is within the grace period and no late charge is allowed.
Payment received 12 days after due date (no weekend extension applies); Servicer may impose a late charge if allowed by Note and other limits.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: Late Charge Maximum 5% of Monthly P&I Installment
business_rules.description: For Mortgages (other than TAH bond credit enhancement Mortgages) originated prior to July 8, 1992, the Servicer may not impose a late charge in an amount more than 5% of the monthly principal and interest installment that is late (or any lesser amount specified in the Note).
business_rules.conditions: Applies to pre-1992-07-08 mortgages when calculating late charges
business_rules.consequences: If within cap, late charge may be imposed; if above cap, late fee must be reduced/voided
business_rules.exceptions: Lower cap applies if Note specifies a lesser amount

source_reference.source_text: • May not impose a late charge in an amount more than five percent of the monthly principal and interest installment that is late (or any lesser amount specified in the Note)

examples: Monthly P&I is $18,000; maximum late charge is 5% = $900 unless the Note specifies less.
Note limits late charge to 3%; even though Guide permits up to 5%, Servicer must apply 3%.
Servicer charges $1,200 on a $20,000 monthly P&I (6%); Servicer must reverse or reduce to $1,000 max.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_011_007 limits when a late charge may be imposed (not within 10 days). BR_011_008 limits how much may be imposed (≤5% or lesser note amount). Together they define whether a late charge is permissible and lawful under the Guide constraints for those loans.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If either is missed, servicer may impose or attempt to collect impermissible late charges, requiring reversals/refunds and potentially triggering borrower complaints and audit findings.

business_rules.rule_name: No Collection by Reserves Deductions or Principal Add-On
business_rules.description: For all Mortgages, regardless of origination date or any contrary Loan Document provision, the Servicer may not collect due and unpaid late charges or default interest by (i) charging the Borrower's Reserves, (ii) deducting from a regular payment of the monthly installment, (iii) deducting from a payment made to partially or fully cure a Delinquency, or (iv) adding to the outstanding principal balance of the Mortgage.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when attempting to collect unpaid late charges or default interest
business_rules.consequences: If prohibited methods used, transaction must be reversed/corrected and collections re-applied
business_rules.exceptions: None stated for these prohibited methods

source_reference.source_text: For all Mortgages, regardless of the date of origination or any provision to the contrary in the Loan Documents, the Servicer may not use any of the following methods to collect due and unpaid late charges or default interest: • Charging the Borrower's Reserves • Deducting from a regular payment of the monthly installment • Deducting from a payment made to partially or fully cure a Delinquency • Adding to the outstanding principal balance of the Mortgage

examples: Servicer uses the Tax & Insurance Reserve to pay an unpaid late charge; this is prohibited and must be corrected.
Borrower makes a delinquency cure payment; Servicer withholds $500 from the cure to satisfy old late fees—prohibited deduction method.
Servicer capitalizes default interest by adding it to principal balance; prohibited and must be reversed.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing corrective action / reapplication of funds

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: 10-year reevaluation and 15-day reserve adjustment recommendation
business_rules.description: For a Mortgage with a loan term longer than 10 years where the Loan Documents allow adjustment of the Replacement Reserve amount following an updated property condition report, the Servicer must conduct a re-evaluation of Replacement Reserve adequacy on the 10th anniversary of the Origination Date. Within 15 days after receiving the updated property condition report or completing the required inspection, the Servicer must electronically submit a recommendation to Freddie Mac via General Servicing Request regarding any needed reserve adjustments.
business_rules.conditions: Loan term >10 years and Loan Documents allow reserve adjustment based on updated PCR
business_rules.consequences: If timely completed, Freddie Mac receives reserve adjustment recommendation; if missed, Servicer is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: If Loan Documents lack PCR-at-borrower-expense provision, inspection applies instead

source_reference.source_text:
1. For a Mortgage with a loan term longer than 10 years, the Replacement Reserve
requirements in the Loan Documents may provide that the lender may adjust the
Replacement Reserve amount following receipt of an updated property condition report.
If the Loan D ocuments provide for such an adjustment, then on the 10th anniversary of
the Origination Date of the Mortgage, the Servicer must conduct a re -evaluation of the
adequacy of the Replacement Reserve.

3. Within 15 days following receipt of the updated property condition report or property
inspection, as applicable, the Servicer must electronically submit a recommendation on
the General Servicing Request to Freddie Mac regarding the need for adjustments to
the Replacement Reserve amount.

examples: A 12-year loan originated 2016-07-15 with reserve-adjustment language: Servicer must re-evaluate on 2026-07-15 and submit GSR recommendation within 15 days of receiving the updated PCR/inspection results.
Loan documents allow PCR cost charged to borrower: Servicer orders PCR and, upon receipt, submits GSR recommendation within 15 days.
Loan documents do not allow PCR at borrower expense: Servicer conducts required inspection and uses that output to submit the recommendation within 15 days.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing noncompliance finding; Freddie Mac consent required as applicable

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: Both rules describe the same triggering condition (term >10 years and loan docs allow adjustment after updated PCR) and require the re-evaluation on the 10th anniversary. This can cause conflicting implementation if a system treats them as separate obligations (duplicate tasks/alerts).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If not reconciled, automation may generate duplicate tasks, duplicate noncompliance flags, or inconsistent deadlines depending on which rule is used as the authoritative control.

dependencies.rationale: BR_010_007 already mandates the 15-day recommendation submission after receipt of PCR/inspection. BR_012_006 repeats the same requirement, so two ‘separate’ rules can conflict in control mapping (same deadline measured twice).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Audit/compliance reporting may double-count exceptions or show inconsistent pass/fail outcomes if one rule is updated and the other is not.

business_rules.rule_name: Delegated reserve modification only if risk rating 6 and UPB $30 million
business_rules.description: A Servicer may approve Replacement Reserve requirement modifications without submitting a consent request to Freddie Mac only when a Servicer Delegation Requirement is satisfied and all specified conditions are met, including: Risk Rating of six or less (and if securitized, not on watchlist), UPB less than $30 million, not in default, PCR/PRR completed within six months of the modification request date and required review date, overall property condition 'Good' or better, and no Critical or PR-90 (including Life Safety) repairs identified. The Servicer’s recommendation must align with PCR/PRR findings and escrow analysis.
business_rules.conditions: Servicer seeks to approve Replacement Reserve modification under delegated authority
business_rules.consequences: If all conditions met, Servicer may approve without Freddie Mac consent; otherwise must request consent
business_rules.exceptions: No delegated authority if securitization has non-Freddie directing party or Retained Authority

source_reference.source_text:
Subject to the foregoing paragraph, i f a Servicer Delegation Requirement has been
satisfied, the Servicer is delegated authority to approve modifications to the
Replacement Reserve requirements without submitting a consent request to
Freddie Mac, provided all of the following conditions are met:
• The Mortgage has a Risk Rating of six or less (and if securitized, is not on the
watchlist), has a UPB of less than $30 million, and is not in default.
• The Property Condition Report (PCR) (or SBL Physical Risk Report (PRR), for
SBL Mortgages)  must have been completed within six (6) months of the
modification request date and within six (6) months of the required review date.
• The PCR (or PRR, as applicable)  must identify the overall Property condition as
“Good” or better.
• No Critical or PR -90 (including Life Safety) repairs may be identified in the PCR
(or PRR, as applicable) .

examples: UPB $25,000,000, risk rating 5, not in default, PCR completed 90 days ago with condition 'Good' and no PR-90 repairs: Servicer may approve reserve modification under delegated authority.
UPB $29,000,000 but PCR completed 8 months ago: delegated authority conditions not met; Servicer must request Freddie Mac consent before approving.
Risk rating 6 but property condition is 'Fair': conditions not met; Servicer must submit formal consent request prior to modification.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Freddie Mac consent required; noncompliance finding if exceeded authority

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: Notify Freddie Mac within 2 days after delegated reserve approval
business_rules.description: When a Servicer takes an action under delegated approval for Replacement Reserve modifications, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac within two days after the effective date of the approval by creating a record in CRT indicating Freddie Mac approval is not required due to the satisfied Servicer Delegation Requirement, and must electronically submit via DMS: the General Servicing Request and supporting documentation, a copy of the approval letter sent to the Borrower, and copies of any modified loan documents.
business_rules.conditions: Servicer approves Replacement Reserve modification using delegated authority
business_rules.consequences: If timely notified and documents submitted, delegated action is properly reported; if not, Servicer is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text:
For all Mortgages, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of any actions taken under
this delegated approval within two days after the effective date of the approval by
creating a record in CRT with the selection that Freddie Mac approval is not
required due  to the specified Servicer Delegation Requirement being satisfied and
electronically submitting to Freddie Mac via DMS the following:
• The General Servicing Request form and all supporting documentation.
• A copy of the approval letter that the Servicer sent to the Borrower
• Copies of any modified loan documents.

examples: Delegated approval effective June 1: Servicer must create CRT record and submit required DMS documents by June 3 (within 2 days).
Servicer approves reserve modification but submits the approval letter only (missing modified documents): notification package is incomplete and must be remediated.
Servicer notifies Freddie Mac on day 5 after effective date: late notification and subject to servicing compliance finding.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicing noncompliance finding; potential revocation of delegated authority

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_012_007 defines when the Servicer may approve Replacement Reserve modifications without Freddie consent (delegated authority conditions such as risk rating, UPB, not in default, PCR recency). BR_012_008 then imposes mandatory notification and documentation steps specifically for those delegated approvals. Therefore, the delegated-authority determination is a gating prerequisite to BR_012_008.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_012_007 is misapplied, the servicer may (a) incorrectly use delegated authority and then incorrectly notify under BR_012_008 (masking the need for Freddie consent), or (b) fail to notify Freddie Mac within 2 days for a valid delegated action, triggering servicing noncompliance.

business_rules.rule_name: Repair Agreement Extension Cap 12 Months After Origination
business_rules.description: Delegated authority limits for extending Repair Agreement completion dates: (1) For non-SBL Mortgages, the Servicer may extend by up to the same amount of time initially granted in the Repair Agreement, but not beyond a date that is 12 months after the Mortgage Origination Date (e.g., original completion 90 days after origination allows ≤90-day extension). (2) For SBL Mortgages, the Servicer may extend up to a date that is 12 months after the Origination Date, provided delegated authority conditions are met.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Servicer uses delegated authority to extend Repair Agreement completion date
business_rules.consequences: If met, extension within authority; if violated, Freddie Mac approval is required/extension invalid
business_rules.exceptions: None (outside these limits, Freddie Mac approval is required)

source_reference.source_text: c. For a non -SBL Mortgage, provided that all of the requirements in Section 39.4(b)(2)(a) and (b) are met, the Servicer has delegated authority to extend the completion date of a Repair Agreement by up to the same amount of time initially granted in the Repair Agreement, but not more than a date that is 12 months after the Origination Date of such Mortgage. (For example, if the original completion date was 90 days following the Origination Date of the Mortgage, the Servicer may extend the completion date by no more than 90 days.) For an SBL Mortgage, provided that all of the requirements in Section 39.4(b)(2)(c) are met, the Servicer has delegated authority to extend the completion date of a Repair Agreement up to a date that is 12 months after the Origination Date

examples: Non-SBL: Origination Jan 1; original completion Apr 1 (90 days). Extension cannot exceed 90 days and cannot go past Jan 1 next year.
SBL: Origination Feb 15; Servicer extends completion to Feb 1 next year (within 12 months) if delegation requirements are satisfied.
Non-SBL: Origination Jan 1; Servicer attempts 120-day extension from a 90-day original period—outside delegated authority.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delegated action reversal; Freddie Mac approval required

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: Overdue Repairs: 30-Day Letter, 10-Day Copy, Reserves by 2nd Installment
business_rules.description: When repairs are not completed as or when required: (1) Within 30 calendar days after the repair due date, the Servicer must send a “reservation of rights” letter using the applicable Freddie Mac form. (2) Within 10 Business Days of sending the letter, the Servicer must provide Freddie Mac a copy via email and upload to DMS. (3) If collection of Replacement Reserve deposits was deferred due to the repair item noncompliance, the Servicer must begin collection not later than the second payment installment date immediately following the repair completion due date.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after a Repair Agreement item is not completed by its required due date
business_rules.consequences: If met, timely default/rights preservation steps occur; if violated, servicing breach risk increases
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.source_text: For Repairs not completed as or when required, Freddie Mac requires the Servicer to take the following actions: • Within 30 calendar days following the repair due date, send a “reservation of rights” letter to the Borrower ... • Within 10 Business Days of sending the “reservation of rights” letter to the Borrower, the Servicer must provide Freddie Mac a copy of the letter via email at MF_Surveillance@freddiemac.com and upload a copy to the Document Management System (DMS). If collection of Reserve deposits for Replacement Reserves was deferred at the time of such Repair item noncompliance, the Servicer must begin collection of the Reserve deposits for Replacement Reserves. Collection of Reserve deposits for Replacement Reserves must commence not later than the second payment installment date immediately following the Repair item completion due date.

examples: Repair due date May 1: reservation of rights letter must be sent by May 31 (30 calendar days).
Letter sent May 20: copy must be emailed and uploaded by 10 Business Days after May 20.
Repair due date May 1 with deferred Replacement Reserves: collection must start no later than the 2nd payment installment date after May 1.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_013_009 constrains the servicer’s authority to extend repair completion dates. BR_013_010 is triggered “after a Repair Agreement item is not completed by its required due date.” To correctly determine when BR_013_010 must occur, the system must determine whether the due date was validly extended under BR_013_009 (or whether Freddie approval was required).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If extension authority is misapplied, BR_013_010 notices may be sent too early (borrower harm) or too late (missed reservation of rights and servicing breach).

business_rules.rule_name: Gold PC remittances due by 15th and 3rd business day after 15th
business_rules.description: For Gold PC (45-Day PC) Securitized Mortgages, the Servicer must remit to Freddie Mac by the 15th of the month (or following Business Day if the 15th is not a Business Day) for collections received from the 16th of the previous month through the 13th of the current month. For collections received on the 14th or 15th of the current month, remittances are due by the third Business Day following the 15th.
business_rules.conditions: Mortgage is a Gold PC (45-Day PC) securitized Mortgage; collections received by Servicer
business_rules.consequences: If remitted on schedule, funds available timely; if late, remittance noncompliance may trigger interest on late remittances/other remedies
business_rules.exceptions: 15th falls on non-Business Day: due the following Business Day (for 16th-13th window)

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 53 Monthly remittances(b)(2) (06/12/23); Bulletin Update 10/19/23 (Pages 12-13)
source_reference.source_text: For Gold PC Securitized Mortgages, the Servicer must make principal and interest payments available to Freddie Mac on the following dates: • For all collections received by the Servicer from the 16th of the previous month through the 13th of the current month, the Servicer must make all remittances to Freddie Mac by the 15th of the month (or the following Business Day if the 15th is not a Business Day). • For all collections received by the Servicer on the 14th or the 15th of the current month, the Servicer must make all remittances to Freddie Mac by the third Business Day following the 15th of the month.
source_reference.confidence_note: Word positions are estimated from provided chunk excerpt.

examples: Collections received April 16 through May 13 must be remitted by May 15 (or next BD if May 15 is weekend).
Collections received on May 14 must be remitted by the third Business Day after May 15 (e.g., by May 20 if May 16 is BD1).
If Servicer remits May 16 for the April 16–May 13 window (when May 15 was a Business Day), it is late and may incur interest on late remittances.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Interest on late remittances and other remedies

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_006 and BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 both define remittance deadlines. BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 is explicitly scoped to Gold PC securitized mortgages and specifies a different collection window and due date logic. For automation, the system must treat Gold PC classification as a rule-selection switch to avoid applying an incompatible remittance timetable.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the wrong remittance rule is applied, remittances may be systematically late (or mis-timed), triggering interest on late remittances, remedies, and repeated reconciliation breaks between Servicer and Freddie Mac records.

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_007 states operational timing: submit remittance requests no later than 1:00 p.m. ET one Business Day before the due date to ensure Freddie Mac has use of funds by the due date. BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 sets specific due dates (15th/third Business Day after the 15th). In practice, the submission-cutoff rule is used to operationally meet the due dates.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Servicer plans to remit on the due date in BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 but fails to submit per the cutoff logic in BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_007 (where applicable), the remittance may post a day late, creating late-remittance exposure (e.g., interest on late remittances).

business_rules.rule_name: Delegated SBL Non-Residential Lease Requires 90% Rent and 20% Cap
business_rules.description: If a Servicer Delegation Requirement has been satisfied, the Servicer is delegated authority to approve a non-residential lease for an SBL Mortgage only if all stated conditions are satisfied, including: tenant is not an Affiliate of the Borrower/Guarantor; rents are not less than 90% of prior rents for that portion of the Property (if any); lease term including extension options is 10 years or less; and aggregate income from the leased space is less than 20% of the Property’s gross income as of the lease entry date.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Servicer approval of non-residential leases under SBL Mortgages
business_rules.consequences: If any condition fails, Servicer lacks delegated authority; Freddie Mac approval required
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; all listed conditions must be satisfied

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 43 (02/24/26) Non-residential leases for SBL Mortgages
source_reference.source_text: If a Servicer Delegation Requirement has been satisfied, the Servicer is delegated authority to approve a non-residential lease, provided all of the following conditions are satisfied: •The tenant under the non-residential lease is not an Affiliate of the Borrower or an affiliate of any Guarantor •The rents paid to the Borrower pursuant to the non-residential lease are not less than 90 percent of the rents paid to the Borrower pursuant to the non-residential lease, if any, for that portion of the Property that was in effect prior to the non-residential lease •The term of the non-residential lease, including any option to extend, is 10 years or less •The aggregate of the income derived from the space leased pursuant to the Nonresidential Lease accounts for less than 20 percent of the gross income of the Property on the date that the Borrower enters into the Nonresidential Lease

examples: New retail lease would produce 18% of gross property income and has 8-year term with 2-year option (10 total): meets the 20% and 10-year thresholds (subject to all other conditions).
Proposed lease is 12 years including options: fails the 10-year maximum; Servicer cannot approve under delegation.
Non-residential space income would be 22% of gross income: fails the <20% cap; requires Freddie Mac approval.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicer noncompliance finding; potential reversal/required Freddie Mac approval

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_017_004 governs when the Servicer may approve a non-residential lease under delegated authority. BR_007_003 states Freddie Mac will not 'approve other servicing-related transactions' if it determines a Borrower/Principal/Guarantor is a confirmed match to sanctions/exclusion lists. Therefore, a sanctions screen outcome must be cleared before relying on delegated approval pathways for servicing actions.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 fails (confirmed match) but BR_017_004 is applied anyway, the Servicer may proceed with an action that Freddie Mac will not permit, causing immediate escalation, potential rejection, and heightened regulatory/reputational risk.

business_rules.rule_name: Delegated Non-Residential Lease Notice to Freddie Within 30 Days
business_rules.description: For any non-residential lease that does not require Freddie Mac approval, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of actions taken under delegated approval within 30 days of the effective date of the approval by (1) creating a CRT record indicating the satisfied Servicer Delegation Requirement and (2) electronically delivering via DMS a completed General Servicing Request with supporting documentation.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Servicer uses delegated authority to approve a non-residential lease
business_rules.consequences: If notice/actions not completed timely, Servicer is noncompliant with delegated reporting
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 43 (02/24/26) Non-residential lease notice requirements
source_reference.source_text: For any non-residential lease that does not require Freddie Mac approval, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of any actions taken under the delegated approval within 30 days of the effective date of the approval by taking the following actions: 1.Creating a record in CRT indicating the specific Servicer Delegation Requirement that has been satisfied. 2.Electronically delivering to Freddie Mac via DMS a completed General Servicing Request, along with any supporting documentation.

examples: Lease effective date 08/01: CRT record and DMS General Servicing Request must be completed by 08/31.
Servicer submits DMS package but fails to create CRT record: violates the two-step notification requirement.
Servicer notifies on day 45 after effective date: untimely notice and a compliance finding.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicer corrective action; delegated authority restrictions

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: The 30-day notice/CRT+DMS delivery obligation is triggered by a delegated approval of a non-residential lease. Delegated approval is only permitted when the delegation requirement is satisfied and all lease conditions are met; otherwise Freddie Mac approval is required and the delegated reporting workflow is not the correct path.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SERVICES_ELIGIBILITY_017_004 is not satisfied, the Servicer must not proceed as if it has delegated authority. Any attempt to follow BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_017_005 as a delegated approval would mask an unauthorized action, create audit findings, and require escalation/re-submission for Freddie Mac approval.

business_rules.rule_name: Radon Remediation Notice in 2 Days plus 30/90-Day Deadlines
business_rules.description: When radon follow-up testing is warranted (per environmental consultant determination for a non-SBL Mortgage), the Servicer must follow the prescribed protocol. If remediation is necessary: upload follow-up test results to PRS by the Completion Date for Follow-Up Radon Testing; provide the Borrower a Radon Remediation Notice within two Business Days; Borrower has 30 days after the notice date to provide a signed fixed-price remediation contract and 90 days after notice to complete remediation; after completion, Servicer must upload consultant certification including that post-mitigation radon levels are below 4 pCi/L.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Properties securing non-SBL Mortgages with warranted radon follow-up testing
business_rules.consequences: Failure to follow protocol/documentation timing is servicing noncompliance and may breach loan docs
business_rules.exceptions: SBL Properties are not required to be tested for radon (per Section 43.26)

source_reference.source_text: If the environmental consultant determines based on the follow-up test results that remediation is necessary Remediation will be required along with the following steps: Step 1. Servicer uploads test results to PRS, naming the file as Radon Test Results Follow-Up, by the Completion Date for Follow-Up Radon Testing. Step 2. Servicer must provide Borrower with a Radon Remediation Notice (as defined in the Loan Documents) within two Business Days. Note: Borrower will have 30 days after the date of the Radon Remediation Notice to provide Servicer with a signed, binding fixed price radon remediation contract with a qualified service provider. Borrower will have 90 days to complete remediation after the date of the Radon Remediation Notice. Step 4... units with completed radon remediation now demonstrate radon levels below 4 pCi/L.

examples: Consultant requires remediation; Servicer issues Radon Remediation Notice on 09/10: Borrower must provide fixed-price contract by 10/10 and complete remediation by 12/09.
Servicer issues notice 5 business days after receiving results: violates the within-two-Business-Days requirement.
Post-mitigation test shows 3.8 pCi/L: Servicer uploads consultant certification and post-mitigation results as required; remediation is documented as complete.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Servicer corrective action; potential default remedy enforcement under Loan Documents

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: Confirmed prohibited transfer must be reported to Freddie Mac within 2 business days
business_rules.description: After confirming that a prohibited Transfer of Ownership has occurred, the Servicer must electronically submit required information to Freddie Mac within two Business Days. The submission must include: (1) a letter detailing the prohibited transfer including the Freddie Mac loan number, Borrower name, property address, names/addresses of known connected parties, and discovery details; (2) a written summary of conversations between Servicer personnel and the Borrower/other involved parties; (3) any evidence of the prohibited transfer; and (4) any other information Freddie Mac requests.
business_rules.conditions: Servicer confirms a prohibited/unauthorized Transfer of Ownership has occurred
business_rules.consequences: If met, Freddie Mac receives timely incident package; if violated, Servicer misses required deadline
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in excerpt

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 41.8(a) Information to be provided to Freddie Mac (06/29/18)
source_reference.source_text: a. Information to be p rovided to Freddie Mac (06/29/18 ) The Servicer must electronically submit the following information to Freddie Mac within two Business Days after confirming that the prohibited Transfer of Ownership has occurred: 1. Information detailing the prohibited Transfer of Ownership (The letter must include the Freddie Mac loan number, the name of the Borrower, the property address, the names and addresses of all known parties connected with the prohibited Transfer of Ownershi p and details of the discovery of the Transfer of Ownership.) 2. A written summary of any conversations between Servicer personnel and the Borrower or any party involved with the Transfer of Ownership 3. Any evidence of the prohibited Transfer of Ownership 4. Any other information requested by Freddie Mac

examples: Prohibited transfer confirmed on Tuesday: Servicer must submit the full electronic package by Thursday (2 business days).
Servicer submits only the letter but omits conversation summary and evidence: incomplete submission and noncompliant with required items.
Freddie Mac requests additional documents beyond items (1)-(3): Servicer must provide those as item (4) 'any other information'.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_020_010 starts "After confirming that a prohibited Transfer of Ownership has occurred" and requires a rapid information package submission. In operational practice, confirmation often occurs while processing or reconciling transfer submissions and required documentation. BR_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_027_010 is part of that transfer submission workflow and is a likely upstream stage where issues are identified (e.g., buyer identity, conditions, or unapproved changes).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Servicer does not integrate prohibited-transfer detection into the transfer documentation workflow, confirmation may be delayed, causing the Servicer to miss the two Business Day reporting window and increasing enforcement/audit exposure.

business_rules.rule_name: Delegated Easement Approval Requires 5 Conditions
business_rules.description: If a Servicer Delegation Requirement has been satisfied, the Servicer is delegated authority to approve the types of easements described in Section 43.10(a)(3) only if all listed conditions are satisfied: (i) if the Mortgage is in a securitization where Freddie Mac is the named master servicer, Freddie Mac must also be the Directing Certificateholder (or Directing Party); (ii) the Mortgage does not secure tax-exempt debt; (iii) the Servicer determines the easement will not materially affect the operation/value of the property or Freddie Mac’s interest; (iv) the Borrower is not requesting lien subordination to the easement (subordination requests must follow Section 43.10(e)); and (v) there are no credit enhancement investors other than a Seller/Servicer, the Borrower, or a Borrower affiliate.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Servicer seeks to approve easement under delegated authority in 43.10(a)
business_rules.consequences: If all conditions met, Servicer may approve; if any fail, Freddie Mac approval path applies
business_rules.exceptions: Section 43.10 does not apply to Infrastructure Agreement Servicing Requests (43.32)

source_reference.section_id: 43.10(a)(1) Easements the Servicer is permitted to approve (02/24/26)
source_reference.source_text: 1. If a Servicer Delegation Requirement  has been satisfied , the Servicer is delegated authority to approve the types of easements described in Section 43.10(a)(3) , provided  all the following conditions are satisfied: • If the Mortgage is in a Securitization where Freddie Mac is the named  master servicer , Freddie Mac must  also be the Directing Certificateholder (or Directing Party). • The Mortgage does not secure tax -exempt debt. • The Servicer determines, in its discretion, that the easement will not materially affect the operation or value of the Property or Freddie Mac’s interest in the Property. • If the Borrower is not requesting subordinat ion of  the lien of its Mortgage to the easement.  For any such Borrower request, see Section 43.10(e). • There are no investors who have provided credit enhancements for the Mortgage other than a Seller/Servicer, the Borrower or an Affiliate of the Borrower.

examples: Mortgage is in a Freddie Mac securitization and Freddie Mac is also Directing Certificateholder; no tax-exempt debt; no subordination request; Servicer documents no material impact and approves under delegation.
Borrower requests Mortgage lien subordination to an easement; Servicer cannot approve under 43.10(a) and must follow 43.10(e) procedures.
Mortgage has a third-party credit enhancement investor (not Seller/Servicer/Borrower/Affiliate); Servicer cannot use delegated authority.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Eligibility is explicitly conditioned on enumerated requirements in 43.10(a)(1).

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_022_001 is about delegated authority to approve easements. Even where delegation exists, BR_007_003 provides an overriding prohibition on approving servicing-related transactions involving sanctioned/excluded parties. Thus, sanctions clearance is a prerequisite compliance gate before executing delegated easement authority workflows.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 fails but BR_022_001 is used, the Servicer may approve an easement in a context Freddie Mac would not permit, creating a high-risk compliance and enforcement issue.

business_rules.rule_name: Third-Party Easement Limits: UPB 30M, Fee 0.5%/$100K
business_rules.description: For third-party easements (other than local residential utility distribution), a Servicer may approve only if additional constraints are satisfied, including: Mortgage UPB must be $30,000,000 or less as of the Borrower’s easement application date; Mortgage Risk Rating must be 6 or less; the Mortgage must be current with no evidence of default; easement compensation must be less than the lesser of 0.5% of UPB or $100,000; and a supervisor or higher-level manager on Servicer staff must approve the easement using the Easement Request.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Servicer approval of non-utility third-party easements under 43.10(a)(3)(C)
business_rules.consequences: If any threshold fails, Servicer may not approve under delegation; escalate for Freddie Mac approval
business_rules.exceptions: None stated within 43.10(a)(3)(C) conditions

source_reference.source_text: C. Easements for the benefit of a third party (other than for local residential distribution of gas, water, electricity or other public utility) if all the following conditions are satisfied: i. The Servicer has determined that there is no material adverse impact on the Property’s value, operation, access, marketability, or on the income production of units or other income -producing improvements. ii. The Property secures a Mortgage having a UPB of $30 million or less as of the date of the Borrower's easement application with the Servicer. iii. The Property secures a Mortgage with a Risk Rating of six or less. iv. The Mortgage is current, with no evidence of default. v. Compensation for the easement is less than the lesser of 0.5 percent of the UPB or $100,000. vi. The proposed easement is compatible with the use of the Property and would be generally acceptable to prudent lenders. vii. A supervisor or higher -level manager on the Servicer’s staff has approved the easement using the Easement Request .

examples: UPB $28,000,000 and compensation $90,000 (<$100,000 and <0.5% of UPB=$140,000); risk rating 5; current; supervisor approves—Servicer may approve.
UPB $31,000,000 (> $30,000,000 threshold); even if risk rating is 4 and loan is current, Servicer cannot approve under this delegated category.
UPB $20,000,000 and compensation $150,000 (> lesser of 0.5%=$100,000 or $100,000); compensation fails, so Servicer cannot approve under delegation.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Multiple explicit numeric constraints (UPB cap, risk rating cap, compensation cap) and approval control are directly stated.

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: Easement Notice and Documents Due Within 30 Days
business_rules.description: Within 30 days after the grant of an easement that does not require Freddie Mac approval, the Servicer must (i) notify Freddie Mac of any actions taken under delegated approval within 30 days of the effective date of approval by electronically delivering the Easement Request, and (ii) electronically deliver a copy of the Borrower request and the executed, recorded easement agreement. If recorder/clerk delays prevent timely delivery with required recorded information, the Servicer may provide a certified true-and-correct copy of the recorded original, and must deliver a copy showing the required information as soon as available.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after grant of an easement not requiring Freddie Mac approval under 43.10
business_rules.consequences: If timely delivery occurs, Freddie Mac receives required notice/docs; if not, Servicer is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: Recorder/clerk delay allows temporary certified copy; final recorded-info copy still required ASAP

source_reference.section_id: 43.10(b) Documentation for an easement not requiring Freddie Mac approval (04/18/24)
source_reference.source_text: b. Documentation for an easement not requiring Freddie Mac approval (0 4/18/24) Within 30 days after the grant of an easement not requiring Freddie Mac approval, the Servicer must: • Notify Freddie Mac of any actions taken under the delegated approval within 30 days of the effective date of the approval by electronically delivering the Easement Request. • Electronically deliver to Freddie Mac a copy of the Borrower request and the executed, recorded easement agreement. If a recorder or clerk delay makes it impossible to effect timely delivery of a copy showing the required information, the Servicer may pro vide a copy that the Title Company or closing attorney  has certified as a "true and correct copy of the recorded original." The Servicer must electronically deliver a copy showing the required information as soon as such information is available.

examples: Easement granted May 1; Servicer electronically delivers Easement Request and recorded easement agreement by May 31—compliant.

Recorder delay prevents stamped recorded copy by day 30; Servicer delivers a title-company-certified true-and-correct copy by day 30 and later delivers the fully recorded version when available—compliant.
Servicer delivers the Easement Request on day 45 with no recorder delay justification—noncompliant with the 30-day requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 30-day deadline and documentation package; includes conditional alternative for recorder delay.

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003 is a delegated reporting workflow. Whether an easement can be handled without Freddie Mac approval depends on meeting the delegated authority conditions, including securitization/master servicer and other eligibility checks.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If eligibility is not met, Servicer cannot treat the easement as delegated/no-Freddie-approval and must route to Freddie Mac approval. Using the delegated notification path would create missing-approval exposure and potential invalid execution of easement documents.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003 applies to delegated easements that do not require Freddie Mac approval. For certain third-party easements, the determination of whether it can be delegated hinges on thresholds (UPB, risk rating, current/no default evidence, compensation cap, supervisor approval).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If constraints fail, the Servicer must not proceed under delegated approval. Freddie Mac approval must be pursued, and any delegated notice/delivery package would be procedurally incorrect and could conceal a breach of delegated authority limits.

business_rules.rule_name: Delegated Easement File Must Include 4 Document Types
business_rules.description: For a delegated easement approval, the Servicer must maintain a file containing: (i) Borrower’s original request for approval and required information; (ii) evidence of the Servicer’s analysis and approval of the request; (iii) copies of the Servicer’s notification of the approval to the Borrower and Freddie Mac; and (iv) copies of the executed easement agreement and any other document signed on Freddie Mac’s behalf. The file must be maintained in accordance with Section 34.4 requirements.
business_rules.conditions: Applies whenever Servicer approves an easement under delegated authority (43.10)
business_rules.consequences: If file is complete and retained per 34.4, supports audit; if missing, Servicer is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: d. File retention requirements for an easement not requiring Freddie Mac approval (09/22/17) The Servicer must maintain a file that contains the following information for a delegated easement approval: • Borrower's original request for approval and required information • Evidence of the Servicer's analysis and approval of the request • Copies of the Servicer's notification of the approval to the Borrower and Freddie Mac • Copies of the executed easement agreement and any other document signed on Freddie Mac's behalf The Servicer must maintain the file in accordance with the requirements set forth in Section 34.4.

examples: Servicer retains borrower request, internal approval memo, and copies of emails/letters to borrower and Freddie Mac plus executed easement—compliant.
Servicer approves easement but fails to retain evidence of analysis/approval; file is incomplete—noncompliant on audit.
Servicer retains executed easement but not the document signed on Freddie Mac’s behalf; file is incomplete—noncompliant.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit file contents list and cross-reference to Section 34.4 retention standard.

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: The delegated easement file must contain evidence of notification to Freddie Mac and copies of the executed/recorded easement agreement. BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003 is the operational step that produces those deliverables and timestamps (within 30 days).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003 is not performed, the file will be missing mandatory artifacts (notice and delivered documents), causing audit failures, inability to evidence compliance with delegated authority, and potential repurchase/servicing remedies depending on Guide enforcement.

business_rules.rule_name: Securitized mortgages cannot add subordinate financing except supplemental product
business_rules.description: For any Mortgage purchased for Securitization, Freddie Mac does not permit Subordinate Financing except Subordinate Financing pursuant to the Freddie Mac Multifamily Supplemental Mortgage Product. If prohibited subordinate financing is requested or present, consent will be denied and Freddie Mac may reject the request and/or require remediation.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when borrower requests subordinate financing on a securitized mortgage
business_rules.consequences: If securitized: request prohibited unless supplemental product; otherwise request denied
business_rules.exceptions: Subordinate financing allowed only if it is a supplemental mortgage under the product

source_reference.source_text: For any Mortgage purchased for Securitization, Freddie Mac does not permit Subordinate Financing other than Subordinate Financing pursuant to the Freddie Mac Multifamily Supplemental Mortgage Product.

43.29 Subordinate Financing for non -SBL Mortgages (04/18/24) a. Conditions for Freddie Mac approval of Subordinate Financing for a non -SBL Mortgage (06/30/16) For any Mortgage purchased for Securitization, Freddie Mac does not permit Subordinate Financing other than Subordinate Financing pursuant to the Freddie Mac Multifamily Supplemental Mortgage Product.

examples: Securitized Mortgage + proposed new subordinate lien → not permitted unless it is a Freddie Mac Multifamily Supplemental Mortgage.
If the subordinate financing is not the Supplemental Mortgage Product, Freddie Mac will deny the request.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules prohibit Subordinate Financing on Mortgages purchased for Securitization except Subordinate Financing under the Freddie Mac Multifamily Supplemental Mortgage Product. The prohibition scope and exception are identical; consequences align (request prohibited/denied; Freddie will not permit subordinate financing).
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 selected as primary: slightly clearer operational framing (borrower request for subordinate financing on a securitized mortgage) while retaining identical prohibition.

business_rules.rule_name: Subordinate Financing Requires Combined LTV ≤ 85% and DCR ≥ 1.20x
business_rules.description: When Freddie Mac considers consenting to Subordinate Financing (generally for non-securitized Mortgages, and specifically for non-SBL Mortgages as stated), the combined debt (first mortgage plus subordinate financing and any other applicable debt included under the Guide’s combined-debt definition) must not result in an LTV Ratio exceeding 85%, and the combined debt service must not result in a DCR below 1.20x. These thresholds are necessary conditions for consent (and may be accompanied by other Guide conditions). If either threshold is not met (LTV > 85% or DCR < 1.20x), Freddie Mac may reject/deny the request.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Servicer requests for Freddie Mac approval of subordinate financing
business_rules.consequences: If thresholds not met, Freddie Mac may reject the subordinate financing request
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Freddie Mac may reject a request for Subordinate Financing at its discretion. Freddie Mac will consider consenting to Subordinate Financing only if the request meets the following conditions (However, the fact that the Subordinate Financing meets these requirements does not mean that Freddie Mac is obligated to consider or approve the Subordinate Financing.): 3. The combined debt may not result in a Loan -to-Value (LTV) Ratio that exceeds 85 percent and the combined debt service may not result in a Debt Coverage Ratio (DCR) that is below 1.20x.
3. The combined debt may not result in a Loan -to-Value (LTV) Ratio that exceeds 85 percent and the combined debt service may not result in a Debt Coverage Ratio (DCR) that is below 1.20x.

examples: Combined LTV = 86% → fails threshold; subordinate financing consent may be denied.
Combined DCR = 1.15x → fails threshold; subordinate financing consent may be denied.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules define the same key quantitative consent thresholds for Freddie Mac to consider consenting to Subordinate Financing: combined debt LTV must not exceed 85% and combined debt service DCR must not fall below 1.20x. 025_006 notes 'among other conditions' while 028_005 clarifies non-SBL scope and securitized mortgage exclusion; these differences do not alter the shared core constraint and can be preserved in the merged description as clarifying language.
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005 selected as primary: more explicit context boundaries (non-SBL Mortgage; expressly excludes securitized Mortgages) with the same LTV/DCR thresholds.

dependencies.rationale: The combined LTV/DCR thresholds apply only when Freddie Mac is considering consent for non-securitized loans. The securitization prohibition is the applicability gate that routes the request to immediate denial/alternate product.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the prohibition gate is missed, Servicer may submit packages for loans Freddie Mac cannot consider under the stated rule, causing process churn and compliance exceptions.

dependencies.rationale: BR_028_005 is a threshold rule governing Freddie Mac consent to subordinate financing. BR_007_003 is an overarching prohibition preventing Freddie Mac purchase/approval of certain transactions when parties are confirmed sanctions/exclusion matches. Thus, BR_007_003 is an upstream gate to determine if consent consideration under BR_028_005 is even permissible.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 fails but BR_028_005 proceeds, the compliance workflow may reach an apparent 'pass' on thresholds while the transaction remains unapprovable, creating control failures and operational waste.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005 provides necessary conditions for Freddie Mac to consider consenting to subordinate financing. BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 blocks Freddie Mac from approving servicing-related transactions when any borrower/principal/property manager is an SCP Named Party. Thus, SCP match is a decisive override.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 triggers, Freddie Mac will not approve consent regardless of satisfying LTV/DCR thresholds. Automation should short-circuit the consent workflow at SCP screening to prevent false-positive approvals and reduce processing time.

business_rules.rule_name: Subordinate Financing Fees: 50% Remit and $500 Counsel Fee
business_rules.description: When submitting a subordinate financing approval request package, the Servicer must handle fees as follows: (i) for the nonrefundable review/processing fee, the Servicer may retain 50% and must remit the remaining 50% to Freddie Mac when submitting the application; and (ii) the Servicer must charge the Borrower a nonrefundable counsel fee of $500 (which may exceed $500 for complex transactions). The Servicer may not charge the Borrower any additional fees for processing a request for Subordinate Financing.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Borrower requests Freddie Mac approval of subordinate financing
business_rules.consequences: If violated, fee submission may be rejected and noncompliance fees may be assessed
business_rules.exceptions: $500 counsel fee may exceed $500 for complex transactions

source_reference.source_text: 1. A nonrefundable review/processing fee in the amount specified in the Loan Documents, or, if no amount is specified, in the amount set forth in Exhibit 10 (The Servicer may retain 50 percent of the review/processing fee and must remit the remaining 50 percent to Freddie Mac when submitting the application for the Subordinate Financing. The Servicer must remit the fee by wire transfer ... The Servicer may not charge the Borrower any additional fees for processing a request for Subordinate Financing.) 2. The Servicer's charge to the Borrower of a nonrefundable counsel fee of $500 (The counsel fee is for the legal fees, expenses and costs of Freddie Mac's legal counsel, including its in -house counsel, and may exceed $500 for complex transactions.

examples: A $2,000 processing fee is collected: Servicer may retain $1,000 and must remit $1,000 to Freddie Mac with the application.
Servicer charges the Borrower $500 counsel fee and no other processing fee beyond the review/processing fee; compliant.
Servicer charges an extra $250 ‘administrative fee’ to the Borrower for the request; prohibited as an additional processing fee.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005 defines a key consent gate for Freddie Mac review. BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_028_006 then prescribes how the Servicer must package and remit fees during submission in that same review pathway.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Failure to meet the gate but proceeding to fee submission creates borrower harm and process waste; meeting the gate but violating fee handling risks package rejection and fee-related compliance findings.

business_rules.rule_name: Excess Payment Requires Borrower Instructions Before Application
business_rules.description: If the Servicer receives a payment in excess of the current amount due, the Servicer must obtain instruction from the Borrower regarding application of the excess amount before applying the payment. After the Servicer has applied the payment, Freddie Mac will not allow the Servicer automatically to reapply that payment.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when borrower payment exceeds the current amount due
business_rules.consequences: If not followed, payment application is noncompliant and may require correction
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Reapplication of payments (1 0/20/22 ) If a Servicer receives a payment in excess of the current amount due, the Servicer must obtain instruction from the Borrower as to the application of the excess amount prior to applying the payment . After a Servicer has applied the payment, Freddie Mac will not allow the Servicer automatically to reapply that payment.

examples: Borrower pays $60,000 when $50,000 is due; Servicer must ask whether the $10,000 should be applied as curtailment, reserve deposit, or held, before posting.
Servicer posts the extra $10,000 as a principal curtailment without borrower instruction; noncompliant with the requirement to obtain instructions first.
After posting a curtailment based on borrower instruction, Servicer cannot later ‘auto-reapply’ the same payment to a different category without a new borrower instruction.
Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: Repair extensions require notice 30 days prior and review 10 days prior
business_rules.description: If the Servicer determines repairs are partially complete, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac at least 30 days prior to the applicable completion date using the Borrower Certification – Partial Completion of Repairs form submitted to PRS, and submit an extension request if appropriate. If repairs will not be completed and an extension is not appropriate, the Servicer must provide Freddie Mac a detailed PRS update at least 30 days prior to the completion date. Notwithstanding the 30-day timing, at least 10 days prior to the applicable completion date, for any repairs that remain outstanding, the Servicer must evaluate whether an extension is appropriate and, if appropriate, submit an extension request and update PRS comments accordingly.
business_rules.conditions: When required repairs will miss the completion date or are partially complete
business_rules.consequences: If met: extension tracked in PRS/CRT; if violated: servicing noncompliance
business_rules.exceptions: None stated (10-day evaluation applies notwithstanding 30-day rule)

source_reference.source_text: At least 30 days prior to the applicable completion date, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of partial completion of repairs on the Borrower Certification – Partial Completion of Repairs form, which must be submitted to PRS. In addition, the Servicer (or its permitted delegee) must submit an extension request, if appropriate... If the Servicer determines that repairs will not be completed by the applicable completion date and that an extension is not appropriate, then at least 30 days prior to the completion date the Servicer must provide Freddie Mac with a detailed update in PRS... Notwithstanding Section 39.4(b)(1)(a) or (b) above, at least 10 days prior to the applicable completion date, for any repairs that remain outstanding, the Servicer must evaluate whether an extension is appropriate.

examples: Completion date is Aug 31; on Aug 1 Servicer submits Partial Completion form in PRS and requests an extension: compliant (≥30 days).
Completion date is Aug 31; on Aug 1 Servicer decides no extension and posts PRS update with repair status and borrower reasons: compliant.
On Aug 25 (6 days prior) repairs still outstanding; Servicer fails to reassess extension appropriateness: violates 10-day evaluation requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Multiple explicit timeframes (30 days prior; 10 days prior) and required forms/systems.

examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency

business_rules.rule_name: Upload good faith deposit receipt evidence to DMS within 2 business days after Rate Lock
business_rules.description: Within two Business Days following Rate Lock, the Seller must upload confirmation of receipt of the required good faith deposit to DMS. Evidence depends on deposit type: (1) Cash: copy of the wire confirmation to the Custodial Account. (2) Letter of credit: copies of the letter of credit, opinion of issuer’s counsel, and Letter of Credit Certification form. (3) Demand note: copy of the demand note.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to early rate lock good faith deposits upon Rate Lock for a Mortgage.
business_rules.consequences: If not uploaded timely/complete, Freddie Mac may treat as noncompliance and impose remedies.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: As security for all or a portion of the Seller’s obligations under the early rate lock application, the Seller must collect the required good faith deposit from Borrower and hold it in trust for Freddie Mac. Within two Business Days following Rate Lock, the Seller must upload confirmation of its receipt of the required good faith deposit to DMS as follows: Cash ... Copy of the wire confirmation to the Custodial Account Letter of credit ... Copies of the: Letter of credit ... Opinion of issuer’s counsel ... Letter of Credit Certification form Demand note ... Copy of the demand note

examples: Rate Lock on Monday: Seller uploads wire confirmation to DMS by Wednesday close of business (2 Business Days).
Letter of credit GFD: Seller uploads LOC + issuer counsel opinion + LOC certification within 2 Business Days; missing opinion triggers a documentation exception.
Demand note GFD: Seller uploads the executed demand note to DMS within the 2-Business-Day window.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Contract remedies; potential delay/denial of transaction processing
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing (two Business Days) and document-by-type requirements.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

dependencies.rationale: If Freddie Mac will not accept the early rate-lock application without the required property condition report, the transaction cannot proceed to Rate Lock in that pathway; therefore downstream Rate Lock-triggered obligations (deposit evidence upload) cannot be reached compliantly.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the property condition report is missing, early rate-lock acceptance (and resulting Rate Lock workflow) is blocked; the seller cannot meet the 2-business-day deposit upload requirement because Rate Lock should not occur/stand.

dependencies.rationale: The deposit evidence workflow is downstream of Rate Lock. Since BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_004_001 governs required actions before early rate lock, systems should ensure it is completed before initiating post-lock deposit evidence timers.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Seller locks without completing pre-lock inspection requirements, the file may be deficient even if deposit evidence is uploaded timely; rework may delay underwriting and risk lock fallout.

business_rules.rule_name: Pre-rate lock inspection requires interview walk and documentation
