business_rules.description: Prior to early-rate lock or Index Lock (if applicable), the Seller must (1) interview the property manager or other management company staff, (2) walk the property, and (3) inspect an appropriate sample of units based on Seller discretion, property condition, and identified issues/factors. The Seller must complete and document these inspection requirements as part of the mortgage transaction narrative analysis or on the Property Inspection and Lease Audit form, including the names of all parties participating and the company each represents.
business_rules.conditions: Applies before early-rate lock or Index Lock for the transaction
business_rules.consequences: If completed/documented, rate lock step supported; if not, file lacks required steps
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Prior to early-rate lock or Index Lock (if applicable per Section 3.15) the Seller must: • Interview the property manager or other management company staff, • Walk the Property, and • Inspect an appropriate sample of units based on the Seller’s discretion and expertise, the condition of the Property, and any identified issues or other factors Prior to early rate-lock or Index Lock (if applicable), the Seller must complete and document these inspection requirements as part of the mortgage transaction narrative analysis or on the Property Inspection and Lease Audit form.

examples: Early-rate lock requested on 2026-05-15: Seller interviews onsite manager, walks property, inspects a targeted set of units, and documents participants and companies in the narrative before rate lock.
Index Lock is applicable: Seller completes the three steps and records names/companies on the Property Inspection and Lease Audit form.
Seller walks the property but does not interview management staff: requirement not met because all three steps are mandatory ('must').
business_rules.extraction_notes: Strong 'must' language; no numeric thresholds in this preliminary step.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Unit inspections at underwriting: 80% or 10% with min/max
business_rules.description: At full underwriting, the Seller must conduct a complete property inspection. Unit inspection sampling must follow unit-count thresholds excluding Down Units and commercial units: (a) properties with 30 units or less: inspect 80% of units with no fewer than 5 and no more than 15; (b) properties with greater than 30 units: inspect 10% of units with no fewer than 10 and no more than 30. The sample must include representative vacant and occupied units with at least 50% occupied, across unit types and floor levels (top/bottom), and include any owner-operated guest suites, corporate lease units, or short-term stay operator units. If calculations produce a decimal, truncate to nearest whole number if minimum is still met. If 80%/10% yields insufficient inspected units to meet lease audit requirements, inspect additional units. Seller must inspect all Down Units and all commercial units.
business_rules.conditions: Applies at full underwriting when complete inspection and lease audit required
business_rules.consequences: If met, inspection sampling complies; if not, underwriting inspection is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: Decimal truncation permitted if minimum unit count is met

source_reference.section_id: 8.15(b) Complete property inspection and lease audit (04/21/26)
source_reference.source_text: At full underwriting, in addition to conducting the inspection requirements specified in Section 8.15(a) above, the Seller must conduct a complete property inspection including, but not limited to, the following: 1. The unit inspection must include the following: a. For Properties with 30 units or less: (i) 80 percent of units, with no fewer than 5 units and no more than 15 units. This excludes Down Units and commercial units and must include: • A representative sample of vacant units and occupied units, of which at least 50 percent must be occupied... (ii) All Down Units (iii) All commercial units b. For Properties with greater than 30 units: (i) 10 percent of units, with no fewer than 10 units and no more than 30 units... (ii) All Down Units (iii) All commercial units

examples: Property with 24 units (no Down Units, no commercial): 80% = 19.2 → truncate to 19 units, but apply max 15; inspect 15 units and ensure at least 8 are occupied (≥50%).
Property with 30 units plus 2 Down Units and 1 commercial unit: sample 80% of (30-2-1)=27 units → 21.6 → 21, but cap at 15; inspect 15 regular units plus all 2 Down Units and the 1 commercial unit.
Property with 120 units (no Down Units, 2 commercial): sample 10% of 118 = 11.8 → 11 units (≥10) plus all commercial units; if lease audit needs more, inspect additional units beyond 11.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Multiple explicit numeric thresholds (80%, 10%, min/max, 50% occupied) and explicit inclusion of Down/Commercial units.

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_004_002 sets the unit inspection count thresholds; BR_UNDERWRITES_PROHIBITION_004_004 prevents borrower/property manager influence in which specific units are inspected. Together they form a complete control set for inspection validity.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If unit-selection independence fails, the inspection may be considered compromised even if the correct number of units was inspected, leading to re-inspection, delays, and heightened risk of misrepresentation.

business_rules.rule_name: Tenant notices and unit list due before inspection date
business_rules.description: Prior to the inspection date, the Seller must instruct the Borrower to provide tenant notification and must handle unit selection for inspection access as follows: (a) properties with 30 units or less: the Borrower must notify all tenants; (b) properties with 31 units or more: the Seller must select twice the required number of units for inspection and the Borrower must notify tenants of those selected units. In all cases, a list of units selected for inspection must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date of inspection. Failure to follow these steps may cause inspection access issues and delay underwriting.
business_rules.conditions: Applies before scheduled property inspection for complete inspection/lease audit
business_rules.consequences: If completed, inspection access prep compliant; if not, inspection readiness deficient
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 2. Prior to the inspection date, the Seller must instruct the Borrower to provide appropriate notification to the tenants and select units for inspection as follows: a. Properties with 30 units or less: The Borrower must provide notification to all tenants. b. Properties with 31 units or more: The Seller must select twice the required number of units for inspection, and the Borrower must provide notification to the tenants of those selected units. c. In all cases, a list of the units selected for inspection must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date of inspection.

Prior to the inspection date, the Seller must instruct the Borrower to provide appropriate notification to the tenants and select units for inspection as follows: a. Properties with 30 units or less: The Borrower must provide notification to all tenants. b. Properties with 31 units or more: The Seller must select twice the required number of units for inspection... c. In all cases, a list of the units selected for inspection must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date of inspection... If Freddie Mac is not present, the Seller will select units to be inspected... Neither the Borrower nor the property manager may select or recommend units to be inspected.

examples: A 24-unit property → Borrower must notify all tenants before inspection.
A 120-unit property requiring 12-unit inspection sample → Seller selects 24 units; Borrower notifies tenants in those 24 units.

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules describe the same pre-inspection tenant notification and unit selection procedure for underwriting inspections: for properties with 30 units or less, Borrower notifies all tenants; for 31+ units, Seller selects twice the required number of units for inspection and Borrower notifies those tenants; and a list of selected units must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to inspection. 007_007 is a shortened restatement referencing a section number but does not introduce a different requirement.
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_004_003 selected as primary: clearer pre-inspection procedural steps (tenant notification + unit selection mechanics + list delivery to Freddie Mac) with less truncation than the duplicate.

business_rules.rule_name: Borrower and manager cannot select units for inspection
business_rules.description: On the day of inspection, unit selection authority depends on Freddie Mac presence: if Freddie Mac is on-site and participating, Freddie Mac identifies an appropriate sample of units to inspect from the selected units; if Freddie Mac is not present, the Seller selects units to be inspected. Neither the Borrower nor the property manager may select or recommend units to be inspected.
business_rules.conditions: Applies on inspection day when selecting units to be inspected
business_rules.consequences: If followed, selection independence maintained; if violated, inspection selection is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: 8.15(b)(3) Unit selection on day of inspection
source_reference.source_text: 3. On the day of the inspection, if Freddie Mac is on-site and participating in the inspection, Freddie Mac will identify an appropriate sample of units to inspect from the selected units, ensuring that the required number of units are inspected... If Freddie Mac is not present, the Seller will select units to be inspected. Neither the Borrower nor the property manager may select or recommend units to be inspected.

examples: Freddie Mac attends the inspection: Freddie Mac chooses the inspected units from the preselected list; Borrower does not influence which units are inspected.
Freddie Mac is not present: Seller chooses inspected units; property manager suggests 'only renovated units'—Seller must reject the suggestion to comply.
Borrower provides a preferred unit list and insists those be inspected: violates the 'may not select or recommend' prohibition.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear prohibition and clear conditional selection authority.

dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the pre-inspection selection/notification/list is not performed, the on-day selection process cannot be compliant; inspections may be rescheduled, sampling validity questioned, and underwriting delayed.

business_rules.rule_name: Rent roll due 2 business days before inspection and ≤7 days old
business_rules.description: For SBL property inspections/lease audits with a scheduled site inspection, at least two Business Days prior to the scheduled site inspection the Seller must deliver to Freddie Mac a rent roll dated within seven Business Days. Failure to deliver a timely/current rent roll may delay Freddie Mac’s review/inspection and render underwriting incomplete until cured.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL loans requiring scheduled site inspection under 8SBL.15
business_rules.consequences: If not met, Freddie Mac review/inspection may be delayed and underwriting deemed incomplete
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

Any employee of the Seller responsible (individually or as part of a team) for originating the Mortgage • Any employee of the Seller directly benefitting financially or otherwise from the origination of the Mortgage At least two Business Days prior to the scheduled site inspection, the Seller must deliver a rent roll dated within seven Business Days to Freddie Mac.

business_rules.rule_name: SBL unit inspection minimum is 10% or 5 units with 50% occupied
business_rules.description: At full underwriting for an SBL Mortgage, the Seller must conduct a complete property inspection. The unit inspection must include a minimum number of residential units equal to the greater of (i) 10% of total residential units at the Property or (ii) five units, excluding Down Units and commercial/non-residential units. The inspected sample must include both vacant and occupied units, and at least 50% of the inspected units must be occupied. If the 10% calculation yields a decimal, the Seller may truncate down to a whole number, provided the five-unit minimum is still satisfied. If these requirements are not met, the inspection/lease audit is incomplete and underwriting cannot be finalized per the Guide.
business_rules.conditions: Applies at full underwriting when Seller performs SBL property inspection
business_rules.consequences: If not met, inspection is incomplete and underwriting cannot be finalized per Guide
business_rules.exceptions: May require more than minimum to satisfy lease audit linkage

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 8SBL – SBL Property Fundamentals; 8SBL15(b)(1) Unit inspection
source_reference.source_text: The greater of 10 percent of the total number of units at the Property or five units.   This excludes Down Units and commercial/non -residential units and must  include a representative sample of vacant units  and occupied units  (of which at least 50% must be occupied) , unit types and floor levels, including top floor and bottom floor units , and units subject to  corporate leases. o If the calculation results in a decimal, it can be truncated to the nearest whole number so long as the minimum five-unit requirement is met (e.g., 5.9 units becomes five units).

The unit inspection must include the following: • A minimum number of residential units as follows: • The greater of 10 percent of the total number of units at the Property or five units... must include a representative sample of vacant units and occupied units (of which at least 50% must be occupied)... If the calculation results in a decimal, it can be truncated to the nearest whole number so long as the minimum five-unit requirement is met (e.g., 5.9 units becomes five units).
source_reference.confidence_note: Word positions are estimated from provided chunk excerpt.

examples: Property has 42 units → 10% = 4.2, truncated to 4 but minimum is 5 → must inspect at least 5 units.
If 6 units are inspected, at least 3 must be occupied to meet the 50% occupied requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Multiple numeric thresholds are explicit: 10%, 5-unit minimum, 50% occupied, truncation example.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Freddie Mac underwriting suspense / re-inspection required

Same numeric thresholds and inspection context; same consequence (inspection/lease audit incomplete).
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_006_006 selected as primary: marginally more complete language (explicitly frames as 'Seller performs SBL property inspection') while keeping identical sampling rules.

business_rules.rule_name: Full underwriting unit inspections 80% or 10% with caps
business_rules.description: At full underwriting, the Seller must conduct a complete property inspection and unit sampling based on property size. For properties with 30 units or less: inspect 80% of units, with no fewer than 5 and no more than 15 (excluding Down Units and commercial units), plus inspect all Down Units and all commercial units. For properties with greater than 30 units: inspect 10% of units, with no fewer than 10 and no more than 30 (excluding Down Units and commercial units), plus inspect all Down Units and all commercial units. In both cases, at least 50% of inspected units must be occupied, and decimals may be truncated provided the minimum is met.
business_rules.conditions: Applies at full underwriting when Seller performs complete property inspection.
business_rules.consequences: Compliant sampling supports underwriting; noncompliance requires re-inspection or underwriting delay.
business_rules.exceptions: If lease audit needs more units, additional units must be inspected.

source_reference.section_id: 8.15(b)(1) Complete property inspection and lease audit (04/21/26)
source_reference.source_text: At full underwriting, in addition to conducting the inspection requirements specified in Section 8.15(a) above, the Seller must conduct a complete property inspection including, but not limited to , the following : 1. The unit inspection must include the following : a. For Properties with 30 units or less: (i) 80 percent of units, with no fewer than 5

examples: 24-unit property: 80% = 19.2 → 19 units (truncated) but capped at 15; Seller inspects 15 regular units + all Down Units + all commercial units; at least 8 of the 15 are occupied (≥50%).
120-unit property: 10% = 12 units; Seller inspects 12 regular units (between 10 and 30) + all Down Units + all commercial units; at least 6 of the 12 are occupied.
31-unit property where 10% = 3.1 → minimum 10 applies; Seller must inspect 10 regular units + all Down Units + all commercial units.

business_rules.rule_name: Easement underwriting package must include survey or tax map and photos
business_rules.description: For an SBL Mortgage where access is via an easement, the Seller must include in the underwriting package either (a) a copy of the existing survey, tax map, or satellite images detailing the easement location and (b) photographs from the public street showing the view of the Property from the public street, the easement location, and any applicable signage.
business_rules.conditions: When underwriting an SBL Mortgage on a Property with indirect access via easement
business_rules.consequences: If met: underwriting package is complete for easement evidence; if violated: delivery/underwriting suspense until cured
business_rules.exceptions: None

examples: Seller provides a current survey showing the easement and includes street-view photos showing signage → compliant package.

business_rules.rule_name: Commercial lease ≥5% of GPR requires tenant-signed estoppel
business_rules.description: For SBL underwriting, the Seller must submit a Commercial Lease Analysis and Estoppel – SBL in the full underwriting package for each commercial lease. If income from any one commercial lease represents 5% or more of the Property’s gross potential rent (GPR), then the Commercial Lease Analysis and Estoppel – SBL must be signed by the commercial tenant.

If income from any one commercial lease represents five percent or more of gross potential rent (GPR) for the Property, the Commercial Lease Analysis and Estoppel – SBL must be signed by the commercial tenant.

business_rules.rule_name: Non-Permitted units must be excluded from income, unit count, and occupancy tests while expenses included
business_rules.description: If the Seller cannot confirm required certificates of occupancy for one or more units (Non-Permitted Units), Freddie Mac may purchase the SBL Mortgage only if underwriting excludes the gross potential rent (GPR) from any Non-Permitted Unit and excludes such units from total unit count; underwriting must include all expenses attributable to all Non-Permitted Units; and Non-Permitted Units must be excluded from determining whether minimum occupancy requirements are satisfied.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when CO confirmation cannot be obtained for one or more units on an SBL Property.
business_rules.consequences: If applied, Freddie Mac may purchase subject to conditions; if not, purchase may be declined.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: The underwritten income from the Property must exclude the gross potential rent (GPR) for any Non -Permitted Unit and the total unit count must not include the Non -Permitted Unit. The underwritten expenses for the Property (including management fee and Replacement Reserves) must include all expenses attributable to all Non -Permitted Units. The Non -Permitted Units must be excluded from any determination of whether minimum occupancy requirements are satisfied.

examples: Two basement units lack CO: underwriting removes their GPR and reduces unit count by 2, but still includes their utilities/maintenance expenses.
Occupancy test is calculated using only permitted units; Non-Permitted Units are excluded from the denominator and numerator.
Seller keeps GPR from a Non-Permitted Unit in NOI: violates rule and may render loan ineligible for Freddie Mac purchase.

business_rules.rule_name: Leases must be validated to tenant payment docs and discrepancies explained
business_rules.description: Leases must be validated against documentation reflecting actual rental payments received by the respective tenant (e.g., tenant ledger, general ledger, copies of checks, or similar documentation). If discrepancies are identified, the applicable comments field must be used to provide an explanation (e.g., governmental subsidy such as Section 8/Medicaid, or ledgers reflecting additional payments like late fees).

If discrepancies are identified, the applicable comments field must be used to provide an explanation, such as when a portion of the rental payment is in the form of a governmental subsidy (e.g., Section 8 or Medicaid) or when the ledger reflects additional payments (e.g., late fees or other one -time charges).

examples: Lease shows $1,500 rent; tenant ledger shows $1,500 paid monthly—validation passes with no comments.
Lease shows $1,200 tenant portion but ledger shows $1,800; comments explain $600 Section 8 subsidy—discrepancy is documented.
Ledger shows $1,550 against a $1,500 lease; comments explain $50 late fee—variance is accounted for.

business_rules.rule_name: SBL inspection and lease audit must be within 90 days of package receipt
business_rules.description: The property inspection and lease audit requirements must be completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the full underwriting package. If not completed within 90 days, a new inspection and lease audit must be performed and no recertification is allowed.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL underwriting packages submitted to Freddie Mac
business_rules.consequences: Met: inspection/audit acceptable; Violated: new inspection/audit required; no recert
business_rules.exceptions: No recertification permitted when 90-day window is missed

source_reference.section_id: c. Timing of property inspections and lease audits (06/13/24)
source_reference.source_text: The inspection and lease audit requirements of Section 8SBL.15(b) must have been completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the full underwriting package. If the Seller inspection and lease audit are not completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the underwriting package, a new inspection and lease audit must be performed; no recertification will be allowed.

examples: Freddie receives the full underwriting package on 2026-01-01; inspection and lease audit must be completed by 2026-04-01 (90 days).
Inspection completed on day 95; Seller must order a new inspection and new lease audit—recertification is not allowed.
Lease audit completed timely but inspection is late (beyond 90 days); Seller must re-perform both as required by the rule.
business_rules.enforcement_action: New inspection and lease audit required; processing delay

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review

dependencies.rationale: Without the full underwriting package being received, the 'within 90 days of receipt' requirement cannot be evaluated or tracked. Delivery receipt is the start event for the timing control.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the package is not delivered (or delivery is delayed), the completion window start shifts, potentially compressing downstream inspection/audit scheduling or causing re-performance if the 90-day cap is exceeded after receipt.

business_rules.rule_name: Lease audit size is 50% (5–15) for properties with 30 units
business_rules.description: At full underwriting, the Seller must complete and submit a lease audit form meeting unit-count-based sampling minimums/maximums: (i) Properties with 30 units or less: audit 50% of total units, with no fewer than 5 and no more than 15 units; (ii) Properties with 31 units or more: audit 10% of total units, with no fewer than 10 and no more than 30 units. At least 50% of audited leases must be units inspected, and the audit must include representative samples such as leases signed within the last 60 days and leases from inspected corporate units. If the calculation yields a decimal, it may be truncated to the nearest whole number as long as the minimum 10-unit requirement is met.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller submits property inspection and lease audit at full underwriting
business_rules.consequences: Compliant sample: underwriting package complete; noncompliant: resample/redo lease audit
business_rules.exceptions: Decimal truncation allowed only if minimum 10-unit rule still met

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 8 Property Fundamentals, lease audit form requirements (04/21/26)
source_reference.source_text: e. Completed applicable lease audit form,  reflect ing the following:  • For Properties with 30 units or less:  o A lease audit of 50 percent of total units, with no fewer than 5 units and no more than 15 units   • For Properties with 31 units or more:  o A lease audit of 10 percent of total units, with no fewer than 10 units and no more than 30 units   • At least 50 percent of leases audited must be units inspected ; the remainder can be chosen randomly by the ins pector  after includ ing representative samples of:  o Leases from any inspected corporate units  o Leases signed within the last 60 days  If the calculation results in a decimal, it can be truncated to the nearest whole number so long as the minimum 10 -unit requirement is met

examples: Property has 24 units: 50% = 12 leases; audit 12 (within 5–15) and inspect at least 6 of those units.
Property has 8 units: 50% = 4, but minimum is 5; audit 5 leases and inspect at least 3 of the audited units.
Property has 109 units: 10% = 10.9; truncate to 10 (meets minimum 10) and audit 10 leases, inspecting at least 5 of them.
Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

dependencies.rationale: Lease audit sampling is partially constrained by the inspection population. Without completing inspection sampling per BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_004_002, the lease audit cannot satisfy the '50% audited leases must be units inspected' linkage.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If inspection sampling is incomplete/noncompliant, lease audit composition will also become noncompliant (or unprovable), triggering rework of both inspection and audit and delaying underwriting sign-off.

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_025_001 imposes sampling percentages and min/max unit counts. When computations produce decimals, BR_UNDERWRITES_CALCULATION_023_001 defines how to truncate while preserving a minimum requirement, preventing sampling math errors and rework.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If truncation rules are not followed, the Seller may under-sample or miscalculate the required number of units, leading to an invalid lease audit and re-performance.

business_rules.rule_name: Delegated inspections require at least 2 photos per unit inspected
business_rules.description: At full underwriting, the Seller must submit photographs representative of the Property. If Freddie Mac delegates the property inspection to the Seller, at least two photographs of each unit inspected must be provided as part of the property inspection and lease audit documentation.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Freddie Mac delegates the property inspection to the Seller
business_rules.consequences: If met: evidence supports inspection; if violated: package deficiency and cure required
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: At full underwriting , the Seller must submit the property inspection  and lease audit  documentation  set forth below :  a. Photographs representative of the Property .  If Freddie Mac delegates the property inspection to the Seller, at least two photograph s of each unit inspected  must be provided

examples: Seller inspects 12 units under delegated inspection and provides 24+ photos (2 per unit minimum): compliant.
Seller inspects 10 units but provides only 1 photo for 3 units: noncompliant; must supplement photos or re-inspect.
Seller provides two photos per unit but cannot tie photos to unit identifiers in file: treated as documentation deficiency until corrected.
Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Inspections must be completed within 90 days; recertification not allowed
business_rules.description: The property inspection requirements must be completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the applicable underwriting package (preliminary or full). If the Seller inspection is not completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the underwriting package, a new inspection must be performed and no recertification is allowed.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to inspections submitted with preliminary or full underwriting packages
business_rules.consequences: Within 90 days: inspection acceptable; beyond 90 days: new inspection required
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; explicitly prohibits recertification in lieu of new inspection

source_reference.source_text: The inspection requirements of Section 8.15(a) must have been completed within 90 days of  Freddie Mac’s receipt of the preliminary underwriting package.  • When submitted as part of the full underwriting package:  The inspection requirements of Sections 8.15(a) and 8.15(b) must have been completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the full underwriting package.  If the Seller inspection is not completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the underwriting package, a new inspection must be performed; no recertification will be allowed.

examples: Freddie Mac receives full underwriting package on May 1; inspection completed June 15 (45 days): compliant.
Package received May 1; inspection completed Aug 10 (101 days): noncompliant; new inspection required.
Seller attempts to provide a recertification letter instead of new inspection after 90 days: not allowed.
Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

dependencies.rationale: Receipt of the underwriting package is the timing anchor. The delivery process supplies the prerequisite start point and file context (preliminary vs full).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If package delivery is not properly completed/received, timing controls for inspection acceptability cannot be managed; if inspections occur outside the 90-day window post-receipt, a new inspection is mandatory with no recertification allowed.

business_rules.rule_name: Full underwriting inspects 10% homes with minimum 2
business_rules.description: At full underwriting for MHC Borrower-Owned Homes, the Seller must conduct a complete property inspection. The inspection must include (i) ten percent of Borrower-Owned Homes, excluding Down Home Sites, with a minimum of two, (ii) all Down and vacant Borrower-Owned Homes, and (iii) all non-revenue Borrower-Owned Homes.
business_rules.conditions: At full underwriting for MHC Home Sites and Borrower-Owned Homes
business_rules.consequences: If met: underwriting inspection complete; if violated: underwriting incomplete
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: At full underwriting, the Seller must conduct a complete property inspection including, but not limited to, the following: 1. Inspection of: o Ten percent of Borrower -Owned Homes, excluding Down Home Sites, with a minimum of two o All Down and vacant Borrower -Owned Homes o All non -revenue Borrower -Owned Homes

examples: Property has 12 Borrower-Owned Homes (excluding down): Seller inspects 10% (=1.2) but must inspect minimum 2 units.
Property has 40 Borrower-Owned Homes and 3 vacant units: Seller inspects 4 units (10%) plus all 3 vacant and any down/non-revenue units.
Seller inspects only occupied units and skips vacant homes: inspection does not meet full underwriting requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit sampling percentage and minimum count with required inclusions.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_030_008 defines the pool of homes and notification steps 'Prior to the inspection date' and requires providing the list to Freddie Mac. BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_030_007 then requires inspection of defined minimums and categories; that inspection depends on having selected/accessible units.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If selection/notification/list delivery is not completed, the inspection may be invalid or impossible to perform (no access), requiring rescheduling and delaying underwriting completion.

business_rules.rule_name: Pre-inspection selects 2x units and submits list to Freddie
business_rules.description: Prior to the inspection date, the Seller must select twice the required number of Borrower-Owned Homes for inspection and must instruct the Borrower to provide appropriate notification to tenants. A list of the Borrower-Owned Homes to be selected must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date of inspection.
business_rules.conditions: Before scheduled Seller property inspection of Borrower-Owned Homes
business_rules.consequences: If met: inspection selection process valid; if violated: inspection may be reworked
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Prior to the inspection date, the Seller must select twice the required number of Borrower -Owned Homes for inspection, and the Seller must instruct the Borrower to provide appropriate notification to the tenants of the selected Borrower -Owned Homes. A list of the Borrower -Owned Homes to be selected must be provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date of inspection.

examples: Required inspection sample is 4 homes: Seller pre-selects 8 homes and provides the 8-home list to Freddie before inspection.
Seller pre-selects only 4 homes (not 2x): does not comply; Freddie may require new selection and tenant notices.
Seller selects 2x but fails to send the list to Freddie before inspection date: process defect; inspection may be delayed.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery condition / require rescheduling and proper notification
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 'twice the required number' and timing 'prior to the date of inspection'.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Deliver Borrower Weather Certification if Inspection Pre-Event
business_rules.description: For specified loans in the quote stage (Conventional, Seniors, TAH, Structured & Facility) and for SBL under Seller Application, when a natural disaster or weather-related adverse condition occurs that could impact Property condition and the property condition consultant’s inspection occurred before the event, the Seller/Servicer must deliver the Borrower Certification of Weather-Related Event as part of the full underwriting package and it must specifically address any impact to the Property sustained from the event. Freddie Mac may require additional information or further inspections in its sole discretion.
business_rules.conditions: In-process loan; inspection occurred before the disaster/weather event
business_rules.consequences: If submitted, underwriting package is complete for disaster item; if missing, Freddie may require it and delay/decline review
business_rules.exceptions: If inspection occurs after the event, nothing further required (per Guide)

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 11; 11.9(b)(1) Types and Status of Mortgage Loans (12/12/24)
source_reference.source_text: If the property condition consultant’s inspection has taken place prior to the occurrence of the natural disaster or weather -related adverse condition:  o The Seller/Servicer must deliver the Borrower Certification of Weather -Related Event  as part of the full underwriting package and must specifically address any impact to the Property sustained from the natural disaster or weather -related adverse condition.  o Freddie Mac, in its sole discretion, may require additional information or further inspections of the Property.

examples: Inspection occurred on June 1; hurricane occurs June 10: Seller includes Borrower Certification addressing damage/no-damage in the underwriting package.
Inspection occurred after the wildfire: no Borrower Certification required under this specific bullet (inspection timing exception).
Certification states only 'no impact' without addressing known roof damage: Freddie may require further inspection/information.
Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Forward Commitment Inspection Must Be Completed Within 90 Days
business_rules.description: When a Forward Commitment full underwriting package is submitted, the Forward Commitment Property Inspection requirements must have been completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the underwriting package. If not completed within 90 days, a new inspection must be performed and recertification is not allowed.
business_rules.conditions: Forward Commitment full underwriting package submitted to Freddie Mac
business_rules.consequences: If within 90 days, inspection is acceptable; if not, new inspection required (no recert)
business_rules.exceptions: No recertification allowed

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 8, Timing of the Forward Commitment Property Inspection
source_reference.source_text: When submitted as part of the Forward Commitment full underwriting package, the Forward Commitment Property Inspection requirements must have been completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac’s receipt of the Forward Commitment underwriting package. If not, a new inspection must be performed; no recertification will be allowed.

examples: Freddie Mac receives the package on 2026-06-01; inspection must be dated on/after 2026-03-03 to be within 90 days.
Inspection is 120 days old at receipt; Seller must order and complete a new inspection, not a recertification letter.
Seller attempts to reuse an older inspection with a recertification; this is not permitted and triggers a new inspection requirement.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Underwriting package rejection until new inspection performed
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing requirement (90 days) plus explicit prohibition on recertification.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Property Condition Report Required Before Commitment or Early Rate Lock
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac requires submission of a property condition report (engineering report) meeting Section 62.3 requirements before Freddie Mac will issue a Letter of Commitment or accept an early rate-lock application to purchase a Mortgage.
business_rules.conditions: Prior to Freddie Mac issuing a Letter of Commitment or accepting early rate-lock
business_rules.consequences: If submitted, Freddie Mac may proceed; if not, commitment/rate-lock acceptance will not occur
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: Freddie Mac requires the Seller/Servicer to submit a property condition report (also commonly referred to as an engineering report) meeting the requirements of Section 62.3 before Freddie Mac will issue a Letter of Commitment or accept the early rate -lock application to purchase a Mortgage.

examples: Seller submits early rate-lock request without a Section 62.3-compliant engineering report; Freddie Mac will not accept the early rate-lock application.
Seller provides a complete property condition report that meets Section 62.3; Freddie Mac can issue a Letter of Commitment (subject to other conditions).
Engineering report is missing required elements under Section 62.3; Freddie Mac treats as not meeting requirement and will not proceed to commitment.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Freddie Mac will not issue Letter of Commitment
business_rules.extraction_notes: Strong 'requires' and 'before' language; no numeric thresholds.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Remediation Plan Required When Repairs Exceed 1% or $200,000 After 12/31/2024
business_rules.description: For loans taken under Seller Application after December 31, 2024, if Priority Repairs exceed the greater of 100 basis points (1.00%) of the loan amount or $200,000, or include a material repair likely to impact habitability, the Seller must submit a Borrower remediation plan detailing funding source, whether work will be in-house or third-party, permitting needs, and other pertinent details to remediate all repairs within the required time frame.
business_rules.conditions: Seller Application date is after 2024-12-31 and Priority Repair triggers are met
business_rules.consequences: If plan submitted, underwriting can proceed; if missing, Freddie Mac may condition/decline
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: If the Priority Repairs exceed the greater of 100 b asis points of the loan amount or $200,000 or have a material repair more likely to impact habitability , the Seller must submit a remediation plan from the Borrower indicating the source of funds for completing the work, whether work will be performed in-house or by a third party, if permitting is required and other pertinent details for remediating all repairs within the required time frame.

examples: Loan amount $15,000,000; 1% is $150,000, so the greater threshold is $200,000. If Priority Repairs total $250,000, Seller must submit the remediation plan.
Loan amount $40,000,000; 1% is $400,000, so the threshold is $400,000. If repairs total $350,000 with no habitability-impact repair, remediation plan is not required by this trigger.
Priority Repairs total $120,000 but include foundation structural repair impacting habitability; Seller must submit the remediation plan even though amounts are below $200,000 and 1%.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Underwriting suspense condition; possible purchase denial
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit numeric triggers (100 bps/1% and $200,000) plus date gating (after 12/31/2024).

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Commercial Lease Analysis Required When One Lease Is at Least 5% of GPR
business_rules.description: If income from any one commercial (non-residential) lease represents 5% or more of the Property’s gross potential rent, the Seller must submit a Commercial Lease Analysis and Estoppel – SBL, signed by the tenant, in the full underwriting package for each such lease.
business_rules.conditions: SBL underwriting where any one commercial lease income ≥ 5% of Property GPR
business_rules.consequences: If provided, Freddie Mac can underwrite lease risk; if missing, underwriting package is incomplete
business_rules.exceptions: Freddie Mac may require it in other circumstances in its discretion

source_reference.source_text: If income from any one commercial lease represents five percent or more of the gross potential rent of the Property, the Seller must submit a Commercial Lease Analysis and Estoppel – SBL, signed by the tenant, in the full underwriting package for each such lease.

examples: Property GPR is $1,000,000; a retail lease contributes $60,000 (6%). Seller must include a tenant-signed Commercial Lease Analysis and Estoppel – SBL.
Property GPR is $2,000,000; a laundry lease contributes $90,000 (4.5%). The 5% trigger is not met, though Freddie Mac could still require analysis in its discretion.
Two commercial leases contribute 3% each; neither individually reaches 5%, so the mandatory submission trigger is not met for either lease.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Underwriting package suspense until document provided
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 5% threshold and specific document name; discretionary add-on noted as exception context.

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

dependencies.rationale: Both rules address Commercial Lease Analysis/Estoppel requirements and the 5% of GPR signing trigger. BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_010_005 is broader (each commercial lease, with signing requirement when ≥5%), while BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_009 repeats the ≥5% signed requirement. In an automated graph, BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_009 can validate that the signing trigger was applied correctly under BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_010_005.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the signing trigger is missed, the full underwriting package may be incomplete and underwriting may be suspended until signed tenant estoppels are obtained (often time-consuming).

business_rules.rule_name: Flood Zone Determination Form Effective for Life of Loan
business_rules.description: For each mortgage loan origination, Seller/Servicers must determine whether any buildings at the property are or will be fully or partially located in a SFHA using the FEMA Standard Flood Hazard Determination Form (SFHDF). Seller/Servicers must ensure all structures are evaluated when ordering the SFHDF, provide supplemental legal/parcels to the vendor, ensure the resulting SFHDF (and subsequent versions) is effective for the life of the loan, and include a copy of the SFHDF plus a signed Notice to the Borrower of Special Flood Hazard and Federal Assistance in the Mortgage File.
business_rules.conditions: For each mortgage loan origination where SFHA determination is required
business_rules.consequences: Complete SFHDF + signed notice in file: compliant; missing/outdated: delivery deficiency
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: For each Mortgage loan origination, Seller/Servicers must determine whether any buildings located at the Property are or will be fully or partially located in a SFHA, using the FEMA Standard Flood Hazard Determination Form (SFHDF). Seller/Servicers must ensure that all structures at the Property will be evaluated when ordering the SFHDF. In addition to the Property address listed in the Collateral Description of the SFHDF, Seller/Servicers must provide to the vendor supplemental information such as the Property’s legal description or parcel descriptions. The resulting SFHDF and any subsequent versions, must be effective for the life of the related loan. In addition, the SFHDF should include a map image overlay. A copy of the SFHDF along with a signed copy of the Notice to the Borrower of Special Flood Hazard and Federal Assistance must be included in the Mortgage File.

examples: Seller orders SFHDF for parcel A only in a multi-parcel property: noncompliant (must ensure all structures evaluated).
Mortgage file includes SFHDF and signed borrower notice at closing: compliant documentation set.
Vendor receives only property street address without legal description; SFHDF later found incomplete: noncompliant process; must provide legal/parcels.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Purchase hold / delivery deficiency until corrected

examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows

business_rules.rule_name: Confirmation Sheet executed and uploaded next business day
business_rules.description: After Rate Lock, Freddie Mac will deliver a completed Mortgage Terms Confirmation Sheet. No later than the Business Day immediately following the Seller’s receipt of the Confirmation Sheet, the Seller must execute and upload the Confirmation Sheet to DMS to indicate acceptance of the Rate Lock terms. If the Seller fails to return the executed Confirmation Sheet within the required time, Freddie Mac may treat the failure as a Nondelivery.
business_rules.conditions: Applies immediately after Seller receives the Confirmation Sheet post Rate Lock
business_rules.consequences: If returned timely, Rate Lock terms accepted; if late, may be treated as Nondelivery
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: 27.16 Rate Lock and Mortgage Terms Confirmation Sheet (04/15/21)
source_reference.source_text: After Rate Lock, Freddie Mac will deliver to the Seller a completed Confirmation Sheet. No later than the Business Day immediately following the Seller’s receipt of the Confirmation Sheet, the Seller must execute and upload to DMS the Confirmation Sheet to indicate its acceptance of the Rate Lock terms. If the Seller fails to return the executed Confirmation Sheet to Freddie Mac within the required time, Freddie Mac may, in its discretion, treat such failure as a Nondelivery.

examples: Seller receives Confirmation Sheet on Tuesday: Seller must sign and upload to DMS by Wednesday (next Business Day).
Seller receives Confirmation Sheet on Friday: Seller must sign and upload by Monday (next Business Day) unless Monday is a holiday.
Seller uploads an unsigned Confirmation Sheet: does not satisfy 'must execute and upload' requirement; Freddie Mac may treat as Nondelivery.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing (next Business Day) and explicit consequence (may treat as Nondelivery).

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: The confirmation sheet acceptance process is downstream of Rate Lock. Because Freddie Mac will not accept early rate-lock without the engineering report, the lock/confirmation workflow should not proceed until BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002 is satisfied.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: Missing the property condition report can prevent or unwind Rate Lock; Seller cannot (or should not) execute the confirmation sheet because Rate Lock terms would not be validly issued/accepted.

dependencies.rationale: The Seller must complete and document management interview/property walk/unit inspections before proceeding to the lock stage. Only after lock issuance does the confirmation sheet acceptance step occur.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If pre-lock inspection steps are missed, the Seller may be out of compliance even if the confirmation sheet is executed timely; the deal may be conditioned, re-underwritten, or delayed for cure documentation.

business_rules.rule_name: Early rate lock package due 7–45 days; delivery due 45–105 days
business_rules.description: After Rate Lock, the Seller must deliver the full underwriting package by deadlines tied to the early rate lock type: 4-month (120 Day Funding): 45 days after Rate Lock; Mandatory Delivery Date is the earlier of 105 days after Rate Lock or 25 days after Freddie Mac acceptance of the early rate lock application. 3-month (90 Day Funding): package due 22 days after Rate Lock; Mandatory Delivery Date 75 days after Rate Lock. 2.5-month (75 Day Funding): package due 15 days after Rate Lock; Mandatory Delivery Date 60 days after Rate Lock. 2-month (60 Day Funding): package due 7 days after Rate Lock; Mandatory Delivery Date 45 days after Rate Lock.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to transactions using an early rate lock after Rate Lock occurs
business_rules.consequences: If timely, meets due dates; if late, risks missing Mandatory Delivery requirements
business_rules.exceptions: Unless otherwise specified in the Confirmation Sheet

source_reference.section_id: 27.18 Early rate lock delivery – delivery due dates and requirements (02/17/22)
source_reference.source_text: then after Rate Lock the Seller must deliver the full underwriting package in accordance with the following chart. The Mandatory Delivery Date will also be determined as set forth below unless otherwise specified in the Confirmation Sheet. Type of early rate lock Type of extension (in addition to 120 Day Funding) Date full underwriting package is due Mandatory Delivery Date is the earlier

examples: 2-month early rate lock: Rate Lock on 2026-01-10; full package due by 2026-01-17; Mandatory Delivery Date by 2026-02-24 (45 days after).

4-month early rate lock: Rate Lock on 2026-03-01; full package due by 2026-04-15 (45 days after); Mandatory Delivery Date is earlier of 2026-06-14 (105 days after) or 25 days after Freddie Mac acceptance.
3-month early rate lock: Seller submits full package 30 days after Rate Lock; violates 22-day requirement for package due date.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit chart-driven deadlines; consequence largely operational (delivery timing).

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: The full underwriting package delivery schedule is anchored to the Rate Lock event. The early rate-lock event chain is gated by the engineering report requirement.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002 is not met, early rate lock acceptance may not occur, which prevents or delays starting the delivery timeline and can jeopardize transaction timelines.

dependencies.rationale: If the Seller fails to execute and upload the confirmation sheet timely, Freddie may treat the failure as a Nondelivery. In that scenario, subsequent obligations to deliver the full underwriting package under the lock may be moot or require reinstatement/exception handling.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the confirmation sheet is not returned, the lock may be treated as Nondelivery; package delivery may be rejected, timelines reset, or the transaction may require escalation and potential re-lock.

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_021_005 establishes a timing mandate relative to the full underwriting package submission. Therefore, to deliver a compliant full underwriting package (BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006), the Seller/Servicer must have screened each Third-Party Vendor involved (for applicable applications).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If screening is not completed before package submission, the submission is timing-noncompliant, may require cure/resubmission, and can delay underwriting review and closing; if a vendor is on a restricted list, the transaction may require vendor replacement and rework.

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_037_010 requires the SFHDF determination and a signed Notice to Borrower (plus ongoing effectiveness). The full underwriting package delivery step is the operational point where these required origination documents must be present to avoid suspense/deficiency.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If SFHDF/notice is missing or outdated, Freddie Mac may suspend review, condition the file, or require updated determinations; closing timelines may slip and delivery defects may be assessed.

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_008 specifies additional required documentation based on repair magnitude/habitability triggers. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006 is the submission event for the full underwriting package, so package completeness depends on this conditional doc being present when triggered.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the remediation plan is omitted when required, Freddie Mac may condition or decline the underwriting review until the plan is provided and evaluated; this can delay delivery acceptance and jeopardize the lock timeline.

dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_031_010 explicitly requires delivery of the Borrower Certification of Weather-Related Event 'as part of the full underwriting package' when the triggering event occurred after the inspection.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If omitted when triggered, Freddie Mac may suspend underwriting, require updated condition verification, or delay/decline review pending the certification; can cascade into missed delivery/funding dates.

business_rules.rule_name: Final delivery package due by noon Eastern time on mandatory delivery date and notice email within 24 hours
business_rules.description: The Seller must deliver the Final Delivery Package no later than noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date stated in the applicable Purchase Contract. The Seller must send a delivery notification email to MF_Delivery_Notification@freddiemac.com with the Freddie Mac Loan Number, Property Name, Counsel name and tracking number within 24 hours of expected receipt. The original documents must be delivered in a one inch red-well folder.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to hardcopy/electronic final delivery submissions under a Purchase Contract
business_rules.consequences: If met, delivery is timely for Freddie Mac review; if missed, delivery may be deemed late and purchase/funding may be delayed or rejected
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 32 – Delivery and review period; preferred funding (10/01/25), item 1
source_reference.source_text: 1. The Seller must deliver the Final Delivery Package no later than noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date identified in the applicable Purchase Contract. A delivery notification email must be sent to MF_Delivery_Notification@freddiemac.com with the Freddie Mac Loan Number, Property Name, Counsel name and tracking number within 24 hours of expected receipt. The original documents must be delivered in a one inch red-well folder.

examples: Mandatory Delivery Date is 11/15; Seller ships so Freddie Mac receives by 12:00 PM ET on 11/15 and sends notification email the day before expected receipt.
Seller delivers at 1:30 PM ET on Mandatory Delivery Date; delivery is late and funding may be delayed pending Freddie Mac direction.
Seller fails to include tracking number in the notification email; Seller must resend a corrected email within the 24-hour window.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit time-of-day deadline, 24-hour timeframe, and packaging requirement.

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: A typical Freddie Mac workflow is: rate lock → deliver underwriting package → obtain approvals/close → deliver final delivery package by the Mandatory Delivery Date. While not every detail is explicitly linked, operational sequencing makes final delivery dependent on completing upstream package delivery and closing readiness.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the full underwriting package is late or incomplete, final approvals and closing may slip, increasing risk of missing the Mandatory Delivery Date and delaying/rejecting purchase/funding.

business_rules.rule_name: Preferred funding date must be at least 10 calendar days after Freddie Mac receives final delivery package and no later than mandatory funding date
business_rules.description: The Seller must request in writing its preferred funding date by inserting it on the Final Delivery Table of Contents. The preferred funding date cannot be earlier than 10 calendar days from the date Freddie Mac receives the Final Delivery Package and cannot be later than the Mandatory Funding Date. If the Seller fails to provide a preferred funding date, funding will occur once review is completed, but no later than the Mandatory Funding Date.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller requests a preferred funding date for a delivered Mortgage
business_rules.consequences: If compliant, Freddie Mac targets requested date; if noncompliant/omitted, Freddie Mac funds after review but by Mandatory Funding Date
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 32 – Delivery and review period; preferred funding (10/01/25), item 2
source_reference.source_text: 2. The Seller must request in writing its preferred funding date (no earlier than 10 calendar days from the date on which Freddie Mac receives the Final Delivery Package and no later than the Mandatory Funding Date) by insertion of the preferred funding date on the applicable blank on the Final Delivery Table of Contents. If the Seller fails to provide a preferred funding date, funding will occur once review is completed, but no later than the Mandatory Funding Date.

examples: Freddie Mac receives package on 03/01; earliest allowable preferred funding date is 03/11; Seller requests 03/15 and it is acceptable.
Seller requests preferred funding on 04/05 but Freddie Mac received package on 03/29 (only 7 days earlier); Seller must revise the request to 04/08 or later to meet 10 calendar days.
Seller leaves preferred funding date blank; Freddie Mac funds after completing review, but still by the Mandatory Funding Date.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 10-calendar-day minimum and Mandatory Funding Date cap; includes consequence if preferred date omitted.

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_005_002 is a sub-step of final delivery: you cannot specify a preferred funding date in the final delivery TOC without preparing/submitting the final delivery package governed by BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_005_001.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If final delivery package is not properly delivered, the preferred funding date request is ineffective; funding will be scheduled based on when review completes (and no later than the Mandatory Funding Date), potentially harming borrower/seller funding coordination.

business_rules.rule_name: Reimburse Freddie Mac within 10 business days of request
business_rules.description: For a delivery of a pledged Mortgage, the Seller must reimburse Freddie Mac for reasonable expenses Freddie Mac incurs in connection with delivery of the pledged Mortgage by any warehouse lender. Freddie Mac will specify the nature and amount in a written notice, and the Seller must reimburse the amount within 10 Business Days of Freddie Mac’s request.
business_rules.conditions: Warehouse lender delivers a pledged Mortgage and Freddie Mac issues written expense notice
business_rules.consequences: If paid timely, delivery expense item is cleared; if not, Seller is noncompliant and may face remedies
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: for any reasonable expenses incurred by Freddie Mac in connection with the delivery of a pledged Mortgage by any warehouse lender to Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac will specify the nature and amount of such expenses in a written notice to the Seller. The Seller must reimburse Freddie Mac for the amount of

examples: Freddie Mac sends a written notice for $1,250 in courier and handling expenses; Seller pays within 10 Business Days: compliant.
Seller receives written notice on April 1; payment arrives April 20 (beyond 10 Business Days): noncompliant and subject to Freddie Mac remedies under the Guide.
Multiple expense notices are issued for separate pledged deliveries; each notice starts its own 10 Business Day reimbursement clock.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: While not a strict legal prerequisite, the reimbursement scenario is downstream of a pledged mortgage delivery that settles successfully. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_032_010 is a key delivery/settlement control ('settlements cannot be made if... documentation is not on file'), making it a practical upstream step in the same pledged mortgage delivery lifecycle in which reimbursement requests can later occur.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If authorization documentation and Form 987M are not on file, settlement cannot occur and the pledged mortgage delivery process is interrupted; as a result, the downstream reimbursement event may not occur, but the Seller faces delivery failure/operational disruption and potential rescheduling costs.

business_rules.rule_name: Submit $100 million opinions 3 business days before origination
business_rules.description: For non-SBL Mortgages, the Seller/Servicer must submit specified opinions to Freddie Mac for review and approval not less than three business days prior to the scheduled origination date when (i) the Mortgage has an original principal balance ≥ $100,000,000 (all opinion letters) and/or (ii) the opinion is a Seniors Housing Mortgage licensure opinion (#27 and #28 from the Borrower Opinion form). Opinions must be marked to clearly indicate additions and deletions from the form.
business_rules.conditions: Non-SBL; ≥$100M loan and/or Seniors Housing licensure opinion; before scheduled origination
business_rules.consequences: If timely submitted, Freddie Mac can review; if late/absent, origination/delivery may be delayed or conditioned
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text:
The Seller /Servicer  must submit a copy of the following opinions for Freddie Mac’s review and
approval not less than three  business days prior to the scheduled origination date of the
Mortgage:

• All Opinion Letters for any Mortgage with an original principal balance equal to or greater
than $100,000,000.

• Any Seniors Housing Mortgage licensure opinion, specifically opinions #27 and #28 from
the Borrower Opinion form.

Such opinions must be marked to clearly indicate the additions to and deletions from the
appropriate form of Opinion Letter.

examples: A $150,000,000 non-SBL Mortgage: Seller/Servicer must submit all Opinion Letters at least 3 business days before origination.
A $60,000,000 Seniors Housing Mortgage requiring licensure opinions #27 and #28: those opinions must be submitted ≥3 business days prior to origination even though UPB is below $100,000,000.
Seller submits the opinions 1 business day before origination: noncompliant timing; Freddie Mac review may delay origination or impose conditions.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: Acceptance Letter must be acknowledged and uploaded to DMS or it is a Nondelivery with Breakage Fee
business_rules.description: When Freddie Mac issues an Acceptance Letter (including revised Confirmation Sheet) for an Increased Mortgage Amount, the Seller must acknowledge and upload the Acceptance Letter and its agreement to service the Mortgage to DMS in accordance with the Acceptance Letter terms. Failure to sign and upload to DMS constitutes a Nondelivery, and Freddie Mac may collect the Breakage Fee calculated based on the rate locked Mortgage amount.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Freddie Mac issues Acceptance Letter for Increased Mortgage Amount under early rate lock.
business_rules.consequences: If met, rate locked amount becomes Increased Mortgage Amount; if violated, Nondelivery and Breakage Fee may apply.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: The Seller must acknowledge and upload the Acceptance Letter, including the revised Confirmation Sheet and its agreement to service the Mortgage, to DMS in accordance with the terms specified in the Acceptance Letter. Failure to sign and upload to DMS the Acceptance Letter, including the revised Confirmation Sheet and its agreement to service the Mortgage will constitute a Nondelivery and Freddie Mac, at its option, will collect the Breakage Fee from the Seller. The Breakage Fee will be calculated based on the rate locked Mortgage amount.

examples: Seller uploads signed Acceptance Letter to DMS the same day received: delivery requirement satisfied.
Seller signs but does not upload to DMS: constitutes Nondelivery; Freddie Mac may assess Breakage Fee.
Seller uploads Acceptance Letter but omits agreement to service: treated as failure to upload complete package, risking Nondelivery.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: Non-Independent Property Purchase Request Must Include 4 Written Items
business_rules.description: When requesting Freddie Mac purchase of a mortgage secured by a property that is not an Independent Property, the Seller must submit specified information in writing as part of the applicable underwriting package: (1) description of Essential and Recreational Facilities (including on other properties if cross-collateralized/cross-defaulted/coterminous), (2) legal counsel confirmation of Shared Access/Use Agreement compliance or a detailed gap analysis and recommendation, (3) Seller analysis of feasibility/practicality of creating/constructing required facilities on the property if not all are located on the property (even if Shared Use Agreement exists), and (4) Seller analysis of feasibility/practicality of creating direct access to a publicly dedicated and maintained right of way if primary access is not direct (even if Shared Use Agreement exists). The Seller’s analysis must include financial considerations, zoning issues, and availability of land.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when property collateral is not an Independent Property and purchase is requested
business_rules.consequences: If complete, package is eligible for review; if missing, delivery is deficient
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; analyses required even when Shared Use Agreement exists

source_reference.source_text: If the Seller requests that Freddie Mac purchase a Mortgage secured by a Property that is not an Independent Property, the Seller must submit the following information in writing to Freddie Mac as part of the applicable underwriting package: • Description of the Essential Facilities and Recreational Facilities located on the Property... • Either (i) confirmation from the Seller/Servicer’s legal counsel... or (ii) a detailed analysis... • Seller’s analysis of the feasibility and practicality... (required even if the Property has access... through a Shared Use Agreement) • Seller’s analysis of the practicality and feasibility... (required even if the Property has access... through a Shared Use Agreement) The Seller’s analysis must include financial considerations as well as zoning issues and the availability of land.

examples: Property relies on shared amenities on adjacent parcel; Seller submits facilities description, counsel memo on shared use compliance, and feasibility analyses for on-site facilities and direct public access.
Seller provides only a facilities description but omits zoning/land availability analysis; Freddie Mac delivery package is incomplete and must be cured before review.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit must-submit list; no numeric thresholds in the list itself.

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_019_001 governs what must be submitted in writing in the underwriting package when requesting Freddie Mac purchase for a property that is not an Independent Property. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 is an eligibility rule: a prohibited Exclusionary List role match makes the Mortgage ineligible for sale/approval. In a practical compliance workflow, eligibility failures should stop downstream packaging and submission tasks, including specialized underwriting package components for non-Independent Properties.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 fails, the Seller may be required to halt the purchase request, replace disallowed parties, and re-underwrite/re-document. Even a complete BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_019_001 package will not cure the ineligibility.

dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_019_001 is triggered when requesting purchase with a non-Independent Property and requires submission of specific underwriting information. BR_BORROWER_PROHIBITION_007_003 creates a hard stop for purchase; therefore sanctions clearance should precede (or gate) assembling/submitting this package for Freddie review.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If sanctions screening fails, the Seller expends time and legal resources (shared access/use agreement analysis, counsel confirmations) for a loan that cannot be purchased, causing avoidable cost and timeline slippage.

business_rules.rule_name: TEL Rate Lock Requires Rent Schedule Dated Within 30 Days
business_rules.description: Prior to locking the rate for a TEL Mortgage, the Seller must deliver a rent schedule meeting Guide requirements that is dated no more than 30 days prior to the date of Rate Lock. This item must be in a form acceptable to Freddie Mac as part of the conditions precedent to Rate Lock.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to TEL Mortgages prior to Rate Lock; rent schedule must be Guide-compliant
business_rules.consequences: If dated within 30 days, rate lock condition satisfied; if older, rate lock blocked
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 25 – Conditions Precedent to Rate Lock (08/17/23)
source_reference.source_text: Prior to locking the rate, Seller must deliver each of the following items in form acceptable to Freddie Mac: • Final payment and performance bonds meeting the requirements of Section 19.2(e)(14) • A proforma rehabilitation escrow fund draw schedule • A rent schedule meeting the requirements of this Guide dated no more than 30 days prior to the date of Rate Lock • Form 1133, Certification of Borrower Insurance Compliance and evidence of property insurance for the Property meeting the requirements of this Guide

examples: Rate lock date is 2026-03-15; rent schedule dated 2026-03-01 is acceptable (14 days old).
Rate lock date is 2026-03-15; rent schedule dated 2026-02-01 is not acceptable (42 days old) and must be updated.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 30-day recency limit stated for rent schedule prior to rate lock.

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: SBL final delivery due by noon ET on Mandatory Delivery Date with email notice
business_rules.description: For SBL Mortgages under the standard delivery option, the Seller/Servicer must deliver all documents listed in the Final Delivery Table of Contents – SBL at or before noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date. Freddie Mac may treat as a late delivery if the Seller/Servicer fails to deliver the Final Delivery Package, including sending an email to mf_purchase_boarding_mgrs@freddiemac.com notifying Multifamily Purchase of the delivery of the Electronic Delivery Package, at or before noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date.
business_rules.conditions: SBL Mortgage standard delivery where a Mandatory Delivery Date applies
business_rules.consequences: If met, delivery is timely; if violated, Freddie Mac may treat as late delivery
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in excerpt

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 18SBL.7 and 18SBL.9(a) (12/16/25 text shown; 09/01/16 and 06/30/16 sections)
source_reference.source_text: 18SBL.7 Standard delivery —final delivery (09/01/16) At or before noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date, the Seller/Servicer must deliver to Freddie Mac all of the documents listed in the Final Delivery Table of Contents – SBL. ... 18SBL.9 a. Late delivery (06/30/16) ... The Seller/Servicer fails to deliver the Final Delivery Package to Freddie Mac, including sending an email to mf_purchase_boarding_mgrs@freddiemac.com notifying Multifamily Purchase of the delivery of the Electronic Delivery Package, at or before noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date.

examples: Mandatory Delivery Date is Aug 15: Seller emails purchase boarding and completes e-delivery at 11:30 a.m. ET—timely.
Seller uploads documents by 11:55 a.m. ET but forgets the required email notice until 12:30 p.m. ET—may be treated as late.
