Seller delivers at 12:05 p.m. ET on Mandatory Delivery Date—fails noon ET deadline and may be treated as late.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Terminate the Purchase Contract; charge late delivery extension fee

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 states that if a party on the Exclusionary List played a specified origination/transaction role, the Mortgage 'is not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.' BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_020_006 is the SBL standard delivery requirement for delivering the final delivery package by the Mandatory Delivery Date. Delivering a final package for an ineligible loan is a high-probability waste and commonly disallowed in automated compliance workflows (eligibility checks should gate delivery). Therefore, the Exclusionary List screening should be satisfied before (or at least as a gating prerequisite to) final delivery actions.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 fails (a prohibited party/role match exists), the loan cannot be sold/approved for transfer; the delivery package process becomes moot, and the file may be stopped, cancelled, or restructured with new parties, causing major delays and potential costs.

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_VALIDATION_016_005 states: 'For an SBL Mortgage to be eligible for purchase by Freddie Mac, the Property must conform...' and the Seller must confirm CO issuance/validity or that absence is non-violative and not enforcement-risk. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_020_006 is the SBL final delivery requirement. Because purchase eligibility is a gating criterion, the Seller/Servicer should not proceed to final delivery packaging (or Freddie Mac purchase review/boarding) without satisfying this eligibility confirmation.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_VALIDATION_016_005 fails (unresolved zoning/CO issues), the Mortgage may be deemed ineligible for purchase. This can stop delivery acceptance, force curative actions (CO issuance, legal opinion updates, remediation), and jeopardize scheduled delivery timelines.

dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_020_006 governs SBL final delivery timeliness. However, BR_BORROWER_PROHIBITION_007_003 is a hard ineligibility rule for purchase/approvals. If sanctions/exclusion applies, the delivery workflow should not proceed (or will be rejected), making sanctions clearance a prerequisite gate.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If sanctions screening fails, Seller/Servicer may waste effort assembling and transmitting the Final Delivery Package; Freddie Mac will not purchase/approve the transaction, triggering operational disruption and potential contractual remedies.

dependencies.rationale: Rule 020_006 is about timely delivery of an SBL Final Delivery Package. Rule 035_002 states Freddie Mac will not purchase any Mortgage if an SCP match exists. While 035_002 is not a timing rule, it functionally overrides the need to proceed with delivery workflow because purchase cannot occur. For workflow automation, SCP screening must gate delivery efforts.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 035_002 fails: (1) continuing delivery attempts wastes operational effort and may create fee/exception handling, (2) heightened compliance exposure (attempting to transact with a prohibited counterparty), (3) late-stage transaction failure likely.

business_rules.rule_name: SBL nondelivery breakage fee equals 2% of proposed mortgage amount
business_rules.description: As liquidated damages for the nondelivery of an SBL Mortgage under the standard delivery option, Freddie Mac will charge the Seller/Servicer a Breakage Fee equal to two percent (2%) of the proposed SBL Mortgage amount. Collection of the Breakage Fee does not prevent Freddie Mac from exercising other Guide remedies.
business_rules.conditions: Freddie Mac determines there has been a nondelivery of an SBL Mortgage
business_rules.consequences: If nondelivery occurs, Seller/Servicer is charged Breakage Fee = 0.02 × proposed amount
business_rules.exceptions: None stated in excerpt

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 18SBL.9(e) Calculation of the Breakage Fee (06/30/16)
source_reference.source_text: e. Calculation of the Breakage Fee (06/30/16) As liquidated damages for the nondelivery of an SBL Mortgage, Freddie Mac will charge the Seller/Servicer a Breakage Fee equal to two percent of the proposed SBL Mortgage amount. Freddie Mac’s collection of the Breakage Fee will not prevent it from exercising any other remedies set forth in the Guide.

examples: Proposed SBL Mortgage amount $8,000,000 and nondelivery determined: Breakage Fee = $160,000.
Proposed SBL Mortgage amount $25,500,000 and nondelivery determined: Breakage Fee = $510,000.
Nondelivery occurs and Breakage Fee is collected: Freddie Mac may still terminate the purchase contract or pursue other remedies.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Charge breakage fee; terminate purchase contract

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_020_006 establishes the mandatory timing and completeness requirements for an SBL Final Delivery Package. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_CALCULATION_020_007 then defines liquidated damages for nondelivery. In automated workflows, a missed/failed final delivery is a trigger condition that can escalate to a nondelivery determination and fee calculation.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If final delivery is not completed on time (or not completed at all), Freddie may determine nondelivery and assess the 2% Breakage Fee, along with other Guide remedies. Operationally this drives financial loss, dispute handling, and potential counterparty performance monitoring.

business_rules.rule_name: Conversion requires 85% occupancy for 3 months
business_rules.description: To satisfy the Minimum Occupancy Requirement for Conversion, the Property must have stabilized residential occupancy of 85% for the three consecutive months preceding the submission of the Conversion Underwriting Package. This requirement must be met both at package submission and at Conversion.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Forward Commitment Conversion when submitting the Conversion Underwriting Package
business_rules.consequences: If met, occupancy condition satisfied; if not, Freddie Mac purchase condition is not satisfied
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 17A, Requirements for Conversion, Minimum Occupancy Requirement (12/16/25)
source_reference.source_text: 3. Minimum Occupancy Requirement .  • The Property must h ave stabilized residential occupancy at 85 percent for the three consecutive months preceding the submission of the  Conversion Underwriting Package

examples: Package submitted 2026-06-15: property occupancy must be ≥85% in March, April, and May 2026.
If occupancy was 84% in one of the prior three months (e.g., 86%, 84%, 88%), the condition is not satisfied.
If occupancy is 90% now but did not meet ≥85% for all three preceding months, the condition is not satisfied for submission.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: The minimum occupancy requirement must be satisfied for the three months preceding submission of the Conversion Underwriting Package and again at Conversion. BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_PROCESS_021_002 explicitly states the Seller/Servicer must obtain monthly certified rent rolls 'to support physical occupancy determination for Conversion delivery.' Therefore, the occupancy test depends on the rent roll collection/certification process.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If certified monthly rent rolls are missing/uncertified, the Seller cannot substantiate the 85% stabilized occupancy requirement. Freddie Mac may reject the Conversion Underwriting Package, delay Conversion, or require re-submission once adequate documentation is available.

dependencies.rationale: BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_CONSTRAINT_021_001 is a purchase/conversion eligibility condition (minimum occupancy) for Conversion. BR_BORROWER_PROHIBITION_007_003 is a universal ineligibility rule; thus sanctions clearance gates conversion underwriting and eligibility evaluation.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If sanctions screening fails, conversion cannot proceed even if occupancy is 85% for 3 months; the loan is effectively ineligible and the workflow must stop or replace parties.

business_rules.rule_name: Monthly rent roll required from completion through Conversion
business_rules.description: From and after completion of construction through and including Conversion, the Borrower must provide a current rent roll for each month to the Seller. Each monthly rent roll must be certified as true and correct by both the Borrower and the property manager. The Seller/Servicer must obtain these to support physical occupancy determination for Conversion delivery.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after construction completion through Conversion for Forward Commitment conversions
business_rules.consequences: If provided and certified, supports occupancy verification; if missing/uncertified, condition may fail
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 17A, Requirements for Conversion, Minimum Occupancy Requirement (12/16/25)
source_reference.source_text: • For each month from and after completion of construction , through and including Conversion, the Borrower will provide to the Seller a current rent roll for that month, each certified as true and correct by the Borrower and the property manager for the Property, and such other information as may be reasonably required be the Seller and/or Freddie Mac to determine the physical occupancy of the Property.

examples: Construction completed in January: Borrower provides certified rent rolls for February, March, April, and May leading up to June Conversion.
Borrower provides rent roll but only Borrower signs certification; property manager certification is missing—noncompliant with the stated requirement.
If Freddie Mac requests additional occupancy information beyond rent rolls, Borrower/Seller must provide as reasonably required.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: Additional Actual Loan Amount capped at 10% and TEL balance
business_rules.description: At TEL conversion, the Seller may request an increase in the Actual Loan Amount beyond the Rate Locked Maximum Funding Loan Amount, but the increase may not exceed the Additional Actual Loan Amount Percentage listed in the Confirmation Sheet; if not listed and the Forward Commitment revision date is 2024-02-29 or earlier, the percentage is 10%. Additionally, in no event may the Actual Loan Amount at Conversion exceed the TEL outstanding immediately prior to Conversion.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller requests Additional Actual Loan Amount at TAH TEL Conversion delivery
business_rules.consequences: If within caps, request is eligible for Freddie Mac discretion; if above caps, request is not allowable
business_rules.exceptions: If Confirmation Sheet lists a percentage, that governs instead of 10%

source_reference.source_text: the Seller may request at the time Seller delivers its TAH TEL Conversion Underwriting Package that the Actual Loan Amount be increased beyond the Rate Locked Maximum Funding Loan Amount as set forth in the Confirmation Sheet by an amount that does not exceed the Additional Actual Loan Amount Percentage listed in the Confirmation Sheet (or, if not listed in the Confirmation Sheet, then for those Forward Commitments with revision dates of 2-29-2024 or earlier , 10%) (the “ Additional Actual Loan Amount Percentage ”) of such Rate Locked Maximum Funding Loan Amount (any such increase referred to as the “ Additional Actual Loan Amount ”); provided, however, in no event will the Actual Loan Amount at Conversion exceed the amount of the TEL outstanding immediately prior to Conversion.

examples: Confirmation Sheet lists 7%: Rate Locked Max Funding $10,000,000; max Additional Actual Loan Amount = $700,000, subject to TEL outstanding cap.
No percentage listed and Forward Commitment revision date 2024-02-15: default cap is 10%; Rate Locked Max Funding $8,000,000; max increase $800,000.
If TEL outstanding immediately prior to Conversion is $9,500,000, Actual Loan Amount at Conversion cannot exceed $9,500,000 even if percentage cap would allow more.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: Additional Actual Loan Amount request needs new appraisal and calculation
business_rules.description: When requesting an Additional Actual Loan Amount at TAH TEL Conversion, the Seller must include in the TAH TEL Conversion Underwriting Package (i) the Seller’s calculation of the Actual Loan Amount supporting the request and (ii) a new Appraisal that complies with Chapter 60 and supports the requested Additional Actual Loan Amount.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller requests Additional Actual Loan Amount at TEL Conversion underwriting package delivery
business_rules.consequences: If included, request package is complete for Freddie Mac review; if missing, request may be denied
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: In connection with any such request, Seller will include in its TAH TEL Conversion  Underwriting Package  each of the following:  • Seller’s calculation of the Actual Loan Amount supporting the requested Additional Actual Loan Amount based on the performance of the Property  • A new Appraisal which complies with the requirements of Chapter 60, and which Appraisal supports the requested Additional Actual Loan Amount

examples: Seller requests a $500,000 increase and provides an updated DCR/NOI-based sizing calculation plus a new appraisal dated within the conversion package timeline, compliant with Chapter 60.
Seller provides only a narrative justification but no calculation worksheet—package is incomplete for the stated requirement.
Seller provides a new appraisal but it does not support the higher value/loan sizing; Freddie Mac can deny the requested increase.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_CONSTRAINT_021_003 defines hard limits on any requested Additional Actual Loan Amount. BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 requires a Seller calculation and a new Chapter 60-compliant appraisal supporting the requested amount. If the request exceeds the cap, the appraisal/calc package cannot result in an approvable request, so the cap rule is an upstream gating control.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the cap rule is not satisfied, the Seller may waste time/cost ordering a new appraisal and preparing a calculation that Freddie will reject as not allowable. This creates delays at Conversion and may force the Seller to re-size the request and re-underwrite.

dependencies.rationale: If property condition report issues may materially affect value, the appraiser must be provided the report before the appraisal is transmitted to Freddie. Since BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 requires an appraisal that will be delivered as part of the Conversion Underwriting Package, this appraisal must satisfy the pre-transmission condition report integration rule where applicable.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the PCR is not provided when required, Freddie may reject the appraisal or condition the underwriting review, forcing appraisal revisions and causing Conversion timeline slippage.

dependencies.rationale: A 'new Appraisal that complies with Chapter 60' must include required zoning/legal analysis. Therefore, any appraisal delivered to support the Additional Actual Loan Amount request must satisfy the zoning source and minimum zoning/legal content rules (parking ratio, density, rebuildability).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If zoning/legal issues are not properly addressed and sourced, Freddie may deem the appraisal deficient and refuse to use it to support the increased loan amount, requiring revisions and potentially blocking the request.

dependencies.rationale: If the underlying transaction is a Lease-Up program, then the 'new Appraisal' required by BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 must incorporate the two-value structure required by BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_DOCUMENTATION_034_004, because Freddie Mac Underwriting Value is based on the prospective as-stabilized value.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Lease-Up valuation components are missing, Freddie cannot rely on the appraisal to support underwriting value for the increased loan amount request, triggering appraisal revision and underwriting delay.

dependencies.rationale: BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 requires the new appraisal to comply with Chapter 60; for Mod Rehab transactions, Chapter 60 compliance includes dual valuation scenarios per BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_DOCUMENTATION_034_005.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Mod Rehab dual values are missing, Freddie may reject the appraisal as failing program-specific valuation requirements, delaying the Additional Actual Loan Amount review and potentially Conversion.

dependencies.rationale: If the property includes restricted units affecting rent levels, the appraisal supporting any increase request must capture the required restricted/unrestricted valuation scenarios; otherwise the appraisal is incomplete for underwriting reliance.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If restricted unit valuation analysis is omitted, Freddie may determine value support is insufficient and suspend/condition the increased amount request pending appraisal revision.

business_rules.rule_name: SBL Physical Risk Report required before Letter of Commitment issuance
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac requires the Seller/Servicer to submit an SBL Physical Risk Report that meets Chapter 62SBL requirements before Freddie Mac will issue a Letter of Commitment to purchase an SBL Mortgage.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to SBL mortgages prior to Freddie Mac Letter of Commitment issuance
business_rules.consequences: Met: Freddie may issue commitment; Violated: commitment will not be issued
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: 8SBL.17, requirement to submit Form 1104 report before commitment
source_reference.source_text: 1. Freddie Mac requires the Seller/Servicer to submit an SBL Physical Risk Report meeting the requirements of Chapter 62SBL before Freddie Mac will issue a Letter of Commitment to purchase an SBL Mortgage.

examples: Seller submits a Chapter 62SBL-compliant Form 1104 report; Freddie proceeds with commitment review.
Seller omits the SBL Physical Risk Report; Freddie will not issue the Letter of Commitment until submitted.
Seller submits a report that does not meet Chapter 62SBL requirements; Freddie treats it as not satisfying the prerequisite.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Refuse to issue Letter of Commitment

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 determines fundamental eligibility for sale based on Exclusionary List role matches and requires use of the most current list. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 requires a compliant SBL Physical Risk Report to obtain commitment. While the risk report does not logically require screening results, compliance operations typically sequence eligibility screening early to prevent wasted third-party report costs and cycle time on ineligible transactions.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 fails after the Physical Risk Report is ordered/submitted, the Seller may incur unnecessary vendor expense and delays, then still have to stop/withdraw the loan due to ineligibility.

dependencies.rationale: BR_BORROWER_PROHIBITION_007_003 creates an absolute stop for Freddie Mac approvals (purchase/servicing-related approvals). BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 is an approval step (Letter of Commitment) and is therefore blocked if sanctions screening results in a confirmed match.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If sanctions screening fails (confirmed match), Freddie will not issue the SBL Letter of Commitment regardless of whether the Physical Risk Report is provided; underwriting/commitment workflow terminates and the transaction must be declined or restructured with different parties.

business_rules.rule_name: Form 1104 review requires disclosure of critical repairs including mold
business_rules.description: After receiving and reviewing Form 1104, the Seller must disclose to Freddie Mac any Critical Repairs (as defined in Section 62SBL.3(b)), including actual or suspected structural, mechanical, electrical, or other material physical deficiencies or Mold at the Property, and must inform the Small Balance Loan Team of unusual or questionable conditions.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Seller receives and reviews Form 1104 (SBL Physical Risk Report)
business_rules.consequences: Met: Freddie informed for risk/conditions; Violated: misrepresentation risk and delay
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: 2. Once the Seller has received and reviewed Form 1104 , the Seller must disclose to Freddie Mac any Critical Repairs, as defined in Section 62SBL.3(b), including actual or suspected structural, mechanical, electrical or other material physical deficiencies or Mold at the Property and inform the Small Balance Loan Team of unusual or questionable conditions.

examples: Form 1104 notes suspected electrical panel defects; Seller discloses as a Critical Repair and flags to the Small Balance Loan Team.
Consultant reports visible mold; Seller must disclose mold as part of Critical Repairs to Freddie Mac.
Seller identifies a questionable structural crack during review but does not report it; this violates the disclosure requirement.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 requires the Seller/Servicer to submit the SBL Physical Risk Report (Form 1104) before Freddie Mac will issue a Letter of Commitment. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_COMPLIANCE_023_007 then requires disclosures 'after receiving and reviewing Form 1104.' Operationally, the output artifact from BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 (a compliant Form 1104) is the input/trigger for BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_COMPLIANCE_023_007.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 fails (no compliant Form 1104 submitted/obtained), the Seller cannot perform the required Form 1104-based Critical Repair disclosures. The SBL commitment process stalls because Freddie will not issue the Letter of Commitment, and downstream underwriting conditions may be incomplete or misinformed.

business_rules.rule_name: Critical repairs must be completed before full underwriting package submission
business_rules.description: The Seller must verify that all Critical Repairs have been addressed and must provide evidence of completion to Freddie Mac prior to submission of the full underwriting package to Freddie Mac.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Critical Repairs are identified in SBL Physical Risk Report
business_rules.consequences: Met: underwriting package acceptable; Violated: package incomplete; delays/denial
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: • Verify that all Critical Repairs have been addressed and provide evidence of completion to Freddie Mac prior to submission of the full underwriting package to Freddie Mac

examples: Physical risk report identifies a critical life-safety repair; Seller obtains contractor sign-off and provides evidence to Freddie before submitting full underwriting.
Seller submits the full underwriting package without evidence that a Critical Repair was addressed; Freddie can require resubmission or deny commitment.
Seller verifies completion with photos and paid invoice and includes evidence with submission; requirement is satisfied.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_VALIDATION_023_008 applies 'when Critical Repairs are identified in [the] SBL Physical Risk Report' and requires the Seller to verify repairs are addressed and provide evidence prior to submitting the full underwriting package. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_COMPLIANCE_023_007 is the rule that operationalizes identification/escalation of Critical Repairs to Freddie Mac and the SBL team. In practice, the workflow is: identify/disclose Critical Repairs → cure/verify completion → submit underwriting package.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_COMPLIANCE_023_007 is not performed, Critical Repairs may not be formally recognized/escalated, leading to an incomplete or noncompliant underwriting package submission and heightened misrepresentation risk. Freddie may later condition the deal, require rework, or delay/decline purchase/commitment.

dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_VALIDATION_023_008 cannot be evaluated without a Physical Risk Report identifying whether Critical Repairs exist. While the rule’s immediate trigger is the presence of Critical Repairs, the enabling artifact is Form 1104 produced/obtained through the physical risk reporting requirement.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Form 1104 process is incomplete or noncompliant, the Seller cannot reliably determine whether Critical Repairs exist and cannot complete (or properly scope) the repair verification evidence Freddie requires prior to underwriting package submission.

business_rules.rule_name: UCC searches must include all filing offices and be certified at final delivery
business_rules.description: To ensure a first-lien perfected security interest in UCC collateral, the Seller/Servicer must perform public record searches including UCC searches. Each UCC search must include every office where a financing statement would be filed under Revised Article 9. Prior to the Origination Date, any financing statements found (other than those filed by the current lender to be released at origination) must be explained to the applicable Multifamily Attorney (via amended Legal Issues Analysis for non-SBL or written analysis for SBL). Final delivery must include counsel certification that UCC searches reveal only financing statements that have been/will be terminated at origination or have been approved by Freddie Mac.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to mortgages requiring UCC collateral perfection and UCC search deliverables
business_rules.consequences: If met: delivery can proceed; if violated: legal/delivery hold until cured or approved
business_rules.exceptions: Current lender filings to be released at origination excluded from explanation requirement

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 29.3 Uniform Commercial Code search requirements (04/22/25)
source_reference.source_text: Each UCC search must include every office where a financing statement would be filed in accordance with the provisions of Revised Article 9 of the UCC .  Prior to the Origination Date, an explanation of any financing statements identified in a UCC search other than the financing statements filed by the current lender that will be released at origination of the Mortgage must be provided to the applicable Multifamily Attorney in either (i) an amended Legal Issues Analysis for non -SBL Mortgages or (ii) a written analysi s for SBL Mortgages.  The final delivery of the Mortgage must include  a Seller’s Counsel’s Certification  or Single Counsel’s Certification – SBL, as applicable, in which counsel certifies that the UCC searches reveal only financing statements that (1) have been or will be terminated in connection with the origination of the Mortgage or (2) have been approved by Freddie Mac.

examples: UCC search finds prior equipment lien filing from Vendor A; Seller provides written explanation to Multifamily Attorney before origination and gets Freddie Mac approval: compliant.
UCC search omitted county filing office required under Article 9; counsel cannot certify completeness at delivery: delivery held until corrected searches obtained.
UCC search finds borrower’s prior blanket lien; seller plans termination at origination and includes in counsel certification: compliant if actually terminated.
Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: Rent schedule must be within 7.5% tolerance of actuals
business_rules.description: The rent schedule submitted to Freddie Mac must contain no errors of which the Seller has knowledge and must accurately state both gross potential rents and actual leased rents for the Home Sites and any Borrower-Owned Homes for the Property within a tolerance range of 7.5 percent.
business_rules.conditions: When submitting the rent schedule to Freddie Mac for an MHC Mortgage
business_rules.consequences: If met: schedule acceptable; if violated: cure/credit or delivery conditions
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The rent schedule submitted to Freddie Mac must: • Contain no errors of which the Seller has knowledge, and • Accurately state both the gross potential rents and the actual leased rents for the Home Sites and any Borrower -Owned Homes for the Property within a tolerance range of 7.5 percent

examples: Actual leased rents total $100,000/month; submitted rent schedule states $106,000/month (6% variance): within 7.5% tolerance.
Actual leased rents total $100,000/month; submitted schedule states $110,000/month (10% variance): outside tolerance; must be corrected.
Seller knows a rent roll entry is wrong and submits anyway: violates 'no errors of which the Seller has knowledge'.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery condition / repurchase exposure under R&Ws
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit tolerance threshold (7.5%) and 'must' language.

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: Cash Warehouse Delivery Requires Forms 987M and 996M
business_rules.description: For cash program/product warehouse (pledged mortgage) deliveries, the Seller must submit to Freddie Mac Multifamily Cash Management the authorization documentation required by the referenced section, and settlements cannot be made if the authorization documentation is not on file. With each delivery of a pledged Mortgage to be purchased under a cash program/product, the Seller must include a completed Form 987M (Wire Transfer Authorization – Cash Warehouse Delivery) attached to a properly completed and executed Form 996M (Warehouse form).
business_rules.conditions: When delivering a pledged Mortgage under a cash program/product from a warehouse line
business_rules.consequences: If docs on file and forms included, settlement can proceed; if missing, settlement cannot be made
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Seller must submit to Freddie Mac Multifamily Cash Management the authorization documentation required by Section 32.12(b). Settlements cannot be made if such authorization documentation is not on file with Freddie Mac Multifamily Cash Management. With each delivery of a pledged Mortgage to be purchased by Freddie Mac under a cash program or product, the Seller must include a completed Form 987M, Wire Transfer Authorization – Cash Warehouse Delivery, attached to a properly completed and executed Form 996M, Warehouse

examples: Seller submits Section 32.12(b) authorization docs and includes Form 987M attached to executed Form 996M—settlement proceeds.
Seller delivers pledged mortgage but authorization documentation is not on file—settlement cannot be made until provided.
Seller includes Form 987M but Form 996M is not executed—delivery is document-deficient and settlement is blocked.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit settlement gating and required forms; numeric specificity is via form identifiers (987M/996M) not thresholds.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Settlement blocked (cannot be made) until documentation is on file

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: Insurance Evidence Requires Form 1133 via ICT and Section 31.20 Docs
business_rules.description: To verify that the Property has (or will have as of the Freddie Mac Funding Date) adequate property damage and liability insurance, the Seller must submit to Freddie Mac: (i) fully completed Form 1133 (Seller/Servicer Certification of Insurance Coverage) via the Insurance Compliance Tool (ICT), and (ii) the documents listed in Sections 31.20(a) and 31.20(b), as applicable. For refinance of an existing mortgage not owned by Freddie Mac, prior to the Origination Date, the mortgagee/mortgage holders clause and additional insured clause must be changed to reflect Guide requirements.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to loan delivery/underwriting package where insurance adequacy must be evidenced
business_rules.consequences: If submitted, insurance verification can be satisfied; if not, delivery conditions remain unmet
business_rules.exceptions: Refinance of non-Freddie loan requires clause changes prior to Origination Date

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 55 – Documentation and Deliveries (Evidence of Insurance)
source_reference.source_text: Evidence of Insurance The Seller must submit the following to Freddie Mac to verify that the Property has, or will have as of the Freddie Mac Funding Date, adequate property damage and liability insurance as required by the Purchase and Servicing Documents: • Fully completed Form 1133, Seller/Servicer Certification of Insurance Coverage , via the Insurance Compliance Tool (ICT) • The documents listed in Sections 31.20(a) and 31.20(b), as applicable For an underwriting package pertaining to the refinance of an existing mortgage not owned by Freddie Mac, prior to the Origination Date of the Mortgage, the mortgagee or mortgage holders clause and additional insured clause must be changed to reflect the requirements of the Guide.

examples: Seller uploads completed Form 1133 through ICT and attaches the required Section 31.20 documents; Freddie Mac can verify coverage effective on the Funding Date.
Refinance of a bank-held loan: Seller fails to update the mortgagee clause before Origination Date; delivery is conditioned until clauses are corrected.
Seller provides an insurance ACORD only but does not submit Form 1133 via ICT; requirement is not met and delivery remains incomplete.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery rejection or purchase delay pending insurance evidence
business_rules.extraction_notes: Form and tool requirements are explicit; the timing is tied to Funding Date and refinance clause changes must occur prior to Origination Date.

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

business_rules.rule_name: Insurance Waiver Must Be Requested Before Origination if Noncompliant
business_rules.description: If the Borrower’s insurance will not meet Freddie Mac insurance requirements, the Seller/Servicer must request a waiver prior to the Origination of the Mortgage. This is an exception-handling requirement to address noncompliant coverage before closing/origination.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when submitted insurance coverage does not meet Freddie Mac requirements
business_rules.consequences: If waiver requested timely, Freddie may consider exception; if not, insurance noncompliance blocks delivery
business_rules.exceptions: Waiver permitted only if requested prior to Origination

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 55 – Documentation and Deliveries (Evidence of Insurance waiver sentence)
source_reference.source_text: If the Borrower's insurance will not meet the Freddie Mac insurance requirements, the Seller/Servicer must request a waiver prior to the Origination

examples: Borrower’s policy provides insufficient liability limits; Seller/Servicer submits waiver request before origination for Freddie Mac decision.

Seller/Servicer discovers post-origination that deductible exceeds Freddie requirements; waiver was not requested prior to origination, so cure/endorsement (not waiver) may be required and delivery may be delayed.
Borrower cannot obtain required endorsement in time; Seller requests waiver before origination as required to avoid a post-closing insurance defect.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery rejection or purchase delay pending waiver decision or insurance cure
business_rules.extraction_notes: Sentence is truncated in the provided chunk; still an explicit 'must request a waiver prior to Origination' requirement but should be verified against the full Guide text.

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: The insurance waiver is an exception-handling branch that only exists after the Seller evaluates the Borrower’s insurance coverage against Freddie’s requirements (using Form 1133 and supporting documents). Therefore, the exception request is conditionally dependent on the insurance documentation and compliance determination step.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If insurance documentation is not submitted/evaluated early enough, noncompliant coverage may be discovered too late to request a waiver prior to origination, potentially blocking closing/origination and delaying purchase/funding.

business_rules.rule_name: PLIM Must Be Submitted With Underwriting Package and Transfers
business_rules.description: When the Seller/Servicer submits an underwriting package, an application for approval of a Transfer of Ownership, or certain other Special Servicing Requests (as specified elsewhere in the Guide), the Seller/Servicer or its legal counsel must submit to Freddie Mac a Preliminary Legal Issues Memorandum (PLIM) prepared by the Seller/Servicer’s legal counsel that meets the requirements in Section 6.4(b).
business_rules.conditions: When delivering an underwriting package, Transfer of Ownership, or specified requests
business_rules.consequences: If met: submission is complete; if violated: delivery/approval may be suspended
business_rules.exceptions: Only applies to the listed submission types

source_reference.source_text: 6.4 Preliminary legal issues memorandum (08/26/25) a. When a preliminary legal issues memorandum is required (10/12/17) The Seller/Servicer or its legal counsel must submit to Freddie Mac a memorandum ("preliminary legal issues memorandum" or “PLIM”), prepared by the Seller/Servicer's legal counsel, that meets each requirement set forth in Section 6.4(b) when the Seller/Servicer submits each of the following: • An underwriting package • An application for approval of a Transfer of Ownership • Certain other Special Servicing Requests

examples: When submitting an underwriting package for a Mortgage with nonstandard structure, Seller/Servicer includes a PLIM signed by counsel.
For a Transfer of Ownership approval request, the delivery package includes the PLIM meeting Section 6.4(b) requirements.
If the PLIM is missing from the underwriting package submission, Freddie Mac may place the submission in suspense pending receipt.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery suspense or rejection until PLIM is provided

examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch

dependencies.rationale: The UCC search rule creates issue identification and explanation outputs (financing statements and required explanations/amended analyses) that counsel must consider and typically incorporate into legal issue memoranda. Therefore, completing UCC searches and routing exceptions is an upstream prerequisite to producing a complete and accurate PLIM for underwriting package submission.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If UCC searches and exception explanations are not completed, the PLIM may be incomplete/incorrect, creating legal delivery holds, additional counsel review cycles, and potential origination delays.

business_rules.rule_name: All loan document blanks must be completed with Freddie Mac specified info or 'n/a' and unspecified blanks require contact for instructions
business_rules.description: The Seller must complete each blank in a Loan Document or other Freddie Mac-required form with the information specified by Freddie Mac, with “n/a,” or with a cross-reference to the applicable modification Exhibit, as applicable. If the Purchase Contract or the Guide does not specify how a blank is to be completed, the Seller must contact the applicable Freddie Mac Multifamily Regional Office for non-SBL Mortgages or the Multifamily Small Balance Loan Team for SBL Mortgages for instructions.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to completion of Freddie Mac required loan documents for closing/delivery
business_rules.consequences: If completed per rule, documents are acceptable for delivery; if blanks are incomplete/incorrect, document defects must be cured and may delay funding/purchase
business_rules.exceptions: Different Freddie Mac contact path for SBL vs non-SBL instructions

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 32 – Completion of documents (10/01/25), Completion of blanks by the Seller
source_reference.source_text: The Seller must complete each blank in a Loan Document or other form required by Freddie Mac with the information specified by Freddie Mac, with "n/a," or with a cross-reference to the applicable modification Exhibit, as applicable. If the Purchase Contract or the Guide does not specify how a blank is to be completed, the Seller must contact : • The Applicable Freddie Mac Multifamily Regional Office for instructions, for non-SBL Mortgages • The Multifamily Small Balance Loan Team, for SBL Mortgages

examples: A Loan Agreement blank for 'Property Name' is filled exactly as specified on Freddie Mac’s form instructions; document is acceptable.
A Rider has a blank that does not apply; Seller enters 'n/a' rather than leaving it empty.
A form has an unclear blank not addressed by the Guide; Seller emails the applicable regional office (non-SBL) for instructions before execution.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Strong must-language; numeric precision low because the rule is not numeric.

examples: Closing package assembled with executed note and recorded security instrument
Closing package flagged delivery-ready after final title and insurance evidence received
Closing package returned for correction due to missing signature/acknowledgment

business_rules.rule_name: All required changes must be made by modification exhibit and body text must not be altered except rider redlines
business_rules.description: The Seller must make all authorized and required changes on the appropriate modification exhibit and must mark the applicable box on the document to indicate the exhibit is attached. The Seller may not make any changes to the Loan Documents by altering the text of the body of the document. The only stated exception is that modifications may be made directly to the text of the body of Riders to Loan Documents if clearly identified by showing stricken-through (deleted) text and underlined (new) text.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller/Servicer makes any changes to Freddie Mac loan documents
business_rules.consequences: If followed, changes are properly documented; if violated, documents may be rejected and must be re-executed/cured before purchase
business_rules.exceptions: Riders may be edited with visible strikethrough/underline redlines

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 32 – Completion of documents (10/01/25), Method for making changes
source_reference.source_text: The Seller must make all authorized and required changes on the appropriate modification exhibit to the document, and must mark the applicable box on the document to indicate such an exhibit is attached. The Seller may not make any changes to the Loan Documents by altering the text of the body of the document, except that modifications may be made directly to the text of the body of Riders to Loan Documents so long as such modifications are clearly identified by intentionally showing stricken-through (deleted) text and underlined (new) text.

examples: Seller adds a required program change by completing a modification exhibit and checks the exhibit-attachment box; acceptable.
Seller edits the Loan Agreement body paragraph directly (no exhibit); prohibited and the document must be corrected and re-executed.
Seller modifies a Rider by striking deleted text and underlining new text; permitted if clearly identified.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear prohibition with a specific exception for riders; non-numeric rule.

examples: Closing package assembled with executed note and recorded security instrument
Closing package flagged delivery-ready after final title and insurance evidence received
Closing package returned for correction due to missing signature/acknowledgment

dependencies.rationale: A closing package can be defective either by incomplete/incorrect blanks (005_003) or by impermissible text alterations outside approved modification exhibits (005_004). These controls are complementary quality gates that together ensure documents are in Freddie-acceptable form.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If blanks are not completed correctly or modifications are made improperly, documents may be rejected, requiring cure, re-execution, and potentially delaying funding/purchase.

business_rules.rule_name: Payment and Performance Bonds 100% Contract or 15% LOC Waiver
business_rules.description: Payment and performance bonds are required if (i) the general contractor is unrelated to the Borrower, or (ii) the general contractor is related to the Borrower and will use subcontractors for major elements of rehabilitation (including replacing heating boilers/air chillers, replacing plumbing/electrical systems, site grading, roof replacement, window replacement, or environmental remediation). When required, bonds must: be on a form acceptable to Freddie Mac; cover 100% of the applicable construction contract amount; have a surety licensed through the state in which it operates; have a surety rating of at least A-9 from A.M. Best (or equivalent); and name the Seller/Servicer (and successors/assigns) as a dual obligee. Bonds may not be required if construction monitoring requirements remain in place and any one of the following is met: construction contract amount is less than 15% of UPB; or Mortgage LTV is 65% or less; or cash/letter of credit equal to 15% of the total construction contract is provided (and LOC counterparty acceptable to Freddie Mac).
business_rules.conditions: When a TAH rehab construction contract is part of the transaction/closing file
business_rules.consequences: If bonds meet requirements or waiver applies, closing package is compliant; otherwise deficient
business_rules.exceptions: Waiver allowed with monitoring plus <15% UPB, or ≤65% LTV, or 15% cash/LOC

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 19 – Payment and performance bonds (Page 9-10 context)
source_reference.source_text: Payment and performance bonds are required under either of the following circumstances: • If the general contractor used to complete the construction work is unrelated to the Borrower • If the general contractor is related to the Borrower and will use subcontractors for major elements of the rehabilitation, including: • Replacing heating boilers or air chillers • Replacing plumbing or electrical systems • Site grading • Roof replacement • Window replacement • Environmental remediation Requirements for the payment and performance bonds are as follows: • They must be on a form acceptable to Freddie Mac • They must cover 100 percent of the amount of the applicable construction contract • The surety must be licensed through the State in which it operates • The surety must have a rating of at least A-9 from A.M. Best Company, Inc. or an equivalent rating from a comparable ratings agency • The Seller/Servicer, together with its successors and assigns, must be named a dual obligee Payment and performance bonds may not be required if all construction monitoring requirements remain in place and any of the following conditions are met: • The amount of the construction contract represents less than 15 percent of the UPB • The LTV of the Mortgage is 65 percent or less • Cash or a letter of credit in the amount of 15 percent of the total construction contract is provided, and the letter of credit counterparty is acceptable to Freddie Mac

examples: General contractor is unrelated and contract is $2,000,000; closing package must include bonds covering 100% ($2,000,000) from an A-9+ rated, state-licensed surety naming Seller/Servicer as dual obligee
GC is related and subcontracts roof replacement; bonds are required unless a waiver condition is met (e.g., LTV is 65% or less and monitoring is in place)
Contract is $900,000 and UPB is $7,000,000 (12.86%); with monitoring in place, bonds may be omitted under the <15% UPB waiver, but file must support the waiver basis
business_rules.enforcement_action: Underwriting/closing conditions; loan purchase denial until satisfied

examples: Closing package assembled with executed note and recorded security instrument
Closing package flagged delivery-ready after final title and insurance evidence received
Closing package returned for correction due to missing signature/acknowledgment

business_rules.rule_name: Zoning report not required when appraisal has zoning analysis and loan ≤$20M
business_rules.description: A separate zoning report is not required if the zoning analysis described in Section 60.12(f)(2) is provided in the Appraisal AND all listed conditions are met: (1) initial principal balance is $20 million or less OR the Mortgage is a Supplemental Mortgage and combined initial/prior balances are $25 million or less in the aggregate; (2) Mortgage is not a Moderate Rehabilitation, Lease-up, Value-add, or Forward Commitment Mortgage; and (3) copies of all available Certificates of Occupancy are provided to Freddie Mac in the final underwriting package.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Seller seeks to omit zoning report and rely on appraisal zoning analysis
business_rules.consequences: If conditions not met, a zoning report is required; omission may halt purchase review
business_rules.exceptions: None beyond listed conditions

source_reference.source_text: Notwithstanding the above, a zoning report is not required if the zoning analysis described in Section 60.12( f)(2) is provided in the Appraisal and all of the following conditions are met: • The Mortgage has an initial principal balance of $20 million or less or the Mortgage is a Supplemental Mortgage and the combined initial principal balance of the Supplemental Mortgage and the unpaid principal balances of any senior Mortgages encumbering th e Property are $25 million or less in the aggregate • The Mortgage is not a Moderate Rehabilitation Mortgage, a Lease -up Mortgage, a Value -add Mortgage or a Forward Commitment Mortgage • Copies of all available Certificates of Occupancy are provided to Freddie Mac in the final underwriting package

examples: New mortgage $18,000,000, not lease-up/value-add, appraisal includes 60.12(f)(2) zoning analysis, COs provided: zoning report can be omitted.
Supplemental mortgage $5,000,000 with senior UPB $22,000,000 (combined $27,000,000): exceeds $25,000,000 threshold, so zoning report is required.
Mortgage is $19,500,000 but is a Value-add Mortgage: condition fails; zoning report is required even if appraisal includes zoning analysis.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit exception to zoning report requirement with dollar thresholds and product-type exclusions.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Freddie Mac underwriting suspense / purchase ineligibility until cured

examples: Mortgage requires an appraisal dated within the acceptable recency window at closing
Mortgage requires an appraisal update due to market changes or report staleness
Mortgage requires appraisal review when red flags are identified

dependencies.rationale: The zoning-report exception is only safe/allowable if the appraisal’s zoning analysis is robust and compliant. BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_VALIDATION_022_010 establishes the baseline appraisal zoning/legal discussion requirements (authoritative source, parking/density/rebuildability). Those elements are prerequisites to using an appraisal as the substitute for a separate zoning report under BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_EXCEPTION_006_007.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the appraisal lacks required zoning source and minimum zoning/legal analysis, the Seller cannot rely on the exception and may be required to obtain a separate zoning report—creating delays and potential underwriting package rejection if omitted.

business_rules.rule_name: Appraisal must exclude Non-Permitted units and their income from direct cap and final as-is value
business_rules.description: When units lack required certificate-of-occupancy confirmation (Non-Permitted Units), the Appraisal must not include any Non-Permitted Unit or any income derived from any Non-Permitted Unit in either the direct capitalization approach or the final as-is valuation.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when there are Non-Permitted Units for an SBL Mortgage property.
business_rules.consequences: If met, appraisal is acceptable for purchase conditions; if violated, appraisal is non-compliant and may block purchase.
business_rules.exceptions: None stated.

source_reference.source_text: The Appraisal must not include any Non -Permitted Unit or any income derived from any Non-Permitted Unit in either the direct cap or final as -is valuation.

examples: Appraisal removes the two non-permitted units from rent roll inputs and excludes their rent from direct cap NOI: compliant.
Appraisal includes all units but sets rent at $0 for non-permitted units: not compliant if unit itself is included in valuation.
Final as-is value references income that includes non-permitted unit rent: non-compliant; appraisal must be revised.
Mortgage requires an appraisal dated within the acceptable recency window at closing
Mortgage requires an appraisal update due to market changes or report staleness
Mortgage requires appraisal review when red flags are identified

business_rules.rule_name: 2-Step Draft-to-Final Property Condition Update 10/14/16
business_rules.description: If consultant-identified issues in the property condition report could materially affect value, then before the Seller/Servicer transmits the Appraisal to Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer must provide the appraiser with the property condition report ordered by the Seller/Servicer. The report may be provided in draft form if the appraiser notes it was provided with a draft property condition report. If the final version is materially different from the draft, the appraiser must be given the final version and the valuation must be amended to reflect the new information. If Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac disagree on materiality, Freddie Mac makes the final decision case-by-case.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when property condition report identifies potentially material value-impacting issues
business_rules.consequences: If provided and amended as needed, appraisal supports delivery; if not, appraisal may be rejected/conditioned
business_rules.exceptions: Draft delivery allowed for expediency; Freddie Mac decides materiality if disagreement

source_reference.source_text: If there are issues identified by the consultant that could materially affect the value of the Property, prior to the Seller/Servicer’s transmittal of the Appraisal to Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer must provide the appraiser with the property condition report ordered by the Seller/Servicer as part of the Mortgage loan transaction process. For expediency, the report can be delivered to the appraiser in draft form, as long as the appraiser notes in the Appraisal report that it was provided with a draft pr operty condition report. If the final version is materially different from the draft version, the appraiser must be given the final version and the valuation must be amended to reflect this new information. If the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac do not agree if an issue is material, Freddie Mac will make the final decision of materiality on a case -by-case basis.

examples: Draft PCA notes roof failure likely requiring $500,000 replacement; Seller/Servicer provides draft to appraiser before submission; appraiser references draft in appraisal—compliant.
Final PCA changes repair scope materially (adds structural repairs); Seller/Servicer provides final to appraiser and appraiser amends value conclusion—compliant.
Seller/Servicer submits appraisal to Freddie Mac without providing the material PCA; Freddie Mac conditions or rejects the appraisal pending amendment.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit pre-transmittal requirement, draft allowance, and amendment obligation when final differs materially.

examples: Mortgage requires an appraisal dated within the acceptable recency window at closing
Mortgage requires an appraisal update due to market changes or report staleness
Mortgage requires appraisal review when red flags are identified

business_rules.rule_name: Zoning Minimums plus 20M/25M No-Zoning-Report Thresholds
business_rules.description: For all mortgages, the appraiser must consider, support, and discuss how zoning and other legal issues affect property value, and must reference the authoritative zoning source in the appraisal while addressing at minimum: parking ratio compliance, density compliance, and rebuildability restrictions after substantial damage/casualty loss. Additionally, when the mortgage is a non-SBL mortgage with initial principal balance of $20,000,000 or less, or is a Supplemental Mortgage where combined initial principal balance plus UPBs of any senior mortgages encumbering the property are $25,000,000 or less in the aggregate, and the mortgage is not a Moderate Rehabilitation, Lease-up, Value-add, or Forward Commitment mortgage, a third-party zoning consulting report might not be part of the underwriting package—so the underwriter and appraiser must ensure pertinent zoning compliance information is included and its effect on market value is addressed.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to appraisals for all mortgages; includes special threshold scenarios for zoning report omission
business_rules.consequences: If zoning source and minimum items absent, appraisal is deficient; may delay underwriting/delivery
business_rules.exceptions: Special threshold scenario applies only if loan is not Moderate Rehab, Lease-up, Value-add, Forward Commitment

source_reference.source_text: 1. For all Mortgages, the appraiser must consider, support, and discuss how zoning and other legal issues (including shared access agreements, easements, and compliance with local rent control statutes) affect the value of the Property. The appraiser must, reference the authoritative zoning source in the Appraisal, comparing the Property to competing properties, and addressing at a minimum: • Parking ratio compliance • Density compliance • Rebuildability restrictions in the event of substantial damage or casualty loss to the Property. 2. If a Mortgage meets either of the following conditions, a third -party zoning consulting report might not be part of the underwriting package... • The Mortgage o Is a non -SBL Mortgage with an initial principal balance of $20 million or less, or is a Supplemental Mortgage and the combined initial principal balance of the Supplemental Mortgage and the unpaid principal balances of any senior Mortgages encumbering the Property are $25 million or less in the aggregate, and o Is not a Moderate Rehabilitation Mortgage, a Lease -up Mortgage, a Value -add Mortgage or a Forward Commitment Mortgage.

examples: Non-SBL mortgage with initial principal balance $18,000,000; no third-party zoning report is included; appraisal cites zoning code source and addresses parking, density, rebuildability—compliant.

Supplemental mortgage initial principal balance $5,000,000 with seniors totaling $19,000,000 (aggregate $24,000,000); appraisal must still include authoritative zoning source and minimum zoning items—compliant even without zoning consultant report.
Value-add mortgage with initial principal balance $15,000,000; the threshold carve-out does not apply, and underwriting may still require a zoning consulting report per full Guide requirements.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Includes explicit minimum zoning discussion items and exact $20M/$25M threshold scenario language.

examples: Mortgage requires an appraisal dated within the acceptable recency window at closing
Mortgage requires an appraisal update due to market changes or report staleness
Mortgage requires appraisal review when red flags are identified

business_rules.rule_name: Lease-up appraisals need as-is and as-stabilized values 09/25/15
business_rules.description: For Properties specifically underwritten in Acquisition Lease-Up or Refinance Lease-Up programs, the Appraisal must include (i) the as-is value as of the Appraisal effective date (current date) and (ii) the prospective as-stabilized value as of the prospective future stabilized-operations date. Freddie Mac Underwriting Value is based on the prospective as-stabilized value.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when underwriting a Property under Acquisition or Refinance Lease-Up
business_rules.consequences: If both values provided, underwriting can use as-stabilized value; if missing, appraisal is incomplete
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: For Properties to be specifically underwritten in either of these two programs, the Appraisal must provide the as -is value of the Property as of the effective date of the Appraisal report (i.e., the current date) and the prospective as -stabilized value as of the prospective future date of stabilized operations for the Property. The Freddie Mac Underwriting Value is based on the prospective as -stabilized value.

examples: Acquisition Lease-Up: appraisal includes as-is value as of 2026-06-01 and as-stabilized value as of 2027-03-01; underwriting value uses as-stabilized figure.
Refinance Lease-Up: appraisal provides only as-stabilized value; missing as-is value makes the appraisal noncompliant until corrected.
Lease-up property where stabilization is projected 18 months out: appraisal must still state a prospective as-stabilized value as of that future stabilized date.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery/underwriting suspension until compliant appraisal provided

examples: Mortgage requires an appraisal dated within the acceptable recency window at closing
Mortgage requires an appraisal update due to market changes or report staleness
Mortgage requires appraisal review when red flags are identified

business_rules.rule_name: Mod Rehab appraisal must include 2 values as-is and as-if renovated 02/28/20
business_rules.description: For a Moderate Rehabilitation (Mod Rehab) Mortgage, the appraiser must provide two values of the Property based on all applicable approaches to value: (1) As-is market value; and (2) Hypothetical as-if renovated and stabilized today market value.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when the Mortgage is a Moderate Rehabilitation (Mod Rehab) Mortgage
business_rules.consequences: If both values provided, valuation scenarios are supported; if not, appraisal fails program requirement
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: 60.28 Appraisals for Moderate Rehabilitation (Mod Rehab) Mortgages (02/28/20)
source_reference.source_text: For a Mod Rehab Mortgage, the appraiser must provide the following two values of the Property based on all applicable approaches to value: • As-is market value • Hypothetical as -if renovated and stabilized today market value

examples: Appraiser provides as-is market value based on current condition and a separate hypothetical as-if renovated value assuming renovation complete and stabilized today.
Appraiser provides only a single as-completed value; missing as-is market value requires appraisal revision before Freddie Mac underwriting.
Mod Rehab with limited scope: still must include both as-is and as-if renovated and stabilized today values.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery/underwriting suspension until compliant appraisal provided

examples: Mortgage requires an appraisal dated within the acceptable recency window at closing
Mortgage requires an appraisal update due to market changes or report staleness
Mortgage requires appraisal review when red flags are identified

business_rules.rule_name: Restricted units require 2 values and phase-out time analysis
business_rules.description: If a Property has restricted units, the appraiser must provide (i) an estimate of market value with restricted units in place and (ii) a hypothetical market value without restricted units. The appraiser must also opine (with support) on whether Highest and Best Use is to phase out restricted rents in favor of non-restricted rents and, if so, develop an as-is market value considering the time needed to phase in non-restricted rents.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when the Property includes restricted units affecting rent levels
business_rules.consequences: If both valuations and HBU/time analysis are included, appraisal supports underwriting; if omitted, appraisal is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 60 – Restricted units requirement (item 5 shown in excerpt)
source_reference.source_text: If the Property has restricted units, the appraiser must include an estimate of market value with the restricted units in place and an estimate of hypothetical market value without the restricted units. In addition to the hypothetical value if leased at n on-restricted rents, the appraiser must opine and support its opinion if the Highest and Best Use of the property is to phase out restricted rents in favor of non -restricted rents, and, if so, develop an as -is estimate of market value considering the time period necessary to phase in non -restricted rents.

examples: LIHTC property: appraisal reports value with restricted rents and a hypothetical value at market rents, and analyzes the HBU and phase-out period to reach market rents.
Property has restricted rents but appraisal provides only one value; underwriting requires revision to include both restricted and hypothetical unrestricted values.
Appraiser concludes HBU is to retain restricted rents; appraisal must still include both valuations and supported HBU conclusion.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery/underwriting suspension until compliant appraisal provided

examples: Mortgage requires an appraisal dated within the acceptable recency window at closing
Mortgage requires an appraisal update due to market changes or report staleness
Mortgage requires appraisal review when red flags are identified

dependencies.rationale: BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_DOCUMENTATION_034_006 requires restricted and unrestricted valuation outputs. BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_VALIDATION_034_007 then requires documenting the restricted rent roll source and continuation/expiry methodology, which is necessary to make the valuation outputs reliable and acceptable to Freddie.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If restricted rent roll source/continuation analysis is not documented, Freddie may treat the restricted-rent valuation as unsupported, triggering appraisal revision and underwriting delays.

business_rules.rule_name: Restricted rent roll source and continuation analysis required
business_rules.description: When restricted rents apply, the appraiser must document the source of the Property’s restricted rent roll and provide adequate support, analysis, and discussion for continuation of restricted rents. If the appraiser concludes restricted rents will expire or not continue, the appraiser must use appropriate valuation methodology considering the likelihood of restricted rents expiring.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when appraisal relies on restricted rent roll and restricted rent continuation
business_rules.consequences: If source and continuation analysis documented, rent inputs are supportable; if not, appraisal is deficient
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 60 – Restricted rents documentation requirement (excerpted paragraph)
source_reference.source_text: In the Appraisal, the appraiser must document the source of the Property’s restricted rent roll and provide adequate support, analysis and discussion for the continuation of the restricted rents. If the appraiser concludes that the restricted rents will e xpire or not continue, the appraiser must use the appropriate methodology to value the Property considering the likelihood of the restricted rents expiring.

examples: Appraisal cites the housing agency’s regulatory agreement and current restricted rent roll extract as the source, and explains why restrictions are expected to continue through the loan term.
Appraiser believes restrictions may sunset: appraisal switches to a methodology that weights probability of expiration and reflects transition risk in value.
Appraisal uses restricted rents but fails to identify the rent roll source; lender must obtain a corrected appraisal with source documentation.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery/underwriting suspension until compliant appraisal provided

examples: Mortgage requires an appraisal dated within the acceptable recency window at closing
Mortgage requires an appraisal update due to market changes or report staleness
Mortgage requires appraisal review when red flags are identified

business_rules.rule_name: Appraisal must report total value plus 3 non-real estate allocations
business_rules.description: The Appraisal must clearly and prominently report the total market value of the Property and an allocation for contributory business value, personal property, and/or other non-real estate items. The appraiser must discuss the value segregation process and provide market-derived data supporting allocations (e.g., surveys of market participants, comparable sales data, authoritative sources).
business_rules.conditions: Applies when appraisal includes non-real estate items affecting value conclusion
business_rules.consequences: If segregation is reported and supported, valuation is transparent; if not, appraisal is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 60 – Value segregation and allocation requirement (excerpted paragraph)
source_reference.source_text: The Appraisal will clearly and prominently report the total market value of the Property as well as an allocation for contributory business value, personal property and/or other non -real estate items. The appraiser will clearly, adequately and comprehensi vely discuss the value segregation process and provide market -derived data for the value allocations, including, where applicable, surveys of market participants, comparable sales data and authoritative sources for the appraiser’s allocation methodology.

examples: Hotel-like multifamily asset: appraisal reports total real estate value and separately allocates contributory business value and personal property, citing market participant surveys and comparable sales support.
Appraisal includes personal property in the total value but provides no allocation or support; must be revised to clearly allocate and support the segregation.
Appraisal identifies no non-real estate items; it still must clearly report total market value and confirm allocations are not applicable/are zero if appropriate.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Delivery/underwriting suspension until compliant appraisal provided

examples: Mortgage requires an appraisal dated within the acceptable recency window at closing
Mortgage requires an appraisal update due to market changes or report staleness
Mortgage requires appraisal review when red flags are identified

business_rules.rule_name: Property Inspection Report Due Annually if UPB > $2M or Risk Rating > 6
business_rules.description: For Non-SBL Mortgages, a property inspection report due date is tied to UPB and risk status. If UPB is less than or equal to $2 million, the inspection report is due at the calendar quarter end of the biennial anniversary of the Origination Date (unless otherwise notified). If UPB is greater than $2 million, or Risk Rating is greater than 6, or the mortgage is subordinate to a securitized loan, the inspection report is due at the calendar quarter end of the annual anniversary of the Origination Date (unless otherwise notified). Additionally, for a Mortgage with a Transfer of Ownership or Subordinate Financing, the report is due at the time of the transfer or Subordinate Financing; for a Mortgage with a tax lien, it is due when the tax lien is filed.
business_rules.conditions: When Non-SBL mortgage meets UPB/risk triggers or special events occur
business_rules.consequences: Timely inspection: compliant; late/missing: post-purchase reporting defect
business_rules.exceptions: Unless otherwise notified by Freddie Mac

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 40.2(b) Property inspection report – Non-SBL Mortgages (10/17/24)
source_reference.source_text: Property inspection report – Non-SBL Mortgages  A Mortgage with a UPB ≤ $2 million  The calendar quarter end of the biennial anniversary of the Origination Date. Unless otherwise notified by Freddie Mac: • March 31, • June 30, • September 30, or • December 31  • A Mortgage with a UPB > $2 million, or • A Mortgage with a Risk Rating > 6, or • A Mortgage held by Freddie Mac and subordinate to a securitized loan  The calendar quarter end of the annual anniversary of the Origination Date.

examples: Non-SBL mortgage UPB is $1.8M with Risk Rating 5; origination 2023-02-10: inspection due at quarter-end of biennial anniversary (e.g., 2025-03-31 if that is the applicable quarter-end).
Non-SBL mortgage UPB is $3.5M; origination 2024-07-01: inspection due at quarter-end of annual anniversary (e.g., 2025-09-30 if that is the applicable quarter-end).
Non-SBL mortgage UPB is $1.5M but Risk Rating is 7: annual inspection cadence applies despite UPB ≤ $2M.
business_rules.enforcement_action: Corrective action request / asset management escalation

examples: Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: A-CREFC assessment due March 31 or May 31 for UPB over $250,000
business_rules.description: For Mortgages with unpaid principal balance (UPB) greater than $250,000, the Servicer must submit an annual assessment using A-CREFC-MF or A-CREFC-HC (as applicable). The due date is March 31 for Mortgages with Risk Rating greater than 6 or Special Product Type Mortgages specified in the Guide (except SBL Mortgages). The due date is May 31 for all other Mortgages, including SBL Mortgages. Late or missing submission is a reporting noncompliance item requiring follow-up.
business_rules.conditions: Mortgage has UPB > $250,000 and annual assessment is required under Chapter 40 table
business_rules.consequences: If submitted by required deadline, assessment is timely; if late, Servicer is out of compliance with reporting requirements
business_rules.exceptions: Special Product Type timing excludes SBL Mortgages from March 31 trigger

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 40.2(b) Conditions requiring an assessment and timing (10/17/24)
source_reference.source_text: A-CREFC -MF or A -CREFC -HC, as applicable  A Mortgage with a UPB > $250,000  • March 31 – a Mortgage with a Risk Rating > 6, or a Special Product Type Mortgage specified in Section 40.16, except SBL Mortgages  • May 31 – all other Mortgages, including SBL Mortgages

examples: Risk Rating 7 (non-SBL) → annual assessment due March 31.
Risk Rating 4 SBL Mortgage → annual assessment due May 31.
Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

deduplication_info.rationale: Both rules require the same annual assessment submission rule for Mortgages with UPB > $250,000, including the same due dates and segmentation: due March 31 for Mortgages with Risk Rating > 6 or specified Special Product Type Mortgages (except SBL), and due May 31 for all other Mortgages including SBL. Same thresholds, same dates, same scope, same consequence.
deduplication_info.primary_selection_reason: BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_012_009 selected as primary: equal content, retained per priority 5 (later table reference context in conditions is slightly clearer) with no loss of detail.

dependencies.rationale: Both rules require an annual assessment using A-CREFC-MF/A-CREFC-HC for UPB > $250,000 with the same March 31/May 31 split by risk rating/special product type.
