In a knowledge graph, linking them as complementary reduces user confusion and supports dedup/normalization in compliance workflows.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If treated independently without linkage, automated testing and compliance checklists may create duplicate tasks, conflicting task ownership, or double-count exceptions. Linking supports control rationalization and consistent reporting calendars.

business_rules.rule_name: Quarterly in 30 days and annual in 90 days; rent roll within 5 days of quarter end
business_rules.description: For non-SBL Mortgages subject to Chapter 40.15, the Seller/Servicer must submit via PRS: (a) within 30 days after the end of each calendar quarter following purchase, a current rent roll dated no earlier than five days prior to quarter-end and income/expense reporting for the required period; (b) within 90 days after the end of each fiscal year, annual financial statements including balance sheet and related items. If Loan Documents are modified to allow the Borrower more than 90 days to deliver annual documents, the Seller/Servicer must deliver them within five days after receiving them from the Borrower.
business_rules.conditions: Portfolio loans/loans scheduled for securitization; non-Cooperative property (quarterly); post-purchase
business_rules.consequences: If timely submitted, reporting remains current; if late, Servicer/Seller-Servicer violates post-purchase reporting requirements
business_rules.exceptions: Quarterly timing excludes Mortgages secured by a Cooperative-owned property

source_reference.source_text: for Mortgages secured by a Property owned by a Cooperative, within 30 days after the end of each calendar quarter following purchase: 1. Current rent roll dated no earlier than the date five days prior to the end of such quarter 2. Income and expenses

examples: Quarter ends June 30: rent roll must be dated no earlier than June 25 and submitted by July 30 (30 days after quarter end).
Fiscal year ends December 31: annual financial statements must be submitted by March 31 (90 days after fiscal year end).
Loan documents allow borrower 120 days for annuals; Seller/Servicer receives them on April 25 and must deliver to Freddie Mac by April 30 (within 5 days).
Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Loan-Level Transaction Report Due by 10th or 3rd Business Day
business_rules.description: For bond credit enhancement loan-level transactions: (1) If Borrower payments are received prior to the 10th day of the month, the Servicer’s report must reach Freddie Mac no later than the 10th day of that month. (2) If Borrower payments are received on or after the 10th day, the report must reach Freddie Mac no later than the third Business Day after the Servicer receives payment. If no payments are received, the report must reach Freddie Mac not later than the third Business Day after the 15th day of the month.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to bond credit enhancement loan-level transaction reporting timing each month
business_rules.consequences: If met, reporting is timely; if violated, reporting noncompliance and follow-up risk
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.source_text: 1. For Borrower payments received prior to the 10th day of the month, the Servicer’s report of loan-level transactions must reach Freddie Mac no later than the 10th day of the month in which the payment was received by or on behalf of the Borrower under the reimbursement and security agreement or the interest rate cap or swap agreement. If no payments are received, the Servicer’s report must reach Freddie Mac not later than the third Business Day after the 15th day of the month. ... 2. For Borrower payments received on or after the 10th day of the month, the Servicer’s report of loan-level transactions must reach Freddie Mac not later than the third Business Day after the Servicer receives any payment

examples: Payment received on the 8th: report must reach Freddie Mac by the 10th of the same month.
Payment received on the 10th: report must reach Freddie Mac by the 3rd Business Day after receipt.
No payments received in month: report must reach Freddie Mac by the 3rd Business Day after the 15th.
Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Securitization Reporting Must Populate Non-Cash Writedown UPB After Payment
business_rules.description: For each Mortgage sold in a Securitization, the Servicer must report via myOptigo for Investor Reporting. After reporting the regular scheduled payment, the Servicer must report in the Non-Cash Writedown field the unpaid principal balance (UPB) of the Mortgage after the regular scheduled payment is applied. If applicable, the Servicer must calculate interest and prepayment premium on the exception activity based on the amount shown in the Non-Cash Writedown field.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to each Mortgage sold in a Securitization and reported via myOptigo
business_rules.consequences: If reported correctly, investor reporting reflects correct post-payment UPB; if not, reporting is noncompliant
business_rules.exceptions: Interest/prepayment premium calculation applies only if exception activity is applicable

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 54; d. Mortgages sold in a Securitization (09/14/23)
source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must report via myOptigo for Investor Reporting each Mortgage sold in a Securitization. After reporting the regular scheduled payment, the Servicer must report in the Non-Cash Writedown field the unpaid principal balance (UPB) of the Mortgage after the regular scheduled payment is applied. If applicable, the Servicer must calculate the interest and prepayment premium on the exception activity (see Section 50.8), basing this calculation on the amount appearing in the Non-Cash Writedown field.

examples: Regular scheduled payment is applied and reduces UPB from $8,000,000 to $7,950,000; Servicer reports $7,950,000 in the Non-Cash Writedown field.
Servicer reports the pre-payment premium on exception activity using the Non-Cash Writedown amount rather than the pre-payment UPB; compliant with the basis requirement.
Servicer reports Non-Cash Writedown as the UPB before applying the scheduled payment; noncompliant because field must reflect post-payment UPB.
Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Mortgage Repurchase Must Be Reported as Exception Code 66 by Due Date
business_rules.description: For any repurchase required by Freddie Mac or requested by the Servicer and approved by Freddie Mac, the Servicer must report the repurchase via myOptigo for Investor Reporting coded as a Mortgage repurchase using Exception Code 66 no later than the due date specified by Freddie Mac. The Servicer must set the date on which the exception activity will occur at no more than 10 Business Days after Freddie Mac notified the Servicer that Freddie Mac is requiring or approving the repurchase.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Freddie Mac requires/approves a repurchase of a Mortgage or REO
business_rules.consequences: If timely, repurchase is properly recorded; if late, reporting violates Freddie Mac due date requirements
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: For any repurchase required by Freddie Mac or requested by the Servicer and approved by Freddie Mac, the Servicer must report the repurchase via myOptigo for Investor Reporting (coded as a Mortgage repurchase using Exception Code 66) no later than the due date specified by Freddie Mac... The Servicer must set the date on which the exception activity will occur at no more than 10 Business Days after the date on which Freddie Mac notified the Servicer that Freddie Mac is requiring or approving the repurchase.

examples: Freddie Mac approval notice dated March 1; Servicer schedules exception activity date no later than March 15 (10 Business Days) and reports Exception Code 66 by Freddie Mac’s specified due date.
Servicer reports repurchase as a generic payoff code rather than Exception Code 66; noncompliant coding.
Servicer sets exception activity date 15 Business Days after Freddie Mac notice; noncompliant with the 10 Business Day limit.
Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Monthly delinquency report due by 3:00 pm ET on 3rd business day
business_rules.description: The Servicer must perform the automated transmission of the Monthly Report of Delinquencies and Foreclosures via myOptigo for Investor Reporting by 3:00 p.m. Eastern time on the third Business Day of each month. Submitted transmissions must be usable, accurate, and timely, and the Servicer is responsible for data accuracy and integrity.
business_rules.conditions: For each monthly reporting cycle for delinquencies and foreclosures via myOptigo
business_rules.consequences: Meets: timely accepted report; Violated: report deemed late/omitted/unusable and may trigger fees
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must perform the automated transmission of the Monthly Report of Delinquencies and Foreclosures via myOptigo for Investor Reporting by 3:00 p.m. Eastern time on the third Business Day of each month. The Servicer is responsible for ensuring the accuracy and integrity of the data submitted to Freddie Mac. Transmissions must be usable, accurate and timely.

examples: Third Business Day is Wednesday; Servicer submits at 2:45 pm ET; compliant.
Servicer submits at 3:10 pm ET on the third Business Day; late and subject to noncompliance tracking.
Servicer submits by deadline but file is unusable; treated as unusable reporting data under fee rules.
Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Delinquency reporting fees: $250, $550, $1,000 then $1,000 monthly; due by 5th business day
business_rules.description: If a Servicer fails to provide timely, complete, and accurate monthly Mortgage Delinquency reporting data (late, omitted, or unusable), Freddie Mac may assess delinquency reporting noncompliance fees: 1st month $250; 2nd month $550; 3rd month $1,000; each additional month $1,000. Freddie Mac will assess only one fee per month regardless of number of violations in that month. Fees are due no later than the fifth Business Day after the first accounting cutoff date following the missed reporting due date, and must be remitted using the myOptigo Servicer Remittance system.
business_rules.conditions: When monthly delinquency reporting is late, omitted, or unusable for a given month
business_rules.consequences: Meets: no fee assessed; Violated: fee assessed and must be remitted by due date
business_rules.exceptions: Only one fee per month regardless of number of violations

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 44, Delinquency reporting noncompliance fees (04/22/25)
source_reference.source_text: Any Servicer that fails to comply with the reporting requirements set forth in this section is subject to the following Delinquency reporting noncompliance fees: 1st month - $250 2nd month - $550 3rd month - $1,000 Each additional month - $1,000 Freddie Mac will assess only one Delinquency reporting noncompliance fee per month regardless of the number of violations that occur in that month... Fees are due to Freddie Mac no later than the fifth Business Day after the first accounting cutoff date following the missed reporting due date

examples: Servicer misses the due date in January for the first time; fee assessed is $250 and must be paid by the 5th Business Day after the first cutoff following the missed due date.
In March, Servicer has both an omitted report and an unusable report condition; only one fee applies for the month.
After three consecutive months of failures, the fourth month failure remains $1,000 (each additional month).
Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_029_005 sets the submission requirement (third Business Day by 3:00 p.m. ET; usable, accurate, timely). BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_CONSTRAINT_029_006 defines the graduated noncompliance fees when reporting is late/omitted/unusable. This is a direct trigger relationship used for automated fee assessment.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If delinquency reporting is late/omitted/unusable, fees are assessed and must be remitted by the stated due date, creating immediate financial and compliance consequences and potential escalation with Freddie Mac.

business_rules.rule_name: PRS notice due within 30 calendar days of repair completion date with evidence
business_rules.description: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via PRS within 30 calendar days of the Repair Agreement completion date that the Borrower is on track to complete all repairs by the due date. The Servicer must include evidence in PRS of the Borrower’s intention to complete repairs (e.g., executed repair contract, estimate for repair work, or itemized progress of repair work).
business_rules.conditions: Applies to each Repair Agreement with a defined completion date under Chapter 39.4
business_rules.consequences: If timely PRS notice with evidence is submitted, Freddie Mac is informed of progress; if not, Servicer is noncompliant and may be required to provide remediation/updates
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via PRS within 30 calendar days of the completion date that the Borrower is on track to complete all repairs by the due date. The Servicer must include evidence in PRS of the Borrower’s intention to complete repairs. Such evidence may include an executed repair contract, estimate for repair work, or itemized progress of repair work.

examples: Completion date is June 30; Servicer submits PRS update by July 30 stating Borrower is on track and uploads an executed contractor agreement.
Servicer submits PRS update within 30 days but provides no supporting evidence: violates evidence requirement and must supplement PRS record.
Servicer uploads an itemized progress report and a repair estimate in PRS within the 30-day window: satisfies evidence requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing (30 calendar days) and enumerated acceptable evidence types.

examples: Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Completion notice due within 10 business days after servicer confirmation
business_rules.description: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac within 10 Business Days following the Servicer's confirmation of the Borrower's completion of all repairs under a Repair Agreement.
business_rules.conditions: Applies after Servicer confirms all repairs under a Repair Agreement are complete
business_rules.consequences: If notice is timely, Freddie Mac receives closure confirmation; if late/missing, Servicer must cure reporting deficiency and may face compliance findings
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac within 10 Business Days following the Servicer's confirmation of the Borrower's completion of all repairs under a Repair Agreement.

examples: Servicer confirms completion on March 1; Servicer must notify Freddie Mac no later than the 10th Business Day after March 1.
Servicer confirms completion but waits 15 Business Days to notify: violates the timing requirement and requires remediation.
Servicer confirms completion and submits notice within 3 Business Days: compliant.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 10 Business Day timeframe keyed to 'Servicer confirmation' event.

examples: Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_038_001 requires PRS notification within 30 days of the Repair Agreement completion date that the Borrower is on track, with evidence. BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_038_002 then requires notification within 10 Business Days after the Servicer confirms repairs are complete. They form a standard sequential monitoring-to-closure pattern for repair oversight.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the on-track notice is missed, Freddie may have reduced visibility into repair progress and may increase scrutiny. Even if completion notice is later provided, the Servicer may have a compliance finding for the earlier missed milestone and may need to provide remediation/updates.

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_038_002 sets a general completion-notification requirement. BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_DOCUMENTATION_038_003 is a special-case control that replaces the generic 'notify' mechanism with a specific certification instrument and adds a 30-day certification collection requirement. Thus, for green/utility improvement repairs, compliance with 038_002 is achieved through the more specific 038_003 pathway.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Servicer sends a generic completion notice without the required Verification Certification (or fails to collect it within 30 days), Freddie may deem the completion notification noncompliant, require re-submission in the correct form, and issue compliance findings.

business_rules.rule_name: Green verification certification must be collected within 30 days and include 3 data points
business_rules.description: For repairs classified as Utility Efficiency Improvements or Green Improvements, the Servicer’s completion notification to Freddie Mac must be made on the applicable Verification Certification (Utility Efficiency Improvement Verification Certification or Green Improvements Verification Certification) submitted via PRS. The Servicer must collect the applicable Verification Certification within 30 days of completion of the improvements, and the certification must provide: (i) the completion date; (ii) specifications of completed improvements; and (iii) confirmation that the Benchmarking Data Consultant uploaded current energy and water usage into ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager (or another Freddie Mac-identified tool). If Freddie Mac no longer owns the Mortgage, the Servicer must submit the Verification Certification to Freddie Mac via DMS.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Utility Efficiency Improvements or Green Improvements completion under a Repair Agreement
business_rules.consequences: If certification is collected and submitted timely, green/utility completion is documented; if not, Servicer must cure missing/late certification and may face compliance findings
business_rules.exceptions: If Portfolio Manager unavailable, use Freddie Mac-identified benchmarking tool; DMS route if no longer owned

source_reference.source_text: For repairs classified as Utility Efficiency Improvements or Green Improvements as applicable, this notification must be on the Utility Efficiency Improvement Verification Certification or the Green Improvements Verification Certification ... submitted to Freddie Mac via the Property Reporting System (PRS). The Servicer must collect the applicable Verification Certification within 30 days of completion of the applicable improvements. The Verification Certification must provide the following: o The date of completion of the improvements o The specifications of the completed improvements o Confirmation that the Benchmarking Data Consultant uploaded current energy and water usage ... into ENERGY STAR® Portfolio Manager®

examples: Green improvements completed May 5; Servicer collects the Green Improvements Verification Certification by June 4 (within 30 days) and submits via PRS including completion date/specs/benchmarking confirmation.
Utility efficiency work completed but certification lacks benchmarking upload confirmation: certification is incomplete and must be corrected and re-submitted.
Freddie Mac has transferred ownership; Servicer submits the Verification Certification via DMS instead of PRS per the instruction.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Clear 30-day timeframe and explicit required certification contents; includes conditional submission method.

examples: Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Partial repair completion notice due at least 30 days before due date via PRS
business_rules.description: At least 30 days prior to the applicable completion date, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of partial completion of repairs using the Borrower Certification – Partial Completion of Repairs form submitted to PRS. If processing an extension request, the Servicer must add an update comment to PRS that an extension request will be submitted. Upon approval of the extension request, the Servicer must update PRS with approval information indicating that approval has been reflected in the Consent Request Tracker (CRT).
business_rules.conditions: Applies when repairs are partially completed approaching the Repair Agreement completion date
business_rules.consequences: If timely PRS notice and required PRS updates are made, Freddie Mac has visibility and tracking; if not, Servicer must cure and may be cited for noncompliance
business_rules.exceptions: Extension request submitted only if appropriate

source_reference.source_text: At least 30 days prior to the applicable completion date, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of partial completion of repairs on the Borrower Certification – Partial Completion of Repairs form, which must be submitted to PRS. ... If processing an extension request, the Servicer must add an update comment to PRS that an extension request will be submitted. Upon approval of the extension request, the Servicer must update PRS with approval information, indicating that approval has been reflected in the Consent Request Tracker (“CRT”).

examples: Completion date is August 31; by August 1 the Servicer submits the Borrower Certification – Partial Completion of Repairs form in PRS to report partial completion.
Servicer plans to request an extension and adds a PRS comment stating an extension request will be submitted; after approval, Servicer updates PRS noting CRT reflects approval.
Servicer submits partial completion notice 10 days before the completion date: violates the 'at least 30 days prior' requirement and requires remediation.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit timing (≥30 days prior) plus required PRS workflow steps for extension tracking.

examples: Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation

business_rules.rule_name: Easement must be perpetual and recorded before Funding Date
business_rules.description: For an SBL Mortgage where the Property’s primary access to a public right of way is via an easement, the easement must be perpetual, must not allow loss of use upon breach (though it may allow a subordinate lien for unpaid maintenance costs), and must be recorded in the applicable land records prior to the Freddie Mac Funding Date. The easement must also be included in the insured legal description of the Property.
business_rules.conditions: When an SBL Property relies on an easement for primary access to a public right of way
business_rules.consequences: If met: collateral access condition satisfied for purchase consideration; if violated: not eligible until cured
business_rules.exceptions: Subordinate lien for unpaid maintenance costs is permitted

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 8SBL – SBL Property Fundamentals, d. Indirect access (06/30/16)
source_reference.source_text: 6. The easement must be perpetual. 7. Each party’s rights and responsibilities under the easement (including expenses) and remedies in the event of a breach by any other party must be generally acceptable to a prudent lender. 8. The easement may not allow for loss of use in the event of a breach. However, the easement may permit the placement of a lien which is subordinate to the SBL Mortgage for unpaid maintenance costs for the easement. 9. The easement must be recorded in the applicable land records for the jurisdiction prior to the Freddie Mac Funding Date and must be included in the insured legal description of the Property.

examples: Easement is perpetual and recorded 5 days before Funding Date; title legal description includes it → eligible under the easement recording requirement.
Easement is perpetual but recorded 2 days after Funding Date → noncompliant; mortgage delivery should be held until recorded prior to Funding Date (cannot be retroactively satisfied).
Easement allows a maintenance lien that is expressly subordinate to the SBL Mortgage → permitted under item 8.
A 200-unit property secures a first-lien Freddie Mac mortgage
Property secures a refinanced mortgage after payoff of prior lien
Property secures a mortgage that is later securitized into a PC pool

business_rules.rule_name: Shared Use Agreement must be perpetual and recorded before Funding
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac will consider purchasing an SBL Mortgage on a Property that uses Essential Facilities or Recreational Facilities not located on the Property and not under exclusive Borrower control only if the Shared Use Agreement is recorded in the applicable land records prior to the Freddie Mac Funding Date and is included in the insured legal description, provides perpetual access/use rights that inure to future owners, and does not allow loss of use upon breach (though a subordinate lien for unpaid maintenance costs may be permitted).
business_rules.conditions: When a Property relies on off-site/shared Essential or Recreational Facilities under a Shared Use Agreement
business_rules.consequences: If met: shared-facilities condition satisfied; if violated: mortgage is not eligible for purchase consideration
business_rules.exceptions: Subordinate lien for unpaid maintenance costs may be permitted

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 8SBL – SBL Property Fundamentals, e. Shared Essential/Recreational Facilities (06/29/17)
source_reference.source_text: 2. The Shared Use Agreement must be recorded in the applicable land records for the jurisdiction prior to the Freddie Mac Funding Date and must be included in the insured legal description of the Property. 3. The Shared Use Agreement must contain a stipulation that access to and use and enjoyment of the Essential Facilities and/or Recreational Facilities are perpetual and that such rights will inure to the benefit of all future owners of the Property. 5. Each party’s remedies in the event of a breach by any other party must be generally acceptable to a prudent lender. The Shared Use Agreement may not allow for loss of use in the event of a breach. However, the Shared Use Agreement may permit the placement of a lien which is subordinate to the SBL Mortgage for unpaid maintenance costs.

examples: Shared pool is on adjacent parcel under recorded Shared Use Agreement that grants perpetual rights to all future owners → condition satisfied.
Shared parking agreement is unrecorded at Funding Date → noncompliant; mortgage should not be delivered for purchase until recording/legal description requirements are met.
Agreement permits a maintenance lien, but lien is subordinate to the SBL Mortgage → acceptable under the exception.
A 200-unit property secures a first-lien Freddie Mac mortgage
Property secures a refinanced mortgage after payoff of prior lien
Property secures a mortgage that is later securitized into a PC pool

dependencies.rationale: Eligibility for shared/off-site facilities depends on the Shared Use Agreement meeting specific legal/recording conditions. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_019_001 is the underwriting-package documentation control that collects and presents counsel’s confirmation or gap analysis about Shared Access/Use Agreement compliance and facility descriptions, enabling Freddie to validate that the eligibility requirements in BR_SECURES_ELIGIBILITY_010_003 are actually met.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the documentation package does not include the required shared facility descriptions and counsel confirmation/gap analysis, Freddie may be unable to confirm eligibility and may condition or reject purchase consideration until the missing documentation is cured.

business_rules.rule_name: SBL Property tax parcels must be entire parcel(s) with no extra land
business_rules.description: For an SBL Mortgage, the Property must be identified as a single tax parcel, or if identified as multiple tax parcels, the Property must constitute the entirety of those tax parcels. No parcel containing the Property may include any property that is not subject to the SBL Mortgage sold to Freddie Mac.
business_rules.conditions: When determining SBL Property eligibility based on tax parcel configuration
business_rules.consequences: If met: parcel configuration acceptable; if violated: ineligible until collateral/parcel issues resolved
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.source_text: The Property must be identified as a single tax parcel or, if identified as multiple tax parcels, the Property must constitute the entirety of those tax parcels. Any tax parcel or parcels in which the Property is located may not include property that is not subject to the SBL Mortgage sold to Freddie Mac.

examples: Property is on two contiguous parcels and the collateral includes both parcels entirely → compliant.
Property is on a parcel that also includes an unrelated outparcel not included in the mortgage lien → noncompliant; must restructure collateral or subdivide.
Property is a single parcel exactly matching the improvements and land → compliant.
A 200-unit property secures a first-lien Freddie Mac mortgage
Property secures a refinanced mortgage after payoff of prior lien
Property secures a mortgage that is later securitized into a PC pool

business_rules.rule_name: No purchase unless warehouse assignment effective by funding date
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac will not purchase a pledged Mortgage if the Seller has assigned the Mortgage to the warehouse lender unless the warehouse lender assigns the Mortgage to the Seller effective on or before the Freddie Mac Funding Date. If the assignment to the warehouse lender was recorded, the Seller must record the assignment back to the Seller after the Freddie Mac Funding Date; if not recorded, return of the unrecorded assignment satisfies the requirement.
business_rules.conditions: Pledged Mortgage delivery where Seller previously assigned Mortgage to warehouse lender
business_rules.consequences: If unmet, Freddie Mac will not purchase; if met, purchase may proceed subject to other requirements
business_rules.exceptions: If initial warehouse assignment unrecorded, return of assignment satisfies section

source_reference.source_text:
Freddie Mac will not purchase a pledged Mortgage if the Seller has assigned the Mortgage to the
warehouse lender unless the warehouse lender assigns the Mortgage to the Seller effective on or
before the Freddie Mac Fu nding Date of the pledged Mortgage.

• If the assignment of the pledged Mortgage to the warehouse lender was recorded in the public
records of any jurisdiction, the Seller must record the assignment to the Seller in the public
records of the appropriate juri sdiction after the Freddie Mac Funding Date.

• If the assignment of the pledged Mortgage to the warehouse lender was not recorded, the
return of that unrecorded assignment to the Seller will satisfy the requirements of this section.

examples: Warehouse lender’s assignment back to Seller is effective one day before Funding Date: eligible for purchase; Seller records the assignment after Funding Date if the prior assignment was recorded.
Warehouse lender’s assignment back is effective two days after Funding Date: Freddie Mac will not purchase the pledged Mortgage.
Warehouse assignment was never recorded; lender returns the unrecorded assignment to Seller: requirement is satisfied without recording.
Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

business_rules.rule_name: No purchase unless UCC termination filed after funding date
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac will not purchase a pledged Mortgage if the warehouse lender has filed a financing statement relating to the pledged Mortgage unless the warehouse lender executes a termination statement. The Seller must file the termination statement in the appropriate public records after the Freddie Mac Funding Date.
business_rules.conditions: Pledged Mortgage where warehouse lender filed a financing statement in public records
business_rules.consequences: If termination not executed/filed, Freddie Mac will not purchase the pledged Mortgage
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text:
Freddie Mac will not pu rchase a pledged Mortgage if the warehouse lender has filed a financing
statement relating to the pledged Mortgage in the public records of any jurisdiction unless the
warehouse lender executes a termination statement.  The Seller must file the termination
statement in the public records of the appropriate jurisdiction after the Freddie Mac Funding Date.

examples: Warehouse lender filed a UCC-1 against the pledged Mortgage; lender signs termination and Seller files it after Funding Date: purchase may proceed.
Seller cannot obtain lender-signed termination statement: Freddie Mac will not purchase the pledged Mortgage.
Termination statement signed but not filed in the appropriate jurisdiction: treated as noncompliant and purchase is blocked.
Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

dependencies.rationale: The prohibition rule requires a termination statement if the warehouse lender filed a financing statement. The UCC search rule requires searching every filing office under Revised Article 9 and explaining financing statements found to counsel prior to origination. In practice, you cannot reliably comply with the financing-statement termination prohibition without the upstream UCC search control to detect and document the filing.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If UCC searches are not performed comprehensively, a warehouse financing statement may remain undiscovered and un-terminated, leading Freddie to refuse purchase of the pledged Mortgage and forcing last-minute cures/recording actions after Funding Date.

business_rules.rule_name: Non-Independent Property Purchase May Require Modifications or Reserves
business_rules.description: If a property is not an Independent Property, Freddie Mac may impose one or more purchase conditions: (1) if feasible to create/construct essential facilities, recreational facilities, or direct primary access, Freddie Mac may require the Borrower to make necessary modifications so the property meets Independent Property requirements; (2) Freddie Mac may require the Borrower to establish a Reserve of funds necessary to make required modifications later as determined by Freddie Mac; (3) Freddie Mac may require the mortgage to be cross-collateralized and cross-defaulted with mortgages on the property(ies) where the facilities/access are located; and (4) Freddie Mac may require the Borrower to be personally liable for any loss or damages incurred by Freddie Mac because the property is not an Independent Property.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when delivered mortgage is secured by a property that is not an Independent Property
business_rules.consequences: If conditions satisfied, Freddie Mac may purchase; if not, purchase may be declined
business_rules.exceptions: Conditions depend on Freddie Mac feasibility determination and discretion

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 8 – c. Requirements for purchase (06/30/15)
source_reference.source_text: If a Property is not an Independent Property, Freddie Mac may require one or more of the following as a condition to Freddie Mac’s agreeing to purchase the Mortgage: • If Freddie Mac determines that it is feasible to create or construct Essential Facilities, Recreational Facilities or direct primary access, Freddie Mac may require the Borrower to make the necessary modifications... • Freddie Mac may require the Borrower to establish a Reserve... • Freddie Mac may require the Mortgage to be cross -collateralized and cross -defaulted... • Freddie Mac may require that the Borrower be personally liable for any loss or damages incurred by Freddie Mac because the Property is not an Independent Property

examples: Property lacks direct public access but feasible to build; Freddie Mac conditions purchase on Borrower constructing direct access improvements.
Freddie Mac allows purchase but requires a reserve to fund future creation of essential facilities and requires additional personal liability.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Discretionary 'may require' conditions; no numeric thresholds.

examples: Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_007_003 is an absolute prohibition on purchase/transfer/servicing approvals upon confirmed match to specified lists. BR_019_002 governs conditions Freddie may impose to purchase a loan secured by a non-Independent Property. Even if BR_019_002 conditions could be satisfied, the transaction cannot proceed if BR_007_003 is violated. Therefore sanctions screening is a gating prerequisite before spending effort on BR_019_002 mitigations.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 fails: (1) Freddie will not purchase/approve transaction, (2) underwriting/structuring work under BR_019_002 is wasted, (3) potential legal/operational escalation to resolve false positives or terminate the transaction.

dependencies.rationale: Rule 019_001 requires the Seller to submit specified written information as part of the underwriting package when the property is not an Independent Property. Rule 019_002 describes the potential purchase conditions Freddie Mac may impose in that scenario (modifications, reserves, etc.). Freddie Mac cannot reasonably determine feasibility of modifications, reserve sizing, or access/use compliance without the package content mandated by 019_001, making 019_001 a practical prerequisite to applying 019_002.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 019_001 is incomplete/missing: (1) Freddie Mac cannot evaluate or tailor purchase conditions under 019_002, (2) underwriting review stalls, (3) delivery is treated as deficient, (4) transaction timelines extend while information is cured.

business_rules.rule_name: Non-Independent Property Purchase Allowed Only if Cross-Tied and Coterminous
business_rules.description: If the property is not and will not be an Independent Property as of the Origination Date and Freddie Mac has not required a reserve of funds or additional recourse under Section 8.6(c), Freddie Mac may agree to purchase the mortgage only if all stated requirements are met: the mortgage must be cross-defaulted and cross-collateralized with any mortgage for property where essential facilities/recreational facilities/direct access are located; the mortgage must be coterminous with those mortgages; the properties must be operated as a single unit with unimpeded ingress/egress to each other and to the facilities/access; and the Cross-Collateralization Agreement must not permit release of any property unless each remaining property can meet Independent Property requirements.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when collateral is not/will not be Independent Property as of origination
business_rules.consequences: If all requirements met, Freddie Mac may purchase; if any fail, purchase may be denied
business_rules.exceptions: Only applicable when Freddie Mac has not required reserve or additional recourse

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 8 – d. Cross-collateralized, cross-defaulted and coterminous (06/30/15)
source_reference.source_text: If the Property is not and will not be an Independent Property as of the Origination Date and Freddie Mac has not required a Reserve of the funds or additional recourse pursuant to Section 8.6(c), Freddie Mac may agree to purchase the Mortgage if each of the following requirements is met: • The Mortgage is cross -defaulted and cross -collateralized... • The Mortgage is coterminous... • The Property and the property... are operated as a single unit with the Property and the other property having unimpeded ingress and egress... • The Cross -Collateralization Agreement does not permit the release of any property unless each of the remaining properties is able to meet the requirements for an Independent Property

examples: Two parcels operate as one community; loans are cross-defaulted, cross-collateralized, and share coterminous maturity dates; Freddie Mac may agree to purchase.
Cross-collateral agreement allows one parcel to be released even though the remaining parcel lacks essential facilities; this fails eligibility and purchase may be denied.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit must-meet bullets; conditional applicability is clear.

examples: Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_PURCHASES_ELIGIBILITY_019_003 explicitly conditions its applicability on Freddie Mac not requiring a reserve of funds or additional recourse under Section 8.6(c), which is the same decision space described in BR_PURCHASES_EXCEPTION_019_002 (Freddie may require modifications and/or reserves). Operationally, the workflow must first determine whether Freddie imposed those conditions; only then can the seller know whether they may proceed under the alternate cross-collateralization/cross-default structure path in BR_PURCHASES_ELIGIBILITY_019_003.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_PURCHASES_EXCEPTION_019_002 is not evaluated/cleared (i.e., it is unknown whether Freddie required reserves/recourse), the seller cannot reliably select the correct purchase-eligibility pathway for a non-Independent Property. This can cause mis-structured delivery packages, purchase conditions, or outright purchase denial.

dependencies.rationale: BR_PURCHASES_ELIGIBILITY_019_003 allows purchase eligibility only if certain cross-default/cross-collateralization/coterminous requirements are met. BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_019_004 prohibits using cross-collateralization/cross-default with any other mortgage not simultaneously purchased by Freddie Mac for meeting the non-Independent Property requirements. Therefore, any attempt to satisfy BR_PURCHASES_ELIGIBILITY_019_003 must first pass the prohibition test in BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_019_004.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_019_004 fails, the cross-collateralization/cross-default structure is invalid for compliance. The mortgage becomes ineligible under the non-Independent Property eligibility pathway and Freddie Mac may decline purchase.

dependencies.rationale: BR_019_003 provides conditions under which Freddie may agree to purchase when the property is not/will not be an Independent Property. BR_007_003 overrides any such eligibility pathway by prohibiting purchase/approvals upon confirmed list match. Therefore BR_007_003 must be cleared before BR_019_003 is meaningfully evaluated.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 fails: immediate stop—Freddie will not purchase regardless of BR_019_003 satisfaction; transaction must be terminated or restructured with different parties (where permissible).

dependencies.rationale: Rule 019_003 sets a conditional purchase-eligibility pathway when the property is not/will not be Independent Property. Rule 019_001 requires the Seller to provide detailed facility/access descriptions and legal counsel analysis regarding Shared Access/Use Agreements, which are key to determining whether the 019_003 requirements and structure are necessary and correctly documented. In practice, 019_003 cannot be confidently applied without the information package required by 019_001.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 019_001 fails: (1) eligibility determination under 019_003 may be delayed or inaccurate, (2) Freddie Mac may suspend review pending missing data, (3) higher risk of later discovery that structure fails eligibility conditions, causing purchase denial late in the process.

business_rules.rule_name: Cross-Collateralization Prohibited Unless Other Mortgage Purchased Simultaneously
business_rules.description: A mortgage must not be cross-collateralized and cross-defaulted with any other mortgage that is not being simultaneously purchased by Freddie Mac for the purpose of meeting the non-Independent Property requirements. If the other mortgage is not simultaneously purchased by Freddie Mac, the cross-collateralization/cross-default structure cannot be used to satisfy the subsection requirements.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when structuring cross-collateralization to meet Section 8.6 requirements
business_rules.consequences: If violated, mortgage structure is impermissible and purchase eligibility may fail
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 8 – e. Additional requirements; prohibition sentence (04/13/23)
source_reference.source_text: The Property and the property on which the Essential Facilities, Recreational Facilities, and/or direct access are located must each meet all of the following requirements: • The Property and the property... must be owned and controlled by substantially the same Key Borrower Principals • The Property and the property... must together meet the requirements for an Independent Property... A Mortgage cannot be cross -collateralized and cross -defaulted with any other mortgage not being simultaneously purchased by Freddie Mac in order to meet the requirements set forth in this subsection .

examples: Seller proposes cross-collateralizing a Freddie Mac delivery with an existing bank loan on the adjacent parcel; since the bank loan is not being simultaneously purchased, this is prohibited.
Two mortgages are delivered and purchased by Freddie Mac at the same time and are cross-collateralized/cross-defaulted; structure can be used to meet the subsection requirement.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit prohibition; no numeric thresholds.

examples: Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

business_rules.rule_name: Forward Commitment Conversion Requires 85% Occupancy for 3 Consecutive Months
business_rules.description: For a conventional cash mortgage under a Forward Commitment to be eligible for purchase at Conversion, the property must have stabilized residential occupancy at 85% for the three consecutive months preceding submission of the Conversion Underwriting Package. From completion of construction through Conversion, the Borrower must provide the Seller a current monthly rent roll certified as true and correct by the Borrower and the property manager, plus other information reasonably required to determine physical occupancy.
business_rules.conditions: Applies to Forward Commitment conversions at package submission and on Conversion date
business_rules.consequences: If 85% for 3 months, condition satisfied; if not, Freddie Mac will not purchase at Conversion
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: underwriting package for the Forward Commitment . 3. Minimum Occupancy Requirement . • The Property must h ave stabilized residential occupancy at 85 percent for the three consecutive months preceding the submission of the Conversion Underwriting Package • For each month from and after completion of construction ,

examples: Occupancy is 86%, 88%, and 85% in the three months before package submission: condition is met and Conversion can proceed.
Occupancy is 84% in one of the three months before package submission: condition is not met and Freddie Mac purchase at Conversion is blocked until stabilized.
business_rules.extraction_notes: Explicit 85% and 3-month consecutive measurement window.

examples: Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_019_007 defines stabilized occupancy and rent-roll delivery conditions for eligibility at Conversion. BR_007_003 prohibits purchase and related approvals upon confirmed sanctions/exclusion match. Thus BR_007_003 must be cleared first; otherwise BR_019_007 cannot lead to purchase even if satisfied.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 fails: (1) Freddie will not purchase at Conversion, (2) borrower may incur carrying costs and lose takeout execution, (3) Seller must resolve sanctions match or terminate.

dependencies.rationale: Rule 021_001 defines the stabilized occupancy threshold and timing for submission and at Conversion. Rule 019_007 states that to be eligible for purchase at Conversion, the property must have stabilized residential occupancy at 85% for the three consecutive months preceding submission of the Conversion Underwriting Package (and also requires monthly certified rent rolls). Thus, the occupancy requirement in 021_001 is a gating prerequisite to the purchase eligibility determination in 019_007.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 021_001 fails: (1) the Conversion Underwriting Package is noncompliant, (2) Freddie Mac will not purchase at Conversion under 019_007, (3) Borrower/Seller may incur extension costs, re-underwriting, and potential transaction failure.

dependencies.rationale: Rule 019_007 includes an ongoing documentation requirement: from completion of construction through Conversion, the Borrower must provide a current monthly rent roll certified true/correct by Borrower and property manager. Rule 021_002 operationalizes that same requirement and states the Seller/Servicer must obtain them to support occupancy determination. Therefore, implementing 021_002 is mandatory to satisfy 019_007’s conditions for purchase at Conversion.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If 021_002 fails: (1) 019_007’s documentation condition is unmet, (2) Freddie Mac may refuse purchase at Conversion, (3) Seller faces delays, cure conditions, and potential non-delivery/purchase fallout.

business_rules.rule_name: Private transfer fee covenant created after 2011-02-08 makes mortgage ineligible
business_rules.description: If the Title Policy contains an exception for a Private Transfer Fee Covenant that was created on or after February 8, 2011, the Mortgage is ineligible for purchase by Freddie Mac.
business_rules.conditions: Applies at purchase review when title policy exceptions include private transfer fee covenant
business_rules.consequences: If exception present and covenant date ≥ 2011-02-08: not eligible for purchase
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 29.2(g) Exception for Private Transfer Fee Covenant (04/22/25)
source_reference.source_text: g. Exception for Private Transfer Fee Covenant (0 4/22/25) If the Title Policy contains an exception for a Private Transfer Fee Covenant that was created on or after February 8, 2011, the Mortgage is ineligible for purchase by Freddie Mac.

examples: Title policy lists an exception for a private transfer fee covenant recorded 2013-09-01: ineligible for purchase.
Title policy exception references a private transfer fee covenant created 2011-02-08: ineligible (on or after).
Title policy has no private transfer fee covenant exception: passes this eligibility check.
Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_018_009 is an eligibility screen based on participation by excluded parties. BR_025_007 is a title-policy-based ineligibility rule for private transfer fee covenants created on/after Feb 8, 2011. While neither rule is a literal prerequisite to the other, purchase eligibility workflows should treat them as complementary checks that together determine whether a mortgage can be purchased.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_018_009 is executed but BR_025_007 is not: (1) the loan may progress toward purchase despite a title-policy exception that makes it categorically ineligible, (2) late discovery causes purchase denial and pipeline fallout, and (3) corrective actions (endorsements/curative title work) may be impossible if the covenant is disqualifying.

dependencies.rationale: BR_016_005 requires confirmation of zoning/building code compliance and certificates of occupancy (or confirmation that absence is not a violation with enforcement risk). BR_025_007 makes the mortgage ineligible if the title policy contains a disqualifying private transfer fee covenant exception created on/after Feb 8, 2011. Both must be satisfied for a clean property/legal compliance posture; failure of either can stop purchase.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_016_005 is satisfied but BR_025_007 is missed (or vice versa): (1) the loan file may appear ‘property compliant’ while still being ineligible for purchase due to title exception, (2) late-stage purchase denial occurs, and (3) curative actions may be costly or impossible depending on the covenant/exception.

dependencies.rationale: BR_025_007 is a purchase ineligibility test based on the Title Policy exception for a Private Transfer Fee Covenant created on/after 2/8/2011. BR_007_003 independently prohibits purchase upon confirmed sanctions/exclusion match. Therefore sanctions clearance is a gating prerequisite for proceeding with title-based eligibility analysis and purchase decisioning.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 fails: purchase is denied irrespective of title; compliance workflow should short-circuit to sanctions resolution instead of continuing title review.

business_rules.rule_name: No Purchase If Borrower Principal or Manager Is on FHFA SCP List
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac will not purchase any Mortgage and will not allow or approve any Transfer of Ownership (under Chapters 41 or 41SBL), or approve any other servicing-related transaction, if any Borrower, Borrower Principal, or property management company is a Named Party on the FHFA Suspended Counterparty Program (SCP) List, subject to conditions and/or exclusions in the Named Party’s final suspension order.
business_rules.conditions: When any Borrower, principal, or property manager matches FHFA SCP Named Party
business_rules.consequences: If no match: transaction may proceed; if match: purchase/approval is denied
business_rules.exceptions: Subject to conditions/exclusions in final suspension order

source_reference.source_text: Freddie Mac will not purchase any Mortgage nor allow or approve any Transfer of Ownership under Chapters 41 or 41SBL, or approve any other Servicing -related transaction, in which any Borrower, Borrower Principal or property management company is a Named Party on the FHFA SCP List, subject to any conditions and/or exclusions set forth in each Named Party’s final suspension order.

examples: Borrower principal appears on FHFA SCP List: Freddie Mac denies purchase of the delivered Mortgage.
Property management company is a Named Party: Freddie Mac will not approve a Transfer of Ownership request.
Named Party order includes an exclusion permitting limited activity: decision follows the conditions/exclusions in that final order.
Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings

dependencies.rationale: BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 prohibits purchase/servicing transactions if any borrower/principal/property manager is a Named Party on the FHFA SCP list (subject to final order conditions). BR_RETAINS_RECORDS_UNDER_DOCUMENTATION_021_006 requires retaining evidence of screening against restricted party lists including SCP (with screenshots/copies). In practice, evidence retention is the control that validates the organization complied with the screening obligation that supports the prohibition decision.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_RETAINS_RECORDS_UNDER_DOCUMENTATION_021_006 fails (no evidence), the Servicer may still have made correct decisions, but cannot demonstrate compliance. This increases findings risk, corrective action likelihood, and weakens defensibility if a prohibited counterparty is later discovered.

dependencies.rationale: BR_018_009 requires using the most current Exclusionary List and makes a Mortgage ineligible if a listed party played certain roles in origination/transfer/real estate transaction (subject to an exception). BR_035_002 prohibits purchase/Transfer of Ownership/servicing approvals if Borrower/principal/property manager is a Named Party on FHFA SCP (subject to final suspension order conditions/exclusions). These are complementary negative screens that collectively define counterparty eligibility constraints; both are needed for a complete compliance determination.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_018_009 is followed but BR_035_002 is not (or vice versa): (1) Freddie Mac may later deny purchase/approval or require unwind actions, (2) Seller/Servicer may incur repurchase/indemnification exposure or operational losses, and (3) reputational and compliance findings risk increases due to inadequate restricted-party screening.

dependencies.rationale: BR_007_003 covers multiple lists (OFAC SDN, OFAC Consolidated, FHFA Suspended Counterparty List subject to conditions, Freddie Mac Exclusionary List) for borrower/principal/guarantor. BR_035_002 specifically addresses FHFA SCP Named Party status and adds property management company as a covered party. Operationally, these rules should be navigated together during party screening to avoid gaps (e.g., management company SCP exposure not covered by BR_007_003).
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_007_003 screening is performed without BR_035_002: lender may miss SCP exposure tied to the property management company, resulting in late-stage purchase denial and rework to replace management.

business_rules.rule_name: Remit Reimbursement and Cap or Swap Funds by 2nd Business Day
business_rules.description: For Targeted Affordable Housing Bond Credit Enhancement remittances, on the second Business Day after the Servicer receives any payments due to Freddie Mac under the reimbursement and security agreement and the interest rate cap or swap agreement, the Servicer must remit those payments to Freddie Mac via the myOptigo Servicer Remittance system (per Section 53.7).
business_rules.conditions: Applies while bond credit enhancement agreement is in effect and bonds not redeemed/canceled
business_rules.consequences: If met, timely remittance; if violated, late remittance exposure and reconciliation issues
business_rules.exceptions: Unless otherwise stated; must also meet any differing Chapter 53 requirements

source_reference.source_text: So long as the bond credit enhancement agreement is in effect and all of the bonds have not been redeemed or canceled, the Servicer must remit, deposit or retain the funds collected under the Loan Documents, the reimbursement and security agreement and the interest rate cap or swap agreement in accordance with this section. On the second Business Day after the Servicer receives any payments due to Freddie Mac under the reimbursement and security agreement and the interest rate cap or swap agreement, the Servicer must remit such payments to Freddie Mac via the myOptigo® Servicer Remittance system, as described in Section 53.7.

examples: Servicer receives reimbursement payment on Monday; must remit to Freddie Mac by Wednesday (2nd Business Day after receipt).
Servicer receives cap payment due to Freddie on Friday; must remit by Tuesday (assuming Mon/Tue are Business Days).
Servicer remits on the 4th Business Day after receipt—violates timing requirement.
Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
Accounting report reflects adjusted accounting net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting report flags exception where net yield setup differs from contract

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_013_006 mandates remittance to Freddie Mac via myOptigo on the second Business Day after receipt. Because BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_007 states Freddie Mac does not have use of funds until one Business Day after submission and imposes a 1:00 p.m. ET cutoff, it effectively constrains how the Servicer operationally meets the second Business Day remittance requirement.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If the Servicer waits until after 1:00 p.m. ET on the second Business Day, the payment may post late and breach BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_013_006, causing late remittance exposure and potential fees/reconciliation issues.

business_rules.rule_name: Remit Swap Payment to Provider Within 2 Business Days
business_rules.description: For bond credit enhancement transactions involving a swap, not later than two Business Days following receipt by the Servicer from the Borrower of any swap payment owed to the swap provider under the swap agreement, the Servicer must remit the swap payment to the swap provider.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Borrower pays swap amounts owed under the swap agreement
business_rules.consequences: If met, swap provider paid timely; if violated, swap payment delinquency risk increases
business_rules.exceptions: None

source_reference.source_text: Not later than two Business Days following receipt by the Servicer from the Borrower of any swap payment owed to the swap provider under the swap agreement, the Servicer must remit such swap payment to the swap provider.

examples: Borrower wires swap payment Tuesday; Servicer must remit to swap provider by Thursday.
Borrower payment received Friday; Servicer must remit by Tuesday (assuming Mon/Tue are Business Days).
Servicer remits on the 3rd Business Day after receipt—violates rule.
Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
Accounting report reflects adjusted accounting net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting report flags exception where net yield setup differs from contract

business_rules.rule_name: Remittance Request Due 1:00 p.m. ET 1 Business Day Early
business_rules.description: Because Freddie Mac does not have use of funds until one Business Day after the Servicer submits a remittance request through myOptigo Servicer Remittance, the Servicer must submit each remittance no later than 1:00 p.m. Eastern time one Business Day preceding the remittance due date. Remittances submitted after 1:00 p.m. Eastern Time will be processed the following Business Day. Remittance can be submitted in advance for up to 30 days (excluding non-business days).
business_rules.conditions: Applies to interim or monthly remittances initiated via myOptigo portal
business_rules.consequences: If late, remittance posts next Business Day and interest reimbursement fee may apply
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 53, Availability of funds; timing of remittances (10/19/23)
source_reference.source_text: Since Freddie Mac does not have use of funds until one Business Day after the Servicer submits a remittance request through the myOptigo® Servicer Remittance portal, the Servicer must submit each remittance to the myOptigo® Servicer Remittance System (see Section 53.7) no later than 1:00 p.m. Eastern time one Business Day preceding the remittance due date. Any remittance submitted after 1:00 p.m. Eastern Time will be processed the following Business Day. Remittance can be submitted in advance for up to 30 days, with the exception of non -business days.

examples: Remittance due Thursday: Servicer must submit by 1:00 p.m. ET Wednesday; submitting at 12:45 p.m. ET Wednesday is compliant.
Remittance due Monday: Servicer submits at 2:30 p.m. ET Friday; it will be processed the next Business Day and may be late.
Servicer schedules remittance 25 days in advance for a future due date; allowed (so long as it is not a non-business day).
Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
Accounting report reflects adjusted accounting net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting report flags exception where net yield setup differs from contract

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_008 restricts when a Servicer may begin using new remittance account/routing instructions and requires continuing the existing account until written authorization. Therefore, if the Servicer attempts to meet remittance timing requirements (BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_007) using a new account, they must first satisfy the account-change prerequisites in BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_008.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_008 is violated, remittances may be rejected/misdirected and interest reimbursement fees may apply, even if the Servicer submitted on time under BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_007.

business_rules.rule_name: Form 1064 Must Be Received 15 Business Days Before Account Change
business_rules.description: To change the designated remittance account or modify bank routing instructions, the Servicer must ensure Freddie Mac receives Form 1064 at least 15 Business Days before the Servicer uses the new account or modified routing instructions. The Servicer must continue using the existing account/routing until Freddie Mac provides written authorization to use the new/modified instructions. If the Servicer discontinues the existing account/routing before receiving written authorization, the Servicer will be subject to an interest reimbursement fee.
business_rules.conditions: Applies when Servicer requests a remittance account or routing change
business_rules.consequences: If violated, interest reimbursement fee applies and change may be rejected/delayed
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Chapter 53, Changes in remittance instructions (06/15/23) and timing language
source_reference.source_text: Freddie Mac requires its receipt of Form 1064 at least 15 Business Days before the Servicer's use of the new account or modified bank routing instructions for the Freddie Mac cash remittance system. The Servicer must continue to use the existing account or bank routing instructions until Freddie Mac gives the Servicer written authorization that the new account or modified bank routing instructions may be used. If the Servicer discontinues use of the existing account or bank routing instructions before obtaining written authorization from Freddie Mac, the Servicer will be subject to an interest reimbursement fee as set forth in Section 53.11.

examples: Servicer plans to switch custodial accounts on July 1; Form 1064 must be received by Freddie Mac at least 15 Business Days before July 1.
Servicer stops using the old routing on June 20 without written authorization; Servicer becomes subject to an interest reimbursement fee.
Servicer submits Form 1064 20 Business Days ahead and continues using the old routing until written authorization arrives; compliant.
Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
Accounting report reflects adjusted accounting net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting report flags exception where net yield setup differs from contract

business_rules.rule_name: Correct rejected myOptigo accounting transmission within 24 hours
business_rules.description: If Freddie Mac Multifamily Loan Accounting notifies a Servicer that a myOptigo transmission is unacceptable, the Servicer must provide a corrected transmission via myOptigo for Investor Reporting within 24 hours. Failure to transmit timely and accurate corrected automated accounting reports may subject the Servicer to accounting reporting noncompliance fees.
business_rules.conditions: When Freddie Mac notifies Servicer a myOptigo accounting transmission is unacceptable
business_rules.consequences: Meets: corrected submission accepted; Violated: subject to accounting reporting noncompliance fees
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.section_id: Guide Chapter 50, c. Back-up reporting requirements (04/15/21)
source_reference.source_text: If Freddie Mac Multifamily Loan Accounting notifies a Servicer that a myOptigo transmission is unacceptable, the Servicer must provide Freddie Mac with a corrected transmission via myOptigo for Investor Reporting  within 24 hours. Failure to transmit timely and accurate corrected automated accounting reports may subject the Servicer to the accounting reporting noncompliance fees set forth in Section 54.9.

examples: Transmission rejected at 11:00 am ET Tuesday; corrected transmission submitted by 10:30 am ET Wednesday; compliant.
Transmission rejected Friday 4:00 pm; corrected submitted Monday 5:00 pm; exceeds 24 hours and may trigger fees.
Servicer resubmits within 24 hours but data is still unusable; still at risk of noncompliance fees.
Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
Accounting report reflects adjusted accounting net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting report flags exception where net yield setup differs from contract

dependencies.rationale: The correction rule is activated only after Freddie Mac deems a myOptigo transmission unacceptable. That situation presupposes the Servicer is transmitting via myOptigo as required. In compliance workflow sequencing, the system should (a) enforce mandatory transmissions, (b) handle rejection events, (c) apply the 24-hour correction SLA.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_COMPLIANCE_039_009 is not implemented (or transmissions are not occurring), BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_029_008 cannot operate as a remediation control; reporting defects may persist uncorrected, increasing fee exposure and investor reporting integrity risk.

business_rules.rule_name: Gold PC accounting reports due 3 business days after cutoff; payoff due in 2 days
business_rules.description: For Gold PC Securitized Mortgages reported via myOptigo for Investor Reporting: (1) For each Mortgage without exception activity, accounting reports are due three Business Days after the accounting cutoff date. (2) For each Mortgage paid in full, reports are due by the earlier of two Business Days after the payoff or the last Business Day of the month in which the payoff occurred. (3) For CME sale, reports are due the same Business Day the securitization occurred. (4) For liquidation via third-party foreclosure sale/short payoff/transfer to REO, reports are due by the earlier of three Business Days after receipt of proceeds/notification or the last Business Day of the month of receipt.
business_rules.conditions: When Servicer reports Gold PC securitized mortgages via myOptigo
business_rules.consequences: Meets: timely accounting reporting; Violated: reporting noncompliance exposure and fees
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: For each Mortgage without exception activity, accounting reports are due: Three Business Days after the accounting cutoff date For each Mortgage paid in full, accounting reports are due: By the earlier of: • Two Business Days after the payoff; or • The last Business Day of the month in which the payoff For each Mortgage sold into a securitization (CME sale), accounting reports are due: • The same Business Day that the securitization (CME sale) occurred

examples: Cutoff date is Aug 15; report due Aug 20 (3 Business Days later) for a loan with no exceptions.
Payoff occurs Sept 10; report due Sept 12 (2 Business Days) unless month-end earlier (it is not).
CME sale occurs Oct 3; report due Oct 3 the same Business Day.
Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
Accounting report reflects adjusted accounting net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting report flags exception where net yield setup differs from contract

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_029_009 only applies in the context of 'Gold PC Securitized Mortgages reported via myOptigo'. That reporting channel and requirement is mandated by BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_COMPLIANCE_039_009. In workflow automation, once a loan is classified as Gold PC and subject to myOptigo, BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_COMPLIANCE_039_009 triggers the need to compute deadlines per BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_029_009.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_COMPLIANCE_039_009 is not enforced as mandatory, the Servicer may not apply/monitor the Gold PC deadlines, increasing missed due dates and noncompliance fee exposure.

business_rules.rule_name: 55-Day Multi PC reports due 1 business day after determination; liquidation due in 5 days
business_rules.description: For 55-Day Multi PC Securitized Mortgages reported via myOptigo: (1) For each Mortgage without exception activity, accounting reports are due one Business Day after the 55-Day Multi PC determination date. (2) For each Mortgage paid in full, reports are due by the earlier of two Business Days after payoff or the last Business Day of the month in which payoff occurred. (3) For CME sale, reports are due the same Business Day as the securitization. (4) For liquidation via third-party foreclosure sale/short payoff/transfer to REO, reports are due by the earlier of five Business Days after receipt/notification or the last Business Day of that month.
business_rules.conditions: When Servicer reports 55-Day Multi PC securitized mortgages via myOptigo
business_rules.consequences: Meets: timely accounting reporting; Violated: reporting noncompliance exposure and fees
business_rules.exceptions: None stated

source_reference.source_text: For each Mortgage without exception activity, accounting reports are due: One Business Day after the 55 -Day Multi PC determination date For each Mortgage paid in full, accounting reports are due: By the earlier of: • Two Business Days after the payoff; or • The last Business Day of the month in which the payoff For each Mortgage sold into a securitization (CME sale), accounting reports are due: • The same Business Day that the securitization (CME sale) occurred

examples: Determination date is Nov 6; report due Nov 7 (1 Business Day later) for a loan with no exceptions.
Liquidation notice received Dec 2; report due Dec 9 (5 Business Days later) unless last Business Day occurs earlier.
Payoff occurs on the last Business Day of the month; due date is the earlier of 2 Business Days after payoff or month-end (month-end wins).
Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
Accounting report reflects adjusted accounting net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting report flags exception where net yield setup differs from contract

dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_029_010 specifies due dates for 55-Day Multi PC securitized mortgages reported via myOptigo. That reporting is governed by the mandatory, timely/accurate transmission obligation of BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_COMPLIANCE_039_009. Automation should first recognize the mandatory reporting requirement, then apply the correct timing rule based on securitization type.
dependencies.impact_if_fails: If BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_COMPLIANCE_039_009 is not treated as mandatory, deadline enforcement (BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_029_010) weakens, increasing late reporting, downstream investor reporting issues, and noncompliance fees.

business_rules.rule_name: Noncompliance fees due by 5th business day after cutoff 06/12/23
business_rules.description: Freddie Mac separately monitors servicing reporting, accounting reporting, and delinquency reporting and separately assesses noncompliance fees. Fees are due no later than the fifth Business Day after the first accounting cutoff date following the missed reporting due date. The Servicer must remit the fee using the myOptigo Servicer Remittance system (Section 53.7).
business_rules.conditions: Applies when a reporting due date is missed for required servicing/accounting/delinquency reporting
business_rules.consequences: If remitted by deadline, fee obligation is satisfied; if late, fee is past due and remains owed
business_rules.exceptions: None stated; Freddie Mac may change fees and remedies at its discretion

source_reference.source_text: Freddie Mac separately monitors Servicing reporting, accounting reporting and Delinquency reporting and separately assesses noncompliance fees. Freddie Mac will send the Servicer a written notification of each violation. Fees are due to Freddie Mac no later than the fifth Business Day after the first accounting cutoff date following the missed reporting due date. The Servicer must remit the fee using the myOptigo® Servicer R emittance system described in Section 53.7.

examples: Accounting report due date missed on 2026-06-10; first accounting cutoff date after that is 2026-06-30; fee must be remitted no later than the 5th Business Day after 2026-06-30.
Servicer receives written notification of delinquency reporting violation; Servicer pays assessed fee through myOptigo by the 5th Business Day deadline.
Servicer remits the fee outside myOptigo; payment method violates remittance instruction and may require re-remittance.
Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
