evidence_quotes: “...must have its legal counsel available... including before... issuance of a transfer approval letter.”
“...must obtain a statement from Servicer counsel confirming it examined the UCC search...”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_SERVICER_VALIDATION_020_009 depends on counsel delivering a compliant statement; BR_SELLER_SERVICER_ELIGIBILITY_029_001 defines whether that counsel is acceptable to Freddie Mac (minimum experience). Ineligible counsel creates a major documentation/approval risk for the transfer.
dependencies.rationale: The transfer package requires a counsel statement (BR_020_009). Counsel eligibility (BR_029_001) is a foundational requirement for Freddie Mac acceptance of counsel work/product. Therefore, counsel must satisfy BR_029_001 for the BR_020_009 deliverable to be reliable and acceptable.
dependencies.impact: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_ELIGIBILITY_029_001 fails: (1) Freddie Mac may reject the counsel statement, (2) transfer approval may be withheld pending reissuance by qualified counsel, (3) transfer closing can be delayed or canceled.
examples: Servicer uses counsel with insufficient relevant closing experience; Freddie Mac rejects the UCC statement and requires a new statement from qualified counsel.
Qualified counsel issues the statement and also flags prior UCC filings needing Freddie Mac approval before submission.
evidence_quotes: “...attorney... must have at least two years of experience...”
“...must obtain a statement from Servicer counsel confirming...”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: Coinsurance approval (BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_026_001) necessarily depends on using acceptable title underwriters. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 prohibits use of title companies on the Restricted Vendor List; violating it blocks Freddie Mac acceptance regardless of coinsurance conditions.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_026_001 allows coinsurance only if each underwriter/policy meets requirements and Freddie Mac approves in writing. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 prohibits using a Title Company on the Multifamily Restricted Vendor List. Therefore, restricted-vendor screening is a prerequisite check before pursuing coinsurance approval and submitting coinsured policies for review.
dependencies.impact: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 fails: (1) coinsurance request/policies will be rejected, (2) closing/title workflows must be restructured with new underwriters, (3) rate lock/closing deadlines may be missed, (4) Freddie Mac may refuse purchase/assumption approval.
examples: Transaction proposes two underwriters for coinsurance; one is on the Restricted Vendor List. Even if Freddie Mac might otherwise approve coinsurance, the restricted vendor prohibition blocks acceptance until replaced.
Seller screens underwriters first, removes restricted vendor, then submits coinsured policies for Freddie Mac’s pre-rate-lock approval.
evidence_quotes: “If a Title Company appears on the Multifamily Restricted Vendor List, the Seller/Servicer may not use that Title Company...”
“Coinsurance may be allowed only if... Freddie Mac approves... in writing prior to Rate Lock.”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Coinsurance is a special-case structure, but still relies on compliant, conflict-free selection of title companies/underwriters. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 is a baseline vendor selection integrity rule that must be satisfied to avoid tainting the coinsurance approval process.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_EXCEPTION_026_001 requires Freddie Mac written approval prior to rate lock. Freddie Mac’s decision will be impacted if the title company/underwriter selection violates BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 (selection based on improper consideration). Therefore, vendor selection integrity is a prerequisite compliance gate before requesting coinsurance approval.
dependencies.impact: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 fails: (1) Freddie Mac may refuse the transaction/title structure, (2) potential fraud/anti-kickback concerns, (3) remediation may require vendor replacement and re-issuance of title work, (4) counterparty sanctions risk.
examples: Seller chooses a coinsurance underwriter because it offered something of value to staff; even if coinsurance is otherwise approvable, Freddie Mac may refuse acceptance due to prohibited selection basis.
Seller documents prudent institutional reasons for selecting both underwriters; coinsurance approval request proceeds with reduced compliance risk.
evidence_quotes: “...must select or accept any Title Company based solely on prudent institutional lender considerations...”
“Coinsurance may be allowed only if... Freddie Mac approves... in writing prior to Rate Lock.”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_004 mandates the Title Insurance Underwriter perform Funding Functions for covered transactions. That underwriter must be eligible for use; BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 blocks use of Restricted Vendor List title companies, making it a gating prerequisite.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_004 assigns core settlement/funding duties to the Title Insurance Underwriter (or qualifying affiliate). If that underwriter is on the Restricted Vendor List, BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 prohibits use—making the funding-function model non-executable until a compliant underwriter is selected.
dependencies.impact: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_003 fails: (1) settlement/funding structure violates Guide, (2) underwriting review/closing may be delayed, (3) Freddie Mac acceptance jeopardized due to prohibited vendor usage.
examples: Covered origination requires title underwriter to perform funding functions; chosen underwriter appears on Restricted Vendor List. Seller must replace underwriter and rebuild closing instructions.
Seller screens vendor list monthly and selects an eligible underwriter, enabling BR_026_004 settlement statement delivery and funding workflow.
evidence_quotes: “...may not use that Title Company until Freddie Mac notifies otherwise.”
“...Title Insurance Underwriter... must directly perform all Funding Functions...”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_004 operationalizes a specific funding model using the Title Insurance Underwriter; BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 ensures the underwriter selection is compliant and not based on prohibited consideration. It materially affects acceptability but has potential remediation (vendor change) without logical deadlock.
dependencies.rationale: Because BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_004 requires the title underwriter to perform core settlement functions, Freddie Mac will scrutinize the underwriter relationship. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 is a baseline compliance condition for selecting/accepting that underwriter. Therefore, vendor integrity is a prerequisite check to avoid invalidating the funding functions structure.
dependencies.impact: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROHIBITION_026_002 fails: (1) Freddie Mac may refuse/condition acceptance, (2) transaction may require vendor replacement and re-papering, (3) delays to closing and delivery.
examples: Seller selects underwriter to perform funding functions but selection is tied to improper benefits; Freddie Mac rejects and requires re-selection.
Seller documents prudent jurisdictional considerations (capacity, licensing, claims history) supporting compliant selection before implementing BR_026_004.
evidence_quotes: “...must not base the selection on receipt of anything of value...”
“...Title Insurance Underwriter... must directly perform all Funding Functions...”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Both rules are sanctions/eligibility screening controls applied to parties involved in origination/transactions. They are not strictly sequential, but together form a complete compliance gate for party eligibility (Exclusionary List + OFAC) and should be bundled in a unified screening workflow.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 requires use of the most current Exclusionary List and makes mortgages ineligible if a listed party played a covered role (subject to exceptions). BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_035_004 requires screening against current OFAC lists and retaining evidence in the file. In practice, compliance workflows should require both screenings to be completed and evidenced before eligibility/delivery decisions.
dependencies.impact: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 fails while OFAC is done: (1) mortgage can still be ineligible due to Freddie Mac’s Exclusionary List even without OFAC hits, (2) purchase/approval can be refused. If BR_035_004 fails while Exclusionary is done: (1) sanctions compliance finding, (2) file defects and cure requirements, (3) heightened regulatory risk.
examples: Borrower principals clear OFAC, but the mortgage broker is on Freddie Mac’s Exclusionary List in a covered role; mortgage becomes ineligible under BR_018_009 despite OFAC screening evidence.
Seller runs both checks in one vendor tool; system stores screenshots/date stamps to satisfy BR_035_004 while also verifying the Exclusionary List version date to satisfy BR_018_009.
evidence_quotes: “The Exclusionary List is updated at least monthly... must ensure it uses only the most current version.”
“...must verify that names... do not appear on the most current OFAC Lists... [and] maintain evidence of the screening...”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Both are fraud-prevention / restricted-party screening controls but applied to different populations (employees/contractors vs transaction parties). They should be linked in an enterprise restricted-party compliance program for consistent list governance and auditability.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_021_007 mandates screening employees/contractors against Restricted Party Lists at hire and annually. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 mandates using the current Exclusionary List to screen transaction roles and determine mortgage eligibility. While neither is a strict prerequisite for the other, they are complementary controls that should share list management, attestation, and audit workflows.
dependencies.impact: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_PROCESS_021_007 fails: (1) insider/third-party fraud risk increases, (2) compliance program weakness may undermine the reliability of transaction-level screening. If BR_018_009 fails: (1) ineligible mortgages may be originated/sold, (2) purchase refusals and repurchase exposure increase.
examples: Company updates its restricted-party screening vendor monthly; HR screens new hires (BR_021_007) while operations screens brokers/borrowers/transfer parties (BR_018_009) using consistent list currency controls.
Annual audit finds HR screenings are stale; compliance expands governance to ensure both HR and origination screening use current lists with the same evidence retention standards.
evidence_quotes: “...must screen... Employees and... Contractors against the Restricted Party Lists at the time of hire... and at least annually thereafter...”
“The Exclusionary List is updated at least monthly... must ensure it uses only the most current version.”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: The deductible/SIR constraint (BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_017_007) cannot be practically verified without the proof-of-insurance artifacts required by BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_017_008. This is an evidence dependency for compliance testing and audit.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_017_007 sets a maximum deductible/SIR rule for mortgagee’s E&O coverage and prohibits calculating it based on the actual limit in force. BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_017_008 requires submission of acceptable proof (ACORD 25, certificate, declarations, policy) within 30 days of obtaining/renewing. Those documents are the primary data sources used by Counterparty Risk & Compliance to validate deductible/SIR compliance.
dependencies.impact: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_017_008 fails: (1) Freddie Mac cannot validate compliance with BR_017_007, (2) Seller/Servicer may be treated as out of compliance, (3) corrective action requests or escalations may occur until documentation is provided.
examples: Seller renews E&O policy but does not submit declarations page; Freddie Mac cannot confirm deductible/SIR is within the BR_017_007 cap and flags noncompliance.
Seller submits ACORD 25 showing a $500,000 deductible. Compliance team calculates cap (higher of $250,000 or 10% of minimum required limit) and identifies breach under BR_017_007.
evidence_quotes: “Within 30 days... must submit acceptable proof of insurance...”
“...maximum deductible... must not exceed... $250,000 or... 10% of the minimum E&O limit...”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG co-requisite: both rules define independent eligibility gates for permitted Subordinate Financing; approval is materially compromised if either set of conditions is not met.
dependencies.rationale: 026_008 defines term/amount/remaining-term thresholds for permitted Subordinate Financing in an SBL Securitization. 026_009 separately defines combined leverage and cashflow thresholds (LTV and DCR). In practice, a Servicer’s approval workflow must satisfy BOTH sets to determine permissibility; they function as parallel mandatory checks for the same decision.
dependencies.impact: If 026_008 is not satisfied/checked, the Servicer may approve Subordinate Financing that is structurally ineligible (e.g., too short remaining term) even if LTV/DCR pass, leading to an impermissible transaction and potential Freddie Mac remedies.
examples: Loan has 2.5 years remaining term (fails 026_008) but combined LTV is 75% and DCR is 1.40x (passes 026_009). Approval would still be impermissible.
Proposed subordinate term is shorter than the remaining first-mortgage term (fails 026_008) even though LTV/DCR meet 026_009; the Servicer must deny or restructure.
evidence_quotes: Subordinate Financing is permitted only if (among other conditions) ... three years or more remaining in its loan term ...
combined loan amount ... must not produce an LTV Ratio exceeding 80%, and combined debt service must not produce a DCR below 1.25x

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG trigger relationship: whether Subordinate Financing is 'unauthorized' (and thus triggers immediate notice) is materially determined by failing the permissibility conditions in 026_008.
dependencies.rationale: 026_007 requires notification when the Servicer learns of 'unauthorized Subordinate Financing.' In operational compliance, 'unauthorized' is determined by comparing the financing against permission criteria (including term/amount constraints in 026_008). If those constraints are not met (and the lien is placed or imminent), the notification workflow must activate.
dependencies.impact: If 026_008 is not applied, the Servicer may misclassify an impermissible lien as authorized, delaying required notification and increasing Freddie Mac loss exposure and remedies risk.
examples: Servicer discovers a subordinate loan being recorded with a 20-year term while the SBL Mortgage has 26 years remaining (fails 'term at least remaining term' in 026_008). This should be treated as unauthorized and immediately reported under 026_007.
Borrower proposes a $500,000 subordinate loan (fails $750,000 minimum in 026_008) but indicates closing is next week; Servicer must treat as unauthorized/imminent and notify Freddie Mac.
evidence_quotes: If the Servicer learns that unauthorized Subordinate Financing has been, or is about to be, placed on the Property, the Servicer must immediately notify Freddie Mac in writing.
Subordinate Financing is permitted only if ... three years or more remaining ... amount is not less than $750,000 ... term is at least the remaining term ...

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG trigger relationship: 'unauthorized' Subordinate Financing determination depends on combined LTV/DCR tests; failure commonly defines impermissibility and drives escalation.
dependencies.rationale: 026_009 defines hard limits (combined LTV <= 80% and DCR >= 1.25x) for permissible Subordinate Financing. When the Servicer becomes aware of a lien that would violate these limits, it is operationally 'unauthorized' and the notification obligation in 026_007 becomes actionable.
dependencies.impact: If 026_009 is not evaluated, impermissible leverage/cashflow deterioration can go unreported, delaying Freddie Mac intervention and increasing credit risk and potential servicing remedies.
examples: Existing UPB $8.0M + prior subordinate $1.0M + proposed $2.0M = $11.0M combined; value $13.0M => 84.6% LTV (fails 026_009). If lien is about to close, Servicer must notify Freddie Mac immediately (026_007).
DCR drops to 1.18x post-subordinate financing; even if other thresholds pass, this is impermissible and should be escalated as unauthorized when discovered.
evidence_quotes: combined loan amount ... must not produce an LTV Ratio exceeding 80%, and combined debt service must not produce a DCR below 1.25x.
If the Servicer learns that unauthorized Subordinate Financing has been ... placed on the Property ... immediately notify Freddie Mac

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG prerequisite: Reserve Custodial Accounts must be maintained at an eligible depository; selecting/monitoring the depository is necessary to keep the account compliant 'at all times'.
dependencies.rationale: 033_004 requires establishing and maintaining Reserve Custodial Accounts. 029_003 defines when an FDIC-insured institution is an 'eligible depository' based on ratings and ongoing compliance. Account establishment/maintenance must therefore be done with an institution that satisfies 029_003 (or the custodial setup fails eligibility).
dependencies.impact: If 029_003 is not satisfied, Reserve Custodial Accounts may be placed at an ineligible bank, creating custodial noncompliance and potential remediation (moving accounts, retitling, operational disruption).
examples: Servicer opens a Reserve Custodial Account at an FDIC bank whose short-term rating falls below A-1/P-1/F1; the bank becomes ineligible under 029_003 and the account setup under 033_004 becomes noncompliant.
During annual vendor/depository review, ratings drop and Servicer must move the custodial account to another institution to maintain 029_003 eligibility.
evidence_quotes: eligible depository ... only if it satisfies at all times the minimum rating requirements ...
Servicer must establish and maintain at all times one or more Reserve Custodial Accounts

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL blocker: funds cannot be deposited into the 'applicable Custodial Accounts' unless those accounts exist and are properly structured/titled.
dependencies.rationale: 033_005 mandates deposit of principal/interest and Reserve funds into applicable Custodial Accounts by the first Business Day after receipt. 033_004 defines the required Reserve Custodial Accounts (separate, titled, trustee language, and correct Seller/Servicer number). Deposit compliance requires the accounts to be established correctly first.
dependencies.impact: If 033_004 fails, 033_005 cannot be executed compliantly (no compliant account to deposit into), leading to custodial exceptions, commingling risk, and potential servicing remedies.
examples: Reserve Custodial Account is incorrectly titled (missing 'as trustee for Freddie Mac'); Servicer cannot meet 033_005 deposit requirement into a compliant account until corrected.
Servicer uses a single combined custodial account for P&I and reserves (prohibited by 033_004); deposits under 033_005 become structurally noncompliant.
evidence_quotes: must establish and maintain ... Reserve Custodial Accounts that are separate ...
All principal and interest payments and all Reserve funds must be deposited into the applicable Custodial Accounts no later than the first Business Day after receipt

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG sequencing: timely remittance requires timely internal deposit/availability of funds; late custodial deposit materially jeopardizes remittance timing compliance.
dependencies.rationale: 033_005 governs how quickly borrower payments/reserves enter custodial accounts (by next business day). 027_006 governs when funds must be remitted to Freddie Mac (e.g., by the 2nd Business Day of the following month or within one day after receipt in certain cases). Operationally, the Servicer typically remits from custodial accounts; therefore the deposit step must happen first to reliably meet remittance deadlines.
dependencies.impact: If 033_005 fails (late deposit), the Servicer risks missing 027_006 remittance windows, triggering late remittance exposure and potential Freddie Mac remedies/fees.
examples: Payment received on the 30th is not deposited into custodial until the 3rd of next month; Servicer then struggles to remit by the 2nd Business Day as required by 027_006.
Lockbox receives payment but deposits late into collection clearing; Servicer cannot timely sweep into custodial and then cannot remit per 027_006 timing.
evidence_quotes: must be deposited ... no later than the first Business Day after receipt
must remit to Freddie Mac not later than the second Business Day of the month following the month of receipt

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG compliance prerequisite: 027_006 requires remittance timing; 039_002 defines the mandated remittance channel for many monthly collections, which must be used to execute remittances compliantly.
dependencies.rationale: 027_006 imposes remittance deadlines for regular monthly payments. 039_002 requires that specified collections (including monthly principal and interest) be remitted via the myOptigo Servicer Remittance system. To satisfy 027_006 in a compliant manner for those payment types, the Servicer must use the channel in 039_002.
dependencies.impact: If 039_002 fails (wrong channel), the remittance may be rejected, misapplied, or credited late, causing noncompliance with 027_006 deadlines and increasing fee/escalation risk.
examples: Servicer wires monthly P&I instead of using myOptigo; funds are posted late or misrouted, causing remittance timing breach under 027_006.
Servicer remits a curtailment via wire though 039_002 requires myOptigo; Freddie Mac deems remittance noncompliant even if sent near the deadline.
evidence_quotes: must remit ... via Freddie Mac’s myOptigo Servicer Remittance system, including monthly principal and interest
Servicer must remit to Freddie Mac not later than the second Business Day ...

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG validation: 039_003 determines whether remittances complied with interim/monthly requirements; it necessarily evaluates the timing obligations defined in remittance process rules like 027_006.
dependencies.rationale: 039_003 states Freddie Mac uses the effective date of receipt to determine whether remittance was made in compliance with interim and monthly remittance requirements and may assess an interest reimbursement fee for failure. 027_006 defines concrete monthly remittance timing; 039_003 is the enforcement/assessment layer that validates 027_006 compliance based on receipt timing.
dependencies.impact: If 027_006 is not met, 039_003 indicates the remittance will be deemed noncompliant and may trigger an interest reimbursement fee and escalation.
examples: Servicer remits on the 4th Business Day of the following month; Freddie Mac uses receipt date and assesses interest reimbursement fee per 039_003.
Payment received after month-end is remitted two business days after receipt (instead of within one); 039_003 fee logic is triggered.
evidence_quotes: uses the effective date of receipt to determine whether the Servicer’s remittance was made in compliance with interim and monthly remittance requirements.
must remit ... not later than the second Business Day ...

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE prerequisite: annual certification of Reserve collections/disbursements depends on having executed the operational deposit controls that govern Reserve handling.
dependencies.rationale: 035_007 requires an annual certification that Reserve collections and disbursements were made in accordance with applicable Reserve agreements. 033_005 is a core operational control for Reserve fund handling (timely deposit to custodial accounts). While certification also depends on disbursement rules not included here, 033_005 is a foundational prerequisite for accurate, compliant Reserve accounting and the ability to certify.
dependencies.impact: If 033_005 fails, the Servicer’s Reserve handling may be noncompliant, making 035_007 certification inaccurate or requiring discrepancy explanations and remediation steps.
examples: Reserve payments are routinely held in a clearing account for multiple days; Servicer cannot confidently certify Reserve collections complied with Reserve agreements.
During fiscal-year review, audit finds late Reserve deposits; Servicer must disclose discrepancy in Form 1110M submission.
evidence_quotes: All principal and interest payments and all Reserve funds must be deposited ... no later than the first Business Day after receipt
must certify ... all Reserve collections and disbursements were made in accordance with the applicable Reserve agreements

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE prerequisite: ability to support/defend annual Reserve certifications depends on retaining underlying mortgage records (bank statements, canceled checks) that evidence collections/disbursements.
dependencies.rationale: 035_007 requires annual certification and explanation of discrepancies, which commonly relies on retained records supporting Reserve activity. 039_001 prohibits surrender/destruction of canceled checks, bank statements, or other mortgage records without Freddie Mac approval. Record retention under 039_001 enables evidentiary support for the 035_007 certification process.
dependencies.impact: If 039_001 fails (records destroyed without approval), the Servicer may be unable to evidence Reserve compliance during certification or audit, increasing compliance findings and remediation workload.
examples: Servicer destroys custodial bank statements without Freddie Mac approval; cannot substantiate Reserve disbursements when completing Form 1110M.
During audit, Freddie Mac requests canceled checks supporting Reserve disbursements; Servicer cannot produce them due to improper destruction.
evidence_quotes: must not surrender or destroy canceled checks, bank statements, or other Mortgage records/accounts without Freddie Mac’s prior written approval.
must certify ... and explain ... discrepancies

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL blocker: cost estimation/reporting requires identification and assessment of the underlying issues/components; without the scope assessment, required cost estimates cannot be produced reliably.
dependencies.rationale: 032_003 defines the required scope of physical condition evaluation and the specific risk/condition items to be assessed (deferred maintenance, life safety hazards, environmental/moisture issues, etc.). 032_004 requires estimating and reporting remediation costs for those same issue categories. The estimation obligation depends on the identification and evaluation outputs from 032_003.
dependencies.impact: If 032_003 is incomplete, 032_004 outputs will be incomplete/incorrect, leading to deficient inspections, improper risk rating, and missed borrower/Freddie Mac notifications.
examples: Inspector fails to assess moisture/mold issues (032_003 gap); Servicer cannot provide required remediation cost estimates for moisture/mold (032_004).
Deferred maintenance not documented in the assessment; Servicer cannot produce aggregate remediation estimate or source for those repairs.
evidence_quotes: must assess the current condition and trend of all major components and must include specified risk/condition items ... deferred maintenance ... life safety hazards ... environmental and moisture/mold issues
must estimate and report remediation costs for deferred maintenance, life safety hazards, environmental issues, and moisture/mold issues

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL mathematical trigger: the borrower-notice obligation includes a remediation-cost threshold condition that cannot be evaluated without the cost estimate calculation.
dependencies.rationale: 032_005 is triggered by specified issues, including '(a) estimated sum for remediation of all Deferred Maintenance items > $10,000'. 032_004 is the rule that requires estimating and reporting remediation costs. Therefore, 032_004 is a mandatory prerequisite to determine whether the threshold is met and to populate notice content accurately.
dependencies.impact: If 032_004 fails, the Servicer may miss the 30-day borrower notification requirement or send an incomplete notice, resulting in servicing noncompliance and increased life-safety/legal risk.
examples: Inspection finds deferred maintenance but Servicer does not estimate costs; the system cannot determine whether the total exceeds $10,000, so required corrective-action notice is missed.
Servicer sends notice listing hazards but omits remediation-cost basis and fails to capture that deferred maintenance total is $25,000, violating trigger logic.
evidence_quotes: must estimate and report remediation costs ...
issues triggering this requirement include: (a) estimated sum for remediation of all Deferred Maintenance items > $10,000

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG trigger relationship: borrower notice depends on identification of life-safety/deferred maintenance issues during the inspection assessment.
dependencies.rationale: 032_005 requires borrower notification when specified property-related issues occur after an inspection and requires detailing Imminent/Potential Life Safety Hazards. 032_003 defines the assessment scope that identifies these hazards and deferred maintenance. Without properly performing 032_003, the Servicer cannot reliably determine whether 032_005 is triggered or what must be included in the notice.
dependencies.impact: If 032_003 is deficient, the Servicer may fail to send required notice within 30 days, omit required hazard details, or fail to confirm borrower receipt, increasing safety and compliance exposure.
examples: Inspector does not document life safety hazards; Servicer does not notify borrower, later discovering fire protection system issues that should have triggered 032_005.
Assessment misses prior deferred maintenance status; Servicer fails to include all hazard items in the corrective-action notice.
evidence_quotes: must include specified risk/condition items ... life safety hazards ...
notice must detail all Imminent Life Safety Hazards and Potential Life Safety Hazards

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG trigger relationship: Freddie Mac notification is driven by whether specific issues are present, which is determined by the AIF/inspection assessment scope.
dependencies.rationale: 032_006 requires notifying Freddie Mac when specified issues occur and have not been previously reported, after submitting the AIF assessment into PRS. 032_003 defines the required assessment scope that surfaces those issues. The decision to send the email and the content depend on completing the 032_003 assessment properly.
dependencies.impact: If 032_003 fails, the Servicer may not identify reportable issues and may miss the 10 Business Day notification deadline, leading to surveillance reporting noncompliance.
examples: Assessment fails to capture environmental issue; Servicer does not notify MF_Surveillance within 10 Business Days after PRS submission as required.
Property manager concerns are omitted from assessment; Freddie Mac is not alerted to deteriorating conditions.
evidence_quotes: must assess ... and must include specified risk/condition items ...
must notify Freddie Mac by email promptly and no later than 10 Business Days after submitting the AIF assessment into PRS if specified property-related issues occur

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG trigger relationship: reportable issues and severity often rely on remediation-cost estimation, which supports determining whether issues meet reportable thresholds and communicating meaningful surveillance details.
dependencies.rationale: 032_006 is triggered by specified issues occurring after AIF submission; several of these issue categories are the same ones for which 032_004 mandates cost estimates (deferred maintenance, life safety, environmental, moisture/mold). Practically, Freddie Mac surveillance notification requires accurate identification and characterization, which is materially supported by the 032_004 remediation cost estimation outputs.
dependencies.impact: If 032_004 fails, Freddie Mac may receive incomplete surveillance reporting (missing cost/severity context), leading to follow-up requests, processing delays, and potential noncompliance findings.
examples: Servicer notifies Freddie Mac of deferred maintenance but cannot provide remediation cost magnitude because estimates were not produced; Freddie Mac issues an information request and notes file deficiency.
Moisture/mold issue is identified but no estimate/source is documented; surveillance cannot triage risk promptly.
evidence_quotes: must estimate and report remediation costs for deferred maintenance, life safety hazards, environmental issues, and moisture/mold issues.
must notify Freddie Mac ... if specified property-related issues occur

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE co-requisite: both rules govern photo requirements for the same inspection submission package; compliance requires meeting formatting/captioning AND quantity/justification expectations.
dependencies.rationale: 032_001 imposes formatting and caption requirements for each photograph. 032_002 imposes quantity expectations (20–25 photos) and requires justification if fewer than 20 are provided. These requirements operate together for a compliant inspection submission; while one does not strictly block the other, they are jointly required for a complete package.
dependencies.impact: If 032_001 fails, photo artifacts may be rejected as noncompliant even if quantity/justification is met; if 032_002 fails, submission can be deemed deficient despite properly captioned/compressed photos.
examples: Servicer submits 25 photos but without captions (violates 032_001); submission is noncompliant even though 032_002 is met.
Servicer submits 15 well-captioned photos but provides no justification comment (violates 032_002); submission is noncompliant despite meeting 032_001.
evidence_quotes: must compress photographs ... Each photograph must include an identifying caption.
should submit 20 to 25 photographs ... If fewer than 20 photographs are submitted, the Servicer must provide a justification

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE prerequisite: 027_009 requires a physical inspection and updated AIF; 032_003 defines the required scope of the physical-condition evaluation that populates the AIF assessment.
dependencies.rationale: 027_009 mandates conducting a physical inspection and delivering an updated Annual Inspection Form (AIF) for certain Transfer of Ownership scenarios. 032_003 defines what must be assessed and included when evaluating a property’s physical condition—effectively governing the inspection content needed to produce a compliant updated AIF assessment.
dependencies.impact: If 032_003 is not met, the 'updated AIF' produced to satisfy 027_009 may be incomplete, leading to Freddie Mac requesting resubmission or delaying/denying the transfer request.
examples: Servicer performs a site visit but omits required assessment items (e.g., deferred maintenance trend); Freddie Mac rejects the updated AIF and delays transfer review.
Inspection notes repairs but fails to include property manager concerns required by 032_003; updated AIF is deemed deficient.
evidence_quotes: must conduct a physical inspection of the Property and deliver an updated AIF to Freddie Mac.
must assess the current condition and trend of all major components and must include specified risk/condition items

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG branching prerequisite: 027_009 applies only if neither a property condition report nor a physical risk report is required; 027_010 defines when those reports ARE required, so it must be evaluated first to select the correct branch.
dependencies.rationale: 027_009 begins with 'if neither a property condition report nor a physical risk report is required'. 027_010 defines the triggers that require delivering a property condition report or a Form 1108 Physical Risk Report for Transfers of Ownership. Therefore, the workflow must first apply 027_010 to decide whether 027_009 is even applicable.
dependencies.impact: If 027_010 is skipped, the Servicer may incorrectly rely on an updated AIF inspection (027_009) when a property condition report/1108 is required, resulting in an incomplete transfer package and processing delays/denial.
examples: Property built 20 years ago and buyer proposes significant capital improvements (triggers 027_010); Servicer incorrectly submits only an updated AIF per 027_009 and transfer request is deemed deficient.
Most recent AIF noted significant deferred maintenance (triggers 027_010); Servicer must submit PCR/1108 rather than only updated AIF.
evidence_quotes: if neither a property condition report nor a physical risk report is required ... then ... deliver an updated AIF

must deliver either (i) a property condition report ... or (ii) a Form 1108 Physical Risk Report ... if the Property was built more than 15 years previously ... or ... significant deferred maintenance ... or ... significant capital improvements

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG prerequisite: 038_002 requires maintaining the engineer’s certification 'when required'; 038_001 defines when it is required and who qualifies.
dependencies.rationale: 038_001 sets the conditions under which a qualified engineer certification must be obtained. 038_002 mandates that the engineer’s certification be maintained in the Mortgage File when required. Therefore, determining and obtaining the certification per 038_001 is necessary before 038_002 can be satisfied for those repairs.
dependencies.impact: If 038_001 is not complied with, 038_002 documentation will be incomplete, exposing the Servicer to repair-agreement compliance findings and potential Freddie Mac-directed remediation.
examples: Structural repair completed but no engineer certification obtained (violates 038_001); file cannot include required cert under 038_002.
Servicer uses an unqualified engineer (fails 038_001 qualifications); certification is invalid and 038_002 file support is defective.
evidence_quotes: must cause a qualified engineer to certify repairs were completed ... when ... structural ... or ... major building systems
must maintain supporting documentation ... and (iv) the engineer’s certification when required

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE prerequisite: maintaining repair documentation over time depends on record-retention prohibitions that prevent premature destruction without approval.
dependencies.rationale: 038_002 requires maintaining supporting repair documentation (photos, lists, dates, engineer cert) in the Mortgage File. 039_001 prohibits surrender/destruction of mortgage records without Freddie Mac approval. Record retention under 039_001 supports the ongoing ability to meet the 'maintain' requirement in 038_002.
dependencies.impact: If 039_001 is violated, repair documentation may be missing during audits or asset management reviews, triggering file deficiency findings and remediation requirements.
examples: Servicer destroys repair completion photos as part of routine data cleanup; later cannot demonstrate completed repairs per 038_002.
Servicer purges engineer certifications without approval; repair file is incomplete and fails Freddie Mac review.
evidence_quotes: must maintain supporting documentation in the Mortgage File including ... photographs ... engineer’s certification when required
must not surrender or destroy ... other Mortgage records/accounts without Freddie Mac’s prior written approval

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE sequencing: forwarding funds/correspondence after a transfer becomes effective presupposes a transfer-of-servicing event initiated via transfer submission and processing.
dependencies.rationale: 033_006 governs remitting the transfer processing fee when submitting transfer forms. 037_008 governs handling funds/correspondence received after the transfer becomes effective. While 037_008 is triggered by effectiveness (which may involve other steps not listed), 033_006 is part of the upstream transfer workflow and is operationally sequenced before post-effective-date handling obligations apply.
dependencies.impact: If 033_006 is not done, transfer processing may be delayed, extending the period the transferor remains responsible and increasing risk of misdirected funds/correspondence handling under 037_008.
examples: Transferor submits Form 981M without wiring the $1,000 fee; transfer approval is delayed and transferor continues receiving payments, complicating 037_008 handoff timing.
Fee wire missing reference details; Freddie Mac cannot process transfer timely, increasing risk of post-effective-date confusion and late forwarding.
evidence_quotes: When submitting Form 981M ... the transferor must remit ... $1,000 transfer processing fee
After a transfer of servicing becomes effective ... funds ... received ... must be delivered to the transferee within one day

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL explicit prerequisite: 033_003 requires the Partnership Agreement be provided as an exhibit to Form 1134; without Form 1134 (033_002), 033_003 cannot be satisfied as stated.
dependencies.rationale: 033_003 states the Seller must provide a fully executed EPSH Partnership Agreement 'as an exhibit to Form 1134'. 033_002 requires Form 1134 be executed by the Borrower and provided with the underwriting package to qualify for EPSH benefits. Therefore, completion/submission of Form 1134 is a necessary prerequisite to attaching the exhibit required by 033_003.
dependencies.impact: If 033_002 fails (Form 1134 not executed/submitted), 033_003 cannot be complied with as written and EPSH benefits cannot be applied.
examples: Seller uploads the Partnership Agreement but never uploads executed Form 1134; EPSH benefits are denied because the agreement is not properly exhibited to Form 1134.
Borrower signs Form 1134 after underwriting submission; the package is incomplete at submission, delaying EPSH benefit application.
evidence_quotes: Form 1134 ... must be executed by the Borrower, and must be provided with the full underwriting package.
Seller must provide a fully executed EPSH Partnership Agreement ... as an exhibit to Form 1134.

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG conditional applicability: Qualifying Manager requirements apply specifically in TIC structures (via a TIC Agreement requiring a manager), which is governed by the borrower’s TIC structure eligibility context.
dependencies.rationale: 003_009 establishes when a tenancy-in-common (TIC) borrower structure is eligible (<=10 owners; each owner SPE). 006_004 applies 'where a TIC Agreement requires a property manager' and defines Qualifying Manager criteria. In workflow terms, once a deal is identified as TIC (and proceeds under TIC eligibility), the Servicer/Seller must evaluate the manager qualification gate if the TIC agreement requires a manager.
dependencies.impact: If 003_009 TIC context is not correctly identified/validated, 006_004 may be missed, leading to an unqualified manager under a TIC arrangement and resulting in required replacement or noncompliance.
examples: Borrower is a TIC with 8 SPE owners (passes 003_009) and the TIC agreement requires a manager; the proposed manager lacks 5 years relevant experience (fails 006_004) and must be replaced.
Team fails to flag the borrower as TIC; manager diligence per 006_004 is skipped and later found deficient during Freddie Mac review.
evidence_quotes: A tenancy-in-common (TIC) may be an eligible Borrower only if ...
Where a TIC Agreement requires a property manager, the Manager must be a "Qualifying Manager" meeting all stated criteria

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG prerequisite: 040_007 requires the Mortgage 'is not in default'; determining default status relies on the Mortgage Default/Defaulted Mortgage definition in 002_002 during the relevant period.
dependencies.rationale: 040_007 conditions authorization of a 'critical replacement' disbursement on the Mortgage not being in default. 002_002 defines when a Mortgage Default occurs and when the mortgage is classified as a Defaulted Mortgage. The disbursement-authorization logic depends on the correct default classification.
dependencies.impact: If 002_002 is misapplied, the Servicer may improperly authorize disbursements while the loan is in default (servicing breach) or improperly deny disbursements when the loan is not in default (unnecessary operational disruption).
examples: Borrower event triggers acceleration rights under loan documents (default per 002_002); Servicer must not authorize critical replacement disbursement under 040_007 without Freddie Mac approval.
Borrower is within a cure period (not yet default per 002_002); Servicer incorrectly treats as defaulted and blocks an otherwise permissible critical replacement.
evidence_quotes: "Mortgage Default" occurs upon any event that entitles Freddie Mac to accelerate indebtedness ... (subject to any applicable grace or cure period).
Property secures a Mortgage ... the Mortgage is not in default

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG prerequisite: delivering a custodial account letter agreement for a funded Replacement Reserve presumes the existence of a properly established Reserve Custodial Account structure.
dependencies.rationale: 040_006 requires delivering an executed custodial account letter agreement within 10 days following funding of a Replacement Reserve. 033_004 requires establishing and maintaining reserve custodial accounts with specific separation and titling. The letter agreement requirement is tied to the custodial account arrangement; the account setup is a logical prerequisite to producing the correct executed letter agreement.
dependencies.impact: If 033_004 is noncompliant (wrong account structure/titling), the letter agreement under 040_006 may be incorrect or rejected, causing file deficiencies and potential remediation.
examples: Replacement Reserve is funded, but the reserve account is not titled 'as trustee for Freddie Mac' as required by 033_004; executed custodial letter agreement is inconsistent and must be re-executed.
Servicer uses a combined custodial account; cannot produce a correct Form 1058/1060 custodial agreement for a distinct Replacement Reserve custodial account.
evidence_quotes: must establish and maintain ... Reserve Custodial Accounts ... titled exactly as “(Name of Depositor/Servicer), as trustee for Freddie Mac”
Within 10 days following funding of a Replacement Reserve, the Servicer must deliver ... an original executed custodial account letter agreement

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG prerequisite: the Replacement Reserve custodial account (held by an institution) must be at an eligible depository, and documentation delivered should reflect an eligible custodial institution.
dependencies.rationale: 040_006 requires delivery of a custodial account letter agreement (Form 1058/1060) for a funded Replacement Reserve custodial account. 029_003 defines eligibility criteria for FDIC-insured depositories used for custodial accounts. The custodial account documentation presumes an eligible depository selection and ongoing eligibility monitoring.
dependencies.impact: If 029_003 fails (ineligible bank), 040_006 documentation may need to be redone after moving the account, causing compliance deficiencies and operational delays.
examples: Replacement Reserve account opened at a bank that lacks required ratings; Servicer submits Form 1058 but later must move the account and resubmit executed documentation.
Depository’s ratings fall below minimums after funding; Servicer must remediate to maintain eligibility and update custody arrangements.
evidence_quotes: eligible depository ... only if it satisfies at all times the minimum rating requirements ...
Within 10 days following funding ... deliver ... executed custodial account letter agreement

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE sequencing: authorization of Replacement Reserve disbursements is operationally dependent on having the custodial account documentation in place for a funded reserve, though disbursement eligibility also depends on other criteria.
dependencies.rationale: 040_006 requires delivering executed custodial account letter agreements shortly after funding of a Replacement Reserve. 040_007 governs when the Servicer may authorize a Replacement Reserve disbursement for a critical replacement. In practice, disbursement controls and approvals are typically executed after custodial account setup/documentation is completed, to ensure controlled custody and audit trail.
dependencies.impact: If 040_006 is missed, disbursements may proceed with deficient custodial documentation, increasing audit/compliance findings and potential Freddie Mac-directed remediation.
examples: Servicer authorizes a critical replacement disbursement but failed to send Form 1060 within 10 days of funding; later audit cites custodial documentation deficiency.
Replacement Reserve funded at third-party institution; Form 1058 not executed/delivered; disbursement controls are questioned and payments paused pending documentation.
evidence_quotes: Within 10 days following funding ... deliver ... executed custodial account letter agreement
Servicer may only authorize a Replacement Reserve disbursement ... when all listed conditions are met

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE prerequisite: monthly fee remittance from collected payments depends on timely collection handling and deposit controls.
dependencies.rationale: 034_002 requires calculating and remitting a credit facility fee each month 'from payments collected under the reimbursement and security agreement.' 033_005 governs timely deposit of payments and Reserve funds after receipt. While the exact fee source is not limited to custodial accounts here, deposit controls are foundational to ensuring collected funds are available and tracked for monthly fee remittance.
dependencies.impact: If 033_005 fails, collected funds may be delayed/misapplied, potentially causing late/incorrect monthly fee remittance and reconciliation issues.
examples: Servicer receives reimbursement payments but does not deposit timely; monthly credit facility fee remittance is delayed.
Lockbox deposits late into clearing; Servicer cannot timely process the collected-payment base for the fee calculation/remittance.
evidence_quotes: must remit ... each month from payments collected under the reimbursement and security agreement
must be deposited ... no later than the first Business Day after receipt

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE prerequisite: liquidity facility fee remittance from collected payments depends on timely payment handling and deposit controls.
dependencies.rationale: 034_003 requires calculating and remitting a liquidity facility fee monthly from collected payments under the reimbursement and security agreement. 033_005 ensures prompt deposit/handling of received funds, supporting availability and traceability for monthly remittance processing.
dependencies.impact: If 033_005 fails, remittance execution and reconciliation of monthly liquidity facility fee may be delayed or incorrect, increasing compliance and operational risk.
examples: Variable-rate mortgage liquidity fee due monthly; Servicer’s late deposit practices delay monthly remittance.
Payment received via agent is not swept into appropriate accounts promptly, causing processing backlog and late fee remittance.
evidence_quotes: will remit the liquidity facility fee ... each month from payments collected under the reimbursement and security agreement.
must be deposited ... no later than the first Business Day after receipt

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE policy alignment: both rules restrict Material Vendor usage in sensitive servicing functions; together they form a combined control framework over outsourcing and delegation risk.
dependencies.rationale: 026_010 prohibits using a Material Vendor for credit/performance risk decisions without Freddie Mac approval. 040_008 prohibits Material Vendors from performing accounting/reporting functions that expose borrower financial/PII or allow them to manage borrower relationships/communicate with Freddie. These rules are complementary: outsourcing governance must satisfy both sets of prohibitions depending on function type, and compliance navigation benefits from treating them as a linked control family.
dependencies.impact: If 026_010 is ignored while applying 040_008, the Servicer might still improperly delegate credit-risk decisions to a vendor. Conversely, focusing only on 026_010 could leave the Servicer exposed to PII/borrower-relationship delegation violations under 040_008.
examples: Vendor is barred from borrower communications and handling financial info (040_008), but Servicer also must ensure vendor is not monitoring/deciding actions for Risk Rating >6 loans without approval (026_010).
Servicer uses one vendor for analytics and another for accounting; each engagement must be screened against both 026_010 and 040_008 based on functions performed.
evidence_quotes: may not use a Material Vendor to make decisions related to a Mortgage’s performance or credit risk unless approved by Freddie Mac.

may not use a Material Vendor to perform prohibited functions, including reviewing Borrower/Guarantor financial information or protected personal information ... communicating with Freddie Mac on behalf of the Servicer

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE validation: 039_003 addresses compliance determination based on effective receipt date for remittances; REO proceeds remittance timing in 031_007 is a remittance requirement subject to receipt/crediting timing concepts.
dependencies.rationale: 031_007 requires REO proceeds be remitted by wire transfer immediately after settlement, with crediting rules for after-4 p.m. wires. 039_003 states Freddie Mac uses effective date of receipt to determine compliance with remittance requirements and may assess an interest reimbursement fee for failures. The effective-date concept is used to validate compliance with 031_007 timing/crediting expectations.
dependencies.impact: If 031_007 timing is missed, Freddie Mac can deem remittance noncompliant based on receipt crediting rules, potentially leading to fees or escalation pathways referenced in 039_003.
examples: Escrow agent wires proceeds the day after settlement; Freddie Mac uses receipt/crediting date to evaluate timeliness and may treat as noncompliant.
Wire sent after 4 p.m. ET is credited next business day; compliance assessment relies on effective receipt/crediting convention.
evidence_quotes: must remit ... immediately after settlement. Wire transfers received after 4 p.m. Eastern Time are credited on the next Business Day.
uses the effective date of receipt to determine whether the Servicer’s remittance was made in compliance ...

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE validation: 039_003’s receipt-date compliance construct supports evaluating timeliness of repurchase proceeds remittances governed by 031_008.
dependencies.rationale: 031_008 sets a 10 Business Day deadline after Freddie Mac’s letter for remitting REO repurchase proceeds. 039_003 provides Freddie Mac’s mechanism for determining remittance compliance based on effective receipt date and the consequence of an interest reimbursement fee. The receipt-date rule is used to validate whether the 10-day remittance was timely received.
dependencies.impact: If 031_008 is violated, Freddie Mac may treat remittance as noncompliant and assess fees/escalate under the framework described in 039_003.
examples: Servicer initiates wire on day 10 but it is received day 11 due to cutoff; Freddie Mac evaluates based on effective receipt date and may deem late.
Servicer fails to obtain correct wire instructions promptly; remittance arrives after deadline and triggers fee/escalation logic.
evidence_quotes: within 10 Business Days ... the Servicer must remit ... proceeds by wire transfer.
uses the effective date of receipt to determine whether the Servicer’s remittance was made in compliance ...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: BR_HAZARD_INSURANCE_POLICY_CONSTRAINT_032_008 only applies if earthquake insurance is required, which is determined by BR_HAZARD_INSURANCE_POLICY_ELIGIBILITY_032_007.
dependencies.rationale: BR_HAZARD_INSURANCE_POLICY_ELIGIBILITY_032_007 establishes whether Freddie Mac requires earthquake insurance (based on SEL-475 thresholds and building stability concern). Only after that requirement is triggered can the policy coverage-minimum rule (100% of insurable value plus BI/RV coverage) be evaluated.
dependencies.impact: If eligibility logic is not applied correctly, the workflow may (a) fail to enforce the 100% IV minimum when earthquake insurance is required (purchase ineligibility/QC findings), or (b) impose unnecessary coverage requirements when insurance is not required (borrower cost, closing delays).
examples: Property has SEL-475 = 30% and no building stability concern. BR_032_007 triggers 'insurance required', so BR_032_008 must be applied to verify coverage is at least 100% of IV.
Property has SEL-475 = 15% and no stability concern. BR_032_007 says insurance not required; BR_032_008 should not be evaluated as a purchase condition.
evidence_quotes: “depend on SEL-475 and whether a building stability concern exists… earthquake insurance is not required… insurance is required”
“When Freddie Mac requires earthquake insurance… coverage amount must be at least 100% of… IV”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: Earthquake deductible caps are only relevant if the loan file is evaluating an earthquake policy requirement triggered by BR_032_007.
dependencies.rationale: BR_032_009 governs whether a presented earthquake policy meets deductible limits. That evaluation is only actionable when BR_032_007 indicates earthquake coverage is required for purchase eligibility (or when the lender chooses to include it in response to BR_032_007 triggers).
dependencies.impact: If BR_032_007 is not applied first, deductible compliance may be checked unnecessarily or missed entirely, causing either avoidable policy rework or purchase ineligibility due to noncompliant deductibles.
examples: SEL-475 = 25% (no stability concern) triggers required earthquake insurance; deductible must then be checked against BR_032_009 caps.
SEL-475 = 10% (no stability concern) means no earthquake insurance required; deductible caps should not gate loan eligibility.
evidence_quotes: “If SEL-475… insurance is required”
“Earthquake deductible maximums apply…”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: When earthquake insurance is required, both minimum coverage (BR_032_008) and deductible limits (BR_032_009) must be satisfied for a compliant policy; failing either generally requires policy correction.
dependencies.rationale: Coverage adequacy and deductible permissibility are separate but jointly necessary conditions of an acceptable earthquake policy. In an automated compliance checklist, both checks should be executed as a combined policy-compliance set.
dependencies.impact: If BR_032_008 is satisfied but BR_032_009 fails (or vice versa), the earthquake insurance requirement is not met, leading to underwriting/closing conditions and potential purchase ineligibility.
examples: Policy limit meets 100% IV (passes BR_032_008) but deductible is 10% of TIV (fails BR_032_009) → policy must be amended.
Deductible within caps (passes BR_032_009) but coverage is only 80% of IV (fails BR_032_008) → policy deficient.
evidence_quotes: “coverage amount must be at least 100%… IV”
“Earthquake deductible maximums apply…”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_037_006 requires the State Windpool policy to “meet deductible requirements,” which includes validating deductible caps such as BR_037_001 where applicable.
dependencies.rationale: When coverage is only available through a State Windpool, BR_037_006 imposes minimum limit and deductible compliance. BR_037_001 is one of the deductible cap rules needed to determine if the submitted State Windpool policy is acceptable (for dollar-amount deductibles under Specific Limits).
dependencies.impact: If BR_037_001 is not applied, a State Windpool policy could be accepted with an excessive deductible, creating a noncompliant insurance file and potential Freddie Mac remedies/conditions.
examples: IV = $9M, wind/hail deductible is $75,000 under specific limits → fails BR_037_001; therefore fails BR_037_006 ‘meet deductible requirements’.
IV = $12M, deductible is $90,000 → passes BR_037_001 and supports BR_037_006 compliance (subject to other terms).
evidence_quotes: “State Windpool… must… meet deductible requirements.”
“Wind/Hail… maximum deductible… if IV… < $10 million… $50,000… if IV… ≥ $10 million… $100,000.”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_037_006 requires compliance with deductible requirements, and BR_037_002 defines the allowable percentage deductible structure (5% of TIV with stated minimum cap) for specific limits.
dependencies.rationale: To confirm a State Windpool policy meets Freddie Mac deductible requirements, the system must evaluate percentage-based deductibles using BR_037_002 when that deductible structure is used.
dependencies.impact: If BR_037_002 is skipped, a policy could be accepted with a noncompliant stated minimum (e.g., minimum > $100,000), triggering post-review exceptions and policy rework.
examples: State Windpool policy uses 5% deductible but sets a stated minimum of $150,000 → fails BR_037_002, therefore fails BR_037_006.
Deductible is 5% of TIV with stated minimum $75,000 → passes BR_037_002 (then evaluate other BR_037_006 conditions like 100% TIV coverage).
evidence_quotes: “State Windpool… must… meet deductible requirements.”
“deductible… expressed as a percentage… must be five percent (5%)… with a stated minimum not to exceed $100,000.”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Only applicable where Wind/Hail BI/RV coverage exists; then it becomes part of the deductible requirements referenced by BR_037_006.
dependencies.rationale: BR_037_006 broadly requires meeting deductible requirements. If the State Windpool policy includes business income/rental value coverage, BR_037_003 supplies the deductible cap for that component and should be evaluated to complete compliance.
dependencies.impact: If not evaluated, the file may pass initial review but fail later audit/QC if BI/RV deductible exceeds 15 days or $100,000, requiring endorsement changes.
examples: Policy includes BI/RV with a 30-day deductible → fails BR_037_003; State Windpool policy does not meet BR_037_006 deductible requirements.
Policy includes BI/RV with 10-day deductible → passes BR_037_003; continue evaluating BR_037_006.
evidence_quotes: “meet deductible requirements”
“Wind/Hail business income/rental value coverage… maximum deductible… 15 days… or $100,000”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: For Named Storm coverage under Blanket Limits, both (a) blanket limit adequacy/reinstatement (BR_037_004) and (b) deductible caps (BR_037_005) must be satisfied for an acceptable Named Storm structure.
dependencies.rationale: These rules jointly define Named Storm acceptability: BR_037_004 ensures the blanket limit is not under-sized and reinstates after losses, while BR_037_005 ensures deductibles are within Freddie Mac caps. Together they form a cohesive Named Storm compliance set.
dependencies.impact: If either rule fails, Named Storm insurance terms are noncompliant, risking insurance conditions, delayed delivery/purchase, or forced mid-process policy replacement.
examples: Blanket limit is 80% of largest TIV (fails BR_037_004) even if deductible is compliant (passes BR_037_005) → Named Storm coverage still unacceptable.
Blanket limit is 95% of largest TIV and reinstates (passes BR_037_004) but deductible minimum is $300,000 (fails BR_037_005) → unacceptable.
evidence_quotes: “Named Storm coverage… Blanket Insurance Limit… may not be less than 90%…”
“Named Storm deductible limits apply…”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: The upload/documentation requirement is explicitly triggered by issuance (“within five Business Days following issuance”). Without issuance under BR_001_001, BR_001_002 cannot be satisfied.
dependencies.rationale: BR_001_001 governs the requirement to issue an Adverse Action Notice within 30 days. BR_001_002 requires the Seller/Servicer to upload evidence/copies to DMS within five Business Days following that issuance. The documentation clock and artifact depend on the issuance event.
dependencies.impact: If BR_001_001 is missed or date is wrong, BR_001_002 cannot be timed correctly; this creates ECOA timing violations and missing DMS evidence, increasing regulatory exposure and QC findings.
examples: Freddie declines purchase on May 1; notice issued May 20 (timely under BR_001_001). BR_001_002 requires upload by five Business Days after May 20 with evidence of issuance.
Notice not issued at all (violates BR_001_001); BR_001_002 upload cannot be completed and file will show both ECOA process failure and DMS deficiency.
evidence_quotes: “must issue… Adverse Action Notice within 30 calendar days…”
“must upload… within five Business Days following issuance to the Borrower…”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: BR_002_004’s loss formula requires the “Mortgage Default date”; BR_002_003 defines the correct default date when a grace/cure period exists.
dependencies.rationale: BR_002_003 establishes the deemed default date (date of failure, not cure-end) for SBL loss timing components. BR_002_004 explicitly calculates interest “from the Mortgage Default date until the… Calculation Date,” making BR_002_003 a necessary upstream determiner of inputs to BR_002_004.
dependencies.impact: If BR_002_003 is not applied, interest accrual timing in BR_002_004 can be misstated, causing incorrect SBL Loss amounts, billing/settlement disputes, and required recalculations.
examples: Borrower misses payment on Jan 1 with a 10-day cure period; BR_002_003 deems default date Jan 1. BR_002_004 interest must accrue from Jan 1 (not Jan 11).
System incorrectly uses cure-end date; SBL Loss understated by 10 days of interest → correction required.
evidence_quotes: “Mortgage Default is deemed to have occurred on the date of the failure (not the end of the grace or cure period).”
“interest… from the Mortgage Default date until the SBL Loss Obligation Calculation Date…”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: BR_002_005 prohibits waiving late charges on ARM/SBL Hybrid ARM, which nullifies the permissive waiver workflow in BR_002_006 for those products.
dependencies.rationale: BR_002_006 describes conditions under which a third waiver request may be approved. BR_002_005 categorically prohibits any waiver for an ARM or SBL Hybrid ARM. Therefore, BR_002_005 supersedes/blocks BR_002_006 when the product type is ARM/SBL Hybrid ARM.
dependencies.impact: If override is not enforced, the servicer may unlawfully/impermissibly waive late charges on ARM/SBL Hybrid ARM, triggering Guide noncompliance, make-whole/remittance corrections, and potential servicing remedies.
examples: Borrower with SBL Hybrid ARM requests third late-fee waiver; even if auto-debit and supervisor approval exist (BR_002_006), BR_002_005 blocks the waiver.
Borrower with fixed-rate SBL Mortgage requests third waiver; BR_002_005 does not apply, so BR_002_006 process may proceed if conditions met.
evidence_quotes: “may not waive collection of any late charge… for an ARM or an SBL Hybrid ARM.”
“may only agree to a third late charge waiver request if…”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Both rules are triggered by a casualty loss event; operationally, notification (Form 1140 Part I) typically precedes/feeds the determination of whether Freddie Mac approval of restoration plans is required.
dependencies.rationale: BR_002_007 mandates a prompt notification and DMS upload when the servicer learns of loss/damage above thresholds. BR_002_008 then evaluates whether Freddie Mac approval is required based on loss magnitude, unit impact, defaults, and related triggers. Sequencing supports a consistent workflow: notify, then assess approval needs using the same event data.
dependencies.impact: If BR_002_007 is delayed or omitted, downstream escalation/approval tracking under BR_002_008 may be delayed, causing restoration to proceed without required approvals or causing Freddie Mac to receive incomplete/late reporting.
examples: Non-SBL estimated loss $75,000: BR_002_007 requires Form 1140 Part I within 14 days. Then BR_002_008 checks if any approval triggers apply (e.g., default, % units impacted, etc.).
Servicer evaluates approval need informally but fails to file Part I notice → compliance breach even if approval determination is correct.
evidence_quotes: “must… complete Form 1140 Part I… no later than 14 calendar days…”
“Freddie Mac approval… is required if one or more listed conditions exists…”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Both are required elements of a complete partial release request package requiring Freddie Mac approval (documentation freshness + required fee handling).
dependencies.rationale: BR_002_009 ensures the package includes a current title update report (≤45 days). BR_002_010 ensures correct fee charging/remittance and prohibits extra fees. They operate in parallel but together determine whether a partial release submission is complete and compliant.
dependencies.impact: If either fails, Freddie Mac may reject/delay the request: outdated title (risk of undisclosed liens) or incorrect fees (billing/remittance issues and corrective action).
examples: Title update is dated 60 days before Form 1125 date → fails BR_002_009 even if fees were handled correctly under BR_002_010.
Servicer charges an extra ‘processing’ fee beyond Loan Docs/Exhibit 10 → fails BR_002_010 even if title update is timely.
evidence_quotes: “deliver a title update report dated not earlier than 45 days…”
“must charge… nonrefundable… fee… may not charge… additional fees… must remit…”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_010_007 is the required reporting step that follows the reserve adequacy re-evaluation event and receipt of the updated PCR/inspection contemplated by BR_010_006.
dependencies.rationale: BR_010_006 establishes the requirement to conduct a re-evaluation on the 10th anniversary (when term >10 years and adjustment is permitted). BR_010_007 mandates a time-bound submission “after… receives the updated property condition report or conducts the required property inspection,” which is part of executing the BR_010_006 re-evaluation workflow.
dependencies.impact: If BR_010_006 is not performed/triggered, BR_010_007 submissions may never occur, causing missed Freddie Mac visibility into reserve adequacy and resulting in servicing noncompliance.
examples: 10-year anniversary occurs; servicer orders updated PCR and completes re-evaluation (BR_010_006). Once PCR is received, servicer must submit recommendation in 15 days (BR_010_007).
Servicer receives PCR but did not treat anniversary as a required re-evaluation milestone; recommendation is never submitted → violation.
evidence_quotes: “on the 10th anniversary… must conduct a re-evaluation…”
“After… receives the updated property condition report… must… submit a recommendation… within 15 days…”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: BR_012_006 appears to restate/duplicate BR_010_006 (and BR_010_007) as a single combined rule, creating ambiguity over which rule should drive automation controls and testing.
dependencies.rationale: Both rules describe the same triggering condition (term >10 years and loan docs allow adjustment after updated PCR) and require the re-evaluation on the 10th anniversary. This can cause conflicting implementation if a system treats them as separate obligations (duplicate tasks/alerts).
dependencies.impact: If not reconciled, automation may generate duplicate tasks, duplicate noncompliance flags, or inconsistent deadlines depending on which rule is used as the authoritative control.
examples: System generates two separate ‘10-year reserve re-evaluation’ tasks (one from BR_010_006 and one from BR_012_006), confusing servicing staff and audit trails.
Testing team validates only BR_012_006 and misses BR_010_006 controls, causing gaps in rule traceability.
evidence_quotes: BR_010_006: “on the 10th anniversary… must conduct a re-evaluation…”
BR_012_006: “must conduct a re-evaluation… on the 10th anniversary… Within 15 days… submit a recommendation…”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: BR_012_006 consolidates the 15-day recommendation requirement already stated in BR_010_007, risking duplicated controls and mismatched ‘source of truth’.
dependencies.rationale: BR_010_007 already mandates the 15-day recommendation submission after receipt of PCR/inspection. BR_012_006 repeats the same requirement, so two ‘separate’ rules can conflict in control mapping (same deadline measured twice).
dependencies.impact: Audit/compliance reporting may double-count exceptions or show inconsistent pass/fail outcomes if one rule is updated and the other is not.
examples: A policy update changes the submission channel language in one rule but not the other; staff follows the outdated one and is flagged for noncompliance.
System logs one submission event but marks the second duplicate rule as missing due to mismatched triggering logic.
evidence_quotes: BR_010_007: “must… submit… within 15 days…”
BR_012_006: “Within 15 days… must… submit a recommendation…”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: BR_012_008 is only triggered when the Servicer takes an action “under delegated approval,” which is permitted only if BR_012_007’s delegation eligibility conditions are satisfied.
dependencies.rationale: BR_012_007 defines when the Servicer may approve Replacement Reserve modifications without Freddie consent (delegated authority conditions such as risk rating, UPB, not in default, PCR recency). BR_012_008 then imposes mandatory notification and documentation steps specifically for those delegated approvals. Therefore, the delegated-authority determination is a gating prerequisite to BR_012_008.
dependencies.impact: If BR_012_007 is misapplied, the servicer may (a) incorrectly use delegated authority and then incorrectly notify under BR_012_008 (masking the need for Freddie consent), or (b) fail to notify Freddie Mac within 2 days for a valid delegated action, triggering servicing noncompliance.
examples: UPB $28M, Risk Rating 6, not in default, PCR completed within 6 months → BR_012_007 satisfied; BR_012_008 requires CRT record + DMS package within 2 days.
UPB $35M → BR_012_007 not satisfied; BR_012_008 delegated-notification workflow must not be used (consent request required instead).
evidence_quotes: BR_012_007: “may approve… without submitting a consent request… only when… conditions are met…”
BR_012_008: “When a Servicer takes an action under delegated approval… must notify Freddie Mac within two days…”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: The repair ‘due date’ that triggers BR_013_010’s post-due-date actions may be altered by an extension under BR_013_009; therefore BR_013_010 timing depends on whether a valid delegated extension exists.
dependencies.rationale: BR_013_009 constrains the servicer’s authority to extend repair completion dates. BR_013_010 is triggered “after a Repair Agreement item is not completed by its required due date.” To correctly determine when BR_013_010 must occur, the system must determine whether the due date was validly extended under BR_013_009 (or whether Freddie approval was required).
dependencies.impact: If extension authority is misapplied, BR_013_010 notices may be sent too early (borrower harm) or too late (missed reservation of rights and servicing breach).
examples: Original completion date is 90 days after origination; servicer extends by 120 days under delegated authority (invalid under BR_013_009). The ‘required due date’ should not move, so BR_013_010 should trigger based on the original due date.
Servicer extends within permitted window under BR_013_009; BR_013_010 should trigger 30 days after the valid extended due date if repairs remain incomplete.
evidence_quotes: BR_013_009: “Delegated authority limits for extending Repair Agreement completion dates…”
BR_013_010: “When repairs are not completed as or when required… Within 30 calendar days after the repair due date…”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Both rules address escalation when borrower fails to remediate/cure issues; BR_013_010 is repair-specific (reservation of rights), while BR_010_008 is broader (notice of default after cure period). They can operate as linked steps in a cure/escalation workflow.
dependencies.rationale: Repair non-completion is a common ‘adverse matter.’ BR_013_010 requires reservation of rights and Freddie Mac notification after missed repair due date; BR_010_008 requires issuing a notice of default after borrower fails to remediate or provide an acceptable plan within 30 days, with subsequent Freddie Mac notification. In practice, BR_013_010 often precedes or accompanies the broader BR_010_008 escalation.
dependencies.impact: If BR_013_010 is performed without aligning to BR_010_008 escalation thresholds, the file may lack timely default escalation. If BR_010_008 is triggered without the BR_013_010 reservation-of-rights steps, legal posture and Freddie reporting may be deficient.
examples: Repairs missed: servicer sends reservation of rights within 30 days (BR_013_010). Borrower still fails to cure within the notice period → servicer issues notice of default within required timeline (BR_010_008).
Servicer issues default letter (BR_010_008) but never sent reservation of rights or uploaded it per BR_013_010 → servicing compliance gap.
evidence_quotes: BR_013_010: “When repairs are not completed… send a ‘reservation of rights’ letter…”
BR_010_008: “If the Borrower does not fully remediate identified adverse matters… must issue a notice of default letter…”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: For pre-1992 mortgages, enforceable late charging requires meeting both timing/grace-period restrictions (BR_011_007) and amount cap restrictions (BR_011_008).
