dependencies.rationale: BR_011_007 limits when a late charge may be imposed (not within 10 days). BR_011_008 limits how much may be imposed (≤5% or lesser note amount). Together they define whether a late charge is permissible and lawful under the Guide constraints for those loans.
dependencies.impact: If either is missed, servicer may impose or attempt to collect impermissible late charges, requiring reversals/refunds and potentially triggering borrower complaints and audit findings.
examples: Installment received 7 days late: BR_011_007 prohibits imposing any late charge, so BR_011_008 becomes irrelevant for that payment.
Installment received 20 days late: BR_011_007 allows a late charge, but BR_011_008 caps it at 5% of monthly P&I (or lesser note cap).
evidence_quotes: “may not impose any late charge… received within 10 days…”
“may not impose a late charge… more than 5%…”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_011_004 remittance depends on late charges being properly collected; BR_011_011 prohibits certain collection methods (including charging reserves or deducting from payments), constraining how BR_011_004 can be executed in practice.
dependencies.rationale: BR_011_004 addresses how late charges are split/remitted based on when collected. BR_011_011 sets non-negotiable prohibitions on how unpaid late charges/default interest can be collected. Therefore, any operational workflow implementing BR_011_004 must first ensure collections comply with BR_011_011 or remittance numbers and audit trails will be invalid.
dependencies.impact: If BR_011_011 is violated, late charge transactions may need to be reversed and re-applied, and remittances recalculated, causing accounting breaks and servicing remedies.
examples: Servicer deducts unpaid late charges from a regular payment to net the installment; BR_011_011 prohibits this, so BR_011_004 remittance calculations must be corrected after reapplication.
Servicer tries to charge late fees to borrower reserves; prohibited by BR_011_011 regardless of what BR_011_004 would otherwise require.
evidence_quotes: BR_011_011: “may not collect… by… charging the Borrower's Reserves… deducting from a regular payment…”
BR_011_004: “must remit… 50% of any late charges collected more than 30 days…”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_011_005 remittance of late charges on ARMs requires that collection practices comply with BR_011_011’s prohibitions on collection methods.
dependencies.rationale: BR_011_005 requires remitting 50% of all late charges collected for certain ARMs. BR_011_011 restricts collection methods, which affects what can be considered ‘properly collected’ and prevents impermissible netting/charging practices that would distort remittance amounts and compliance status.
dependencies.impact: If BR_011_011 is violated, remittance reporting under BR_011_005 may be based on impermissible collections and require corrective transaction reapplication and remittance adjustments.
examples: Servicer collects late charges by deducting them from a delinquency cure payment; prohibited by BR_011_011, so any BR_011_005 remittance derived from that collection must be corrected.
Servicer collects late charges separately (not from reserves or payment deductions) → BR_011_011 satisfied; BR_011_005 remittance can proceed.
evidence_quotes: “may not collect… by… deducting from… a payment made to… cure a Delinquency…”
“must remit… 50% of all late charges collected…”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_011_006 requires remittance of collected default interest, and BR_011_011 explicitly restricts how default interest (and late charges) may be collected.
dependencies.rationale: BR_011_006 governs remittance of default interest; BR_011_011 prohibits collecting default interest via reserves, payment deductions, delinquency cure payment deductions, or capitalization to principal. Therefore, correct remittance depends on compliant collection methods and correct transactional application.
dependencies.impact: Violations trigger reversals/reapplications and could create REMIC/accounting issues if default interest is improperly capitalized or netted from borrower payments.
examples: Servicer adds default interest to principal balance (capitalization) → explicitly prohibited by BR_011_011; any BR_011_006 remittance based on that treatment must be corrected.
Servicer bills and collects default interest as a separate receivable/payment → complies with BR_011_011 and supports BR_011_006 remittance.
evidence_quotes: “may not collect… default interest by… adding to the outstanding principal balance…”
“must remit all collected default interest…”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: The accounting cycle definition (16th–15th) relies on the concept of the ‘cutoff date’ being the close of business on the 15th (or preceding business day).
dependencies.rationale: BR_039_005 defines the operational cutoff date used for accounting/reporting. BR_039_006 defines the cycle as 16th through 15th. Systems typically must implement the cutoff date logic (including business-day adjustment) to correctly operationalize the cycle close.
dependencies.impact: If cutoff-date logic is not applied, cycle boundaries can be misapplied (especially when the 15th is not a business day), causing misstatement of monthly accounting transmissions.
examples: 15th is a Sunday; BR_039_005 moves cutoff to Friday close-of-business. Without that, a system might treat Sunday as cutoff and mis-time transmissions for the cycle ending on the 15th.
Cycle reporting job runs based on ‘cutoff date’; BR_039_005 must be computed to determine the correct operational ‘15th’ boundary.
evidence_quotes: “accounting cutoff date… close of business on the 15th… if… not a Business Day… preceding…”
“accounting cycle… 16th… through the 15th…”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Exception activity classification/reporting generally must be aligned to the accounting cycle to ensure correct period placement and transmission.
dependencies.rationale: BR_039_007 defines what qualifies as exception activity (UPB reduced to zero events). BR_039_006 defines the monthly cycle window used for reporting. To report exception activities accurately, the system must place the event into the correct cycle period.
dependencies.impact: If cycle alignment is missing, exception activity could be reported in the wrong month or omitted from required cycle transmissions.
examples: Mortgage is paid in full on the 14th; it should be included in the cycle ending on the 15th (per BR_039_006) and classified as exception activity (BR_039_007).
Mortgage is sold into a securitization on the 16th; it belongs in the next cycle period and must be reported accordingly.
evidence_quotes: “accounting cycle… 16th… through the 15th…”
“exception activity… activity resulting in… UPB… reduced to zero.”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Both rules are appraisal-file adequacy requirements; one addresses zoning legality support (when zoning report not provided) and the other addresses minimum comparables for the sales comparison approach.
dependencies.rationale: Underwriting typically treats appraisal completeness as a bundle of required components. BR_035_005 ensures zoning compliance support is present when a zoning report is absent; BR_038_006 ensures sales comparison methodology meets minimum comparable count. Together they form a practical appraisal QC checklist for acceptance/conditioning.
dependencies.impact: If either requirement fails, the appraisal may be conditioned/rejected, delaying underwriting approval and potentially closing timelines.
examples: No third-party zoning report submitted under the $20MM/$25MM criteria; appraiser must include zoning legality opinion (BR_035_005) AND if using sales comparison, must include 4+ comps (BR_038_006).
Appraisal includes only 3 comparable sales → fails BR_038_006 even if zoning legality opinion is properly included under BR_035_005.
evidence_quotes: “appraiser must include an op…” (zoning legality support when no zoning report)
“must analyze sales of at least four comparable properties.”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_017_005 only applies when the Servicer is exercising delegated authority, which is only available if the delegation requirement and stated conditions in BR_SERVICES_ELIGIBILITY_017_004 are satisfied.
dependencies.rationale: The 30-day notice/CRT+DMS delivery obligation is triggered by a delegated approval of a non-residential lease. Delegated approval is only permitted when the delegation requirement is satisfied and all lease conditions are met; otherwise Freddie Mac approval is required and the delegated reporting workflow is not the correct path.
dependencies.impact: If BR_SERVICES_ELIGIBILITY_017_004 is not satisfied, the Servicer must not proceed as if it has delegated authority. Any attempt to follow BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_017_005 as a delegated approval would mask an unauthorized action, create audit findings, and require escalation/re-submission for Freddie Mac approval.
examples: Tenant is an Affiliate of the Borrower. Delegated authority is not available; Servicer must seek Freddie Mac approval instead of filing delegated-notice CRT/DMS package.
Lease term including options is 12 years. Servicer cannot rely on delegated process; any delegated reporting would be procedurally incorrect.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_ELIGIBILITY_017_004: "Servicer is delegated authority ... only if all stated conditions are satisfied"
BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_017_005: "For any non-residential lease that does not require Freddie Mac approval... actions taken under delegated approval"

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: The 30-day delegated easement notice/delivery process only applies to easements that "do not require Freddie Mac approval," which is determined by satisfying delegated-eligibility conditions in BR_SERVICES_ELIGIBILITY_022_001.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003 is a delegated reporting workflow. Whether an easement can be handled without Freddie Mac approval depends on meeting the delegated authority conditions, including securitization/master servicer and other eligibility checks.
dependencies.impact: If eligibility is not met, Servicer cannot treat the easement as delegated/no-Freddie-approval and must route to Freddie Mac approval. Using the delegated notification path would create missing-approval exposure and potential invalid execution of easement documents.
examples: Mortgage is in a securitization but Freddie Mac is not the Directing Certificateholder/Party; Servicer must obtain Freddie Mac approval and cannot use the delegated 30-day notice-only workflow.
Mortgage secures tax-exempt debt; delegated eligibility fails and Freddie Mac approval is required.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_ELIGIBILITY_022_001: "delegated authority ... only if all listed conditions are satisfied"
BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003: "after the grant of an easement that does not require Freddie Mac approval"

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: For third-party easements under the specified category, delegated approval is permitted only if the additional constraints are satisfied; otherwise the easement requires Freddie Mac approval and BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003 is not the correct path.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003 applies to delegated easements that do not require Freddie Mac approval. For certain third-party easements, the determination of whether it can be delegated hinges on thresholds (UPB, risk rating, current/no default evidence, compensation cap, supervisor approval).
dependencies.impact: If constraints fail, the Servicer must not proceed under delegated approval. Freddie Mac approval must be pursued, and any delegated notice/delivery package would be procedurally incorrect and could conceal a breach of delegated authority limits.
examples: UPB is $33,000,000 on application date; Servicer cannot treat the easement as delegated/no-Freddie approval and must escalate.
Risk Rating is 7; delegated approval is not permitted, so BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003 timing is not the governing workflow.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_022_002: "a Servicer may approve only if additional constraints are satisfied"
BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003: "easement that does not require Freddie Mac approval"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_SERVICES_DOCUMENTATION_022_004 requires retention of copies of notifications to Borrower and Freddie Mac and executed/recorded documents that are created/delivered by BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003.
dependencies.rationale: The delegated easement file must contain evidence of notification to Freddie Mac and copies of the executed/recorded easement agreement. BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003 is the operational step that produces those deliverables and timestamps (within 30 days).
dependencies.impact: If BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003 is not performed, the file will be missing mandatory artifacts (notice and delivered documents), causing audit failures, inability to evidence compliance with delegated authority, and potential repurchase/servicing remedies depending on Guide enforcement.
examples: Servicer approves easement and records it but fails to deliver the Easement Request and executed agreement within 30 days; later, the delegated approval file lacks proof of timely notice.
Recorder delay occurs; without using the allowed workaround and documenting it, the file cannot demonstrate compliant delivery and retention.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_022_003: "notify Freddie Mac... by electronically delivering the Easement Request... deliver... executed, recorded easement agreement"
BR_SERVICES_DOCUMENTATION_022_004: "file containing... copies of the Servicer's notification... to... Freddie Mac... executed easement agreement"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005 explicitly excludes securitized Mortgages ('restricted'), so BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 must be evaluated first to determine applicability.
dependencies.rationale: The combined LTV/DCR thresholds apply only when Freddie Mac is considering consent for non-securitized loans. The securitization prohibition is the applicability gate that routes the request to immediate denial/alternate product.
dependencies.impact: If the prohibition gate is missed, Servicer may submit packages for loans Freddie Mac cannot consider under the stated rule, causing process churn and compliance exceptions.
examples: Loan is securitized; subordinate financing request should be blocked without running combined DCR/LTV checks.
Loan is not securitized; then BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005 thresholds become relevant for the Freddie Mac consent evaluation.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005: "other than securitized Mortgages, which are restricted"
BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005: "does not permit Subordinate Financing"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: Fee-handling requirements apply at submission time; submission should occur only in the branch where Freddie Mac is considering consent (i.e., after the combined LTV/DCR consent thresholds are met or at least evaluated).
dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005 defines a key consent gate for Freddie Mac review. BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_028_006 then prescribes how the Servicer must package and remit fees during submission in that same review pathway.
dependencies.impact: Failure to meet the gate but proceeding to fee submission creates borrower harm and process waste; meeting the gate but violating fee handling risks package rejection and fee-related compliance findings.
examples: Servicer submits the package without remitting the required 50% of the review/processing fee to Freddie Mac; submission is noncompliant and may be rejected.
Servicer charges an additional internal 'processing fee' beyond what is allowed; violates the 'may not charge' restriction.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005: "When Freddie Mac considers consenting... combined debt must not result in..."
BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_028_006: "When submitting a subordinate financing approval request package..."

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: The good faith deposit upload obligation is triggered by Rate Lock; BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002 is a prerequisite to Freddie Mac accepting an early rate-lock application, which is a gating condition to reaching Rate Lock in the early-rate-lock workflow.
dependencies.rationale: If Freddie Mac will not accept the early rate-lock application without the required property condition report, the transaction cannot proceed to Rate Lock in that pathway; therefore downstream Rate Lock-triggered obligations (deposit evidence upload) cannot be reached compliantly.
dependencies.impact: If the property condition report is missing, early rate-lock acceptance (and resulting Rate Lock workflow) is blocked; the seller cannot meet the 2-business-day deposit upload requirement because Rate Lock should not occur/stand.
examples: Seller attempts to early-rate lock but has not submitted an engineering report meeting Section 62.3; Freddie Mac does not accept the lock request, so deposit-upload timers should not start.
Engineering report is submitted and accepted; Rate Lock occurs; system starts the 2-business-day timer to upload wire confirmation/LOC/demand note evidence.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002: "before Freddie Mac will... accept an early rate-lock application"
BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_003_006: "Within two Business Days following Rate Lock... upload confirmation... good faith deposit"

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: Executing/uploading the Mortgage Terms Confirmation Sheet occurs 'After Rate Lock'; early rate-lock acceptance is blocked without the property condition report, preventing the Rate Lock state that triggers the confirmation sheet process.
dependencies.rationale: The confirmation sheet acceptance process is downstream of Rate Lock. Because Freddie Mac will not accept early rate-lock without the engineering report, the lock/confirmation workflow should not proceed until BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002 is satisfied.
dependencies.impact: Missing the property condition report can prevent or unwind Rate Lock; Seller cannot (or should not) execute the confirmation sheet because Rate Lock terms would not be validly issued/accepted.
examples: Seller receives a request to provide the engineering report; until submitted, there is no valid confirmation sheet to execute because the lock is not accepted.
After submission, Freddie issues the confirmation sheet and Seller must return it the next business day.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002: "before Freddie Mac will... accept an early rate-lock application"
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_005: "After Rate Lock... Seller must execute and upload the Confirmation Sheet"

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006 deadlines are 'After Rate Lock' for early rate lock transactions; early rate-lock acceptance is blocked without BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002, so the downstream package-delivery workflow depends on it.
dependencies.rationale: The full underwriting package delivery schedule is anchored to the Rate Lock event. The early rate-lock event chain is gated by the engineering report requirement.
dependencies.impact: If BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002 is not met, early rate lock acceptance may not occur, which prevents or delays starting the delivery timeline and can jeopardize transaction timelines.
examples: Engineering report is missing; lock not accepted; Seller cannot reliably manage the 15/22/45-day package deadline timers.
Engineering report submitted; Rate Lock accepted; Seller must deliver full underwriting package within the applicable days after Rate Lock.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_002: "before... accept an early rate-lock application"
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006: "After Rate Lock, the Seller must deliver the full underwriting package..."

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_004_001 is explicitly required 'Prior to early-rate lock'; BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_005 is triggered 'After Rate Lock'. This creates a mandatory order in the early-rate-lock workflow.
dependencies.rationale: The Seller must complete and document management interview/property walk/unit inspections before proceeding to the lock stage. Only after lock issuance does the confirmation sheet acceptance step occur.
dependencies.impact: If pre-lock inspection steps are missed, the Seller may be out of compliance even if the confirmation sheet is executed timely; the deal may be conditioned, re-underwritten, or delayed for cure documentation.
examples: Seller executes confirmation sheet on time but cannot evidence the pre-lock property walk/manager interview; Freddie may condition the file and delay underwriting acceptance.
Seller completes pre-lock inspection narrative and then proceeds to lock and confirmation sheet acceptance.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_004_001: "Prior to early-rate lock... interview... walk... inspect..."
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_005: "After Rate Lock... execute and upload the Confirmation Sheet"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: Pre-lock inspection must occur before the Rate Lock event, while good-faith deposit evidence upload is time-bound after Rate Lock—enforcing a practical execution order in automation.
dependencies.rationale: The deposit evidence workflow is downstream of Rate Lock. Since BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_004_001 governs required actions before early rate lock, systems should ensure it is completed before initiating post-lock deposit evidence timers.
dependencies.impact: If the Seller locks without completing pre-lock inspection requirements, the file may be deficient even if deposit evidence is uploaded timely; rework may delay underwriting and risk lock fallout.
examples: Seller uploads wire confirmation within 2 business days but never documented the pre-lock unit sampling inspection; Freddie flags inspection deficiency.
Seller completes and documents the pre-lock property walk first, then locks, then uploads deposit evidence.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_004_001: "Prior to early-rate lock..."
BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_003_006: "Within two Business Days following Rate Lock... upload confirmation..."

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: The executed confirmation sheet is the Seller’s acceptance of Rate Lock terms; the full underwriting package delivery deadlines are predicated on the transaction being in an active post-lock state.
dependencies.rationale: If the Seller fails to execute and upload the confirmation sheet timely, Freddie may treat the failure as a Nondelivery. In that scenario, subsequent obligations to deliver the full underwriting package under the lock may be moot or require reinstatement/exception handling.
dependencies.impact: If the confirmation sheet is not returned, the lock may be treated as Nondelivery; package delivery may be rejected, timelines reset, or the transaction may require escalation and potential re-lock.
examples: Seller misses the next-business-day confirmation upload; Freddie treats as Nondelivery; Seller later attempts to deliver underwriting package but must resolve lock acceptance first.
Seller uploads executed confirmation sheet on time; then proceeds with the package delivery timeline (15/22/45 days depending on lock type).
evidence_quotes: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_005: "If the Seller fails to return... Freddie Mac may treat the failure as a Nondelivery."
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006: "After Rate Lock, the Seller must deliver the full underwriting package..."

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: Vendor restricted-party screening must occur before submission of the full underwriting package (or before closing if engaged later), making it a hard gate for compliant package submission under BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006.
dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_021_005 establishes a timing mandate relative to the full underwriting package submission. Therefore, to deliver a compliant full underwriting package (BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006), the Seller/Servicer must have screened each Third-Party Vendor involved (for applicable applications).
dependencies.impact: If screening is not completed before package submission, the submission is timing-noncompliant, may require cure/resubmission, and can delay underwriting review and closing; if a vendor is on a restricted list, the transaction may require vendor replacement and rework.
examples: Engineering firm and environmental consultant are engaged; Seller submits underwriting package without screening—must cure by screening and documenting results, potentially delaying review.
Vendor engaged after package submission; Seller screens as soon as practicable and before closing to remain compliant.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_021_005: "Screening must occur before submission of the full underwriting package"
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006: "Seller must deliver the full underwriting package..."

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: Flood determination and signed notice are required file contents for origination and are typically required elements of an underwritten/complete package; missing items can block acceptance as a 'full underwriting package'.
dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_037_010 requires the SFHDF determination and a signed Notice to Borrower (plus ongoing effectiveness). The full underwriting package delivery step is the operational point where these required origination documents must be present to avoid suspense/deficiency.
dependencies.impact: If SFHDF/notice is missing or outdated, Freddie Mac may suspend review, condition the file, or require updated determinations; closing timelines may slip and delivery defects may be assessed.
examples: Property has multiple parcels but vendor only evaluated one; SFHDF is incomplete—package is conditioned until all structures/parcels are evaluated and updated forms are provided.
Seller includes SFHDF and signed notice in the underwriting package; review proceeds without flood-document suspense.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_037_010: "determine whether... located in a SFHA using... SFHDF... include a copy... plus a signed Notice"
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006: "deliver the full underwriting package"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: When priority repair triggers are met, the borrower remediation plan is a required underwriting submission component; therefore the ability to deliver a complete 'full underwriting package' depends on including it in that branch.
dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_008 specifies additional required documentation based on repair magnitude/habitability triggers. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006 is the submission event for the full underwriting package, so package completeness depends on this conditional doc being present when triggered.
dependencies.impact: If the remediation plan is omitted when required, Freddie Mac may condition or decline the underwriting review until the plan is provided and evaluated; this can delay delivery acceptance and jeopardize the lock timeline.
examples: Priority Repairs total $300,000 on a $20,000,000 loan (> $200,000 trigger); remediation plan must be included with the full underwriting package submission.
Priority Repairs are $150,000 and not habitability-impacting; remediation plan requirement does not trigger and is not required for submission.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_008: "Seller must submit a Borrower remediation plan..."
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006: "deliver the full underwriting package"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: When the stated disaster/weather condition scenario occurs, the borrower certification must be delivered as part of the full underwriting package—making it a conditional completeness gate for submission.
dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_031_010 explicitly requires delivery of the Borrower Certification of Weather-Related Event 'as part of the full underwriting package' when the triggering event occurred after the inspection.
dependencies.impact: If omitted when triggered, Freddie Mac may suspend underwriting, require updated condition verification, or delay/decline review pending the certification; can cascade into missed delivery/funding dates.
examples: Hurricane occurs after property condition consultant inspection; Seller must include the certification addressing impacts in the full underwriting package submission.
No intervening adverse event; certification is not required and does not affect the submission package.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_031_010: "must deliver... as part of the full underwriting package"
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006: "deliver the full underwriting package"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: The 90-day inspection/lease audit completion clock in BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_023_005 is explicitly measured from Freddie Mac’s receipt of the full underwriting package, which is delivered under BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006.
dependencies.rationale: Without the full underwriting package being received, the 'within 90 days of receipt' requirement cannot be evaluated or tracked. Delivery receipt is the start event for the timing control.
dependencies.impact: If the package is not delivered (or delivery is delayed), the completion window start shifts, potentially compressing downstream inspection/audit scheduling or causing re-performance if the 90-day cap is exceeded after receipt.
examples: Seller delivers full underwriting package; inspection and lease audit must be completed within 90 days of Freddie receipt or must be redone (no recertification).
Seller delays package delivery; the 90-day timer does not start, but the overall transaction timeline may be jeopardized.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_023_005: "must be completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac's receipt of the full underwriting package"
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006: "deliver the full underwriting package"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_025_003’s 90-day inspection timing is measured from Freddie Mac’s receipt of the underwriting package, which is delivered under BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006 for full underwriting submissions.
dependencies.rationale: Receipt of the underwriting package is the timing anchor. The delivery process supplies the prerequisite start point and file context (preliminary vs full).
dependencies.impact: If package delivery is not properly completed/received, timing controls for inspection acceptability cannot be managed; if inspections occur outside the 90-day window post-receipt, a new inspection is mandatory with no recertification allowed.
examples: Full underwriting package received on May 1; inspection must be completed by July 30 (approx.) or a new inspection is required.
Seller submits package but it is not accepted/received due to missing components; the receipt date shifts, impacting the 90-day window.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_025_003: "completed within 90 days of Freddie Mac's receipt of the applicable underwriting package"
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006: "deliver the full underwriting package"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: On inspection day, unit selection is constrained to the 'selected units' prepared pre-inspection; BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_004_003 defines how units are selected/notified and requires the list be provided to Freddie Mac prior to inspection date.
dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROHIBITION_004_004’s control ('Freddie Mac identifies from the selected units' / 'Seller selects') presupposes that an eligible pool of selected units exists, and that tenant notification/list delivery occurred as required.
dependencies.impact: If the pre-inspection selection/notification/list is not performed, the on-day selection process cannot be compliant; inspections may be rescheduled, sampling validity questioned, and underwriting delayed.
examples: Property has 50 units; Seller failed to select twice the required units and did not provide list to Freddie Mac—on-site selection cannot proceed per rule; inspection must be reset.
Property has 20 units; Borrower notified all tenants as required; Freddie Mac can select the sample on-site without borrower/property manager influence.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_004_003: "select units... list of units selected... provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date"
BR_UNDERWRITES_PROHIBITION_004_004: "identifies... sample... from the selected units"

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Sampling-size compliance (how many units) is distinct from sampling-integrity compliance (who selects units). Both are needed for an acceptable inspection, but one can be calculated without the other.
dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_004_002 sets the unit inspection count thresholds; BR_UNDERWRITES_PROHIBITION_004_004 prevents borrower/property manager influence in which specific units are inspected. Together they form a complete control set for inspection validity.
dependencies.impact: If unit-selection independence fails, the inspection may be considered compromised even if the correct number of units was inspected, leading to re-inspection, delays, and heightened risk of misrepresentation.
examples: Seller inspects 10% of units as required but allows property manager to recommend 'best' units—violates independence; Freddie may require re-selection/re-inspection.
Seller follows independence rule and inspects correct counts—inspection results are more defensible in underwriting.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROHIBITION_004_004: "Neither the Borrower nor the property manager may select or recommend units"
BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_004_002: "Unit inspection sampling must follow unit-count thresholds"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_UNDERWRITES_VALIDATION_007_006 restates the sampling thresholds and adds explicit requirements (inspect all Down Units and all commercial units), effectively tightening/augmenting the baseline sampling rule.
dependencies.rationale: Operationally, teams may first apply the basic sampling thresholds (excluding Down/commercial from sample sizing) and must then apply the validation augmentation to ensure Down/commercial units are also inspected. This functions as a 'more complete' rule that supersedes a partial reading of the constraint.
dependencies.impact: If only BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_004_002 is followed, Down Units and commercial units may be omitted from inspection, causing noncompliance and requiring re-inspection or underwriting delay.
examples: Property has 40 units including 2 Down Units and 3 commercial units: Seller inspects 10% residential but forgets Down/commercial; violates BR_UNDERWRITES_VALIDATION_007_006.
Seller inspects 10% residential + all Down Units + all commercial units, satisfying the augmented requirement.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_VALIDATION_007_006: "plus inspect all Down Units and all commercial units"
BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_004_002: "excluding Down Units and commercial units"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_025_001 requires that at least 50% of audited leases must be units inspected, so the lease-audit sampling rule depends on having a compliant inspected-unit set established under the inspection sampling rule.
dependencies.rationale: Lease audit sampling is partially constrained by the inspection population. Without completing inspection sampling per BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_004_002, the lease audit cannot satisfy the '50% audited leases must be units inspected' linkage.
dependencies.impact: If inspection sampling is incomplete/noncompliant, lease audit composition will also become noncompliant (or unprovable), triggering rework of both inspection and audit and delaying underwriting sign-off.
examples: Seller audits 10 leases but only inspected 2 of those units; fails the 50% linkage and must redo sampling or expand inspections.
Seller first completes inspections per unit-count thresholds, then selects lease audit sample to ensure ≥50% overlap with inspected units.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_025_001: "At least 50% of audited leases must be units inspected"
BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_004_002: "Seller must conduct a complete property inspection... Unit inspection sampling must follow..."

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: BR_UNDERWRITES_CALCULATION_023_001 provides an allowed rounding/truncation method used when computing unit counts for lease-related verification, supporting correct execution of lease audit sampling counts.
dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_025_001 imposes sampling percentages and min/max unit counts. When computations produce decimals, BR_UNDERWRITES_CALCULATION_023_001 defines how to truncate while preserving a minimum requirement, preventing sampling math errors and rework.
dependencies.impact: If truncation rules are not followed, the Seller may under-sample or miscalculate the required number of units, leading to an invalid lease audit and re-performance.
examples: 10% of 109 units = 10.9; Seller truncates to 10 only if minimum requirement is still satisfied per the truncation rule.
Seller rounds down incorrectly below the minimum, resulting in too few audited leases and a required resample.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_CALCULATION_023_001: "may truncate it down to the nearest whole number, provided the minimum... requirement is still met"
BR_UNDERWRITES_CONSTRAINT_025_001: "lease audit form meeting unit-count-based sampling minimums/maximums"

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: Where lease files are on-site, BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_023_002 fixes the audit timing to the site inspection, and BR_UNDERWRITES_VALIDATION_023_003 governs how leases are validated during that audit. The validation step is executed during/after the audit event.
dependencies.rationale: Validation against tenant payment documentation is part of performing the lease audit. When the audit must occur at the site inspection (on-site files), the practical sequence is: conduct lease audit at inspection → validate leases against payment evidence → document discrepancies/comments.
dependencies.impact: If the audit is not conducted at the time required, validation may be delayed or performed without proper access to on-site files, increasing rework risk and potentially requiring a return site visit.
examples: Lease files are kept in the onsite management office; Seller performs the lease audit during inspection and validates rents using tenant ledgers on-site.
Seller skips onsite audit and tries to validate later; missing ledgers/check images leads to discrepancies and a second visit.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_023_002: "must conduct the lease audit at the time of the site inspection"
BR_UNDERWRITES_VALIDATION_023_003: "Leases must be validated against documentation reflecting actual rental payments"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: The pre-inspection selection/list/tenant notification process must occur before the inspection can be executed compliantly for Borrower-Owned Homes.
dependencies.rationale: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_030_008 defines the pool of homes and notification steps 'Prior to the inspection date' and requires providing the list to Freddie Mac. BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_030_007 then requires inspection of defined minimums and categories; that inspection depends on having selected/accessible units.
dependencies.impact: If selection/notification/list delivery is not completed, the inspection may be invalid or impossible to perform (no access), requiring rescheduling and delaying underwriting completion.
examples: Seller fails to select twice the required homes and does not notify tenants; inspection team cannot access enough homes to meet the 10%/minimum-two requirement.
Seller provides Freddie Mac the selected-home list before inspection; inspection proceeds and includes all Down/vacant/non-revenue homes as required.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_030_008: "Prior to the inspection date... select twice the required number... list... provided to Freddie Mac prior to the date"
BR_UNDERWRITES_PROCESS_030_007: "Seller must conduct a complete property inspection"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: The Final Delivery Package is governed by the Purchase Contract Mandatory Delivery Date, which is downstream of earlier delivery milestones; in practice, successful full underwriting package delivery supports proceeding to final delivery.
dependencies.rationale: A typical Freddie Mac workflow is: rate lock → deliver underwriting package → obtain approvals/close → deliver final delivery package by the Mandatory Delivery Date. While not every detail is explicitly linked, operational sequencing makes final delivery dependent on completing upstream package delivery and closing readiness.
dependencies.impact: If the full underwriting package is late or incomplete, final approvals and closing may slip, increasing risk of missing the Mandatory Delivery Date and delaying/rejecting purchase/funding.
examples: Seller misses the 22-day post-lock full underwriting package deadline; underwriting review starts late and closing pushes, leading to late final delivery.
Seller delivers full underwriting package timely; after approval and closing, final delivery package is shipped/uploaded by noon ET on Mandatory Delivery Date.
evidence_quotes: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_004_006: "After Rate Lock... deliver the full underwriting package by deadlines..."
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_005_001: "deliver the Final Delivery Package... on the Mandatory Delivery Date"

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: The preferred funding date is requested by inserting it on the Final Delivery Table of Contents, which is part of (and therefore depends on) the Final Delivery Package submission process.
dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_005_002 is a sub-step of final delivery: you cannot specify a preferred funding date in the final delivery TOC without preparing/submitting the final delivery package governed by BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_005_001.
dependencies.impact: If final delivery package is not properly delivered, the preferred funding date request is ineffective; funding will be scheduled based on when review completes (and no later than the Mandatory Funding Date), potentially harming borrower/seller funding coordination.
examples: Seller omits preferred funding date on the final delivery TOC; Freddie funds after review is complete but by Mandatory Funding Date.
Seller includes a preferred funding date that is 5 days after receipt; Freddie rejects/ignores it because it must be at least 10 calendar days after receipt.
evidence_quotes: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_005_002: "preferred funding date... inserting it on the Final Delivery Table of Contents"
BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_005_001: "deliver the Final Delivery Package"

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_009 is a specific restatement of the signed-tenant condition already contained in BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_010_005, useful as a validation cross-check for SBL packages.
dependencies.rationale: Both rules address Commercial Lease Analysis/Estoppel requirements and the 5% of GPR signing trigger. BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_010_005 is broader (each commercial lease, with signing requirement when ≥5%), while BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_009 repeats the ≥5% signed requirement. In an automated graph, BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_009 can validate that the signing trigger was applied correctly under BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_010_005.
dependencies.impact: If the signing trigger is missed, the full underwriting package may be incomplete and underwriting may be suspended until signed tenant estoppels are obtained (often time-consuming).
examples: One commercial lease is 7% of GPR; system flags that the Commercial Lease Analysis and Estoppel must be tenant-signed (cross-validated by both rules).
Commercial leases exist but none exceed 5% individually; analyses/estoppels are still required under the broader rule, but tenant signature trigger does not apply.
evidence_quotes: BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_010_005: "If income from any one commercial lease represents 5% or more... must be signed by the commercial tenant"
BR_UNDERWRITES_DOCUMENTATION_036_009: "If income from any one commercial... lease represents 5% or more... signed by the tenant"

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) because BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_COMPLIANCE_023_007 is explicitly triggered only after the Seller receives/reviews Form 1104, which is the SBL Physical Risk Report required by BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006. Without the report, the disclosure obligation cannot be executed.
dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 requires the Seller/Servicer to submit the SBL Physical Risk Report (Form 1104) before Freddie Mac will issue a Letter of Commitment. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_COMPLIANCE_023_007 then requires disclosures 'after receiving and reviewing Form 1104.' Operationally, the output artifact from BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 (a compliant Form 1104) is the input/trigger for BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_COMPLIANCE_023_007.
dependencies.impact: If BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 fails (no compliant Form 1104 submitted/obtained), the Seller cannot perform the required Form 1104-based Critical Repair disclosures. The SBL commitment process stalls because Freddie will not issue the Letter of Commitment, and downstream underwriting conditions may be incomplete or misinformed.
examples: Seller has an SBL deal ready for commitment but has not obtained a Chapter 62SBL-compliant Physical Risk Report; Seller cannot credibly disclose Critical Repairs because Form 1104 does not exist in the file.

Form 1104 is received but does not meet Chapter 62SBL requirements (e.g., missing required sections); Seller cannot rely on it to drive the Critical Repair disclosure workflow and Freddie may refuse to proceed to commitment.
evidence_quotes: “submit an SBL Physical Risk Report... before Freddie Mac will issue a Letter of Commitment”
“After receiving and reviewing Form 1104, the Seller must disclose...”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) because the repair-completion verification requirement in BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_VALIDATION_023_008 is only applicable once Critical Repairs are identified/disclosed; it is the gating control before the full underwriting package can be submitted.
dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_VALIDATION_023_008 applies 'when Critical Repairs are identified in [the] SBL Physical Risk Report' and requires the Seller to verify repairs are addressed and provide evidence prior to submitting the full underwriting package. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_COMPLIANCE_023_007 is the rule that operationalizes identification/escalation of Critical Repairs to Freddie Mac and the SBL team. In practice, the workflow is: identify/disclose Critical Repairs → cure/verify completion → submit underwriting package.
dependencies.impact: If BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_COMPLIANCE_023_007 is not performed, Critical Repairs may not be formally recognized/escalated, leading to an incomplete or noncompliant underwriting package submission and heightened misrepresentation risk. Freddie may later condition the deal, require rework, or delay/decline purchase/commitment.
examples: Form 1104 notes suspected structural deficiency; Seller discloses it to Freddie per BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_COMPLIANCE_023_007, triggering BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_VALIDATION_023_008 to collect contractor sign-off and invoices before package submission.
Seller fails to disclose Mold flagged in Form 1104; the underwriting package is submitted without evidence of remediation, leading Freddie to suspend review and request cure documentation.
evidence_quotes: “must disclose to Freddie Mac any Critical Repairs...”
“must verify that all Critical Repairs have been addressed... prior to submission of the full underwriting package”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_VALIDATION_023_008 is triggered by Critical Repairs identified in the SBL Physical Risk Report (Form 1104), which is the deliverable required by BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006.
dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_VALIDATION_023_008 cannot be evaluated without a Physical Risk Report identifying whether Critical Repairs exist. While the rule’s immediate trigger is the presence of Critical Repairs, the enabling artifact is Form 1104 produced/obtained through the physical risk reporting requirement.
dependencies.impact: If the Form 1104 process is incomplete or noncompliant, the Seller cannot reliably determine whether Critical Repairs exist and cannot complete (or properly scope) the repair verification evidence Freddie requires prior to underwriting package submission.
examples: Seller attempts to submit the full SBL underwriting package but has not obtained a compliant Form 1104; Seller cannot demonstrate whether Critical Repairs were present and cured.
Form 1104 is outdated/deficient; Freddie requests a new report, delaying the repair verification and underwriting package submission.
evidence_quotes: “must deliver a rent schedule... as part of the conditions precedent”
“must verify that all Critical Repairs have been addressed...”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) because BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_CONSTRAINT_021_001 (85% occupancy for 3 consecutive months) requires evidentiary support; BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_PROCESS_021_002 mandates the monthly certified rent rolls the Seller/Servicer uses to determine/validate occupancy for Conversion delivery.
dependencies.rationale: The minimum occupancy requirement must be satisfied for the three months preceding submission of the Conversion Underwriting Package and again at Conversion. BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_PROCESS_021_002 explicitly states the Seller/Servicer must obtain monthly certified rent rolls 'to support physical occupancy determination for Conversion delivery.' Therefore, the occupancy test depends on the rent roll collection/certification process.
dependencies.impact: If certified monthly rent rolls are missing/uncertified, the Seller cannot substantiate the 85% stabilized occupancy requirement. Freddie Mac may reject the Conversion Underwriting Package, delay Conversion, or require re-submission once adequate documentation is available.
examples: Property hits 86% occupancy but the property manager did not certify the last two monthly rent rolls; Seller cannot support the 3-month consecutive requirement and must delay package submission.
Borrower provides a rent roll, but it is not certified by both Borrower and property manager; Seller must cure certification before using it to evidence minimum occupancy at Conversion.
evidence_quotes: “must have stabilized residential occupancy of 85% for the three consecutive months preceding the submission...”
“must obtain these to support physical occupancy determination for Conversion delivery”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because the documentation package in BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 is only actionable if the request itself is allowable under the caps/limits in BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_CONSTRAINT_021_003. Cap checks should be performed before commissioning/submitting a new appraisal and loan amount calculation package.
dependencies.rationale: BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_CONSTRAINT_021_003 defines hard limits on any requested Additional Actual Loan Amount. BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 requires a Seller calculation and a new Chapter 60-compliant appraisal supporting the requested amount. If the request exceeds the cap, the appraisal/calc package cannot result in an approvable request, so the cap rule is an upstream gating control.
dependencies.impact: If the cap rule is not satisfied, the Seller may waste time/cost ordering a new appraisal and preparing a calculation that Freddie will reject as not allowable. This creates delays at Conversion and may force the Seller to re-size the request and re-underwrite.
examples: Confirmation Sheet allows 10% Additional Actual Loan Amount; Seller requests 15%. Even with a new appraisal, Freddie will deny the request as exceeding the cap—cap check should have blocked the documentation workflow.
Seller requests an increase but the Actual Loan Amount would exceed the TEL outstanding immediately prior to Conversion; the request is disallowed regardless of appraisal support.
evidence_quotes: “increase may not exceed the Additional Actual Loan Amount Percentage...”
“must include... calculation... and a new Appraisal... supports the requested Additional Actual Loan Amount”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 requires a 'new Appraisal that complies with Chapter 60'; BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_VALIDATION_022_009 is a Chapter-60 appraisal transmission control (PCR issues provided to appraiser before appraisal is sent to Freddie).
dependencies.rationale: If property condition report issues may materially affect value, the appraiser must be provided the report before the appraisal is transmitted to Freddie. Since BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 requires an appraisal that will be delivered as part of the Conversion Underwriting Package, this appraisal must satisfy the pre-transmission condition report integration rule where applicable.
dependencies.impact: If the PCR is not provided when required, Freddie may reject the appraisal or condition the underwriting review, forcing appraisal revisions and causing Conversion timeline slippage.
examples: Seller orders a new appraisal for Additional Actual Loan Amount, but fails to provide the consultant’s property condition report noting foundation cracking; Freddie requires appraisal revision after the appraiser reviews PCR, delaying Conversion.
Draft PCR is provided but final PCR changes materially; appraiser is not given final, causing Freddie to require an updated appraisal addendum.
evidence_quotes: “before the Seller/Servicer transmits the Appraisal to Freddie Mac, ... provide the appraiser with the property condition report”
“must include... a new Appraisal that complies with Chapter 60”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 requires a Chapter 60 compliant appraisal; BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_VALIDATION_022_010 sets baseline appraisal requirements for zoning/legal issue discussion and authoritative zoning source citation.
dependencies.rationale: A 'new Appraisal that complies with Chapter 60' must include required zoning/legal analysis. Therefore, any appraisal delivered to support the Additional Actual Loan Amount request must satisfy the zoning source and minimum zoning/legal content rules (parking ratio, density, rebuildability).
dependencies.impact: If zoning/legal issues are not properly addressed and sourced, Freddie may deem the appraisal deficient and refuse to use it to support the increased loan amount, requiring revisions and potentially blocking the request.
examples: Appraisal supports higher value but fails to cite the authoritative zoning source or discuss rebuildability after casualty; Freddie conditions the appraisal and pauses the Additional Actual Loan Amount review.
Appraisal omits density compliance discussion for a multifamily asset in a constrained zoning district; Freddie rejects the appraisal as not Chapter 60 compliant.
evidence_quotes: “For all mortgages, the appraiser must consider... zoning and other legal issues...”
“must include... a new Appraisal that complies with Chapter 60”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE (3) because it applies only for Acquisition Lease-Up or Refinance Lease-Up programs; when applicable, the appraisal delivered under BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 must contain the required as-is and as-stabilized values to be considered compliant/supportive.
dependencies.rationale: If the underlying transaction is a Lease-Up program, then the 'new Appraisal' required by BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 must incorporate the two-value structure required by BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_DOCUMENTATION_034_004, because Freddie Mac Underwriting Value is based on the prospective as-stabilized value.
dependencies.impact: If the Lease-Up valuation components are missing, Freddie cannot rely on the appraisal to support underwriting value for the increased loan amount request, triggering appraisal revision and underwriting delay.
examples: Lease-Up refinance at TEL Conversion: appraisal includes only as-is value; Freddie requires addition of prospective as-stabilized value before considering Additional Actual Loan Amount.
Appraisal includes as-stabilized value but not clearly dated to the prospective stabilized-operations date; Freddie conditions the file.
evidence_quotes: “For Properties... Lease-Up... the Appraisal must include (i) the as-is value... and (ii) the prospective as-stabilized value...”
“must include... a new Appraisal...”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE (3) because it applies only when the Mortgage is a Moderate Rehabilitation Mortgage; in those cases, the appraisal in BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 must contain both as-is and hypothetical as-if renovated/stabilized values.
dependencies.rationale: BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_DOCUMENTATION_021_004 requires the new appraisal to comply with Chapter 60; for Mod Rehab transactions, Chapter 60 compliance includes dual valuation scenarios per BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_DOCUMENTATION_034_005.
dependencies.impact: If the Mod Rehab dual values are missing, Freddie may reject the appraisal as failing program-specific valuation requirements, delaying the Additional Actual Loan Amount review and potentially Conversion.
examples: TAH transaction includes Mod Rehab scope; appraisal supports value only 'as-is' and omits hypothetical as-if renovated value; Freddie requires a revised appraisal.
Appraiser provides as-if renovated value but does not treat it as hypothetical 'renovated and stabilized today'—Freddie conditions the appraisal methodology.
evidence_quotes: “For a Moderate Rehabilitation (Mod Rehab) Mortgage, the appraiser must provide two values...”
“must include... a new Appraisal that complies with Chapter 60”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE (3) because it applies when restricted units are present; the appraisal used to support Additional Actual Loan Amount must then include both restricted and hypothetical unrestricted values plus the HBU/phase-out analysis.
dependencies.rationale: If the property includes restricted units affecting rent levels, the appraisal supporting any increase request must capture the required restricted/unrestricted valuation scenarios; otherwise the appraisal is incomplete for underwriting reliance.
dependencies.impact: If restricted unit valuation analysis is omitted, Freddie may determine value support is insufficient and suspend/condition the increased amount request pending appraisal revision.
examples: TEL Conversion increase request for a LIHTC property: appraisal provides only value with restrictions; Freddie requires hypothetical unrestricted value and phase-out timing analysis.
Appraisal asserts HBU is to phase out restrictions but fails to reflect the time needed to phase in market rents in the as-is valuation; Freddie conditions the appraisal.
evidence_quotes: “If a Property has restricted units, the appraiser must provide... market value with restricted units... and... without restricted units.”
“must include... a new Appraisal...”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_VALIDATION_034_007 validates the core rent roll assumptions and continuation of restricted rents that underpin the restricted/unrestricted valuation scenarios required by BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_DOCUMENTATION_034_006.
dependencies.rationale: BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_DOCUMENTATION_034_006 requires restricted and unrestricted valuation outputs. BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_VALIDATION_034_007 then requires documenting the restricted rent roll source and continuation/expiry methodology, which is necessary to make the valuation outputs reliable and acceptable to Freddie.
dependencies.impact: If restricted rent roll source/continuation analysis is not documented, Freddie may treat the restricted-rent valuation as unsupported, triggering appraisal revision and underwriting delays.
examples: Appraisal includes both values but provides no source for the restricted rent roll; Freddie conditions the appraisal for lack of support.
Appraiser assumes restrictions expire soon but fails to apply methodology reflecting likelihood/timing; Freddie requires revised valuation approach.
evidence_quotes: “must document the source of the Property’s restricted rent roll...”
“must provide... value with restricted units... and... without restricted units.”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_EXCEPTION_006_007 allows omission of a separate zoning report only if the appraisal contains the specified zoning analysis; BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_VALIDATION_022_010 defines what that zoning/legal analysis must include and how it must be sourced.
dependencies.rationale: The zoning-report exception is only safe/allowable if the appraisal’s zoning analysis is robust and compliant. BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_VALIDATION_022_010 establishes the baseline appraisal zoning/legal discussion requirements (authoritative source, parking/density/rebuildability). Those elements are prerequisites to using an appraisal as the substitute for a separate zoning report under BR_REQUIRES_APPRAISAL_EXCEPTION_006_007.
dependencies.impact: If the appraisal lacks required zoning source and minimum zoning/legal analysis, the Seller cannot rely on the exception and may be required to obtain a separate zoning report—creating delays and potential underwriting package rejection if omitted.
examples: Non-SBL $18MM refinance: Seller omits zoning report relying on appraisal, but appraisal does not cite authoritative zoning source; Freddie requires a zoning report or appraisal revision.
Appraisal addresses zoning generally but omits rebuildability restrictions after casualty; Freddie deems the appraisal insufficient to replace the zoning report.
evidence_quotes: “A separate zoning report is not required if the zoning analysis... is provided in the Appraisal...”
“appraiser must... reference the authoritative zoning source... addressing at minimum: parking... density... rebuildability...”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_EXCEPTION_033_010 is triggered when the insurance evidenced/submitted under BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_033_009 does not meet Freddie Mac requirements; the waiver workflow depends on first collecting/submitting insurance information to identify the deficiency.
dependencies.rationale: The insurance waiver is an exception-handling branch that only exists after the Seller evaluates the Borrower’s insurance coverage against Freddie’s requirements (using Form 1133 and supporting documents). Therefore, the exception request is conditionally dependent on the insurance documentation and compliance determination step.
dependencies.impact: If insurance documentation is not submitted/evaluated early enough, noncompliant coverage may be discovered too late to request a waiver prior to origination, potentially blocking closing/origination and delaying purchase/funding.
examples: ICT submission shows liability limits below Freddie minimums; Seller requests waiver before origination per BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_EXCEPTION_033_010.
Seller delays collecting insurance documents until just before origination; deficiency is found but waiver cannot be processed in time, causing origination to slip.
evidence_quotes: “must submit... Form 1133... and the documents listed...”
“If the Borrower’s insurance will not meet... requirements, ... request a waiver prior to the Origination”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because the Breakage Fee in BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_CALCULATION_020_007 applies only upon nondelivery; failure to deliver the final package by the Mandatory Delivery Date (BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_020_006) is a primary operational cause of nondelivery under the standard delivery option.
dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_020_006 establishes the mandatory timing and completeness requirements for an SBL Final Delivery Package. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_CALCULATION_020_007 then defines liquidated damages for nondelivery. In automated workflows, a missed/failed final delivery is a trigger condition that can escalate to a nondelivery determination and fee calculation.
dependencies.impact: If final delivery is not completed on time (or not completed at all), Freddie may determine nondelivery and assess the 2% Breakage Fee, along with other Guide remedies. Operationally this drives financial loss, dispute handling, and potential counterparty performance monitoring.
examples: Seller misses noon ET on Mandatory Delivery Date and does not deliver the Electronic Delivery Package; Freddie declares nondelivery and assesses 2% of the proposed SBL amount.
Seller delivers partial documents but omits required items and does not cure by the deadline; nondelivery is determined and the Breakage Fee is applied.
evidence_quotes: “must deliver all documents... at or before noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date”
“As liquidated damages for the nondelivery of an SBL Mortgage... Freddie Mac will charge... a Breakage Fee...”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) because BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_CONSTRAINT_029_006 (fee assessment) is explicitly conditioned on failure of BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_029_005 (timely/accurate delinquency reporting). The fee rule cannot apply unless the reporting rule is violated.
dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_029_005 sets the submission requirement (third Business Day by 3:00 p.m. ET; usable, accurate, timely). BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_CONSTRAINT_029_006 defines the graduated noncompliance fees when reporting is late/omitted/unusable. This is a direct trigger relationship used for automated fee assessment.
dependencies.impact: If delinquency reporting is late/omitted/unusable, fees are assessed and must be remitted by the stated due date, creating immediate financial and compliance consequences and potential escalation with Freddie Mac.
examples: Servicer transmits the report on the fourth Business Day; Freddie assesses the first-month $250 fee and sets the fee due date per the accounting cutoff rule.
Servicer submits on time but data file is unusable; Freddie treats it as a violation and assesses the applicable noncompliance fee.
evidence_quotes: “must perform the automated transmission... by... the third Business Day of each month”
“If a Servicer fails to provide timely, complete, and accurate... data... Freddie Mac may assess... noncompliance fees...”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE (3) because these are lifecycle steps in the same Repair Agreement tracking workflow: progress/on-track notice (038_001) logically precedes completion confirmation notice (038_002), although completion notice can still occur even if the earlier progress notice was missed.
dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_038_001 requires PRS notification within 30 days of the Repair Agreement completion date that the Borrower is on track, with evidence. BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_038_002 then requires notification within 10 Business Days after the Servicer confirms repairs are complete. They form a standard sequential monitoring-to-closure pattern for repair oversight.
dependencies.impact: If the on-track notice is missed, Freddie may have reduced visibility into repair progress and may increase scrutiny. Even if completion notice is later provided, the Servicer may have a compliance finding for the earlier missed milestone and may need to provide remediation/updates.
examples: Servicer posts PRS evidence (executed repair contract) within 30 days of completion date, then later confirms completion and notifies Freddie within 10 business days.
Servicer fails to submit the on-track notice, then submits completion notice; Freddie flags the missing milestone and requires corrective action.
evidence_quotes: “notify Freddie Mac... within 30 calendar days of the Repair Agreement completion date...”
“notify Freddie Mac within 10 Business Days following... confirmation... repairs... complete”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because when repairs are Utility Efficiency Improvements or Green Improvements, BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_DOCUMENTATION_038_003 prescribes the mandatory form (Verification Certification via PRS) and timing constraints that govern how the Servicer satisfies the completion notification obligation in BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_038_002.
dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_PROCESS_038_002 sets a general completion-notification requirement. BR_REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY_DOCUMENTATION_038_003 is a special-case control that replaces the generic 'notify' mechanism with a specific certification instrument and adds a 30-day certification collection requirement. Thus, for green/utility improvement repairs, compliance with 038_002 is achieved through the more specific 038_003 pathway.
dependencies.impact: If the Servicer sends a generic completion notice without the required Verification Certification (or fails to collect it within 30 days), Freddie may deem the completion notification noncompliant, require re-submission in the correct form, and issue compliance findings.
examples: Green Improvements completed: Servicer collects the Green Improvements Verification Certification within 30 days and submits it via PRS as the completion notice.
Servicer emails completion confirmation but does not submit the required certification via PRS; Freddie rejects the notice and requires correct certification submission.
evidence_quotes: “completion notification... must be made on the applicable Verification Certification... submitted via PRS”
“must notify Freddie Mac within 10 Business Days...”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE (3) because BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_012_003 (expense reimbursement) only arises in the operational context of pledged mortgage deliveries by a warehouse lender; BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_032_010 governs the authorization documentation and Form 987M needed to settle those pledged mortgage deliveries under cash programs/products.
dependencies.rationale: While not a strict legal prerequisite, the reimbursement scenario is downstream of a pledged mortgage delivery that settles successfully. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_032_010 is a key delivery/settlement control ('settlements cannot be made if... documentation is not on file'), making it a practical upstream step in the same pledged mortgage delivery lifecycle in which reimbursement requests can later occur.
dependencies.impact: If authorization documentation and Form 987M are not on file, settlement cannot occur and the pledged mortgage delivery process is interrupted; as a result, the downstream reimbursement event may not occur, but the Seller faces delivery failure/operational disruption and potential rescheduling costs.
examples: Seller attempts cash warehouse delivery but Form 987M is missing; settlement is blocked and the delivery does not complete—preventing downstream reconciliation events such as expense notices.
Authorization is on file, settlement completes, and later Freddie issues a written notice of delivery-related expenses; Seller must reimburse within 10 Business Days.
evidence_quotes: “settlements cannot be made if the authorization documentation is not on file”
“For a delivery of a pledged Mortgage... Seller must reimburse...”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_012_002 is conditioned on the existence of a warehouse-lender financing statement; BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_025_004 requires UCC/public record searches that are the practical mechanism to discover those financing statements and route them for resolution.
dependencies.rationale: The prohibition rule requires a termination statement if the warehouse lender filed a financing statement. The UCC search rule requires searching every filing office under Revised Article 9 and explaining financing statements found to counsel prior to origination. In practice, you cannot reliably comply with the financing-statement termination prohibition without the upstream UCC search control to detect and document the filing.
dependencies.impact: If UCC searches are not performed comprehensively, a warehouse financing statement may remain undiscovered and un-terminated, leading Freddie to refuse purchase of the pledged Mortgage and forcing last-minute cures/recording actions after Funding Date.
examples: UCC search reveals warehouse lender UCC-1 filed against pledged mortgage collateral; Seller obtains executed termination statement as required to preserve purchase eligibility.
Seller performs an incomplete UCC search and misses a filing in an alternate jurisdiction; Freddie later identifies the filing and blocks purchase pending termination.
evidence_quotes: “must perform public record searches including UCC searches... include every office where a financing statement would be filed”
“will not purchase... if the warehouse lender has filed a financing statement... unless... executes a termination statement”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because the PLIM (BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_035_001) is a counsel-prepared legal issues memorandum supporting underwriting/package submission; BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_025_004 requires identifying and explaining UCC financing statements (a core legal issue) to the applicable Multifamily Attorney prior to origination, which is a key input to PLIM/legal issue documentation.
dependencies.rationale: The UCC search rule creates issue identification and explanation outputs (financing statements and required explanations/amended analyses) that counsel must consider and typically incorporate into legal issue memoranda. Therefore, completing UCC searches and routing exceptions is an upstream prerequisite to producing a complete and accurate PLIM for underwriting package submission.
dependencies.impact: If UCC searches and exception explanations are not completed, the PLIM may be incomplete/incorrect, creating legal delivery holds, additional counsel review cycles, and potential origination delays.
examples: UCC search identifies an unreleased filing unrelated to the current lender; counsel includes the issue and resolution path in the PLIM submitted with the underwriting package.
PLIM is drafted before UCC searches complete; later-discovered filings require an amended PLIM and re-review, delaying approval.
evidence_quotes: “any financing statements found... must be explained to the applicable Multifamily Attorney”
“must submit... a Preliminary Legal Issues Memorandum (PLIM)... when... submits an underwriting package...”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because BR_SECURES_ELIGIBILITY_010_003 requires recorded Shared Use Agreement terms (perpetual access/use, included in insured legal description, etc.); BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_019_001 requires the underwriting package to include facility descriptions and legal counsel confirmation/gap analysis regarding Shared Access/Use Agreement compliance—i.e., the evidentiary package used to validate eligibility.
dependencies.rationale: Eligibility for shared/off-site facilities depends on the Shared Use Agreement meeting specific legal/recording conditions. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_019_001 is the underwriting-package documentation control that collects and presents counsel’s confirmation or gap analysis about Shared Access/Use Agreement compliance and facility descriptions, enabling Freddie to validate that the eligibility requirements in BR_SECURES_ELIGIBILITY_010_003 are actually met.
dependencies.impact: If the documentation package does not include the required shared facility descriptions and counsel confirmation/gap analysis, Freddie may be unable to confirm eligibility and may condition or reject purchase consideration until the missing documentation is cured.
examples: SBL property uses recreational facilities on an adjacent parcel; Seller provides counsel confirmation that the Shared Use Agreement is recorded and meets perpetual access requirements—supporting Freddie’s eligibility review.
Seller submits an SBL package without a shared facility gap analysis; Freddie conditions the file and pauses purchase review pending legal documentation.
evidence_quotes: “must submit... legal counsel confirmation of Shared Access/Use Agreement compliance or a detailed gap analysis”
“only if the Shared Use Agreement is recorded... included in the insured legal description... provides perpetual access/use rights...”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) because both rules jointly determine loan document acceptability for delivery/purchase: 005_003 governs completion of blanks; 005_004 governs permissible modification mechanics. Failure of either commonly results in document rejection/re-execution.
dependencies.rationale: A closing package can be defective either by incomplete/incorrect blanks (005_003) or by impermissible text alterations outside approved modification exhibits (005_004). These controls are complementary quality gates that together ensure documents are in Freddie-acceptable form.
dependencies.impact: If blanks are not completed correctly or modifications are made improperly, documents may be rejected, requiring cure, re-execution, and potentially delaying funding/purchase.
examples: Seller properly completes blanks but changes body text directly instead of using a modification exhibit; Freddie rejects the document under 005_004 and requires re-execution.
Seller uses the correct modification exhibit but leaves required form fields blank; the document is deficient under 005_003 and must be corrected.
evidence_quotes: “must complete each blank in a Loan Document...”
“may not make any changes... by altering the text of the body of the document...”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG conditional dependency: BR_PURCHASES_ELIGIBILITY_019_003 only becomes the operative eligibility path when Freddie Mac has NOT required the reserve/additional recourse described in BR_PURCHASES_EXCEPTION_019_002 (Section 8.6(c) reference).
dependencies.rationale: BR_PURCHASES_ELIGIBILITY_019_003 explicitly conditions its applicability on Freddie Mac not requiring a reserve of funds or additional recourse under Section 8.6(c), which is the same decision space described in BR_PURCHASES_EXCEPTION_019_002 (Freddie may require modifications and/or reserves). Operationally, the workflow must first determine whether Freddie imposed those conditions; only then can the seller know whether they may proceed under the alternate cross-collateralization/cross-default structure path in BR_PURCHASES_ELIGIBILITY_019_003.
