Local zoning enforcement action is pending and could affect the contemplated use; Seller cannot make the required confirmation, and Freddie conditions purchase on resolution.
evidence_quotes: For an SBL Mortgage to be eligible for purchase by Freddie Mac, the Property must conform...
Seller must confirm that all required certificates of occupancy... have been issued and are in effect

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE (3) because Exclusionary List screening and physical risk report submission can be performed in parallel, but sequencing screening early is a practical workflow dependency to avoid investing in reports for loans that will be ineligible for sale.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 determines fundamental eligibility for sale based on Exclusionary List role matches and requires use of the most current list. BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 requires a compliant SBL Physical Risk Report to obtain commitment. While the risk report does not logically require screening results, compliance operations typically sequence eligibility screening early to prevent wasted third-party report costs and cycle time on ineligible transactions.
dependencies.impact: If BR_SELLER_SERVICER_COMPLIANCE_018_009 fails after the Physical Risk Report is ordered/submitted, the Seller may incur unnecessary vendor expense and delays, then still have to stop/withdraw the loan due to ineligibility.
examples: Seller orders a Physical Risk Report immediately; later, the mortgage broker is discovered on the Exclusionary List in a prohibited role, and the loan is cancelled—report cost is sunk.
Pipeline workflow: system prompts 'Run Exclusionary List screen' before allowing 'Upload Physical Risk Report for commitment issuance' to reduce rework.
evidence_quotes: Seller/Servicer must ensure it uses only the most current version
Mortgage is not eligible for sale... if a party on the Exclusionary List played one of the specified roles
requires... submit an SBL Physical Risk Report... before Freddie Mac will issue a Letter of Commitment

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG dependency: both rules are independent purchase/approval blockers based on restricted parties lists. A robust eligibility workflow must apply both screens; checking only one creates a high risk of selling/approving an ineligible transaction.
dependencies.rationale: BR_018_009 requires using the most current Exclusionary List and makes a Mortgage ineligible if a listed party played certain roles in origination/transfer/real estate transaction (subject to an exception). BR_035_002 prohibits purchase/Transfer of Ownership/servicing approvals if Borrower/principal/property manager is a Named Party on FHFA SCP (subject to final suspension order conditions/exclusions). These are complementary negative screens that collectively define counterparty eligibility constraints; both are needed for a complete compliance determination.
dependencies.impact: If BR_018_009 is followed but BR_035_002 is not (or vice versa): (1) Freddie Mac may later deny purchase/approval or require unwind actions, (2) Seller/Servicer may incur repurchase/indemnification exposure or operational losses, and (3) reputational and compliance findings risk increases due to inadequate restricted-party screening.
examples: Borrower is not on Freddie’s Exclusionary List (BR_018_009 passes) but the property management company is a Named Party on FHFA SCP. Under BR_035_002, Freddie will not purchase/approve the transaction; failure to run the SCP screen causes late-stage denial.

Originator uses the latest FHFA SCP list but fails to use the most current Freddie Exclusionary List updated monthly; an excluded broker participated in origination, making the mortgage ineligible under BR_018_009.
evidence_quotes: BR_018_009: "Exclusionary List is updated at least monthly... must ensure it uses only the most current version... Mortgage is not eligible for sale"
BR_035_002: "will not purchase any Mortgage... if any Borrower... or property management company is a Named Party on the FHFA... SCP List"

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE dependency: both are purchase-eligibility gates that must be cleared during a purchase review; they address different ineligibility vectors (restricted parties vs title policy exceptions). They are commonly implemented as a combined eligibility checklist.
dependencies.rationale: BR_018_009 is an eligibility screen based on participation by excluded parties. BR_025_007 is a title-policy-based ineligibility rule for private transfer fee covenants created on/after Feb 8, 2011. While neither rule is a literal prerequisite to the other, purchase eligibility workflows should treat them as complementary checks that together determine whether a mortgage can be purchased.
dependencies.impact: If BR_018_009 is executed but BR_025_007 is not: (1) the loan may progress toward purchase despite a title-policy exception that makes it categorically ineligible, (2) late discovery causes purchase denial and pipeline fallout, and (3) corrective actions (endorsements/curative title work) may be impossible if the covenant is disqualifying.
examples: Seller confirms all parties clear the Exclusionary List, but title review later identifies a Private Transfer Fee Covenant created in 2015. Under BR_025_007 the mortgage is ineligible; the deal is declined late due to missing the title-based check.
Title policy includes a private transfer fee exception; compliance flags ineligibility early when BR_025_007 is run as part of the same eligibility checklist as BR_018_009.
evidence_quotes: BR_018_009: "Mortgage is not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac" (when excluded party played specified role)
BR_025_007: "If the Title Policy contains an exception for a Private Transfer Fee Covenant... the Mortgage is ineligible for purchase"

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE dependency: both are property-related purchase eligibility checks (zoning/CO legality vs. title policy exception). For SBL purchase readiness, they operate as complementary file completion/eligibility validations.
dependencies.rationale: BR_016_005 requires confirmation of zoning/building code compliance and certificates of occupancy (or confirmation that absence is not a violation with enforcement risk). BR_025_007 makes the mortgage ineligible if the title policy contains a disqualifying private transfer fee covenant exception created on/after Feb 8, 2011. Both must be satisfied for a clean property/legal compliance posture; failure of either can stop purchase.
dependencies.impact: If BR_016_005 is satisfied but BR_025_007 is missed (or vice versa): (1) the loan file may appear ‘property compliant’ while still being ineligible for purchase due to title exception, (2) late-stage purchase denial occurs, and (3) curative actions may be costly or impossible depending on the covenant/exception.
examples: SBL deal has valid certificates of occupancy and no zoning issues (BR_016_005 satisfied), but the title policy includes an exception for a 2012 private transfer fee covenant; BR_025_007 makes it ineligible and the purchase is declined.

Title policy is clean of private transfer fee covenants (BR_025_007 satisfied) but the property lacks a required certificate of occupancy and local enforcement action risk exists; BR_016_005 fails and the loan is not eligible for purchase until resolved.
evidence_quotes: BR_016_005: "Property must conform to... zoning... building and housing codes... confirm... certificates of occupancy... are in effect"
BR_025_007: "Title Policy contains an exception for a Private Transfer Fee Covenant... Mortgage is ineligible"

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: If a sanctions/exclusion confirmed match exists, Freddie Mac will not approve a Transfer of Ownership; the property-condition documentation package becomes moot for approval purposes.
dependencies.rationale: BR_007_003 states Freddie Mac will not 'allow a Transfer of Ownership' if the Borrower/Borrower Principal/Guarantor is a confirmed sanctions/exclusion match. Therefore, sanctions clearance is a hard gate before expending effort on BR_027_010 documentation to support a transaction that cannot be approved.
dependencies.impact: If BR_007_003 fails (confirmed match), the Transfer of Ownership cannot be approved regardless of BR_027_010 compliance; continuing processing wastes time/cost and can create escalation/audit issues for servicing-related approvals.
examples: Incoming buyer’s principal matches OFAC SDN: Transfer of Ownership is blocked under BR_007_003 even if a complete PCR/1108 package under BR_027_010 is delivered.
Guarantor is on the Freddie Mac Exclusionary List: Freddie will not allow the transfer; ordering a Form 1108 becomes unnecessary rework.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not purchase a Mortgage, allow a Transfer of Ownership... if it determines the Borrower, Borrower Principal, or Guarantor is a confirmed match to specified sanctions/exclusion lists...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: Sanctions/exclusion match blocks Transfer of Ownership approvals; the AIF/inspection update requirement is irrelevant if the transfer cannot proceed.
dependencies.rationale: BR_027_009 is part of the Transfer of Ownership submission workflow. BR_007_003 is an absolute approval prohibition for a confirmed sanctions match. In compliance workflow automation, sanctions screening is therefore a must-pass gate before completing BR_027_009 tasks.
dependencies.impact: If BR_007_003 fails, Transfer of Ownership is denied/blocked; performing BR_027_009 inspections and updating AIF creates avoidable operational cost and delay without any path to approval.
examples: AIF is 14 months old (SBL) so BR_027_009 would require an updated inspection/AIF; however, buyer’s principal is on the FHFA Suspended Counterparty List → the transfer cannot be approved.
Seller requests transfer; servicer schedules physical inspection for AIF update, but later sanctions screening identifies an OFAC match; work must be canceled/escalated.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not... allow a Transfer of Ownership...
the Servicer must conduct a physical inspection of the Property and deliver an updated AIF to Freddie Mac.

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE dependency (3) because prohibited transfer reporting (BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_020_010) is commonly triggered during Transfer of Ownership intake/review workflows where documentation such as PCR/1108/AIF is being gathered and assessed (BR_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_027_010). Not a strict logical prerequisite, but a practical workflow sequencing dependency for automation and case handling.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_020_010 starts "After confirming that a prohibited Transfer of Ownership has occurred" and requires a rapid information package submission. In operational practice, confirmation often occurs while processing or reconciling transfer submissions and required documentation. BR_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_027_010 is part of that transfer submission workflow and is a likely upstream stage where issues are identified (e.g., buyer identity, conditions, or unapproved changes).
dependencies.impact: If the Servicer does not integrate prohibited-transfer detection into the transfer documentation workflow, confirmation may be delayed, causing the Servicer to miss the two Business Day reporting window and increasing enforcement/audit exposure.
examples: During review of the Transfer of Ownership package, Servicer discovers ownership already changed without approval. The Servicer must pivot from transfer documentation processing to the prohibited-transfer incident package submission within two Business Days per BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_020_010.

A transfer request arrives lacking required details; while curing documentation, Servicer confirms the property was already conveyed. This triggers BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_020_010 reporting deadlines immediately.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_020_010: "After confirming that a prohibited Transfer of Ownership has occurred... submit required information... within two Business Days."
BR_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_027_010: "For Transfers of Ownership, the Servicer must deliver either..."

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG conflict (4) because BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 states subordinate financing is not permitted for any securitized mortgage except the Supplemental Mortgage Product, while BR_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_026_008 describes conditions under which subordinate financing is permitted for an SBL Mortgage securitized in an SBL Securitization. If both are applied to the same population without an explicit exception mapping, compliance decisions can directly diverge.
dependencies.rationale: One rule sets a broad prohibition for securitized mortgages; the other provides conditional permission for a specific securitized SBL scenario. This is either (a) an intended exception (e.g., 026_008 applies only when the subordinate financing is the permitted Supplemental Mortgage Product), or (b) a true inconsistency between guide sections. Either way, rule logic must be reconciled in automation and policy guidance.
dependencies.impact: If unresolved, the Servicer may (i) incorrectly deny permissible supplemental subordinate financing (business disruption), or (ii) incorrectly approve subordinate financing that is prohibited under securitization rules (high compliance breach, forced remediation, Freddie Mac enforcement/remedies).
examples: Borrower requests subordinate financing on an SBL Mortgage in an SBL Securitization. System checks BR_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_026_008 and approves based on 3+ years remaining and $750k minimum, but BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 would deny unless it is explicitly the Supplemental Mortgage Product.

Servicer policy states all securitized mortgages cannot have subordinate financing; a request that should be allowed under a defined supplemental product pathway is incorrectly rejected because the system does not map 026_008 as an exception under 025_005.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005: "For any Mortgage purchased for Securitization, Freddie Mac does not permit Subordinate Financing except... Supplemental Mortgage Product."
BR_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_026_008: "For an SBL Mortgage securitized in an SBL Securitization, Subordinate Financing is permitted only if..."

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG conflict (4) for the same reason as the 025_005 → 026_008 conflict: BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 broadly prohibits subordinate financing on securitized mortgages except a narrow product, while BR_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_026_009 provides underwriting thresholds for subordinate financing on a securitized SBL Mortgage.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_026_009 is meaningful only if subordinate financing is allowed to be considered. BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 may categorically bar consideration unless the request falls within the Supplemental Mortgage Product exception. The knowledge graph should force a rule selection step: determine whether 026_009 is reachable under the 025_005 exception logic.
dependencies.impact: If unresolved, automation may approve a transaction based on 80% LTV / 1.25x DCR that is still prohibited for securitization purposes, or may incorrectly block a permitted supplemental transaction by never evaluating 026_009.
examples: A securitized SBL Mortgage subordinate loan meets LTV 78% and DCR 1.30x under BR_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_026_009; if the request is not the Supplemental Mortgage Product, BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 still mandates denial.
Underwriter assumes 026_009 governs all securitized SBL cases; compliance audit flags violation citing 025_005’s securitization prohibition.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005: "...does not permit Subordinate Financing except... Supplemental Mortgage Product."
BR_SERVICER_CONSTRAINT_026_009: "For Subordinate Financing on an SBL Mortgage securitized in an SBL Securitization... combined loan amount... must not produce an LTV Ratio exceeding 80%..."

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE dependency (3) because BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 provides a categorical definition of when subordinate financing is not permitted for securitized mortgages; that prohibition is a strong driver of whether a lien is 'unauthorized' (triggering BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_007). Not absolute because 'unauthorized' can also arise from missing approvals even where a product exception exists.
dependencies.rationale: When a mortgage is purchased for securitization, BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 states subordinate financing is not permitted except the Supplemental Mortgage Product. If subordinate financing is placed or imminent outside the exception, it is by definition unauthorized/prohibited, which should trigger the immediate notice requirement in BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_007 upon the Servicer learning of it.
dependencies.impact: If the Servicer does not apply the securitization prohibition logic, it may fail to classify a subordinate lien as unauthorized and miss the immediate written notice requirement, increasing Freddie Mac risk and exposing the Servicer to remedies.
examples: Securitized mortgage: borrower records a second lien that is not a Freddie Mac Supplemental Mortgage Product. Under BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005 it is prohibited, so upon discovery the Servicer must notify immediately under BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_007.

Securitized mortgage: borrower requests subordinate financing; Servicer mistakenly treats it like a non-securitized consent case and delays escalation, leading to a late notification breach under BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_007.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_PROHIBITION_025_005: "...does not permit Subordinate Financing except... Supplemental Mortgage Product."
BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_026_007: "...unauthorized Subordinate Financing... Servicer must immediately notify..."

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE dependency (3) because both rules govern remittance timing, but BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 applies specifically to 'Gold PC (45-Day PC) Securitized Mortgages' with a distinct schedule. In practice, the remittance engine must select the correct schedule; the product-specific rule functions as an override/alternate remittance regime versus a general remittance timing rule.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_006 and BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 both define remittance deadlines. BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 is explicitly scoped to Gold PC securitized mortgages and specifies a different collection window and due date logic. For automation, the system must treat Gold PC classification as a rule-selection switch to avoid applying an incompatible remittance timetable.
dependencies.impact: If the wrong remittance rule is applied, remittances may be systematically late (or mis-timed), triggering interest on late remittances, remedies, and repeated reconciliation breaks between Servicer and Freddie Mac records.
examples: Mortgage is classified as Gold PC securitized; Servicer remits on the 15th schedule per BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 instead of a next-month second-business-day schedule.
System mistakenly applies BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_006 to a Gold PC loan; remittances occur off-cycle relative to required Gold PC windows, leading to late-remittance findings.
evidence_quotes: BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010: "For Gold PC (45-Day PC) Securitized Mortgages, the Servicer must remit... by the 15th of the month..."
BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_006: "...Servicer must remit... not later than the second Business Day of the month following the month of receipt..."

dependencies.strength_justification: Critical override: the transfer cannot be approved if any relevant party is on the FHFA SCP List, regardless of AIF/PCR/inspection compliance. This is an absolute blocker to the transaction approval.
dependencies.rationale: BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 states Freddie Mac will not allow or approve any Transfer of Ownership if a Borrower/Borrower Principal/property management company is a Named Party on the FHFA SCP List. Therefore, Transfer-of-Ownership processing steps (including AIF/inspection requirements in BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_009) are subordinate to and overridden by the SCP screening outcome.
dependencies.impact: If the SCP-screening prohibition is triggered, completing BR_SERVICER_PROCESS_027_009 does not salvage the transaction; the Transfer of Ownership approval must be denied/blocked. If the SCP check is missed, the Servicer risks advancing a prohibited transaction and creating severe compliance/audit findings.
examples: Transfer request package includes updated AIF and inspection, but the proposed new property manager matches an SCP Named Party; Freddie Mac will not approve the transfer.
Servicer schedules inspection and updates AIF, but later discovers borrower principal is on SCP List; effort is wasted and the transfer is stopped.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not purchase any Mortgage and will not allow or approve any Transfer of Ownership ... if any Borrower, Borrower Principal, or property management company is a Named Party on the FHFA Suspended Counterparty Program (SCP) List...

dependencies.strength_justification: Critical override: documentation completeness for a transfer package is irrelevant if SCP prohibition applies; Freddie Mac cannot approve the transfer.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICER_DOCUMENTATION_027_010 governs which physical condition reports are required for a Transfer of Ownership submission. BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 blocks Freddie Mac approval of any Transfer of Ownership if an SCP match exists, overriding the otherwise-applicable transfer documentation requirements.
dependencies.impact: Missing the SCP screen may lead to extensive PCR/1108 procurement for a transfer that must be denied; conversely, detecting SCP match early prevents unnecessary third-party report costs and prevents prohibited approval attempts.
examples: Servicer orders a property condition report due to deferred maintenance triggers, but an SCP hit on the buyer’s property management company blocks approval.
Transfer package is complete with a compliant Form 1108, yet borrower principal is a Named Party; the transfer cannot be approved.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not ... allow or approve any Transfer of Ownership ... if any Borrower, Borrower Principal, or property management company is a Named Party on the ... SCP List...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: final delivery package compilation is downstream of a purchasable transaction; sanctions prohibition stops purchase, making delivery package submission non-actionable.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not purchase a Mortgage... if it determines the Borrower... is a confirmed match
Final Delivery Package must include a Non-Consolidation Opinion...
dependencies.rationale: A Non-Consolidation Opinion is a final delivery requirement for certain non-SBL transactions. However, if the deal is prohibited under sanctions screening, the delivery process should stop before document assembly/review to avoid operational waste and reduce legal risk.
dependencies.impact: If sanctions screening fails after preparing the opinion, the lender incurs unnecessary legal cost and time; automated workflow should gate document requests on a cleared sanctions status.
examples: Legal counsel drafts a non-consolidation opinion for a $50MM loan; sanctions match later blocks purchase—avoidable cost if sanctions was screened first.
Seller/Servicer attempts to upload final delivery docs; system blocks submission due to confirmed sanctions match.

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: earthquake insurance/retrofit eligibility only matters if the transaction is not prohibited by sanctions; otherwise purchase is blocked regardless of property risk mitigations.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not purchase a Mortgage... if it determines the Borrower... is a confirmed match
dependencies.rationale: Property risk and insurance compliance checks are downstream of basic counterparty permissibility. Sanctions is a global prohibition across purchase and servicing-related transactions; it should be evaluated early and gate further underwriting conditions.
dependencies.impact: If sanctions screening fails, requiring retrofit/insurance changes provides no path to purchase; the file should be terminated or escalated per sanctions policy.
examples: Property has SEL-475 > 20% and insurance is required, but borrower is a confirmed match on exclusion lists—purchase is denied.
System queues seismic consultant and insurance review; it should first validate sanctions clearance.

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL: earthquake insurance/retrofit decisions in BR_HAZARD_INSURANCE_POLICY_ELIGIBILITY_032_007 depend on seismic risk results (e.g., SEL-475 and stability concerns) that are typically established by the required seismic investigation workflow in BR_THIRD_PARTY_REPORT_DOCUMENTATION_004_008.
evidence_quotes: A Level 1 seismic investigation... is required... Consultant must complete and submit Form 1102...
Earthquake insurance purchase eligibility and retrofit requirements depend on SEL-475 and whether a building stability concern exists.
dependencies.rationale: BR_HAZARD_INSURANCE_POLICY_ELIGIBILITY_032_007 requires inputs (SEL-475, building stability concern) that are commonly outputs of seismic risk assessment. Operationally, the compliance workflow should obtain/validate the required SRA documentation before attempting to apply the insurance/retrofit decision logic.
dependencies.impact: If the SRA is missing/deficient, the lender may be unable to justify why earthquake insurance is or is not required, increasing the risk of purchase ineligibility or post-review findings.
examples: Property in Elevated Seismic Hazard Region with risk factors: without the Level 1 SRA and Form 1102, the file cannot reliably determine SEL-475, blocking correct insurance requirements.
Seller/servicer attempts to set earthquake insurance requirement but has not uploaded the USGS output PDF and SRA to the underwriting system.

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_007_003 is a gating prohibition for 'other servicing-related transactions' if a confirmed sanctions/exclusion list match exists, which would prevent proceeding with delegated lease approvals.
dependencies.rationale: BR_017_004 governs when the Servicer may approve a non-residential lease under delegated authority. BR_007_003 states Freddie Mac will not 'approve other servicing-related transactions' if it determines a Borrower/Principal/Guarantor is a confirmed match to sanctions/exclusion lists. Therefore, a sanctions screen outcome must be cleared before relying on delegated approval pathways for servicing actions.
dependencies.impact: If BR_007_003 fails (confirmed match) but BR_017_004 is applied anyway, the Servicer may proceed with an action that Freddie Mac will not permit, causing immediate escalation, potential rejection, and heightened regulatory/reputational risk.
examples: Guarantor is a confirmed OFAC SDN match: BR_007_003 blocks Freddie Mac approval of servicing-related actions; Servicer should not proceed with non-residential lease approval workflow.
No confirmed sanctions match: BR_007_003 clears; Servicer may then evaluate BR_017_004 delegated authority conditions (affiliate test, rent level, term, income percentage).
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not ... approve other servicing-related transactions if it determines the Borrower, Borrower Principal, or Guarantor is a confirmed match...

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: A confirmed sanctions/exclusion match blocks Freddie Mac approval of servicing-related transactions, which functionally blocks easement approvals/processing regardless of delegation criteria.
dependencies.rationale: BR_022_001 is about delegated authority to approve easements. Even where delegation exists, BR_007_003 provides an overriding prohibition on approving servicing-related transactions involving sanctioned/excluded parties. Thus, sanctions clearance is a prerequisite compliance gate before executing delegated easement authority workflows.
dependencies.impact: If BR_007_003 fails but BR_022_001 is used, the Servicer may approve an easement in a context Freddie Mac would not permit, creating a high-risk compliance and enforcement issue.
examples: Borrower Principal matches Freddie Mac Exclusionary List: BR_007_003 blocks transaction approvals; Servicer must not proceed under delegated easement authority.
No match: BR_007_003 clears; Servicer then applies BR_022_001 delegation criteria (directing party, tax-exempt debt, materiality determination, etc.).
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not ... approve other servicing-related transactions if it determines ... a confirmed match...

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG: BR_028_005 applies when Freddie Mac considers consenting; BR_007_003 can prevent Freddie Mac from approving/processing servicing-related transactions at all when sanctions matches exist.
dependencies.rationale: BR_028_005 is a threshold rule governing Freddie Mac consent to subordinate financing. BR_007_003 is an overarching prohibition preventing Freddie Mac purchase/approval of certain transactions when parties are confirmed sanctions/exclusion matches. Thus, BR_007_003 is an upstream gate to determine if consent consideration under BR_028_005 is even permissible.
dependencies.impact: If BR_007_003 fails but BR_028_005 proceeds, the compliance workflow may reach an apparent 'pass' on thresholds while the transaction remains unapprovable, creating control failures and operational waste.
examples: Confirmed FHFA Suspended Counterparty List match (subject to order conditions): Freddie Mac will not approve; BR_028_005 combined-debt thresholds are irrelevant.
No match: proceed to BR_028_005 combined-debt calculation and determine if LTV ≤ 85% and DCR ≥ 1.20x.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not ... approve other servicing-related transactions if it determines ... a confirmed match...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL gating dependency: if any Borrower/Principal/Guarantor is a confirmed sanctions/exclusion match, Freddie Mac will not approve the transaction. Commitment issuance cannot proceed if the party is ineligible.
dependencies.rationale: BR_BORROWER_PROHIBITION_007_003 creates an absolute stop for Freddie Mac approvals (purchase/servicing-related approvals). BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_023_006 is an approval step (Letter of Commitment) and is therefore blocked if sanctions screening results in a confirmed match.
dependencies.impact: If sanctions screening fails (confirmed match), Freddie will not issue the SBL Letter of Commitment regardless of whether the Physical Risk Report is provided; underwriting/commitment workflow terminates and the transaction must be declined or restructured with different parties.
examples: During SBL underwriting, the guarantor is a confirmed match to OFAC SDN. The system blocks commitment issuance even though the Physical Risk Report is complete.
Borrower principal appears on the Freddie Mac Exclusionary List; underwriting stops before commitment conditions are evaluated.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not purchase... or approve other servicing-related transactions if it determines the Borrower, Borrower Principal, or Guarantor is a confirmed match...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL gating dependency: delivery is only meaningful if the mortgage is eligible for purchase/acceptance; sanctioned/excluded parties make the delivery non-actionable for Freddie Mac purchase.
dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_PROCESS_020_006 governs SBL final delivery timeliness. However, BR_BORROWER_PROHIBITION_007_003 is a hard ineligibility rule for purchase/approvals. If sanctions/exclusion applies, the delivery workflow should not proceed (or will be rejected), making sanctions clearance a prerequisite gate.
dependencies.impact: If sanctions screening fails, Seller/Servicer may waste effort assembling and transmitting the Final Delivery Package; Freddie Mac will not purchase/approve the transaction, triggering operational disruption and potential contractual remedies.
examples: Seller prepares Final Delivery Package before running sanctions checks; later a confirmed match is found and Freddie rejects purchase—delivery becomes moot.
Automated delivery portal prevents upload/boarding when sanctions flag is confirmed for borrower principal.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not purchase a Mortgage... if it determines the Borrower... or Guarantor is a confirmed match...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL gating dependency: underwriting package documentation is only actionable if the parties are not sanctioned/excluded; otherwise Freddie will not purchase.
dependencies.rationale: BR_DELIVERS_LOAN_DOCUMENTATION_019_001 is triggered when requesting purchase with a non-Independent Property and requires submission of specific underwriting information. BR_BORROWER_PROHIBITION_007_003 creates a hard stop for purchase; therefore sanctions clearance should precede (or gate) assembling/submitting this package for Freddie review.
dependencies.impact: If sanctions screening fails, the Seller expends time and legal resources (shared access/use agreement analysis, counsel confirmations) for a loan that cannot be purchased, causing avoidable cost and timeline slippage.
examples: Counsel completes shared facilities gap analysis; later OFAC confirmed match is identified and purchase request is rejected.
Workflow automation: sanctions screening must be 'pass' before enabling the 'Submit non-Independent Property package' task.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not purchase a Mortgage... if it determines the Borrower... or Guarantor is a confirmed match...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL gating dependency: conversion delivery/purchase conditions are irrelevant if a confirmed sanctions/exclusion match exists because Freddie will not purchase/approve the transaction.
dependencies.rationale: BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_CONSTRAINT_021_001 is a purchase/conversion eligibility condition (minimum occupancy) for Conversion. BR_BORROWER_PROHIBITION_007_003 is a universal ineligibility rule; thus sanctions clearance gates conversion underwriting and eligibility evaluation.
dependencies.impact: If sanctions screening fails, conversion cannot proceed even if occupancy is 85% for 3 months; the loan is effectively ineligible and the workflow must stop or replace parties.
examples: Construction completes and rent rolls show stabilized occupancy, but borrower principal is on FHFA Suspended Counterparty List; Freddie will not approve conversion.
System logic: do not compute/validate Minimum Occupancy Requirement until sanctions screening is cleared.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not purchase a Mortgage... or approve other servicing-related transactions if it determines... confirmed match...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) - If any borrower/principal/guarantor is a confirmed sanctions/exclusion match, Freddie will not purchase/approve; property-eligibility mitigants (like non-Independent Property purchase conditions) become irrelevant.
dependencies.rationale: BR_007_003 is an absolute prohibition on purchase/transfer/servicing approvals upon confirmed match to specified lists. BR_019_002 governs conditions Freddie may impose to purchase a loan secured by a non-Independent Property. Even if BR_019_002 conditions could be satisfied, the transaction cannot proceed if BR_007_003 is violated. Therefore sanctions screening is a gating prerequisite before spending effort on BR_019_002 mitigations.
dependencies.impact: If BR_007_003 fails: (1) Freddie will not purchase/approve transaction, (2) underwriting/structuring work under BR_019_002 is wasted, (3) potential legal/operational escalation to resolve false positives or terminate the transaction.
examples: Borrower principal matches OFAC SDN; Freddie denies the purchase—no amount of reserves/modifications under BR_019_002 can cure the prohibition.
Guarantor appears on Freddie Mac Exclusionary List; Seller stops Independent Property mitigation analysis and escalates for sanctions resolution.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not purchase a Mortgage... if it determines the Borrower, Borrower Principal, or Guarantor is a confirmed match...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) - Purchase eligibility structuring (non-Independent Property path) cannot proceed if parties are confirmed sanctions/exclusion matches; BR_007_003 blocks purchase outright.
dependencies.rationale: BR_019_003 provides conditions under which Freddie may agree to purchase when the property is not/will not be an Independent Property. BR_007_003 overrides any such eligibility pathway by prohibiting purchase/approvals upon confirmed list match. Therefore BR_007_003 must be cleared before BR_019_003 is meaningfully evaluated.
dependencies.impact: If BR_007_003 fails: immediate stop—Freddie will not purchase regardless of BR_019_003 satisfaction; transaction must be terminated or restructured with different parties (where permissible).
examples: Cross-collateral/coterminous structure meets BR_019_003, but the guarantor matches an exclusion list; Freddie denies purchase.
Borrower principal is flagged on OFAC consolidated list; eligibility memo for BR_019_003 is not advanced to Freddie because sanctions fail.
evidence_quotes: will not purchase a Mortgage... if ... confirmed match to specified sanctions/exclusion lists

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) - Conversion purchase eligibility (occupancy/rent roll conditions) is moot if sanctions/exclusion screening fails because Freddie will not purchase.
dependencies.rationale: BR_019_007 defines stabilized occupancy and rent-roll delivery conditions for eligibility at Conversion. BR_007_003 prohibits purchase and related approvals upon confirmed sanctions/exclusion match. Thus BR_007_003 must be cleared first; otherwise BR_019_007 cannot lead to purchase even if satisfied.
dependencies.impact: If BR_007_003 fails: (1) Freddie will not purchase at Conversion, (2) borrower may incur carrying costs and lose takeout execution, (3) Seller must resolve sanctions match or terminate.
examples: Forward Commitment conversion meets 85% occupancy for 3 months, but borrower principal is on OFAC SDN—Freddie denies purchase at Conversion.
Rent rolls are complete and certified, but guarantor is on Freddie Exclusionary List; conversion package is dead on arrival.
evidence_quotes: will not purchase a Mortgage... if ... confirmed match...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) - Title-policy private transfer fee covenant eligibility is irrelevant if sanctions/exclusion screening fails; purchase is prohibited regardless of title findings.
dependencies.rationale: BR_025_007 is a purchase ineligibility test based on the Title Policy exception for a Private Transfer Fee Covenant created on/after 2/8/2011. BR_007_003 independently prohibits purchase upon confirmed sanctions/exclusion match. Therefore sanctions clearance is a gating prerequisite for proceeding with title-based eligibility analysis and purchase decisioning.
dependencies.impact: If BR_007_003 fails: purchase is denied irrespective of title; compliance workflow should short-circuit to sanctions resolution instead of continuing title review.
examples: Title policy is clean (no private transfer fee covenant), but borrower principal is a confirmed OFAC match; Freddie denies purchase.
Private transfer fee covenant review is underway, but sanctions screening later flags a guarantor; the file is stopped and escalated.
evidence_quotes: will not purchase a Mortgage... if ... confirmed match...
If the Title Policy contains an exception for a Private Transfer Fee Covenant... the Mortgage is ineligible...

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE (3) - Both rules address sanctions/exclusion screening and purchase/approval denial; BR_035_002 expands scope to include property management company and is specific to FHFA SCP Named Parties, complementing the broader list-based screening in BR_007_003.
dependencies.rationale: BR_007_003 covers multiple lists (OFAC SDN, OFAC Consolidated, FHFA Suspended Counterparty List subject to conditions, Freddie Mac Exclusionary List) for borrower/principal/guarantor. BR_035_002 specifically addresses FHFA SCP Named Party status and adds property management company as a covered party. Operationally, these rules should be navigated together during party screening to avoid gaps (e.g., management company SCP exposure not covered by BR_007_003).
dependencies.impact: If BR_007_003 screening is performed without BR_035_002: lender may miss SCP exposure tied to the property management company, resulting in late-stage purchase denial and rework to replace management.
examples: Borrower/principal/guarantor clear OFAC and exclusion lists, but the property management company is on SCP; BR_035_002 catches it while BR_007_003 alone would not.
Sanctions workflow bundles BR_007_003 checks with an additional SCP-management-company check per BR_035_002 before submission.
evidence_quotes: confirmed match to specified sanctions/exclusion lists (OFAC... FHFA Suspended Counterparty List... Freddie Mac Exclusionary List)
if any ... property management company is a Named Party on the ... SCP List

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE dependency: BR_012_005 requires specified opinions to be submitted to Freddie for review/approval at least three business days before origination under certain triggers; BR_012_004 requires a Non-Consolidation Opinion to be included in the Final Delivery Package under certain triggers. Pre-origination submission commonly precedes final delivery inclusion for the same/related legal opinions.
dependencies.rationale: When an opinion is required for delivery, it often must be drafted and (for certain high-balance/special opinions) submitted for Freddie review prior to origination. This creates an operational sequence: submit opinion(s) for review early → later include final executed opinion in delivery package. Automations can prompt early submission based on the same trigger set (loan size/type/opinion type) to prevent downstream delivery defects.
dependencies.impact: If BR_012_005 is missed when applicable, origination/delivery may be delayed or conditioned, increasing the risk that the Final Delivery Package will be incomplete or not accepted on the expected timeline under BR_012_004.
examples: Non-SBL $100M loan: opinions must be submitted ≥3 business days pre-origination (BR_012_005); later, the Non-Consolidation Opinion must appear in the Final Delivery Package if applicable (BR_012_004).
Seller fails to submit required Seniors Housing licensure opinions timely; origination is delayed and final delivery package completion slips, risking delivery conditions.
evidence_quotes: “...must submit specified opinions... not less than three business days prior to the scheduled origination date...”
“Final Delivery Package must include a Non-Consolidation Opinion...”

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE sequencing dependency: BR_012_004 governs final delivery contents, but BR_035_002 can hard-stop purchase/delivery decision-making; it is typically executed earlier to prevent unnecessary final-delivery assembly.
dependencies.rationale: While a lender could technically assemble a Final Delivery Package even if an SCP match exists, in an automated workflow the SCP screen should occur before (or at least concurrently with) costly legal deliverables like Non-Consolidation Opinions. This enables efficient rule navigation: if BR_035_002 fails, BR_012_004 becomes non-actionable for a purchase path.
dependencies.impact: If BR_035_002 fails late (after delivery package prepared), significant legal cost and cycle-time are wasted (e.g., obtaining Non-Consolidation Opinions), and delivery will still be rejected/denied.
examples: Lender orders a Non-Consolidation Opinion for a $50MM non-SBL deal, then later learns the property manager is an SCP named party; the opinion cost/timeline becomes sunk.
Automated workflow: SCP screening is a first-step gate before triggering legal document requests under BR_012_004.
evidence_quotes: Freddie Mac will not purchase any Mortgage...
the Final Delivery Package must include a Non-Consolidation Opinion...

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL dependency (5) because BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 blocks Freddie Mac approvals for servicing-related transactions upon SCP match, superseding the otherwise-available threshold-based consent pathway in BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005.
dependencies.rationale: BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005 provides necessary conditions for Freddie Mac to consider consenting to subordinate financing. BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 blocks Freddie Mac from approving servicing-related transactions when any borrower/principal/property manager is an SCP Named Party. Thus, SCP match is a decisive override.
dependencies.impact: If BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002 triggers, Freddie Mac will not approve consent regardless of satisfying LTV/DCR thresholds. Automation should short-circuit the consent workflow at SCP screening to prevent false-positive approvals and reduce processing time.
examples: Servicer meets combined-debt definition and computes LTV 80% / DCR 1.25x (passes BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005), but borrower is on SCP list; Freddie Mac approval is blocked.
No SCP match: proceed to evaluate combined-debt thresholds per BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005.
evidence_quotes: BR_PURCHASES_PROHIBITION_035_002: "...will not allow or approve... or approve any other servicing-related transaction..."
BR_SERVICES_CONSTRAINT_028_005: "When Freddie Mac considers consenting to Subordinate Financing..."

dependencies.strength_justification: MODERATE dependency (3) because BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_007 provides a general myOptigo remittance submission cutoff that is commonly required to meet any stated remittance due date, including the calendar-based Gold PC remittance deadlines in BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010; however, BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 does not explicitly name myOptigo, so this is a workflow-level sequencing relationship.
dependencies.rationale: BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_007 states operational timing: submit remittance requests no later than 1:00 p.m. ET one Business Day before the due date to ensure Freddie Mac has use of funds by the due date. BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 sets specific due dates (15th/third Business Day after the 15th). In practice, the submission-cutoff rule is used to operationally meet the due dates.
dependencies.impact: If the Servicer plans to remit on the due date in BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010 but fails to submit per the cutoff logic in BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_007 (where applicable), the remittance may post a day late, creating late-remittance exposure (e.g., interest on late remittances).
examples: 15th falls on a Wednesday; Servicer intends to meet the 15th due date for Gold PC remittance but submits the remittance request after 1:00 p.m. ET Tuesday; processing slips and Freddie Mac receives funds late.
Servicer schedules remittance submission on Monday (before 1:00 p.m. ET) to ensure funds availability by the 15th Wednesday.
evidence_quotes: BR_REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD_PROCESS_028_007: "Servicer must submit each remittance no later than 1:00 p.m. Eastern time one Business Day preceding the remittance due date"
BR_SERVICES_PROCESS_015_010: "Servicer must remit to Freddie Mac by the 15th of the month..."

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) — BR_PURCHASES_CONSTRAINT_019_007 makes Freddie Mac purchase eligibility at Conversion contingent on the same 85%/3-month stabilized occupancy standard described in BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_CONSTRAINT_021_001. If 021_001 is not satisfied, purchase at Conversion fails under 019_007.
dependencies.rationale: Rule 021_001 defines the stabilized occupancy threshold and timing for submission and at Conversion. Rule 019_007 states that to be eligible for purchase at Conversion, the property must have stabilized residential occupancy at 85% for the three consecutive months preceding submission of the Conversion Underwriting Package (and also requires monthly certified rent rolls). Thus, the occupancy requirement in 021_001 is a gating prerequisite to the purchase eligibility determination in 019_007.
dependencies.impact: If 021_001 fails: (1) the Conversion Underwriting Package is noncompliant, (2) Freddie Mac will not purchase at Conversion under 019_007, (3) Borrower/Seller may incur extension costs, re-underwriting, and potential transaction failure.
examples: Occupancy is 83%, 86%, 87% in the three months prior to package submission. Because one month is below 85%, the loan is not eligible for purchase at Conversion.
Occupancy is 90% at submission but drops to 82% at Conversion; 021_001 requires the condition be met at both times, so purchase eligibility fails at Conversion.
evidence_quotes: “This requirement must be met both at package submission and at Conversion.”

“To be eligible for purchase at Conversion, the property must have stabilized residential occupancy at 85% for the three consecutive months preceding submission of the Conversion Underwriting Package.”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) — BR_PURCHASES_CONSTRAINT_019_007 requires certified monthly rent rolls from completion of construction through Conversion; BR_LOAN_DELIVERY_PROCESS_021_002 provides the required cadence and certification parties needed to satisfy that purchase-eligibility condition.
dependencies.rationale: Rule 019_007 includes an ongoing documentation requirement: from completion of construction through Conversion, the Borrower must provide a current monthly rent roll certified true/correct by Borrower and property manager. Rule 021_002 operationalizes that same requirement and states the Seller/Servicer must obtain them to support occupancy determination. Therefore, implementing 021_002 is mandatory to satisfy 019_007’s conditions for purchase at Conversion.
dependencies.impact: If 021_002 fails: (1) 019_007’s documentation condition is unmet, (2) Freddie Mac may refuse purchase at Conversion, (3) Seller faces delays, cure conditions, and potential non-delivery/purchase fallout.
examples: Borrower provides rent rolls but only quarterly; the monthly requirement is unmet, causing a purchase condition failure under 019_007.
Rent rolls are monthly but not certified by both required parties; Freddie Mac conditions the file, delaying Conversion.
evidence_quotes: “From completion of construction through Conversion, the Borrower must provide the Seller a current monthly rent roll certified as true and correct by the Borrower and the property manager...”
“Seller/Servicer must obtain these to support physical occupancy determination for Conversion delivery.”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) — Freddie Mac’s decision to impose non-Independent Property purchase conditions (019_002) depends on receiving the non-Independent Property underwriting package information required by 019_001 (facility descriptions, shared access/use agreement analysis, etc.).
dependencies.rationale: Rule 019_001 requires the Seller to submit specified written information as part of the underwriting package when the property is not an Independent Property. Rule 019_002 describes the potential purchase conditions Freddie Mac may impose in that scenario (modifications, reserves, etc.). Freddie Mac cannot reasonably determine feasibility of modifications, reserve sizing, or access/use compliance without the package content mandated by 019_001, making 019_001 a practical prerequisite to applying 019_002.
dependencies.impact: If 019_001 is incomplete/missing: (1) Freddie Mac cannot evaluate or tailor purchase conditions under 019_002, (2) underwriting review stalls, (3) delivery is treated as deficient, (4) transaction timelines extend while information is cured.
examples: Seller requests purchase for a property relying on shared recreational facilities but does not include counsel’s shared access/use agreement compliance confirmation; Freddie Mac cannot determine whether to require modifications or reserves.
Cross-collateralized facilities exist on a separate parcel, but Seller omits the required description of essential facilities on other properties; Freddie Mac conditions the file.
evidence_quotes: “Seller must submit specified information in writing as part of the applicable underwriting package...”
“Freddie Mac may impose one or more purchase conditions...”

dependencies.strength_justification: STRONG (4) — Eligibility path for mortgages secured by non-Independent Properties (019_003) requires structural/legal facts (cross-default, cross-collateralization, coterminous terms, etc.) that are typically established and evidenced via the underwriting package information required by 019_001.
dependencies.rationale: Rule 019_003 sets a conditional purchase-eligibility pathway when the property is not/will not be Independent Property. Rule 019_001 requires the Seller to provide detailed facility/access descriptions and legal counsel analysis regarding Shared Access/Use Agreements, which are key to determining whether the 019_003 requirements and structure are necessary and correctly documented. In practice, 019_003 cannot be confidently applied without the information package required by 019_001.
dependencies.impact: If 019_001 fails: (1) eligibility determination under 019_003 may be delayed or inaccurate, (2) Freddie Mac may suspend review pending missing data, (3) higher risk of later discovery that structure fails eligibility conditions, causing purchase denial late in the process.
examples: Seller asserts the loan is properly cross-collateralized, but omits documentation showing where essential facilities are located; Freddie Mac cannot confirm 019_003 structure aligns with facility locations.
Seller omits gap analysis for shared access agreements; Freddie Mac cannot determine whether independence can be achieved or whether 019_003 alternative requirements must be satisfied.
evidence_quotes: “When requesting Freddie Mac purchase... not an Independent Property, the Seller must submit specified information... as part of the applicable underwriting package...”
“Freddie Mac may agree to purchase the mortgage only if all stated requirements are met...”

dependencies.strength_justification: CRITICAL (5) — SBL delivery timing/package rules (020_006) become effectively moot if Freddie Mac will not purchase due to SCP prohibition (035_002). Operationally, the file should be stopped/blocked before proceeding to delivery.
dependencies.rationale: Rule 020_006 is about timely delivery of an SBL Final Delivery Package. Rule 035_002 states Freddie Mac will not purchase any Mortgage if an SCP match exists. While 035_002 is not a timing rule, it functionally overrides the need to proceed with delivery workflow because purchase cannot occur. For workflow automation, SCP screening must gate delivery efforts.
dependencies.impact: If 035_002 fails: (1) continuing delivery attempts wastes operational effort and may create fee/exception handling, (2) heightened compliance exposure (attempting to transact with a prohibited counterparty), (3) late-stage transaction failure likely.
examples: Seller prepares and submits the SBL Final Delivery Package by noon ET, but an SCP match is identified; Freddie Mac will not purchase, so delivery work provides no value.
SCP match is discovered after documents are uploaded; Seller must unwind the transaction and potentially address reputational/compliance reporting requirements.
evidence_quotes: “Freddie Mac will not purchase any Mortgage...”
“Seller/Servicer must deliver all documents... at or before noon Eastern time on the Mandatory Delivery Date.”

SELLER_SERVICER.definition: A Freddie Mac-approved counterparty that sells mortgages to Freddie Mac and/or services Freddie Mac-owned mortgages under the Multifamily Seller/Servicer Guide and related agreements.
examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings
business_rules: Seller/Servicers must comply with the Freddie Mac Multifamily Seller/Servicer Guide and applicable Bulletins with specific effective dates.
Delegated authorities (e.g., underwriting/servicing) constrain what actions a Seller/Servicer may take without Freddie Mac approval.
Seller/Servicers must maintain operational capacity and financial condition consistent with Freddie Mac eligibility requirements.

FREDDIE_MAC.definition: The government-sponsored enterprise that purchases, securitizes, and oversees servicing of multifamily mortgages in accordance with its Guide, Bulletins, and program requirements.
examples: Freddie Mac Multifamily purchase of a delivered mortgage
Freddie Mac issuance of Bulletin M2026-2 dated 04/21/26
Freddie Mac securitization into a Participation Certificate (PC)
business_rules: Freddie Mac establishes Guide requirements and enforces compliance through delivery reviews, QC, and servicing oversight.
Bulletins introduce changes with effective dates; impacted transactions must apply the correct version by date and applicability.
Freddie Mac may require corrective action or repurchase/remedies for noncompliance.

BORROWER.definition: The obligor(s) responsible for repayment under a multifamily mortgage and for making payments that are remitted through the Servicer to Freddie Mac or security holders.
examples: Single-asset entity (SAE) borrower owning a 150-unit property
Limited partnership borrower with a managing member
Borrower under a loan modification during servicing
business_rules: Borrower eligibility and credit profile affect underwriting decisions and ongoing servicing risk assessments.
Borrower payment performance drives delinquency status and default management actions.
Borrower communications (e.g., adverse action, default notices) must satisfy legal and Guide requirements.

LOAN_APPLICATION.definition: The borrower’s formal request for credit for a mortgage, including financial, property, and sponsorship information used for underwriting and approval decisions.
examples: New acquisition loan application for a multifamily property
Refinance loan application submitted via a delegated Seller/Servicer
Loan application declined triggering an adverse action notice
business_rules: Incomplete applications may not be underwritten or delivered until required third-party reports and borrower information are obtained.
Approval/denial decisions must be supported by documented underwriting rationale and required notices.
Application data must be consistent with delivered loan data used for purchase and securitization.

MORTGAGE.definition: A multifamily real estate loan (including note and security instrument) that may be originated, delivered to Freddie Mac, serviced, and potentially securitized into PCs.
examples: Fixed-rate multifamily mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac
Floating-rate mortgage held in a warehouse line prior to delivery
Mortgage pooled into a 55-Day Multi PC securitization
business_rules: Delivered mortgages must satisfy Freddie Mac underwriting, documentation, title/insurance, and delivery requirements.
Mortgage terms and servicing parameters must align with the remittance and reporting rules applicable to the product/PC structure.
Noncompliance can trigger purchase denial, delivery conditions, repurchase, or servicing remedies.

PROMISSORY_NOTE.definition: The executed debt instrument evidencing the borrower’s obligation to repay the mortgage under specified terms.
examples: Executed note with fixed interest rate and 30-year amortization
Modified note executed as part of a loan modification agreement
Note endorsed to Freddie Mac upon delivery
business_rules: The note must be properly executed and endorsed/assigned as required for delivery and enforceability.
Note terms must match delivered data and servicing setup to avoid reporting and remittance errors.
Any modification must be documented and, where required, approved and reported per Guide requirements.

SECURITY_INSTRUMENT.definition: The mortgage/deed of trust/security deed granting a lien on the property as collateral for the mortgage obligation.
examples: Deed of trust recorded in county land records
Mortgage security instrument with first-lien position
Security instrument amended via recorded modification
business_rules: Recording and lien perfection must satisfy delivery requirements and title insurance coverage expectations.
Lien position and permitted exceptions are constrained by program and Guide requirements.
Recorded instrument data must align with the title policy and closing documentation.

MULTIFAMILY_PROPERTY.definition: The income-producing residential real estate collateral securing a multifamily mortgage, including physical characteristics and legal/ownership attributes.
examples: 200-unit garden-style apartment community in Texas
Mixed-use property with residential units above retail space
Affordable housing property with regulatory restrictions
business_rules: Property eligibility and risk attributes drive underwriting standards and third-party report requirements.
Collateral must be properly insured and covered by acceptable title insurance with required endorsements/exceptions.
Changes to property condition, occupancy, or ownership can trigger servicing reporting and covenant compliance actions.

APPRAISAL_REPORT.definition: A valuation report prepared by a qualified appraiser to estimate the market value and/or other collateral metrics used in underwriting and delivery.
examples: MAI appraisal supporting a $35M value conclusion
Update appraisal required due to aging report date at closing
Appraisal with extraordinary assumptions disclosed
business_rules: Appraisal must meet scope, recency, and independence requirements applicable to the transaction and product.
Appraised value and assumptions must be consistent with underwriting conclusions and loan sizing.
Material appraisal deficiencies can block approval, delivery, or require remediation and re-review.

CREDIT_REPORT.definition: A third-party report summarizing credit history and obligations for borrower principals/guarantors as required for underwriting and risk assessment.
examples: Tri-merge credit report for key principals
Business credit report for borrowing entity
Credit report showing recent derogatory items
business_rules: Credit reports must be current enough for underwriting and reflect the correct parties per program requirements.
Material adverse findings require documented evaluation and may affect eligibility/terms or trigger decline.
Credit report data used in underwriting must reconcile to application and delivered loan file documentation.

TITLE_INSURANCE_POLICY.definition: A title insurance policy insuring the lien priority and marketability of title subject to allowed exceptions and endorsements required for Freddie Mac delivery.
examples: ALTA loan policy issued with required endorsements
Title policy with specific permitted easement exceptions
Title policy requiring curative action for a recorded lien
business_rules: Title insurance must meet minimum coverage, insured party, and endorsement requirements for delivery.
Unpermitted exceptions or defects must be cured or otherwise addressed per Guide before delivery.
Policy amount and insured lien position must align with the mortgage and recording details.

HAZARD_INSURANCE_POLICY.definition: Property insurance coverage protecting the collateral against specified hazards and satisfying Freddie Mac insurance requirements for the mortgage.
examples: Property hazard policy with Freddie Mac named as mortgagee/loss payee
Insurance renewal evidence provided prior to policy expiration
Policy change increasing deductible beyond permitted limits
business_rules: Minimum coverage types/amounts and maximum deductible limits must be satisfied throughout the loan term.
Lapses or insufficient coverage trigger servicing actions and may constitute an event of default.
Policy endorsements and loss payee language must align with Guide requirements and closing instructions.

CLOSING_PACKAGE.definition: The executed set of closing deliverables (loan documents and required certificates/evidence) supporting enforceability, delivery, and file retention requirements.
examples: Complete closing package including recorded security instrument and final title policy
Closing package pending receipt of updated insurance certificate
Digitally stored closing package in an approved eVault
business_rules: All required executed and recorded documents must be present and compliant before final delivery to Freddie Mac.
Defects (missing signatures, incorrect vesting, recording issues) require cure within specified timelines.
File retention must satisfy Guide retention periods and audit accessibility requirements.

LOAN_DELIVERY.definition: The process and event of submitting an originated mortgage and required data/documents to Freddie Mac for purchase and/or securitization eligibility.
examples: Final delivery accepted after satisfaction of title insurance conditions
Delivery suspended pending third-party report update
Delivery rejected due to data/document mismatch
business_rules: Delivery requires complete and accurate data and documentation per Guide chapters for documentation and deliveries.
Outstanding delivery conditions must be resolved before purchase/settlement or within permitted post-closing timeframes.
Delivered loan attributes must match legal documents and third-party reports to avoid purchase and securitization defects.

WAREHOUSING.definition: The interim holding period/process where a mortgage is funded and held prior to final delivery/purchase, often under a warehouse line with controls for collateral and documentation.
examples: Loan held in warehouse pending final title policy receipt
Bailee letter executed for note custody
Warehouse release after Freddie Mac settlement
business_rules: Custody and control of original notes and collateral documents must be maintained to preserve enforceability and delivery integrity.
Warehousing timelines and documentation controls must prevent stale reports and post-closing defects.
Warehouse release must align with settlement/purchase and custodial transfer requirements.

SERVICER.definition: The party responsible for collecting borrower payments, administering escrow/insurance, managing defaults, and performing accounting and reporting to Freddie Mac.
examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors
business_rules: Servicers must report and remit on a monthly basis using the correct net yield method and product rules.
Servicers must track and report delinquency status accurately and timely for Active/Delinquent/Deactivated classifications.
Servicing actions (e.g., modifications, defaults) may require approvals and must be documented and reported per Guide.

SERVICING_REPORT.definition: The periodic reporting submitted by the Servicer covering loan status, payment activity, delinquency, and other servicing metrics required by Freddie Mac.
examples: Monthly servicing report indicating 30 days delinquent status
Servicing report reflecting partial payment and suspense handling
Corrected servicing report resubmitted after reconciliation
business_rules: Reports must be submitted on schedule and reconcile to remittances and servicing system records.
Delinquency and status classifications must follow defined terms (e.g., Active Mortgage, deactivation for accounting/reporting).
Material reporting errors require correction, root-cause analysis, and may trigger QC findings.

ACCOUNTING_REPORT.definition: The Servicer’s accounting and remittance-related reporting to Freddie Mac, including interest calculations, net yield reporting, and reconciliation items.
examples: Monthly accounting report where accounting net yield differs due to purchase premium
Reconciliation report identifying remittance variance
Exception report for incorrect net yield setup
business_rules: Accounting Net Yield must be reported and used for remittances consistent with Required Net Yield rules, adjusted for premium/discount where applicable.
Monthly remittance and reporting must reconcile; variances require timely correction and explanation.
Reporting logic must align with product type (e.g., PC structures with payment delay) and loan status.

MORTGAGE_STATUS.definition: A classification of a mortgage for servicing, accounting, and reporting purposes, including terms such as Active Mortgage, Delinquent Mortgage, and deactivation status.
examples: ACTIVE status where borrower payments are current
DELINQUENT status at 30 days delinquent
DEACTIVATED for accounting/reporting after specified servicing action
business_rules: Status must be determined consistently using defined delinquency measures and Guide definitions.
Deactivation for accounting/reporting must meet criteria and be tracked with reasons and effective dates.
Status drives downstream actions: default management workflow, reporting requirements, and potentially securitization cashflow treatment.

DELINQUENCY.definition: A servicing measure indicating how late a borrower is on required payments, commonly expressed in days delinquent (e.g., 30 days delinquent).
examples: 30 days delinquent classification as of month-end
60 days delinquent with two installments past due
Cured delinquency after full reinstatement payment
business_rules: Days delinquent must be calculated consistently and drive correct status and reporting classification.
Delinquency buckets trigger escalation timelines, notices, and default management actions.
Delinquency reporting must reconcile to borrower payment posting and suspense rules.

PARTICIPATION_CERTIFICATE.definition: A securitization instrument (PC) representing interests in pooled mortgages, where borrower payments are passed through to PC holders under defined delay and remittance structures.
examples: 55-Day Multi PC with 55-day payment delay
Standard PC backed by a defined pool of multifamily mortgages
PC tranche issued from a securitization transaction
business_rules: PC type and payment delay determine timing of cashflow pass-through and servicing remittance obligations.
Only eligible mortgages meeting pooling criteria may be securitized into a given PC type.
Servicer reporting and remittance must align with PC structure to prevent investor distribution errors.

MORTGAGE_POOL.definition: A defined group of securitized mortgages aggregated for issuance of a Participation Certificate (PC), subject to pooling eligibility criteria and servicing/remittance rules.
examples: Pool of 55-Day Multi PC Securitized Mortgages
Pool assembled from multiple delivered mortgages in a month
Pool with weighted average coupon reported to investors
business_rules: Pooling criteria restrict which mortgages can be included based on product features and compliance status.
Pool metrics must be computed from loan-level data and reconcile to issuance disclosures.
Servicer of record must support pooling cashflow rules and reporting timelines.

ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD.definition: The net yield rate used by the Servicer to report and remit interest to Freddie Mac on a monthly basis; it equals Required Net Yield except when mortgages were sold at a discount or premium.
examples: Accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase
Accounting net yield adjusted due to premium purchase price
Accounting net yield adjusted due to discount purchase price
business_rules: Accounting Net Yield must be set and maintained accurately to drive correct monthly interest remittance.
When a mortgage is sold at premium/discount, Accounting Net Yield deviates from Required Net Yield as defined by Guide accounting rules.
Net yield setup errors require correction and may require reconciliations and make-whole adjustments.

REQUIRED_NET_YIELD.definition: The baseline net yield rate required under Freddie Mac remittance rules; used as a reference point for Accounting Net Yield reporting and remittances.
examples: Required net yield for a fixed-rate mortgage remittance type
Required net yield for a specific PC program
Required net yield updated due to contract amendment
business_rules: Required Net Yield is determined by product/remittance terms and must be applied consistently to eligible loans.
Servicer must validate Required Net Yield inputs and changes against program/contract references.
Differences between Required Net Yield and Accounting Net Yield must be explainable under premium/discount rules.

ADVERSE_ACTION_NOTICE.definition: An oral or written communication informing a borrower of refusal to grant an application for an extension of credit, including information required by applicable law.
examples: Written adverse action notice sent after application denial
Oral adverse action notice followed by required written disclosures
Adverse action notice citing insufficient credit history and DSCR
business_rules: If an application is refused, the adverse action notice must include all legally required content and be delivered within applicable timelines.
Notice content must align with underwriting decision rationale and file documentation.
Notices must be retained for audit and compliance review according to retention requirements.

FILE_RETENTION_POLICY.definition: The requirements defining how long and in what form loan origination, delivery, and servicing records must be retained and made available for review.
examples: Retention policy for closing package originals vs. electronic copies
Retention policy for servicing reports and remittance reconciliations
Legal hold applied to a loan file under dispute
business_rules: Loan files must be retained for the required period and be retrievable for Freddie Mac reviews, audits, and regulatory requests.
Electronic storage must preserve integrity, authenticity, and access controls for sensitive records.
Destruction must be suspended under legal hold or active investigation requirements.

THIRD_PARTY_REPORT.definition: An externally produced report used in underwriting, delivery, or servicing (e.g., appraisal, credit, engineering, environmental), subject to currency and quality requirements.
examples: Environmental site assessment report used in underwriting
Property condition assessment report required for delivery
Engineering report triggering repair escrow requirements
business_rules: Third-party reports must meet eligibility, independence, and recency requirements for the applicable loan product and stage.
Material findings must be evaluated and may require conditions, reserves, repairs, or denial.
Reports must be retained and traceable to underwriting decisions and delivered loan terms.

QUALITY_CONTROL_REVIEW.definition: A formal review process assessing loan file completeness, data accuracy, and compliance with Guide requirements across origination, delivery, and servicing.
examples: Post-delivery QC review identifying missing endorsement on title policy
Servicing QC review identifying delinquency misclassification
QC review requiring remediation plan and re-testing
business_rules: QC reviews must be performed at required frequencies and cover required scopes (origination, delivery, servicing).
Findings must be tracked to remediation and validated for closure within timelines.
Repeat findings may trigger escalated oversight, restrictions, or remedies.

GUIDE_BULLETIN.definition: A Freddie Mac-issued bulletin that updates, clarifies, or amends Multifamily Seller/Servicer Guide requirements with stated publication and effective dates.
examples: Bulletin M2026-2 published 04/21/26 affecting multiple chapters
Bulletin updating servicing reporting requirements
Bulletin clarifying delivery documentation expectations
business_rules: Transactions must follow the Guide requirements as amended by applicable Bulletins based on effective dates.
Seller/Servicers must monitor Bulletins and implement changes by required deadlines.
Conflicts between prior guidance and Bulletin updates are resolved per Freddie Mac’s stated applicability rules.

SUBMITS_APPLICATION.definition: The borrower initiates the credit request by providing an application and supporting information for underwriting and decisioning.
examples: Borrower submits a refinance application with sponsorship and property details
Borrower submits a new acquisition application through a Seller/Servicer channel
Borrower submits a revised application after requested corrections
business_rules: Submission completeness requirements determine whether underwriting can proceed and what follow-up documents are needed.
Application data must be traceable to source records used in underwriting and delivery.
Material changes after submission may require re-underwriting or updated third-party reports.

UNDERWRITES.definition: The Seller/Servicer (or its underwriting function) evaluates the application for eligibility, risk, and compliance with program and Guide requirements.
examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows
business_rules: Underwriting must consider required third-party reports and document rationale for key decisions.
Delegated authority limits and approval conditions must be followed and recorded.
Underwriting conclusions must align with delivered loan data and legal documents.

APPROVES_MORTGAGE.definition: The Seller/Servicer issues an approval/commitment for the mortgage consistent with underwriting results and applicable authorities.
examples: Seller/Servicer approves a fixed-rate mortgage subject to final title policy conditions
Approval includes covenants requiring insurance coverage levels and reporting
Approval denied due to unacceptable credit or collateral risk
business_rules: Approval must satisfy delegated authority and Guide eligibility requirements for the selected product.
Approval conditions must be satisfied prior to closing and/or delivery as required.
Denials or adverse terms must be supported by documented reasons and required notices.

SECURES.definition: The property serves as the collateral that secures repayment obligations under the mortgage.
examples: A 200-unit property secures a first-lien Freddie Mac mortgage
Property secures a refinanced mortgage after payoff of prior lien
Property secures a mortgage that is later securitized into a PC pool
business_rules: Collateral eligibility, condition, and legal attributes affect underwriting and required third-party reports.
Collateral must maintain required insurance coverage and title protection through the loan term.
Changes impacting collateral (damage, loss of coverage) require servicing actions and reporting.

EVIDENCES_DEBT.definition: The promissory note evidences the debt obligation that the mortgage represents.
examples: Note executed at closing evidences the mortgage note amount and interest rate
Modified note evidences updated payment terms after a modification
